Service Comment:
EXCELLENT - nothing more needs to be said . . . 
Service (in the lounge, restaurant, stateroom) was always highest quality but never intrusive.  
Best of all river cruises we have been on to date (total of 6 on 4 different brands)
Product Comment:
ENTIRE Staff was exceptional.  By day 2 they already knew our dietary needs, likes and dislikes, timing of meals, etc.  Front desk was all about help addressing the smallest things immediately.  Food at every meal was excellent - not a single concern or dislike.
On the downside, the ship was in need of some TLC and was cramped in many places - particularly, the stateroom, lounge and restaurant.  A bit more free room is required, especially, in the lounge and restaurant - the room I can understand and didn't spend a lot of time there except sleeping but 2 adults trying to dress to get to meals or tours was a bit of a challenge.
All of the 3rd party guides for the excursions were very good - easy to understand, knowledgeable and always seemed to walk at the right pace. Very mindful of slower walkers and keeping the groups together but being sure we saw everything.
While the ship di have some seasonal decorations for Christmas, they were pretty generic and could have been more. What was there, was tastefully done.

Service Comment: Very good.  Our Butler was good and well as the dining room and the bar staff.
Product Comment: Our first UniWorld Cruise was eleven years ago.  Since then the food at dinner has improved significantly and is very good.  The breakfast and lunch buffets are just okay.  The excursions are still consistently good.  The boat was not in as good of condition as expected.  For example, the drapes in our suite 411 were torn and/or rotted and the room was just not fresh and attractive, there was a sizable stain in the hallway carpet down from our room, fabric was stained in the lounge and the windows frames in the dining room looked dirty but were really chipped. The uniformed staff were very well groomed and professional.  I would suggest that staff who do not wear uniforms dress in a comparable well groomed, professional  manner.  The attire of some passengers has become more casual/sloppy over the years.  I believe that it would be good idea to at least request that passengers dress a minimum of upscale casual for dinners.  Passengers' attire does impact the enjoyment of other passengers in the dining room.  Otherwise, UniWorld will become much more like Viking.  We will do another UniWorld trip but will be more inquisitive and selective about the condition of the boat.  We have recommended UniWorld to many people who only knew about Viking.  We are going to be less likely to continue doing so until we are more comfortable that are recommendations are accurate.  We hope that these comments have been helpful.  We want UniWorld to continue to succeed.  One last thought.  We received a very nice blue Uniworld bag on our first cruise.  The bag on this cruise was not a good bag.

Service Comment: In general meal service was slow
Product Comment: We were excited to cruise in a French balcony cabin with Uniworld especially since they were supposed to be a step above Viking or Amawaterways both of which we cruised before. Unfortunately, Uniworld only exceeded the others in the general decor of the ship and stateroom. The food, service and shore excursions were no better than the others and in some cases worse. Upon returning from shore excursions we were only welcomed back aboard one time with a cup of lukewarm tea. Upon departure at 6am to meet an early flight no one bade us farewell and the only food offered were some cookies in the Cafe. The cruise director did make a cursory appearance on the bus before departing to the airport but we were sitting halfway back in the bus and couldn't understand him. There are other examples of the lack of caring customer service but you get the idea. Needless to say we will be cruising with other companies in the future and save a lot of dollars.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are glad to hear that you enjoyed the decor of the ship and stateroom. Please feel free to reach out to us at customerservice@uniworld.com if you would like to provide more details.

Product Comment:
PreCruise Transfer - POOR - No one was at the assigned pick-up point at the airport.  The wrong # was provided for transfer agent (in Great Britain).  When we called, the number was not connected.  Thus, when no one was there for pick-up -- PANIC -- Fortunately, the agent called us from the UK and we connected.  VERY poor communication from UniWorld for this.  They 'dropped the ball' on this one.   We should not have had to go through this VERY stressful beginning of our journey.

PreCruise Experience - Engelberg - FANTASTIC -- Àlvaro Morcillo is by far the best Host/Guide we have ever had during a pre/post cruise experience.  UniWorld is fortunate to have him representing them.

Cruise itself -- 
The ship - Comfortable with fantastic insulation between cabins.  We did not hear our 'neighbors' at all.  That was appreciated!    However, the acoustics in the restaurant were NOT good.  It was LOUD when near and at capacity.  It was difficult to communicate comfortably at times.

Wonderful cruise manager - Patricia Queioros.  Her planning, personality and communication were excellent.
Food good.  
Itinerary lived up to its billing.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jeffrey Weinman, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Danube Holiday Markets (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20107-50183
Service Comment: See my first comment
Product Comment:
This is our second cruise with you and possibly our last. On our first cruise, which was Amsterdam to Switzerland everything was beyond expectations, especially when it came to the chef and his creations.

The chef on board the Teresa Maria in my opinion is anything but a professional chef. His lack of creativity and caring was obvious through the whole cruise, especially to the inconsistencies we experienced. Chef served the same lukewarm chicken soup, five out of six nights. Dinners were either overcooked or undercooked.  Breakfast made to order eggs were always overcooked no matter how much we asked for eggs over easy, the morning bacon  served was either overcooked or close to being raw.   The buffet lunches which we were shocked to see were surprisingly bad and came off as being of a low quality cruise. 

One evening, the chef was carving and serving lamb. I asked him for a small piece,  when I sat down to eat it, as soon as I put my knife into it I was shocked to find it was nothing but a fat slice that did not have enough meat on it for me to even take one bite. (See photo) He should be ashamed of what he put on that plate. 
 
With regards to the staff on board, the only challenges we had was with the food and beverage department. There were a couple servers that we would do our best to avoid because of their lack of professionalism. One evening a gentlemen, dressed in a white suit, which I took is the restaurant manager, came over to ask how our dinner was. I told him honestly that it was average at best and explained the wonderful experience we had on the first cruise we took with you. There was a couple sitting next to us that chimed in about how truly poor their dinner was. 

I came from the hospitality industry, owned a corporate event company and retired after 28 years. I produced incentive, globally, and worked with many chefs during my career. When I explained this to the restaurant manager and showed him a photo of the lamb(fat) the chef serve me he tried making excuses instead of apologizing. 

Additionally, I explained to him that I felt the marketing of this cruise was incomplete in that nowhere's did it say they would be serving buffet lunches and a couple buffet dinners. For the amount of money we spent on this cruise, the last thing we expected was a Viking style buffet. 

The next morning when I came into the dining room to have breakfast the same restaurant manager told me he hopes "I'm in a better mood today". A completely unprofessional way to treat a guest. 

We were planning to take your Italian cruise from Venice down to Greece in 2026 but after this cruise the odds of that happening are extremely low.

A very disappointed client, 
Jeffrey Weinman
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback which has been passed on for further review. We are sorry to hear that your trip did not meet the standards of your previous cruise. If you would like to follow-up with our team regarding your experience, please email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Product Comment:
This my 4th Uniworld cruise and it was good. Food is at its best at dinner. The buffets are tired. No real change in 5 years.

I have noticed the country club casual is gone to caps, logo t-shirts and dirty tennis shoes at dinner. I prefer the country club casual.

The attention to detail seems to have diminished. Examples: shower drain made noises almost constantly. The response was “they just do that”. The drapes weren’t working and dirty, I rehung part of them.  There was a huge spill in the hall midway through cruise that remained.

My impression was this was the last cruise of the year and the ship was to be refurbished so things were “let go”.

The excursions were good. The gift exchange was a good idea. 


Gabriel was great. Enjoyed his service. The staff wearing uniforms always looked nice. It may help for all staff to wear uniforms.

Thanks for the opportunity to evaluate my experience. Hope to see you soon. On another cruise.

Product Comment: We included the Jordan pre-cruise extension and were very glad we did.  Jordan was amazing.  From the Roman ruins in Jerash, to Mt Nebo and the Dead Sea, to Aqaba on the Red Sea!   But Wadi Rum and Petra were the highlights!   Amazing places and we would highly recommend the extension!   It is well worth the additional cost.  But of course, that isn’t to say the main itinerary in Egypt wasn’t also spectacular!   Truly a fantastic trip seeing all the antiquities that Egypt has to offer!   We had excellent guides in both Jordan and Egypt.  They were very knowledgeable and were also a pleasure to spend our time with.  Everything was top shelf, the food was amazing, the staff everywhere we stayed was exceptional, the accommodations on and off the ship were exceptional!   Can’t say enough good about our trip!   Thank you to Uniworld and all the staff for a trip we will remember forever!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Mary Ann Adamson, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Sphinx, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the S.S. Sphinx (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=23862-55106
Product Comment:
Uniworld is truly five-star in every possible way. From the moment we stepped aboard, the ship itself felt elegant and serene, and sailing the Nile was nothing short of a fairy tale—timeless, magical, and unforgettable. What truly elevated this journey, though, was the incredible crew. Their warmth, professionalism, and attention to detail were unmatched, from the gracious daily greetings to the thoughtful turndown service each evening.

Every aspect of the experience was exceptional: expertly curated tours, outstanding cuisine, seamless service, and an atmosphere that made us feel completely cared for. A special highlight was our Egyptologist, whose passion and depth of knowledge were remarkable. His ability to bring thousands of years of history to life over the course of just ten days was extraordinary and made every site more meaningful. This was more than a vacation—it was an inspiring, immersive experience that we will treasure forever.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you very much for your detailed review. We are so happy to hear that our staff provided you with a five-star experience while sailing with Uniworld on the S.S. Sphinx. We appreciate that you took note of our expertly curated tours, outstanding cuisine, seamless service, and atmosphere. Additionally, we are pleased to hear that the Egyptologist was able to offer you a unique and memorable experience capturing the details of Egypt's history while connecting with our guests. We continuously strive to offer a high level of hospitality and professionalism for our guests when sailing with us. We look forward to having an opportunity to welcome you on board again soon!

Service Comment: Staff was wonderful. Got to know you and your likes. Looked after your every wish. Room was cleaned 2 times a day and often they would leave a little surprise for you. Food done to your liking. We liked trying different foods offered every day.
Product Comment:
The cruise on the Nile was wonderful. Staff was welcoming and always had a smile for us. When returning from sightseeing they always welcomed us with refreshments. The food was excellent and they were very accommodating for special food requests. Our room was spacious with plenty of closet space. Bathroom also roomy enough for getting ready every day. Had a table and couple of chairs and we were able to make tea everyday in our room.
The tours started early to ensure arrival before the crowds. Our Egyptologist was extremely knowledgeable and we went to every site we had ever hoped we'd see. Every last detail down to airport transfers and getting Visa at the airport were handled expertly. The only negative was our stay at the Ritz for 2 days before and 2 days after the actual river cruise. Kept up each night by extremely loud music playing all hours of the night. We didn't sleep at all there. But on the Tosca sleeping was great! I would definitely recommend this cruise with 5 stars. Took a star away for the stay at the Ritz.

Service Comment: Excellent service. Delicious cuisine.  We enjoyed the regional cuisine and evening entertainment.
Product Comment:
The Jordan pre-cruise tour and the River Nile cruise were both excellent. The guides were knowledgeable and informative.  Although in Jordan, we did feel like several of the stops were a high pressure sales opportunity, especially the mosaic workshop,  and even wondered if the guide had a financial interest in sales made there.

We liked the itinerary for Egypt but would have preferred more time in Wadi Rum and Petra on the precruise tour. In Wadi Rum, we arrived just before dinner and left immediately after breakfast, which meant we didn’t really have any daylight to explore or enjoy the surroundings.  The day we went to Petra had a lot of driving, making it seem like the time in Petra was very compressed.

In Egypt, we enjoyed all of the excursions and Mohamed was a superb guide.  However, were a disappointed that so many excursions (“masterpiece experiences”) were not included in the cruise price.  It seemed that for an all inclusive fare, it really didn’t live up to the brand promise.

The evening entertainment and the regional food added to the experience. The ship staff were all very professional and friendly.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Emma Sczudlo, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Duchess, tour=Classic Christmas Markets (FRA-NUE) 25, tourdirector=21609-51800
Service Comment:
Onboard, we were particularly impressed by: 
- Norman - he sought me out at the beginning of every meal and pointed out vegan options on the buffet or menu with a smile. He checked in on us throughout our meals to make sure we were happy -- and we always were, especially when we got to chat with him a bit. 
- Chef Darko - his menus were delicious and gorgeously plated. He even made sure that there were vegan passed h'ors deurves for the receptions in the Lounge; I've never felt so included, being able to eat at every occasion! 
- Desi - it was such a pleasure to chat with him; he was so friendly and easy to talk to. At lunch the first day, I asked for a half cup of water to make a vitamin packet, and he brought me a half cup of water every day thereafter; that was so thoughtful! 
- Gabriel - was very helpful, always ready to identify and address our needs. He was kind and always smiling; it was a pleasure whenever we were served by him. 
-  Milla - was so sweet. We found ourselves lingering by the soup area to chat with her over the bar! 
- Costin - I was blown away that he remembered my name the first morning on the cruise, after only meeting me once the night before. He made lots of special cocktails for me because I never knew what I wanted to order, and they were always delicious and totally my style! We always looked forward to seeing him after dinner. 
- Venelin - he was so kind and professional! He always remembered my dad's orders and he always brought me water, without me having to ask, because he knew I'd want it. I will think of him and smile whenever I use my Nuremberg Christmas Market mug!
Product Comment:
My parents and I had such a fabulous time on our recent Uniworld Classic Christmas Markets cruise (Dec 8-15). We were blown away by the attentive service of the crew, and we loved the itinerary. It was so easy to board the busses and participate in the tours. We appreciated the cleanliness of the busses, the professionalism and high level of knowledge of the tour guides, and the excellent driving too. 

We loved the events on board too - especially the Black and White Party (see attached picture) and the singer who came for the Onesie party. 

We can't wait to cruise with Uniworld again!

Service Comment: Excellent service
Product Comment:
We had a great time. Uniworld did everything right, always good service, great food, and a comfortable, although older ship (Tosca). It helped that our ship was half full and the weather in December was in the low 70s. The WiFi was spotty on the ship, and that would be something to be aware of. When in Cairo we stayed at the Ritz-Carlton, and that was just okay. It is Egypt after all.
Special shout out to our wonderful guide and egyptologist Mohammed who was a limitless source of information, warm, and entertaining, always with a smile on his face. He made the trip really special.
We highly recommend this cruise.

Service Comment: Meal service was excellent, very attentive waiters. Chef circulated through the dining room regularly to be sure everything met his standards and everyone was happy. Bar staff remembered drink orders and were fun. Good room attendants, excellent massage therapist, everyone smiled all the time.
Product Comment:
Our Egypt visit and Nile cruise were fantastic. The ship and rooms were attractive and comfortable, the food was great, all of the tours and activities were well-planned and executed, and the ship staff were excellent and pleasant. Our group’s Egyptologist, was great at providing detail, insight and context about everything we were seeing and visiting and he greatly enriched our experience. 
Only caution about this tour are that the Uniworld Tosca has no elevator so be prepared for lots of stairs and lots of walking on uneven ground during many of the tour activities. If they offer you a tour of the ship’s kitchen and bridge, take it - great to see behind the scenes and say thank you in person.

Product Comment:
cruise overall was great. food was hit and miss, but the kitchen made every effort to make what we liked. The cuisine is inherently limited due to location. house staff was terrific. front desk was terrific. Lounge staff was terrific. five stars for that service.

My complaint is really about the accommodation for a repeat client. Nile was the second cruise with Uniworld this year. The boat was only half full. I put in for a bid for an upgrade to the next class of room. I know FOR A FACT that there were various vacant rooms on the second and third level of the ship because the cruise ship manager took me on a tour when I asked. I saw the vacant rooms. Unused. Since the rooms were available, and only a half full boat, I think the reservations staff should have upgraded me. And as an aside, our bus group included a "travel influencer" from the UK that had a free Royal Suite. Yes, they filmed our trip (with me in the video) for use by Uniworld to use in their marketing and their travel blog.

Product Comment:
Before I agreed to go on the river cruise of the Christmas Markets in Switzerland, France and Germany I informed my wife that this would be my last  bus for our transfer to the airport in Cologne I was worn out and longing to be home. About half way to the airport I realized I had left my passport in the ship safe!
My wife and I considered our options…
When began to talk with the driver, but he couldn’t help.
But it was the young man on the bus and the young woman who met us at the airport that saved our trip from disaster. And believe me, it was their third iteration that worked! In the end it was a colleague of theirs that was able to pick up my passport and bring it to the airport! We were so relieved and happy that we tried to give each of them a gratuity to reflect our appreciation! They told us this was just doing their jobs! Well I can’t tell you what to do, but the fourth star I gave was ALL because of those three young employees of yours. Their refusal to give up saved my departure and final memories!
My gratitude to each of them!

Product Comment:
This was our 5th Uniworld cruise and our favorite. The staff was the absolute best. The only comments I have are these:
*there was never any mention of a 4 hour bus ride to the boat.
*it would be good to have some small sandwiches or appetizers late afternoon around 3:00-4:00….the only items we could get were chips or nuts. 
*there was never premium wine list had some wines to purchase but they ran out of several.
*the day we took the 2 hour bus trip to Ho Chi Minh we ate breakfast at 7:00am…boarded a bus for over 2 hours…did a tour for 2 hours in an un air conditioned building….then dropped at hotel. We didn’t eat lunch until past 2:00pm. Everyone was hot, hungry and miserable. There should have been some bananas, fruit or something on the bus or a lunch stop. 7 hours with no food in that heat was a bad idea.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Sandra Forney, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=European Holiday Markets (NUE-VIE) 25, tourdirector=20120-49984
Service Comment:
Excellent

The staff service we outstanding and made the journey remarkable. The food ranged from average to very good;however, very disappointed in the Wiener schnitzel and some of the buffet offerings, as they were bland and dry. The cruise and hotel managers were wonderful and quickly responded to issues.
Product Comment:
The Uniworld SS Beatrice Holiday Markets cruise was a nice experience, althoughI don’t plan to take this trip again. Our grand suite was very nice, but still very small for the price in comparison to ocean cruises like Seabourn. There was a foul odor of sewer gas that came and went in the bathroom, and made the whole suite smell when we relived in Vienna.  The propeller entanglement caused us to miss our visit to Linz, which I was looking forward to.

I found it disappointing that there was a piano in the lounge that wasn’t played regularly, and only used by entertainers once. Having a piano player onboard daily would have been a nice enhancement to the journey.

The tour guides were hit and miss, some amazing and some red from a script.  I would prefer to have more time to just walk around by ourselves vs have a planned tour. Maybe 2 nights in a less ports versus hitting a new port every night would be more relaxing. 

Having an option for people that like to dance on more than one night would be great too. The Black and White party was fun and many thought  second or third night would be great. I loved the Waltz lesson and would like to see more of that.

Overall, a great experience, but since it was so cold and required a lot of walking, it felt a bit rushed to see all the markets so quickly. Our accidental two nights in Passau gave us time to see the sites and have time to explore.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Charles Alston, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Client Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=European Holiday Markets (NUE-VIE) 25, tourdirector=20120-49984

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Anthony Aiello, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=European Holiday Markets (NUE-VIE) 25, tourdirector=20120-49984
Product Comment:
Our embarkation experience was smooth, and we appreciated having access to the lounge until our stateroom was ready. However, several aspects of the ship and itinerary fell short of expectations.

Ship & Accommodation  
While the ship was attractively decorated, the garland on the stair handrails created challenges for guests with limited mobility. Our cabin was significantly smaller than anticipated, with a large gap around the door that allowed in both noise and light. The chair and table beside the bed were not functional and posed a tripping hazard. The bathroom layout was particularly problematic; the placement of the toilet paper holder caused repeated injury, and housekeeping twice serviced the room without replenishing toilet paper. With only one lounge on board and limited seating in the cabin, there was no quiet space to retreat from noise.

Food and beverage service was also disappointing. The number of buffets was unexpected, and the lack of regional dishes contradicted what had been advertised. Non‑alcoholic beverage options were limited, and I was served Coke Zero without being informed. Drink refills at dinner were inconsistent unless ordering wine, and repeated requests for iced tea refills went unanswered.

Port Experiences  
Travel into Nuremberg was easy, but the return to the ship in Roth was disorganized. The shuttle bus arrived 45 minutes late, could not accommodate all waiting guests, and no alternative transportation was offered.

The stop in Regensburg was underwhelming; both the morning and evening markets were extremely small. Our guide did not bring a microphone, and once at the market, no meeting point or return instructions were provided.

Passau was enjoyable, though the audio devices and heavy winter gear were unnecessary since most of the tour was indoors. It would have been helpful to know in advance that the market was cash‑only.

We were unable to visit Linz due to a propeller issue, reportedly caused by the ship striking something earlier in the journey. Although we were allowed off the ship briefly, the delay significantly impacted the schedule. Many guests expressed that they would have preferred visiting Linz over the brief stop in Dürnstein.

In Vienna, the morning excursion options were presented as a combination of the Belvedere and the market, but in reality, there was only enough time for one. The excursion was scheduled until 12:30, yet we returned to the ship shortly after noon. The optional Schönbrunn Palace tour also felt rushed, with insufficient time for both the tour and shopping.

Departure  
Despite indicating a 10:00 a.m. departure on the required form, we were instructed to vacate our room by 8:30 a.m., with breakfast ending at 8:00. Our request to adjust the taxi time was denied, and no additional assistance was offered.

Service  
Communication throughout the trip was inconsistent, and on several occasions, staff appeared unhelpful or dismissive. Christian, in particular, often made guests feel as though asking questions was an inconvenience. The only standout service we experienced was from our guide Chris in Passau, who was exceptional.
This was our first Uniworld cruise, and based on this experience, we are uncertain whether we would book with the company again. Several returning guests shared similar concerns, noting that this trip did not align with their past experiences with Uniworld.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We're sorry to hear about your recent experience and appreciate you bringing this to our attention. A member of our team will be reaching out to you directly to discuss your concerns. Please feel free to reach out to us at customerservice@uniworld.com if you would like to provide more details.

We look forward to cruising on Uniworld again.

Product Comment: My husband and I enjoyed the Parisian Noel cruise. The ship was elegantly decorated and the excursions were fun. However, I have a couple of suggestions. The ship could have used some outside decoration, such as garland and lights. The staff was wonderful, but seemed tired at times. The food was excellent, but I would have enjoyed more French cuisine choices. The excursions were fun, but, at times, too rushed, especially when shopping was involved. Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Donald Eade, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Magical Parisian Holiday (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=23992-55967
Service Comment: Staff tried hard to please us.
Product Comment:
The staff were very good. The food was average and unadventurous, sometimes cold when delivered to the table. The Steak was inedible as commented by many guests. Coffee and Tea was never hot, mainly because the water was being kept hot in a vacuum flask not freshly boiled. Not nearly as good as previous Uniworld trips.  Our cabin (a suite) was disappointing - the wall lights either side of the bed didn't work from day 1 and then the whole time we were there, a picture light was the same, the television didn't work either. There was only one reading light on the wall beside the bed. We couldn't get any of these defects repaired, a problem experienced by other guests.  The relevant staff on the ship must have known about the cabin defects as they were there on our arrival.
In the main, the excursions onshore resulted in long coach journeys which were too long for what was offered at the destination.  As some American friends said - more bucks, less bang!  The trips that required an extra cost were vastly overpriced, in our opinion. Virtually every move on the river was carried out at night so no chance to see any scenery.  Uniworld boasts to be one of the best River Cruising firms, however, on this accasion they weren't.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that your trip did not meet the standards of your previous cruises. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the service of our staff onboard. If you would like to follow-up with our team regarding your experience, please email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Mary Divito, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Belgian Holiday Markets (BRU-BRU) 25, tourdirector=21601-51794
Service Comment: Service was good in all regards. Staff was well trained and friendly.
Product Comment:
The ship is ugly. Not up to Uniworld standards. It is 'Rooms to Go' furnishings and plastic chandeliers and light fixtures. If you are looking for beauty, this is not for you. Mattresses by 'Mattress World' and even the mattress toppers could improve the comfort. Bed coverings by 'Marshalls'.  Everything was cheap looking and comfort was apparently not important. Food was mediocre, not the foie gras and caviar as on other cruises. Lunch buffet was sparse and doing buffet for dinner was very low class. 

That being said, I felt I was on a Viking cruise; but everywhere, walk, walk, walk, and then bus again. Guides were second rate, except for the guide for the printing museum. She was exceptional. Belgium Christmas markets are not great and the "river" is not a river, it's a bunch of industrial locks.  If you want to do Christmas markets, do Germany or Austria.

All in all, a great disappointment.  The only saving grace was the excellent staff, some fun entertainment and the nice people we met on board. That is why I gave it 3 stars as opposed to 2. My previous Uniworld cruises have been 5 star, but given this experience, I will be very wary of booking with them again.

Please feel free to reach out to us at customerservice@uniworld.com if you would like to provide more details.

Service Comment:
Meals were uniformly excellent, and I particularly liked the fact they almost always included a dish or product (cheeses, fruits, wines) from the local area each day. 
The staff in the Lounge were very attentive, and after the first day or so remembered what the guests preferred to drink.
Housekeeping service was also very good; our suite was always clean and well taken care of. 
And the fact that the suite included the assistance of a butler was a real treat.
Product Comment: Our cruise along the Danube was spectacular.  The ship is very well appointed, and our suite was exceptionally comfortable.  One wonderful  perk was having our own butler!  Boris was helpful, courteous and attentive, ensuring that we were well looked after. Uniworld does a wonderful job with the food onboard, and usually included some dishes or produce from the local area each day. The Christmas Markets themselves are good fun and very different as you sail from Budapest to Passau.  We all enjoyed the port talks by Heidicha, and the local guides who led the town walks each day did a great job, mixing history, culture and local tidbits of information and reminiscence into their narratives.  I would recommend this cruise wholeheartedly. Once you've tried Uniworld you won't want to sail on any other line!

That said, it seems that the quality and overall ambience of our cruise experiences have been slipping since our first cruise. What bothers me the most about cruising is the need for early departures for the excursions. I also felt like too much time is spent on buses and not enough time at the excursions locations. On two excursions we arrived so early in the towns that the local Christmas markets were not even open yet. The lunch stop in Dieppe was a total waste of time. At the Christmas Market we visited at the privately owned chateau we were rushed through so as  to board the bus on time. We meet some nice folks on the cruise and one couple said there was nothing Magical about this cruise. I missed the breakfast buffet but thought the lunch buffet was superior to the menu choices for dinner. In all honesty this may be my last river cruise as I don’t like be so tightly scheduled and controlled.

Description: Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto
Product Comment:
Rooms did not have ability to separate beds for two women traveling together who did not want to sleep in same bed.   NO SEPARATION available and the room stewardess did not want to try to move the beds apart but my roommate insisted and we had to start shoving around the furniture ourselves to make it able to separate the beds.   There are too many heavy marble topped tables in the room that are too wide.  Smaller end tables would be better.
It messed up the whole trip for us.    Food was not as good as when I sailed on Uniworld before either.

Bad close space also.   No drawers.   Not enough shelf space.  Bathroom door in wrong place.  Should be on end or on corner like AMA Waterways, Avalon or Viking.  They have room to move beds apart also.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Manjubhashini Sivanathan, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Sphinx, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the S.S. Sphinx (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=23862-55102
Service Comment: Absolutely brilliant. Every aspect delivered by every member of staff was simply great. Thanks
Product Comment:
I just returned from a marvellous , culture filled cruise to Egypt , land of Pharaohs, on Uniworld/Spring Tours SS SPHINX. 
This is my 5th cruise with Uniworld.  But first to Egypt. 

The whole itinerary was well organised. From start to finish. 

We were introduced to our group's Egyptologist Ramadan  , in Cairo , who was there with us and for us, his 'Angels'  from start to finish. About him later. ..

We stayed at Four Seasons Nile Plaza in Cairo which was lovely. Would have helped if company had provided vouchers ( atleast) for dinner..

Boarding the ship in Luxor...SS Sphinx ...so awesome interior. But it was the staff/crew who made it very special  and unforgettable. .
The Hotel Manager Sam and his team ...in each department gave their all. Made us welcome and treated as  family. 

Suites were very comfortable and felt at home. Food under the guidance of Exec Chef Hamdy was simply SUPERB , Served well by F&B manager Ahmed and his wonderful team .

Our cabins were meticulously cared for by brill housekeeping. 

Coming to today excursions....all well organised. Transport, security etc. Big ++++. Ramadan was a nasser of knowledge as our Egyptologist. Fill of enthusiasm and shared information to the max. But most importantly, he took care of us so well. Kudos to him and also our other Egyptologist Hasem too. They were like great brothers and full of fun.


All in all , I felt blessed and fortunate to have travelled on this Uniworld package to Egypt .

Thank you and well done to ONE &ALL.
Dr Manju

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Lesley Dickinson, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Magical Parisian Holiday (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=23992-55966
Product Comment:
As usual the cruise was very good. I've marked down a bit this time because the ship seemed to have a lot of Uniworld staff, friends and family including the CEO. A few were quite noisy and seemed to get some preferential treatment. I know this is normal in the upper suites but I doubt they paid as much as we did.
Some of the coach journeys were very long. We were told this was because the river height was up and we couldn't berth in Paris where we were picked up. I have checked the water height and they didn't change. Maybe you have an explanation? 
We were told a recent ship crashing into a bridge contributed to the decision. We were on that cruise I believe in Brussels and it was a misdirection at a Lock and nothing to do with the water height. Or have there been more accidents.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=David Wirtes Jr, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Rhine Holiday Markets (BSL-CGN) 25, tourdirector=21666-51820
Service Comment: Butler service was marginal. Marco, our waiter, was excellent. Sylvia, the bartendress, was excellent. Sorin, the Sommelier, was excellent. Front desk attendees were courteous and responsive.
Product Comment:
This experience did not meet Uniworld's advertising nor our expectations. Comparatively, our previous Christmas markets cruise aboard Scenic on the Danube was far superior in many respects. First, please do not waste my time with your bogus bidding for superior accommodations scheme. My time is valuable, and you wasted my time with this marketing ploy when the superior suites were not available. Second, please do not advertise that butlers are available for each suite when your butlers are spread and shared among all the suites, such that they physically cannot afford the time to accommodate guests appropriately in each of the suites (as an examply, Yuri greeted us on arrival, but had no time to orient us to the controls in our suite for television, windows, shades, or climate control). Third, please do not advertise that foods are locally sourced and carefully curated when we observed delivery of frozen and refrigerated food stuffs from wholesalers at dockside. Your food (choices, portions and quality of cuts of meat and fish) was mediocre. Ask our waiter Marco: my bride chose chicken and french fries for dinner each evening. Fourth, our suite (no. 403 on S.S. Antoinette) had repeated issues with a) heating as engineers were in our suite four separate times resetting the fuse for the furnace to function properly, b) one of the florets on the mirror adjacent to the port side of the bed was broken so that it was unsightly and perhaps even a safety hazard, 3) the sound system for the television was impaired (it acts like the speakers are being turned on and off each time a character emits sound) which was so annoying we left the system on silent, and 4) there was no closed captioning available for the entertainment options which were available on the suite's television.  Fifth, we had one good local guide in Cologne. The rest were at best marginal. The lady on the bus to Lucerne was as interesting as dry, unbuttered toast. The lady from the Basque region of Spain in Baden Baden was tolerable for about five minutes, but then we had to walk away on our own. Sixth, we were rushed during our short stay in Strasbourg, the bus ride there was entirely too long, and as the vessel was moved closer while we were there (so that we would not be confronted with a two-hour return trip) we should have been given more time on the ground to enjoy Strasbourg. Seventh, please don't advertise the cruise as a Christmas markets cruise starting in Basel when there were no - zero - Christmas markets open in Basel when we arrived prior to embarkation or during our first night aboard your vessel.
 
Finally, if true that Uniworld is now owned by a private equity group, please just tell the truth so consumers can make informed choices. It seems like food selection, maintenance and staffing issues are now governed by cost issues and profit margins, not stellar service as advertised. Bottom line: we didn't get what we paid for.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback which has been passed on for further review. We appreciate you taking the time to share your concerns. We are sorry to hear that your trip did not meet the standards of your previous cruise. Please feel free to reach out to us at customerservice@uniworld.com if you would like to provide more details.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Diane Blake, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Sphinx, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the S.S. Sphinx (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=23862-55095
Service Comment: Everyone was very friendly and helpful.
Product Comment:
We first did the Jordan extension. It was an interesting experience. Loved the itinary, especially Wadi Rum, Petra, and Aqaba. There were, however very long bus trips without a proper meal breaks.  A number of days we had to choose packaged snacks from a petrol station. Our guide was knowledgeable and friendly, but also very opinionated and at times, we felt like we were being lectured. 
The 12 night Cairo and Nile cruise was wonderful. Our only complaints were the meats and shellfish were overcooked and there weren’t enough activities on the ship., especially in the afternoons. Overall, it was not up to the usual Uni World standards. The staff was friendly and professional. The ship was clean and pretty. The main lounge furniture was very uncomfortable. We also did the Sharm e Sheik post extension. The resort was absolutely beautiful!  Our biggest problem was that we went with our guide to a seafood restaurant on the city night tour. After being extremely careful with what we ate and drank during our entire trip, we trusted our uni World guide, which was a hugh mistake. My husband experienced nausea and diarrhea for the next 5 days. He then was hospitalized for 5 days with an extreme bacterial infection and was on sepsis protocol. The infectious disease doctors feel it was caused by the shrimp he ate that night. 
Although Egypt was wonderful, our experience makes us question future travel with UniWorld.

Service Comment:
The service was fantastic, from the cabin stewardess to the guys behind the bar. Smiley was so helpful and they all remembered everyone’s names!
The tour guides were so friendly and helpful and very knowledgeable about their country.
Everything ran on time, like clockwork.
The staff in the Metropole Hotel were amazing, especially the concierge and the bell boys who helped with our luggage.
Product Comment:
Fabulous Uniworld trip with stays in Saigon,Siem Reap and Hanoi all lovely comfortable hotels. The Mekong Jewel ship was a lovely comfortable well kept ship and the staff were wonderful, nothing too much trouble. The bed was amazing!
My only complaint would be the hot food at lunch time in the buffet wasn’t HOT , just lukewarm on most days, so address this and the food is great.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Simon Eames, loyalty=NO, package=India, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=Ganges Voyager II, tour=India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges (DEL-CCU) 25, tourdirector=20138-50669
Description: India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges
Product Comment:
Only three reasons for deducting one star:

1) While the quality of the food throughout was excellent, there was a very disappointing lack of "Indian" choices in the evenings. Uniworld seemed to assume that all guests are boring and unadventurous, and that they all just want familiar "western" dishes. We met several others who felt the same as we did, and fortunately the very obliging chef was more than happy every night to prepare, just for a group of us, a special Indian main course! We always left this completely up to him to surprise us, and every night we had something invariably superb. Travellers who want to taste the real India will be badly disappointed with the standard menus on this trip. At the start of the cruise that chef did invite guests to make special requests, and seemed genuinely pleased to oblige, but you will have to ask.

2) Gratuities! This has been a source of embarrassment and awkwardness on all our cruises with other companies, so we were delighted that Uniworld go out of their way to stress that their cruises are fully all-inclusive, and that this includes all gratuities. So it was highly disappointing to be told, on our very first meeting with the tour manager, that although tips are indeed already included, any extra gratuities would be welcome from "those who would like to go the extra mile and show their appreciation". He then went on to outline suggested amounts per person per day for drivers, tour guides etc, and that tips for the crew could be left at the bursar's office.  This resulted in much awkward discussion between guests about shall we or shan't we, and an unseemly, embarrassing scramble for cash at the end of the cruise, when everyone felt morally compelled to tip all and sundry. The subject of gratuities should NEVER be mentioned by the tour manager. They are already included - paid for in advance within the price of the cruise. End of story. Fully inclusive should mean just that, and whilst individual guests might make a personal choice to pay extra here and there, they should be allowed to do so quietly, privately, and without the moral thumbscrews being put on other guests.

3) For sun lovers the large "sun deck" of the boat is woefully lacking in sun! It is mostly covered over. The little area at the back that is left open has only 6 sunbeds that are usually over-subscribed, and these catch the sun only when it's in the right direction due to the canopy covering the deck.

I have stated the above in the hope these points will be useful to prospective travellers, but I must stress that these three points were literally the only things that detracted from an otherwise absolutely superb experience. The standard of the hotels and cruise staterooms was every bit as good as expected from an "ultra-luxury" cruise company, but their level of service and fastidious attention to cleanliness, safety and welfare surpassed expectations, resulting in nobody (to our knowledge) suffering any digestive issues. 

Uniworld's itineraries are very cleverly structured: They cover all the cultural, historical and touristy essentials, with excellent and knowledgeable tour guides. But equally important are their guided walks, tuktuk rides and rickshaws into the busiest bustling streets and markets in order to fully immerse their guests into the nitty gritty of everyday life. With all of its contrast between opulent luxury and real abject poverty (the raw reality of which some guests found very difficult), we really did feel we left having fully experienced the "real" India, and not just the tourist trail. 

Thus we highly recommend this tour. If you can justify the extra cost, go for one of the "Heritage" suites that have curved windows giving both front and side views.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Denise Cohen, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Sao Gabriel, tour=Portugal, Spain & the Douro River Valley (OPO-LIS) 25, tourdirector=20153-50440

Service Comment:
Uniworld’s attentive service truly sets them apart. When my husband became ill, the team immediately stepped in with care and compassion—Paula at the front desk ensured he received meals in our room and checked on us regularly. Our housekeepers were wonderful, keeping our room spotless and welcoming throughout the cruise.
We also enjoyed the excellent onboard presentations from the wine steward and pastry chef—they added so much to the experience. And our cruise manager, Mattias, was exceptional. Even when unexpected bumps occurred, he made sure we had plenty to see, do, and enjoy.
This level of thoughtful, personal service is one of the reasons we keep returning—this was our third Uniworld cruise, and it certainly lived up to the standard we’ve come to love.
Product Comment: This is our third Uniworld cruise and once again they were awesome.  The shore execursions are fun and informative.  If you do not want to be in a group or go off on your own just let them know and you can do your own exploring.  The food on this cruise was exceptional.  The staff was attentive to our every need.  Overall we would recommend Uniworld to anyone with a sense of adventure.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Janis Chung, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Client Direct, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=River Duchess, tour=European Jewels (BUD-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21049-50377
Product Comment: The autumn landscape was spectacular. We were very impressed with the service in the dining room, especially George and Victoria. The fine details at dinner of clearing the excess cutlery at each course, setting up for dessert etc. enhanced the fine dining experience. There were a few courses that could have been better, but serving so many people at one time makes it challenging and we understand that. The soups each night were a highlight. We thought there might be more local specialties, such as goulash. The norovirus outbreak was handled so quickly, it was impressive. It was unfortunate that those of us quarantined in our rooms could not hear the presentation or talk because the speakers were not set up in the lounge. Getting information was impossible. We loved the silent disco. We did not love the short number of hours in some of the major cities. We like that everything is included in one price, drinks, gratuities, entrance fees etc. We will likely sail again with Uniworld because the size of the ship and numbers of passengers was reasonable - one sitting for dinner is reasonable.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Scott Paegelow, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-55241
Service Comment: Attentive, respectful, accommodating and engaging.  We were always greeting with a smile and the question if anything else could be done to add to our comfort.
Product Comment:
This was our 5th Uniworld Cruise.  Once again, Uniworld provided a fabulous experience in Paris and on our cruise down the Seine to Normandy.  
I have to admit that we've gotten very spoiled by the accommodations, service, food, escorted tours and unexpected experiences that each journey has provided.  
On this particular cruise there were two outstanding outings.  The first was the "Night Out" excursion to the Notre Dame Cathedral in Rouen where we were treated to a private choir and organ concert.  Truly a memorable and enjoyable evening.  
The second was our trip to the Normandy Beaches.  The last event of the day was a return to Omaha Beach for a remembrance ceremony.  We were each provided a long-stemmed white rose and escorted to the Les Braves sculpture.  A bugler then played the National Anthem followed by Taps.  Very few dry eyes were noted at the close of the ceremony.
The on-board evening entertainment was provided by very talented singers and musicians.  And the Black and White night was a wonderful celebration of our journey.
A special thanks to Pierre, our cruise director and the entire staff of the SS Joie de Vivre.  Each and every staff member ensured that our journey would be wonderful and memorable. It's for these very reasons that we choose Uniworld Boutique River Cruises.

The other system I did not like was our cabin attendant made two trips to clean once. She went through the first time and made the beds and came back the second time to clean the bathroom. Beyond that it was a wonderful trip, with a lovely crew, but those 2 areas of service

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Laura Ann Jacobson Turner, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (MXP-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20142-51612

Service Comment: As I mentioned in my previous comment. Ana, Anca, Louisa, Borghezie, Petra, Maria-everyone was great.
Product Comment: We had a wonderful time on the cruise and everyone on board was a pleasure from start to finish.  Ana, Anca and Deylan at the reception desk were always so helpful and friendly. Boziheisur(spelling is incorrect) but he waited on our table and was outstanding.  He has braces.  Truly a natural.  Petra, Maria were also terrific.  Louisa was fabulous.  The river cruise portion with the excursions were unique and always punctual by the leaders who were great. I had an easy time booking with Cindy as well and she was great.  I do want to point out I was very disappointed in the Milan Extension.  I received my documents on 10/1, and the itinerary was in very small print as I printed everything out.  In small small fine print at the bottom was where it said The Last Supper would not be viewed. I never noticed as I followed the itinerary from the website.  I reached out to the Uniworld number and a guy I spoke to when I asked what happened- he really could not answer and at first said-You are seeing the Last Supper.  Needless to say, I did not see it until 2 days from my departure where it took me 4 hours to buy a tour where I paid several hundred dollars that included The Last Supper on the Saturday, we should have been taken by Uniworld.  I was told nothing was planned on that Saturday.  I am glad my husband and I arrived at the hotel at 3:30 as I was told there is a meeting at 4pm.  I was among 39 people who were very disappointed.  In Fact, during Milan- There was only one tour that was included until we left to go to Verona and on to Venice.  I was also not given a king bed after I asked at the hotel and was put on the first floor with queen.  I feel it is important to point this out as I was not expecting this from an extension.  We should be accommodated for spending money on a tour that should have been included.  Overall, we had a wonderful time and will certainly recommend your line to our friends.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Miriam Chook Yin Foong, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (PRG-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20110-50253
Service Comment: Kudos to Alexandru the hotel manager - he always has a smile for everyone and every helpful!
Product Comment:
Overall it's been a memorable trip - but somehow, I'm unable to rate it a 5 (compared to my previous cruises with Uniworld) as we experience somewhat of less the boutique Uniworld cruise that my family and I were accustomed to previously.  For example, instead of the main foyer for a big bowl of fresh green apples daily - that has been replaced by commercial gummy candies and stringy licorice which brings up a college cafeteria feel. (especially we have older guests who have limited ability to enjoy these sweet chewier "treats").  We missed having those quality Valrhona chocolates in our room's candy jar too! Also, we had 2 buffet dinners in our program instead of our usual sit down dinners - that's unusual compared to our previous boutique cruises.  

The table service staff seemed less experienced and I found at one instance, mud in the spinach leaves during a buffet lunch. Also, during dinner service, we  had to correct the plate servings to the appropriate reciepients - which is so unlike the service staff of our previous cruises.  

Meantime, the shining service star for this cruise (SS Beatrice) is our Sommelier, Kermena and she's fantastic. She seems to be working everywhere - donning several roles beyond her wine specialty - tending the bar, serving dinner plates impeccably, even entertaining us with her refined palate knowledge and local history. Amazing - she's a brilliant gem to the Uniworld team!  Thanks!

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Mollie Roy, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21658-51818
Service Comment:
Dining service was excellent.
We wanted to have a wine and cheese party in our room one evening, and our butler helped us make those arrangements with wine glasses and crackers.
Product Comment:
This was our first Uniworld river cruise, and we were very pleased: with the accommodations, the food service, the responsiveness of the staff, and the included excursions.  
Two comments:
First, we had to ask about the distance traveled up the Rhine River. On the last evening, we stopped the captain and he told us.  Why was this such a secret?
Second, we are land lubbers so anything related to water travel is fascinating to us. Thus, I was very interested in the history and  development of the Rhine River for such a level of travel. In addition, an explanation of the 12 locks we traversed would have made for an interesting chat with our Cruise Director.  We learned on the last evening that this was his first cruise, and I think that he did a great job! We had a French Canadian group onboard, and I think that coordinating with that cruise director in advance might have improved some of the excursion departures.  Last, departures for some of the excursions was a little confusing among the specific Jewish Heritage tours, the regular tours and the "slower than normal" tours. No major inconveniences, but just a note that the guidance about where to go and when could have been a bit better.
Hopefully, we'll be able to enjoy another Uniworld river cruise in the future!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Judith Goldston, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21650-51814

Service Comment: Some of the staff were excellent whilst others were not at all interested in ensuring the customers were happy.  Example is my first night there having rotten fruit on my fruit plate.  When I mentioned this to the waitress she was quite rude as was the restaurant manager.
Product Comment: Dreadful experience with Uniworld.  They are NOT 5 star.  Food was substandard with buffet breakfast and lunch.  Ship was not clean.  A lot of mould in my bathroom.  I had advised Uniworld of my severe mould allergies yet they did nothing to ensure my bathroom was clean.  On my first night there, I was served food unfit for human consumption.  The grapes on my cheese pate were old and soft.  A constant were the crumbs on the dining room chairs that hadn’t been cleaned off from previous nights dinners.  No cocktail menus.  No offer of snacks with drinks.  Uniworld hide their lack of service and lack of cleanliness under opulent surroundings.  The tours were often held in towns that were completely closed due to timing or public holidays.  Because of this there were no museums or galleries open also no stores for shopping.  Surely Uniworld would have been aware of this before sending tours to these towns. The “masterpiece” experiences that you pay additional for were severely lacking.  Definitely NOT 5 star experience.  I am a seasoned 5 star traveller and Uniworld is well below this standard.  Very very disappointing.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Franklin Howard, loyalty=NO, package=India, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=Ganges Voyager II, tour=India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges (DEL-CCU) 25, tourdirector=20138-50668
Description: India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges
Service Comment: Outstanding service on the boat, excluding the bar, which was subpar for Uniworld.
Product Comment: I’ve travel with Uniworld to Russia, Vietnam, Amazon, and Europe to many times to count.  Will be looking for something better for my next river trip.  Boat was wonderful.  Service on the boat outstanding.  Everyone did an exceptional job, except bar, so it make it hard to give a middle of the road review - which for a luxury cruise is a poor review.  First the days that were suppose to be included prior to the cruise were a mess.  Drinks included / then not included.  Some meals included / most others not.  Then the hotels were pretty a-some.  Then the flight to Calcuta.  All checked Baggage over 32 was heavily charge for - MADE EVERYONE MAD - myself the MADEST.  That is not luxury.  Having to get up at 3 am to catch a plane in not relaxing travel so Uniworld can save a few bucks.  The wine on the boat was bad.  2.4 rated.  I can buy all the 3.5 to 4 rated wine for $10 that I want.  REALLY 2.4 wine.  The drinks and bar service was worse.  You want a Manhattan with Indian whisky - there is a charge for the Vermouth.  You want a martini with Indian gin, oh there is a charge for a few drops of vermouth.  Uniaworld just proved to me that they are more interested in scraping a few extra dollars out of their guest than providing a luxury cruise.   The real no go, that will make sure I don’t consider Uniworld in the future, was the lack of internet.  I even called before booking, because reviews complained about poor internet.  I was assured before booking internet was good most the time, but did have some poor areas.  It wasn’t poor, it was generally non existent.  After complaining because it was important to conduct business and needed it badly, they made it work for a few hours.  I even got up at 2 and 3am several morning to try and get out when nobody else should be on and it still didn’t work.  I recently got back from a 3 week cruise on Ponant through the Northwest passage, some of the most desolate parts of the world and they had great internet all the time.  I have recommended Uniworld to a lot of people.  I have brought friends and family, and large groups (24 people) on Uniworld.  To me Uniworld was synonymous with relaxation,  a great product, lower blood pressure, and luxury.  What I got was nickled and dimed for drinks,  blood boiling after getting at 3 am to be charged for luggage by a third rate airline, and a week of frustration trying to solve some business problems without the promised internet.  Won’t happen again.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback which has been passed on for further review. We are sorry to hear that your trip did not meet the standards of your previous cruises. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the service of our staff onboard. If you would like to follow-up with our team regarding your experience, please email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Hilary Purvis, loyalty=YES, package=India, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=Ganges Voyager II, tour=India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges (DEL-CCU) 25, tourdirector=20138-50668
Description: India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges
Service Comment:
The food and service were exceptional.  the beds were really comfortable.    I ended up with a moderate case of Delhi Belly and the crew bent over backwards to look after me.  One of the crew noticed I wasn’t feeling well during one of the excursions and came to talk to me.  He indicated that he’d request the chef to make me a special meal to settle  my stomach.  The chef not only did that, he monitored my meals and made recommendations for each meal.  It was an ongoing joke that he was watching everything I ate.  The bartender also made me delicious mocktails.
The daily morning excursions were really interesting.  Again, we were assigned a large bus, which included a driver, guide and a ship assistant.   The assistant provided great help with getting on and off the buses, Tuk tuks,  steps, etc.  and carried any purchases we made. He also had a backpack filled with supplies like toilet paper and sanitizer.  
The yoga instructor was wonderful and modified the practices  to match the participants abilities.  Morning sunrise yoga on the Gagnes was magical.

Overall, we really enjoyed ourselves.
Product Comment:
The on land tour was excellent.  The group of 30 was broken out into 2 buses, so we each got a double row on the bus.  Our tour guide was wonderful.  Our travel companions were a mix of US, UK, Aussie’s, 60-75 years old*  10 more people joined on the ship, as they were doing the post cruise Maharaja Express instead of the Golden Triangle.  We were the only Canadians.
We also had a fabulous tour guide that was with us the whole trip, from Delhi to the end of the cruise.  
The Ganges Voyageur ship and crew were amazing.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Patricia Lee, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Charter, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (PAS-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20119-50181
Product Comment:
Overall, my Uniworld cruise experience was excellent. The itinerary was thoughtfully designed, and the local tour guides were knowledgeable, engaging, and added significant value to each excursion. I was very pleased with the variety and quality of the excursions offered. Pre- and post-cruise transportation was also well organized and very smooth, which made the overall journey even more seamless.

Onboard service was generally outstanding. The front desk team and other staff members were consistently friendly, polite, and helpful. My stateroom was always clean, and the housekeeping service was efficient and uneventful in the best possible way.

However, service in the restaurant was sometimes slow. While the staff were eager to assist, they often appeared very busy and overwhelmed—particularly during the morning breakfast rush when many guests were preparing for their scheduled tours. I would like to recognize Eva, a server who consistently went above and beyond. She ensured I was taken care of regardless of where I was seated, and her attentiveness truly stood out. In contrast, some other servers, understandably busy, would decline service if I was seated outside their assigned area, which left me feeling overlooked on multiple mornings.

Regarding dining, I found the food quality to be overall average, especially compared to previous cruises I have taken, including earlier Uniworld sailings. While many dishes were cooked well, the freshness of the ingredients was not always consistent. This was particularly noticeable with seafood—on several occasions the fish and shrimp were mushy in texture, suggesting they were not at peak quality.

On a very positive note, the curated wine list was excellent and exceeded my expectations. The selections were thoughtful, well-paired, and added an enjoyable dimension to the dining experience.

Despite a few areas for improvement, I remain very satisfied with my overall cruise and appreciative of the many positive aspects of the experience. I hope this feedback is helpful in continuing to refine both the dining and service experience for future guests.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Albert Clifford, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube (BUD-NUE) 25, tourdirector=20111-50266
Service Comment: Always willing and elpful
Product Comment:
Decadent Danube trip Uniworld Boutique River Cruise October 2025

S S Beatrice is a beautifully presented ship, finished and kept to a high standard.   Our cabin, called a Stateroom, was on deck 3 at the stern.  Modest sized but adequate, with an opening window but no balcony.  A neat shower in a compact bathroom.  Every day the bedding and towels were changed, the room was cleaned, and toiletries were replaced as needed.  There was no tea/coffee making facility.

Large choice of food in the restaurant, buffet at breakfast and lunch and table service in the evening.  Well furnished dining room with leather chairs. Good choice of wines, introduced by a witty female sommelier.  The serving staff worked well together.

Every morning Ingrid, the Cruise Manager, addressed the guests, with useful and comprehensive information about that day’s events.  There was also a daily note left on our bed, showing the following day’s timetable.  Sometimes we felt there were too many communications.:

Excursions were mostly good with knowledgeable guides, particularly the first one, a young lady fresh from university, who was most entertaining.   Some excursions were occasionally too rushed, particularly the Belvedere visit, where one small gallery was just walked through.  One extended excursion on foot was to a cathedral with a male guide who walked very fast, ignoring the 80+ age of three ladies in the group.  Extraordinarily, having walked around the outside of the vast cathedral, the group was not allowed to see inside the cathedral, even for a quick peek, the excuse being that the coach would be kept waiting.  In the event, the group was kept waiting for the coach for over half an hour.  Based on our experience, that guide let Uniworld down.  

There was a visit to hear a very good male voice choir, and an enjoyable afternoon Strauss/Mozart private concert at extra cost. Other excursions included a privately owned castle and an excellent boat museum.  There were interesting and well presented talks about the route of the cruise and about the locks structures.  On our last evening there was a fun performance from a top-rate female singer-comedian.l

We were lucky with the weather - all sunny days except in Nuremberg on the day we returned home.   Beautiful autumn colours on the river banks and little sign of industry.

Now for the not-so-good. Departure from Heathrow was at 6am, so our small group stayed overnight but still had a disturbed night.  We won’t do that again.  Instead we will fly out a day or two earlier and take a hotel there.  The flight back was easier but still involved a coach to Munich, the flight to Heathrow, and a taxi home - a lot of sitting down.

As mentioned, our cabin was at the stern, over the engine room.  I have good hearing and could not believe the noise the engines made at night, sometimes over 90dB.  Simple plastic earplugs were no match for the engines. I tried to get a move to another cabin but the ship was absolutely full.  In fairness, towards the end of the cruise the captain kept the engine speed down at night but my sleep was down to four to five  hours a night, and I am usually a good sleeper.  That means we will never to take a river cruise again without being assured of a cabin well away from serious noise.

More good news: it was all inclusive, including gratuities.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Maria Cannata, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Tosca, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the River Tosca (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=20162-51308
Service Comment:
I think I covered this in my earlier statement.  Everything and everyone was topnotch.  Absolutely no complaints.  Our Egyptologist, Mohammed, was the best guide we have ever had.  The consummate professional.
My husband promised me 40 years ago that he would take me to Egypt.  It was worth the wait!
Product Comment:
Uniworld deliverad a topnotch experience, from boarding to disembarkation. The staff and crew were significantly outstanding in hospitality compared to other cruises we have been on.  Food was varied, delicious, beautifully presented.  The chef,cooks, and wait staff were all friendly, talented and professional. Room stewards, support personnel, captain, cruise director, including the gentleman in the laundry, everyone was 100% on the ball seeing to all our comforts.  Answering all questions.
Our Egyptologist, Mohammed, was unbelievable.  He knew almost everything about the history, culture, and people of Egypt.  He obviously knew a treasure worth of Ancient Egyptian culture and the irreplaceable artifacts, both small and massive.  He should be your number one choice every chance you can get him.  The audio units were worth every penny.  Mohammed instinctively knew how to handle everything and everyone.   1000% the best.
The River Tosca herself was a beautiful, elegant ship.  I feel that any other vessel will never live up to her charms and comforts.
The excursions were excellent, a bit more physical due to unseasonable heat, but nicely paced.  Uniworld's policies of getting out early ahead of the heat and the crowds was worth every penny paid.  There was one additional excursion that I would not recommend to anyone I knew going on their cruise.  The evening sound and light show at Luxor was not worth the money.  It was crowded, stifling hot and airless inside the temple.  The actual narration and light show was not that entertaining.  It was a bit boring and lacking quality.  
Glad we were given the opportunity to see Abu Simbal.
Would recommend this trip to anyone.  Will travel with Uniworld again.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Fiona Hughes, loyalty=NO, package=India, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=Ganges Voyager II, tour=India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges (DEL-CCU) 25, tourdirector=20138-50667
Description: India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges
Service Comment: Service at every stage of the tour was superb.
Product Comment: I have just taken the Uniworld Ganges River 2 cruise in India. I chose this fully escorted option as I am a senior single lady. The cruise is very expensive but I have to say it was worth every penny! The care and attention to support, health and safety and comfort are absolutely superb.I truly felt “ looked after”.The Oberoi hotels used on the Golden Triangle were absolutely fabulous and the food was delicious. The only complaint I have is that on our stay in Jaipur, we enjoyed a dinner with entertainment. The entertainment lasted quite a long time while the food sat out in buffet style presentation. I made this known to the hotel as many of our party commented that it would have been better to serve the food before the entertainment. The grounds in the Oberoi Jaipur Hotel are quite dark with often tricky pathways.The Riverboat was squeaky clean and beautifully appointed. Rooms are very well equipped and maintained to an excellent standard. The beds are top quality. Service is beyond fabulous. The staff are exceptional. My room was 310 and my only complaint I have is that the bathroom drains were very smelly. Service in every way was 5 star. Food was very good but not quite 5 star. Entertainment on board was excellent and very appropriate. Visits and excursions were perfect.Guides were extremely knowledgeable but some were easier to follow than others.For me with a hearing impairment attention to the lectures was often a struggle. The whole experience was wonderful. The management of the tour faultless. My niggles are small details and I thoroughly recommend this tour. I would go again in a flash!

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Stephen Carpenter, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=River Tosca, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the River Tosca (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=20162-51308
Service Comment: The service on all levels was just outstanding. I have detailed this earlier, in my comments, but we could not fault the service from any of the staff from room servicing, lobby greeting, or restaurant - catering and waiting staff.
Product Comment:
After a less than satisfying ship experience on our last cruise, we were delighted to be aboard the River Tosca for our cruise of the Nile River. The size and outfitting of the room was excellent, the service provided by the staff from servicing the room, to catering and general attention to our needs was outstanding. 
The overall experience was magnificent. We found the couple of ‘very early’ wake up excursions demanding, but then to come back to our lovely room, relax in the coolness and serenity of the room and relax on the very comfortable bed was a real delight. 
The Egyptologist tour guide, Mohammed, was knowledgeable and informative about all aspects of the archeological antiquities sites visited, his commentary was outstanding. 
The buffet breakfast/lunch catering was superb with a huge variety and excellent presentation of food. The restaurant staff were attentive to our every need. The a la carte dinner offerings were varied and extremely well prepared.   
We were very lucky in that our tour group only numbered 23!! This meant that the bus tours were not jam packed and it was easy to gather as a group on our excursions. 
Our overall experience on this tour was “magnificent". 
The ‘only’ negative for me, was that with some minor mobility issues, that without an elevator, moving up and down from our third floor suite to the restaurant below deck, was slow and exhausting. But having the third floor suite was fantastic. An elevator at the spiral staircase would be a real boon for the River Tosca.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Anne Williams, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=S.S. Sphinx, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the S.S. Sphinx (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=23862-55091
Product Comment: I thought the service was executed in a friendly and professional manner. Our cabin was comfortable and maintained favourably by the housekeeping team. The restuarant was friendly and comfortable and the wait staff were totally focused on the best outcome. The chef (very friendly and amusing) gave of his best always, the food was fabulous.
