Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Robert Guthrie, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=River Princess, tour=European Jewels (BUD-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21049-50378
Service Comment: I have outlined previously why I thought Excellent was a proper rating to give you.  I would add that your whole TEAM seemed to work so well-- from the kitchen staff to the cabin staff to the Cruise Director's team -- and the bar staff-- You all seemed to work in cohesion for the benefit of we passengers.
Product Comment:
I could not fault UniWorld after our Budapest- Amsterdam journey in October ( 15th -- 29th). 
The River Princess was a great boat ( its " age" did not make one bit of difference); the crew were marvellous-- all of them, from the captain to our cabin steward to the bar star to the numerous waiters/ waitresses to the Cruise Director ( especially Anthony) to the entertainers-- the whole contingents who were designed to look after us.  And this applies even though there were some ' difficult' passengers on board-- none of this seemed to trouble the Uniworld personnel. The numerous excursions off the boat were handled VERY efficiently -- and were well though-out. Even though our cabin was 108-- on the lower decks of the boat-- this did not matter-- it was comfortable, all the facilities worked well and we were NEVER treated as ' inferior' customers. As I said in my feedback provided after the cruise on the boat-- we can see why Uniworld does not have to ' advertise' widely.  We were prompted to take this cruise by previous clients who spoke so highly of the company.  We are so glad that we did-- and we will now speak highly of Uniworld to our many ' travelling' friends.

Service Comment: Excellent service at every point. Loved the bar staff and their friendly demeanor as well as their ability to remember what you liked to order.  Front Desk staff were on top of any request.
Product Comment:
My journey on the River Princess was just a delight. A quiet, well appointed room, friendly and interactive staff and flawless arrangements.  The European Jewels trip from Budapest to Amsterdam passes through some stunning countryside, and one of the best things was the pace.  Sliding along the river slowly on the River Princess was a special “pause” in the chaos of today’s world.  It allowed me the ability to relax and just enjoy the ride knowing that everything was taken care of and all I had to do was show up.  I met some lovely people from around the globe and the smaller size of the River Princess allowed time to get to know these fellow passengers.  
This trip was just what I was needing at this point in time, and I highly recommend it to anyone wanting a small ship, relaxing experience!

Service Comment:
Ivan, the tour manager, was very organized but did not respond well to questions. He was easily frustrated and could be a little short with guests. I would describe him as a bit edgy. Many of us felt this way. We joked about not wanting to ask him questions. We loved all the  bartenders on our boat. They did their best to serve you - always pleasant. There was one name Dibi (?...I can't remember his name!) He took great pride in his drinks - especially his margaritas.  He went out of his way to please the guests. Our room had too many large chairs - we only needed one. The staff quickly removed one of them when asked.  We really appreciated this! Other guests complained about the unnecessary chairs which took up much needed floor space. Last - the coffee service in the morning inside the dining room was slow. Many mornings it seemed to take forever to get a cup of coffee. I would have been happy to serve myself but this was not allowed. It would be nice if there was a simple self-serve coffee station inside the dining room. I think this would also help the staff.

Beyond these few things, the cruise was fabulous - great food and great accommodations! We would do Uniworld again and also recommend it to others!
Product Comment:
We throrouhgly enjoyed the cruise. Great food and great accommodations…only two suggestions. Our tour director (Ivan) was a bit edgy and seemed somewhat irritated in dealing with guests at times. Many of us felt this way. Perhaps it was the end of the season, and he was exhausted. Also - it seemed to take FOREVER to get your coffee poured when we sat down for breakfast. I wwas happy to get up and pour it myself but this was not allowed. It would have been nice to have had a simple self-serve coffee bar when you walk in the dining area. 
We would definitely do Uniworld again and would recommend it to others!

Service Comment:
Excellent and valued.  Service was top-shelf.  Staff attentive, rooms had many amenities above standard.  Excursions (truffle farm, olive farm, wineries) well organized with locals that were knowledgeable of history and cultures.  List of visited cities gave excellent contrast of small villages, towns with bigger cities.
S.S. Catherine was excellently maintained.  Nightly entertainment was superbly planned and organized by Sabrina.  The "Gypsy Kings" like group were far and away the most entertaining and representative of the Spanish and French culture. 4 Days in Paris extension completed our tour.
Would recommend serious consideration if you have one opportunity to visit France.  Only regret is that we did not get to visit Normandy.  Two weeks on the road is about our limit. Other suggestions are to not overbook excursions, leave time for relaxation, kick back.  Also guided tours are the only way to visit museums if time is limited.  Paris museums are crowded and too much to see if you only have a half-a-day for museum.

Your fee structure that includes tips and open bar is tremendous.  Not thinking about the nickel/dime/hundred euro cost that is constantly thrust in your face elsewhere makes Uniworld touring much more pleasant.                   We are not evening entertainment needy, but we did enjoy the singers and dancers.                                                                                                             A recommendation-- Pope's Palace; tell people that the handheld computer tablet guided tour is a confusing waste of time; just walk the incredible palace.

Product Comment: We had a nice cruise on Uniworld's Delightful Danube and Prague itinerary. The tours were excellent and the staff on the ship (SS Beatrice.) was very kind an helpful. We found the food to be just okay and the service for the evening meal was very slow. Also, the dining room was fairly inflexible with dining hours. The ship was old and, while clean, needed an update, as some furniture was faded and cushions were worn out making for uncomfortable seating. We will cruise Uniworld in the future only for a unique itinerary not available on other luxury river cruise lines.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=James Yong Thye Wong, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21650-51813
Service Comment:
Every crew member at the Bar : Lina, Ralitsa, Sorin : Simply Superb !
Those at the Restaurant : Liliya, Kristiyan, Roxana, Kaloyan, Glasielle, Desi, Jose, Constantin : Impossible to forget all these angels. !!
Housekeeping : Zvezdelina & Piri : just two beautiful people doing amazing work !

But our two Butlers : Eugen & Reni : below average .. very procedural - just doing their job - no connection all 11 days (unlike our previous butler Svilen on La Venezia).
Product Comment:
This was our 3rd Uniworld experience and unfortunately, we’re feeling very disappointed with the ship’s condition and definitely feeling we have way overpaid Uniworld a premium fee but not getting the premium product that matches the payment. SS Antoinette is long overdue for serious refurbishment, in need of major overhaul & intense comprehensive fixing of furnitures; our Suite 405 drawers do not close, our balcony window slides up/down annoyingly noisy and loud. If I have a poor inferior product in my portfolio, I won’t sell it in order to preserve my reputation, but this is not the case and not a principle which Uniworld possess. Quoting Uniworld’s CEO Ellen Bettridge : “You Deserve The Best” - I wish to suggest she takes a walk around SS Antoinette and perhaps rediscover a new slogan because we did not get what we deserve, not after paying a premium for the cruise. Comfort, Aesthetics, Maintenance - you scored Minus 10 (the lowest on our scale).
The Day Tours : Uniworld should seriously consider removing Oberwesel from the itinerary. It was a complete waste of time to walk around a ghost town and to have really below par tour guides only made that experience totally forgettable - time definitely better spent reading a book on the sundeck of the ship.
The one & only bright spark was that Chef Bogdan and his kitchen produced magic at every meal keeping all our tummies happy. The crew members at the Bar & the Restaurant : they were all 6-Star angels. 
Our two butlers : cordial, polite but very procedural - just doing their job unlike our previous Butler Svilen on La Venezia (Milan to Venice) - Svilen represents the pinnacle of sincere thoughtful ground-breaking service standard and Svilen is just one solo person who more than delivered and we had two butlers (Eugen & Reni) on SS Antoinette - both failed to deliver or meet the lowest of expectations.

On the note about La Venezia : I strongly recommend CEO Ellen Bettridge to have a look at Suite 411 - which we booked for the Venice Lagoon cruise previously, and it will be evident to you and to anyone to fully grasp and understand why I would write : SS Antoinette Suite 405’s bathroom is embarrassing and shabby to have for 11 days.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We're sorry to hear about your recent experience and appreciate you bringing this to our attention. A member of our team will be reaching out to you directly to discuss your concerns. Please feel free to reach out to us at customerservice@uniworld.com if you would like to provide more details.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jeffrey Russell, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Grande Italia (MXP-FCO) 25, tourdirector=21272-51547
Service Comment: Onboard service was excellent a great staff we were spoiled. The tours during the onboard experience were very good
Product Comment: The actual Uniworld cruise was a 5 star as were the hotels especially the Anantara in Rome. The Rome tour coordinator was a disaster. Because there was no gentle walker options the Roman fora were not visited on the walking tour. There was no coliseum experience unless you purchased the extra masterpiece thing.  Your tour leader in Rome mis communicated with the local guide regarding visiting the Vatican. The Vatican museum and Cistine Chapel were good but the guide did not know we were to also see St. Peter Bascillica during the tour. We got to the square late and were not admitted to the Bascillica.  To visit Rome and not see the Forum, Colosseum, and Bascillica is unfortunate and not forgivable for a first class tour. Fortunately We stayed an extra day on our own planning to see the coliseum attic tour and added a visit to the Bascillica. But most of your guests left town without those experiences.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Robin Goad, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51882
Service Comment: Very attentive and pleasant from everyone on the ship.
Product Comment: Ship was well appointed, clean and comfortable, state rooms were spacious and well designed, staff were excellent - attentive and very pleasant, day trips were well organized and generally interesting, with good tour leaders.  The medieval towns became a bit repetitive with four very similar locations.  The bike trip did not point out any points of interest - just a 25km bike ride.  I would have enjoyed a winery tour and / or food destination.  The canal boat experience in Amsterdam did not have an audio system and you could not hear the tour guide.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jennifer Cowley, loyalty=NO, package=India, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=Ganges Voyager II, tour=India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges (DEL-CCU) 25, tourdirector=20138-50665
Description: India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges
Service Comment: Service was good, meals were varied
Product Comment:
The tour was very intense - not much time to enjoy the beautiful Oberoi hotels, lots of travelling and lots of early morning starts. Traffic is chaotic as lines painted on roads are for decorative purposes only and dual carriageways do not mean one way only. The guide for the Golden Triangle was exellent with just the right amount of information.
The river is the Hooghly, the westernmost distributary of the Ganges, and looked filthy, with dead cows and a pig floating downriver. However, the locals bravely bathed in it. Most of the temples we saw were relatively modern and well maintained, the oldest being the Taj Mahal (1630's) and one still under construction, but it is difficult to reconcile the abject poverty in which the people live.
The Ganges Voyager II was well appointed and the staff were wonderful and very versatile. Our cabin on deck 2 was perfectly adequate with a king size bed, beautiful decoration and large walk-in shower. The food was good and varied with a selection of Indian and European cuisine. Entertainment was also good with excellent dance troupes on three occasions, a magician and demonstrations of Indian national dress. 
The people we met in various locations were extremely friendly, many asking for selfies with us. The variety and colour of dress was amazing and I took lots of photos.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Alan Lipka, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51859
Service Comment: Exceptional.  Whatever we asked for, the answer was always YES.  When I couldn't find any chocolate chip cookies, Denis and Yuri assured me there would always be some fresh ones in our suite.  Illyia made sure everyone at our dinner table had exactly what we wanted.  Lukas made the best omelettes I have ever had.  My biggest thanks go to the ladies and gentleman who cooked our food, washed our dishes, cleaned the common areas, and did all of the things that go unseen and made our journey so enjoyable.  Honestly, the Ritz-Carlton could not have done better.
Product Comment: What a wonderful cruise !  We took advantage of the pre-cruise tour of Amsterdam and met 7 other Uniworld travelers who all became fast friends.  The cruise was filled with all the best things of life ... frequent laughter, bad dancing, fine food, adult beverages, service fit for royalty, the most beautiful suite, incredible scenery, unique excursions (I mean, a vinegar tasting ?!?!), more adult beverages, more new friends, and not a moment of anxiety.  A big shout out to our butlers Denis and Yuri, our waitress Illyia, chef Lukas, the best hostess in the world *Sylvia*, our cruise director Louisa, hotel manager Sylvia, and all of the ladies and gentlemen who's back of the house efforts made our time aboard so enjoyable.  Oh, and our Amsterdam tour guide Rita !  Our memories are precious and we plan to visit Venice in 2027 on the S.S. La Venezia.  Too bad 2026 is already filled with travel plans.  But wait ... oh yes ! I see a week this year in early December that's open !  I'm in the running for Husband of the Year by booking Paris for Christmas on the S.S. Joie de Vivre !  We are looking forward to enjoying all the best things in life again.  (Except for the suite.  Ran out of money for 2025.  Ha !)

Service Comment:
This is the third review I am completing. It seems like Uniworld is very disorganized. Or only wants perfect reviews. 
On my last few reviews I forgot to mention the little fish bones in my fish order.  The chefs should be more careful when cleaning and cooking fish.  In the dining room the waiters sometimes leaned over to pick up plates on the opposite side of the table.  If Uniworld wants to be the best in river cruising, then they need to hire staff who are well informed and able to communicate with their staff. Regent hired one of their supervisors to retrain their management staff in the dining rooms.  Also food needs to be consistently appealing and taste good. The entertainment on the ship was very poor.  Is it because Uniworld does not want to pay good entertainers or poor management and quality?

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=John Tepperman, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50533
Service Comment: See above kudos. The Rialto and room crew were superb.
Product Comment:
The Venice itinerary is so unique, and Gyongi ran a fantastic week.  The three things that keep us coming back to Uniworld are the gorgeous and uniquely designed ships (Viking Ikea furniture is not our style), superb service, food and drink onboard, and top notch guides (Dr Susan Steer is a world treasure, and Torcello was unforgettable).  Ewa the hotel manager set the standard for personal touch and onboard bliss. Uniworld doesn't settle for good or great. Amazing and unforgettable is the standard you uphold, and that earns our loyalty.  Every guest feels like a VIP  regardless of their cabin category, a key distinction from ocean cruising. 
Some shoutouts for staff: 
Sveti and Elisava from housekeeping were always so kind. Iben, the head butler, was kind and welcoming to every guest and made us all feel at home. We enjoyed his champagne sabering. 
Alejandro, Christophe, and Hristo from bar made our experience there a real treat, and Mariya the sommelier, did a wonderful wine tasting and made great cocktails to boot. We appreciate that, although you have a diamond list for ultra premium drinks and wine, that you have a broad selection of included drinks. This keeps pricing reasonable for those who are easy going with drink selection and provides special options for those who are willing to pay extra 
Yana at front desk was very helpful.
In Rialto restaurant, Bozhidar, Cyntia, David, Adrian, Mihai, Rosita, Yordan, and Executive Chef Robert
always made us feel like we were dining at a friend's home but with five star cuisine (so, a friend who happens to be a Duke).  They worked as such a seamless team, and you could feel the camaraderie. 

Thank you for a memorable cruise!

Service Comment: Everything was great as usual on Uniworld.  Food was excellent, the cruise director was super helpful, and onboard staff were great.  We do have one suggestion - shore excursions began suggesting there be two groups: "gentle walkers" and "regular walkers."  We suggest an added category - "active walkers."  Several passengers considered themselves to be regular walkers but many had difficulty with stairs and cobblestone streets.
Product Comment: This was our third Uniworld cruise - we've done Lyon to Arles and Amsterdam to Basel.  Both wonderful experiences.  This was also great - the visit to the Normandy beaches and the American cemetery was sobering.  We must not forget the sacrifices made on D-Day.  If you take this cruise, spend time learning a bit about D-Day.  As usual, everything about the cruise was just excellent.  To top it off, we met what has turned out to be really good friends.

Service was first class in every way and in every department.
The organised trips and the guides were excellent and we particularly appreciated the out of hours arrangements for all the main “ attractions” and all the excursions were stress free and well managed .
Information on board was very good.
The ability of the ship to moor so close to Venice , Burano etc. was a huge plus .
The lunches and breakfasts were very good  but dinners were slightly disappointing ( this is only a slight criticism)
The restaurant, however, was too small and the buffet arrangements for breakfast and lunch were overly busy .
The ship is pretty good but needs a little tlc.
Having said all of the above I would thoroughly recommend this Venetian cruise . Overall a splendid experience, certainly value for money and a fantastic way to visit Venice .

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Victoria Thoits, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Tosca, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the River Tosca (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=20162-51305
Service Comment: Excellent professional service throughout.
Product Comment:
Just returned from the Egypt/Nile River cruise with the added land excursion to Jordan.  This is definitely one of the trips of a lifetime!  Pictures simply do not capture the grandeur and magnitude of all the sites that you visit.  The guides that travel with your small group stay with you in each country so you get to know them and gain a greater understanding of the culture and life in each country.  We were on the Sphinx riverboat which is quite beautifully decorated, with superb staff and excellent cuisine.  Each of the hotels for the land excursions were 5 star, and earned that rating.

Unfortunately, at the end of our cruise, I suffered a hip implant dislocation while onboard when we were docked in Aswan.  I cannot say or thank the entire staff enough for all of the help that they provided.  They secured the ambulance, our guide stayed with us in the hospital, well past midnight, to translate with the doctor, they brought food, rented a wheelchair, and made arrangements for our hotel stay in Aswan pending our air medical evacuation back to the US.  Were it not for the assistance of Mohammed and his colleagues, I doubt that we would have been able to obtain the immediate services of a young Egyptian orthopedic surgeon who was able to successfully perform the procedure to reinsert the dislocated ball back into the hip socket implant.  Kudos to all and an unqualified recommendation for this trip and our most sincere thanks for the very genuine care and assistance rendered by the Uniworld staff.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Pau Yuen Ho, loyalty=NO, package=India, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=Ganges Voyager II, tour=India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges (DEL-CCU) 25, tourdirector=20138-50664
Description: India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges
Service Comment: Onboard service was really excellent.  I requested for garlic naan as I preferred that to the normal naan, once and I had it served to me after that for every meal without me asking.  The chef cooked for me one customised meal as I told him that I could take spicy (as in chilli spicy) and there was no need to moderate the spice level too much for me. I love the fact that the chef also provided street food, such as pani puri. The staff were multitalented and basically conducted most of the activities themselves, including henna painting, Indian dancing, sari and turban tying.
Product Comment: The Uniworld cruise in India was a good combination of a land tour of the Golden Triangle of New Delhi, Agra and Japur, and a cruise along the Ganges River, with visits along the way. For the Maharaja suite, the complementary laundry service was useful as we perspired a lot due to the humidity.  The ship's crew also took good care of us, providing umbrellas, lots of bottled water and feet covers (in places where we had to take off our shoes), and cleaning our shoes after each tour.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jeffrey Levy, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Portraits of Eastern Europe (BUH-PRG) 25, tourdirector=20147-50498
Service Comment: First-rate.  The staff quickly learned our dietary issues and they, along with the chef, went out of their way to meet them.  We were greeted by name and made to feel welcome.
Product Comment:
Firstly, the people from ship Captain on down, the food and wine, the opportunities to go on superb tours and the comfort of the ship were absolutely first rate.  Uniworld has a first-rate team and this is reflected in the personal warmth, quality of service and comfort we experienced.

This was a trip of two parts.  Twenty something of us were on the trip for two weeks, while the remainder were on for only one: either Bucharest-Budapest, or Budapest-Prague.  The two weeks were very different in tone, perhaps reflecting the less touristy aspects of the first week.  The travelers in that week seemed more serious and interested in getting more deeply into what was available.  The travelers in the second week seemed more of a middle-aged party crowd, and they were certainly focused more on drinking than the first week's group.

Why not 5/5?  A couple of niggles.  One is that there were fewer in-depth lectures than we had hoped for.  Romania, Bulgaria, Serbia, Croatia and Hungary have complex histories and it would have been welcome to have more substance covering them.  Second, the tours could be a bit superficial.  Ironically, the second week had what was probably the (optional) tour with the largest amount of serious content -- the visit to Terezin -- but justice could not be done in a 1/2-day tour.  We wound up speeding through the museum in the Terezin ghetto in record time, without time to absorb a huge amount of material.  We came away with a lot less than we could have.  Please, seriously consider making this a full-day tour in the future.  Third, we also found that several of the local guides, knowledgeable though they might be, had very thick accents when speaking in English.  This might sound churlish, in that I cannot speak their native languages at all, but it did make it difficult to understand all they were saying.  Fourth ... well, we are experienced travelers, and the second week in particular was a bit disappointing.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Glyn Sime, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50162
Service Comment: As we have come to expect with Uniworld, the service on board the SS Maria Theresa (and beforehand at the Kempinski Hotel during our city break in Budapest) was first-class from all the crew, from top to bottom, whatever their role on board. Every single member of crew whom we encountered during our trip was friendly, knowledgeable, and efficient, and nothing was ever too much trouble. The service was 100% and professional, and the smiles genuine. Our whole holiday was seamless in every aspect from beginning to end. Thank you Uniworld.
Product Comment: This was our second river cruise with Uniworld and it lived up to all our expectations. We started our holiday with a Uniworld city break in Budapest before joining the SS Maria Theresa. The Kempinski Hotel in Budapest was stunning in every aspect and located in the city centre, which was perfect for exploring both sides of the city. We enjoyed a good mix of Buda and Pest. The SS Maria Theresa is a floating boutique hotel and decorated as an imperial Habsburg palace. She is beautiful. The staff were helpful, courteous and knowledgeable, and nothing was too much trouble. Everyone did an efficient and great job. Our State Room was a good size and we loved the furnishings, and to be able to open the window as and when we required. The food selection and fine wines and drinks selection were second-to-none and catered for all tastes. The ship was always clean and well presented, and all the facilities are available as and when passengers require. The entertainment provided was 'just right' for this trip. All the included tours that we participated in were really enjoyable and all the local guides that Uniworld used were knowledgeable and friendly. They knew 'their stuff' and conducted their tours at a decent pace to cater for all walking abilities. A bonus for us was the lecture given by a local historian and expert on the Empress Maria Theresa. Again, we also enjoyed mixing with other passengers from other countries and have again made some good friends. Thank you Uniworld for another lovely and memorable holiday.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Emily Allen, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Burgundy & Provence (LYS-ARL) 25, tourdirector=20101-50146
Product Comment:
Overall, very nice. Accommodations- not sure what kind of room we had- but they were mostly very comfortable. Plenty of clothing space; large bathroom; the space within the suite required some “dancing “ but overall quite spacious and well appointed. The bed was v comfortable.

Food was good- not amazing. Really great variety and portions were nice, without being excessive. 
We REALLY would have appreciated an early dinner. 7 is just too late to eat a full meal. We opted out on a few evenings or went off boat earlier. An early dinner option would reduce the “scrum” at 7P and allow those who prefer/need to not go to bed on a full stomach, and still enjoy the onboard experience. 
The tours were wonderful!
We loved that the guides were local to the city toured- their personal touches and insights really added to the experience. 

I experienced a few safety concerns- that where the score dropped. 
When up late, there was no one to be found- on at least 2 nights. 
A way to order food or anything in the middle of the night requires use of the suite phone; but one doesn’t want to wake their partner to do so- a front desk person would have been helpful. Soup in the middle of the night would have been a blessing. 
On one evening/2AM, a person on the “attached” boat couldn’t get on to our boat to pass over. She was frantic. Someone should have been available to assist her.
Lastly, the rain “trough” in the street directly in front of the boats dock in Avalon nearly ruined our trip- and certainly could to someone else. 
Again, being up in the wee hours 5AM, no one to be found on ship/ front desk I left to see the city as my plans for the day didn’t allow time. I proceeded to walk across the basically empty street - not at the cross walk. - when I fell unexpectedly into the rain trough! Luckily I only sustained a few scrapes and I believe a cracked rib- ouch for the rest of the cruise. But I was lucky!
I informed 2staff members who expressed interest and indicated they would “inform the governor”- whatever that means. 
I was surprised and disappointed no announcement or signage was issued for the 2nd night of the stay in Avalon. 
This is a serious risk that ought to have been addressed IMMEDIATELY and never should have been allowed in the first place. 

If I were a more critical person I’m sure the score would be lower and perhaps legal issues pursued- but I’m not. Others might be. 
Kind regards,
Emily & Raymond Allen
St Catherine passengerSept 29- October 5 2025.

PS- I list a gold swirl earring on the last night and forgot to ask after it. 
How might I learn if it was found and have it returned to me.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Barbara Ferguson, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (MXP-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20142-51607
Description: Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto
Service Comment:
This was my first cruise with Uniworld, and as an advertised luxury cruise line, I have to say I was not very impressed.

The ship itself felt dated. The rooms were extremely small, and the balloon blinds in the cabins covered half of the windows—frustrating, since I had paid extra for the view. On the positive side, the beds were very comfortable. I also felt the bar/entertainment were poorly located, right above staterooms, which meant entertainment often impacted guests who were trying to sleep.

The excursions were interesting but rushed, leaving little free time to explore and enjoy the beautiful places we visited. WiFi was very spotty and often unavailable, which is unacceptable these days, especially on a luxury cruise line.

The food was average at best. I enjoy seafood, but only local fresh fish was offered once; otherwise, farmed fish dominated the menu. On buffet night, I picked up a dirty plate—only to find several more dirty ones underneath. This was disappointing and not what I’d expect from a luxury experience.

Most of the staff were friendly, but many seemed undertrained compared to other luxury hotels and cruise lines I’ve experienced.

Overall, while there were some positives, I don’t feel the experience lived up to the “luxury” branding. At this point, I’m not sure I could recommend Uniworld or book another cruise with them in the future.

Service Comment:
Really couldn't fault the service, excellent from everyone.

From the airport to the ship, from the welcome onboard to the maid, the bar staff, the restaurant staff, and all of the tour guides. Simply excellent.
Product Comment:
This was our first river cruise. It was a wonderful week and we couldn't have asked for more in terms of the friendliness & attentiveness of the staff. Cabins were comfortable, and quiet, with a good en-suite bathroom and a modest balcony. Food and drink were excellent, and we particularly liked having the easily accessible coffee stations 24/7. Some of the ship decor was a bit fancy, but there was no imposition of a fussy dress code. The organised tours were also good, although sometimes it's better to do your own thing or just relax rather than being part of a large group (although the tours also come with extras).  

Some minor niggles? As with most cruises & even some hotel rooms there was nowhere to hang your outdoor coats in the room. The low ceilings in the bar & dining room meant that they could get rather noisy, making it hard for those of us with declining hearing to hold conversations. One easy change would be to have English/Canadian bacon available at breakfast as well as American, but there was so much else to choose from.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Deborah Way, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Elisabeth, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51881
Service Comment: We loved our waiter at dinner (Judit) but the rest of the staff didn't seem to be too happy to be there and taking care of people.
Product Comment: Really disliked how far we docked from locations that were assigned to visit. Really dislike buffet meals - don't understand why lunch had to be a buffet. Entertainment at night was poor. Room was very small with little storage - no shelves in the bathroom? Come on. Attitude of many staff members bordered on rudeness. Not enough excursions or activities. Heidelberg activity should not have had the extra charge as no other excursions were offered - it wasn't worth the price for the short time. Our room had a sewer smell we had to complain about several times and was never fixed. And nobody ever followed up to check on if it was better - or let us know what they had done to fix it. Very surprising that there is no bathroom on the deck where the lounge is located - most activities are held there and no bathroom? Feel bad for people who had a more difficult time taking stairs. Time in ports was too short. Docking locations differed from the info on the Uniworld website (& in our documents) - I had done a lot of research and planning for the ports and most of that was wasted as we only had a few hours as we were docked far (not really in the locations they listed). Food at dinner was excellent and we LOVED our server Judit! Would have enjoyed more exercise classes (more strenuous options than what was offered), and more activities like cooking classes/cocktail classes/etc. We paid extra to "upgrade" our room yet it was smaller, with less storage, no shelves in bathroom, bed not facing window, etc. than our original room. I don't understand why it "cost" more $! When we saw the room our friends had who had not been upgraded, we were pretty upset as it was much nicer than ours. Not to mention the sewer smell we had to deal with and sounds from the deck above (water being sprayed, furniture moving, etc.). Also, the coffee in the dining room was really bad.

Product Comment: The Vietnam and Cambodia tour was excellent in every regard. The crew and staff on the boat were just wonderful — helpful, kind, and attentive. Bien, the tour director, was fantastic — excellent communication and detail. Our Cambodian tour leader, Sean, was the best we’ve ever had on ANY tour, having a deep knowledge of Cambodia and a willingness to share details of every experience.  He is a gift. Woody, our guide in Saigon, was young and energetic, and we enjoyed him, also. The hotel in Hanoi was perhaps not quite as up to date as others in the Uniworld repertoire, but it was ideally located and gave a feel of the history of this city. We would have liked to have had the option of visiting the Lady Temple in Cambodia. Food was excellent throughout.  It was a wonderful tour.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=John Hines, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=European Jewels (BUD-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21049-50376
Service Comment:
I’m hoping I’ve got their names correct. It was sometimes hard to read badges.

Housekeeping was well done, by Emilia. Always smiling.

Dining staff: Oliveira, Viviene, Bogdon and Fernando were very attentive 

Bar staff: Blamen, Eddie and Francisco were willing to adapt to our preferences.

Of course , Veronika 😀 What can you say, she was everywhere! On top of things.

Special call out to the professional work of the sailors.
Product Comment:
Positives:

* Exceptionally friendly and attentive crew.
* Best laundry service of any cruise we’ve taken.
* Housekeeping was exemplary.
* Loved that everything was included.
* Tour host was entertaining and well-organised.
* Most tours ranged from good to excellent.
* Meals were generally very good — highlights were the foie gras ravioli and parsnip soup.
* Salad selections were great, though dressings could be less watery.
* Docking location in Amsterdam was excellent.

Areas for Improvement:

* Boarding in Budapest required a 1 km walk; it would have been better to board directly where the ship was docked that evening.
* Vienna palace tour was overly crowded — the guide often moved ahead, and some guests were left behind. Perhaps an alternative to the Sisi tour could be offered.
* The Strauss experience was wonderful but too short and a bit rushed.
* Cologne tour was fine, but the city’s post-war reconstruction limits visual appeal — a nearby destination like Düsseldorf, Bonn, or even Belgium might make a more interesting excursion.
* Occasional sewage smell in the bathroom (though housekeeping handled it well).
* Coffee machine on the lower deck made better coffee than the restaurant.
* One fish dish appeared to have a meat-based gravy — two of us at our table were unable to finish it.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Virginia Larence, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Duchess, tour=European Jewels (AMS-BUD) 25, tourdirector=21041-50365
Service Comment: The best service was our laundry service provided by Niki our butler. He was above and beyond in every way .
Product Comment:
It was disappointing for a number of reasons.
1- Captain ran us into  something that caused the boat to lose both motors on day two of our sail. Because of this we were in emergency vests on deck for several hours before learning that we were being towed to a dock and would miss one entire day of our itinerary. 
2- the upper outdoor portion of the boat was closed for over 70% of the time of on the river due to low bridges. I wasn’t told this would happen to this extent and was counting on running / walking on the upper deck daily to escape the confined situation below
3- I was aware that we would be going through a few (usually means three) locks during our voyage and yet …68 locks. This meant the upper deck closes, again, and for several hours at each lock  we are staring at concrete walls instead of the scenery I was expecting. 68 times .
4- I was told that we could choose to eat on different areas of the ship -per the Uniworld brochure! - and yet we were confined to the one dining room with all passengers . I wanted to take our lunch on deck on one of the rare days it was open and they said no. One of the waiters then made an exception for us but said never again. ?! There was a forward enclosed portion that would have been an excellent option and that too I was told we could not use for a private small group dinner. Very very disappointing.
5- the room was comfortable but very outdated with tattered curtains and stuffing come out of the tops of them. Uncomfortable chairs and those were the only two private places during the entire cruise where we could sit
6- the brochure brags about excellent wine and variety but we were only offered a red or a white each night and it took a week or so before they could come up with a bottle of Savignion Blanc ! Not expensive mind you but seems like an easy one. The wait staff was excellent once they got it and had it ready for us each time.The red wine was mediocre at best.
7-there were opportunities missed when an optional audio could have been provided to tell us what we were sailing past.
8- food was overall very good and our wait staff for the most part excellent
9- Our Butler on the River Duchess , Niki, was outstanding - a star.
10- My largest disappointment came when , as my travel agent told me to do once I was on board, I went to the front desk to discuss my daily biking options along side the boat or to the next port and that was a flat out no discussion. I was led to believe it would be available with a signed waiver. 
11- The groups for tours was way too large and we moved with the slowest common denominator. There was no room for questions and answers as the groups were way too big…should be under 10 max. The guides were all over the map, some excellent , some poor. We have always used A&K and their guides were significantly more trained and educated than the ones we used.
12-Early in the trip , once our motors died and we were confined to inside quarters , the entire ship started becoming ill. It was a mucousy coughing nightmare. No doctor on board so we started to mask up to protect ourselves but alas it got us at the very end and I now have pneumonia that developed from my ship infection.

The River Dutches is an old and tired ship.  The engine broke down on the 3rd day into our river cruise.  The decor was also old and felt dirty. The chairs in the main lounge were too big for the space and heavy to move. The seating areas were not condusive to watching the entertainment.  The cabins were nice, althought very small for the large chunky furniture. The entire ships needs to be retired and put out of use.

The dinning and bar staff were excellent.  The food was excellent.
The communication between the office staff/captian to the passengers was sorely lacking. Specifically when the engine broke.  There was no clear direction on what was going on and what to expect. When we were allowed to get off the ship for awhile there were mixed messages on when and where we should return for the ship. We almost left some folks behind. 

I have been on better river cruises with nicer ships. I will not recommend Uniworld.

Product Comment: The cruise was excellent and the staff were very attentive.  Matias was an excellent Cruise Director, one of the best we've ever had.  We had a group of 44 on this sailing and both Matias and Maria the hotel manager were always available to help.  They also met with us shortly after we boarded which doesn't always happen.  Often we don't even see the cruise and hotel managers, unless we go looking for them, so they get high grades for their professionalism. clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Dennis Murray, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Elisabeth, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51848
Service Comment: The staff on board were very friendly and helpful.  By the end of the cruise, the gentleman bartender even remembered my husband's drink of choice!
Product Comment:
Our room on the boat was very nice.  The little touches, such as the candy and the replenished drinking water were appreciated.  The bed was situated in the room perfectly, so we could lie in bed and enjoy the view.  Jessica honored our request by adding a top sheet on the bed, which we Americans appreciate.

The food was outstanding.  All of the staff were most attentive and friendly.  I was surprised with the special birthday cake from the chef and the restaurant manager!  Such a nice touch. The sommelier was attentive as well and offered information about the wines she had selected.

The entertainment on board was minimal.  There was a beautiful piano there that no one played.  Piano music during cocktail hour and after dinner would have added so much to our experience.  We missed seeing the crew join in during the Black and White party.  We never really saw the captain and other staff (outside of announcements, introductions etc.), and it would have been fun to "party" with them.  A DJ would have added something, too, rather than recorded music.  We enjoyed the violinist and very much appreciated her talent.

It was too bad that we visited the castle at night.  Seeing the river from the castle during the day would have been beautiful.  Also, our visit seemed very rushed.  We didn't get to see it all with the limited time we had there.

Service Comment: Service was impecable. Everything was fantastic and we were particularly impressed with the service of 3 crew members in the restaurants: Glasiele, Jose and Marco. They were always very attentive to our table and provided outstanding service at all times.
Product Comment: The experience was excellent. The entire crew was well-trained and kind, which made the trip even more enjoyable. The only aspect that was somewhat disappointing was the limited opportunity to use the balcony for several reasons: sometimes the docking locations were unattractive and left a poor impression; at other times, when the scenery was nicer, another ship would be docked just a few feet away, forcing us to close the curtains for privacy; and on a few occasions, the diesel smell was too strong to keep the balcony door open. As a result, the charm of having a balcony cabin was practically lost.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Catherine Davies, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21650-51812
Service Comment: Service was excellent.   The ship itself was very tired and in need of refurbishing.
Product Comment:
After having a wonderful experience on the SS Beatrice, we decided to do the Rhine.
On both trips we had a suite.
From the minute we disembarked from the plane, we were disappointed.   Our prearranged private pickup didn’t meet us.
When we finally arrived at the ship it was in bad need of a paint job, and it was docked in an awful, industrial area.
The interior of the ship was tired, worn,  and lacking in elegance.
On some days our suite had a distinct odour coming from the holding tank!
The 6:45 talks every night felt like nursery school…..unnecessary.
Most of our dockage’s were in industrial areas.
To top it off the day we left was total confusion.  Our prearranged ride was late.   There was no staff to help people get into the correct taxi’s.   I could go on, but I think you get the picture.
After our exceptional experience on the Beatrice we talked our friends into joining us on the Antoinette.    It was very embarrassing.   
I don’t think we would be able to feel confident in booking another cruise with Uniworld.
I would like to emphasize that the staff was exceptional.
Sincerely 
      Cathy and Geoff Davies.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We're sorry to hear about your recent experience and appreciate you bringing this to our attention. A member of our team will be reaching out to you directly to discuss your concerns. Please feel free to reach out to us at customerservice@uniworld.com if you would like to provide more details.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Edwin Lee Tuck Wong, loyalty=NO, package=South America, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=Aria Amazon, tour=Peruvian Amazon & Machu Picchu Exploration (LIM-LIM) 25, tourdirector=20144-50623
Product Comment:
The Outstanding:
- The program for the pre-cruise (Iguazu Falls) and cruise proper (Amazon & Machu Pichu) was very well paced, giving us more than enough time to enjoy the spectacular hotel facilities and leisurely shopping.
- Our cruise director (Carlos) and his team took extraordinary effort to give us extraordinary experiences day-after-day (many of which came as a total surprise).
- The hotels (other than the one in Machu Pichu itself) were spectacular.
- Baggage transfers and flight assistance was handled excellently (other than upon our initial arrival in Lima).
- The 4 naturalists and ship crew were beyond excellent, displaying endless passion for helping us to discover new species.
- The food, especially on the ship, was incredibly good. We loved the table sharing concept as it allowed us to eat what we wanted with no wastage.
- The support of local communities and under-privileged was incredible and with real heart. Carlos brought us to tears more than once as he showed how we can do little things that can go a long way.

The Not So Good:
- There was no rating of how difficult some of the excursions were (especially the jungle walk) resulting in some guests finding it very difficult going and suffer minor falls.
- There was no warning of how many transit flights were involved! The pre-cruise involved 2 transit flights and the cruise proper involved 1 transit flight, resulting in 3 flights more than we expected, a loss of 2 days just travelling. We understand this cannot be avoided (due to many direct flights being discontinued after Covid) but there should be full disclosure in your sales materials.

The Bad:
- We booked an extra 4 nights at the final hotel listed in your sales material (Wyndham airport hotel), but were never informed that the hotel was changed due to the new airport terminal being opened. We only found out when we arrived in Lima by which time we had passed the refund deadline.
- We immediately highlighted this to Carlos and requeted Uniworld's assistance to change our hotel booking from the Wyndham at the old terminal to the Wyndham at the new terminal, but sad to say that even after 14 days, Uniworld di nothing. Not even an apology which makes us wonder if they even tried.
- Very slow pre-cruise response to questions. We find this a consistant issue with Uniworld and for this trip, we got tired of waiting for a timely and complete response that we even contacted Aqua Expeditions directly. Happy to note that they replied within 24 hours and answered every question that we had. It appears to us that the team handling enquiries do not know much about the cruises we book, and either come back to us very slowly and/or with incomplete information.

This is our 3rd cruise with Uniworld and we have loved the ground experience very much but we cannot rate it 5 stars when you do one or two thiings badly.

So as to give a more balancd rating, we would rate Uniworld as follows:
- Ground/ship crew, guides, food, hotels - 6 stars.
- HQ (handling of pre-cruise enquiries, problems arising outside of the itinerary proper) - 3 stars.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Charles Applin, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=River Duchess, tour=European Jewels (AMS-BUD) 25, tourdirector=21041-50363
Service Comment:
Information, communication and passenger care were exceptional in all aspects. Staff were always approachable, kind and thoughtful, and provided a wonderful service regardless of what else was happening.
This was a genuine boutique cruise not to be missed.
Of note was how well the changeover of ships from the River Princess to the River Duchess was handled due to low water levels. There was a minimum change to routine, and the changeover was seamless. Super job.
Product Comment:
This was a wonderful, and well organised River Cruise. From start to finish, it was a magical experience, added to by the professionalism of all the staff, from Captain, Dining room, reception desk, and room service.
The meals were a standout feature, with a lovely variety of food at every meal, generous portions, top class wines, served with panache by the staff. Whether Breakfast, Lunch, or Dinner, all meals were a culinary experience, and always delivered on time.
Cruise Director Heidicha provided excellent guidance and information for all the excursions, as well as very interesting commentary throughout the trip: what a gem, and no-one got lost or mislaid. Well done.
The ship was well laid out, and our stateroom was fabulous - not that we spent much time there except to sleep - there was always so much to do and see. The on-cruise talks and entertainment were of TOP calibre, whether lectures, the Oompah band, magician or musical presentations, all was well received.
It was a great feature to see and be introduced to all the cruise staff, and to show them how much we appreciated their efforts. 
Well done.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Ilene Robinson Sunshine, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51847
Product Comment: We signed up for the “Castles on the Rhine” trip from Amsterdam to Basel.  The boat broke down before it ever left Amsterdam. After a day wasted at the dock we finally traveled for a few hours and then the boat broke down again somewhere in the Netherlands.  Uniworld ultimately canceled the trip and we were forced to evacuate the boat on Day 3. Some passengers made their own travel arrangements to stay on in Europe but we were traveling with an older relative who could not accommodate that kind of travel and so we were forced to return to Amsterdam and fly home from our starting point.  As a result, we forfeited our original return tickets from Basel and had to incur the expense of buying new tickets for next-day travel— a significant expense.  Uniworld customer service said that all of the lost airfare was our problem since we “chose to leave the boat.”  They didn’t even offer us the $500-per-person payment that they said they would offer to cover additional expenses due to the trip being canceled.  Now the company is continuing to spam us with marketing materials for future trips even though we asked to be taken off their marketing lists because we will not travel with Uniworld again.  A disappointing, stressful and very expensive waste of time.  From what we experienced on the boat, the crew were wonderful . . . But the corporate office is another story.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We're sorry to hear about your recent experience and appreciate you bringing this to our attention. A member of our team will be reaching out to you directly to discuss your concerns. Please feel free to reach out to us at customerservice@uniworld.com if you would like to provide more details.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Margaret Browne, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Highlights of Eastern Europe (BUD-BUH) 25, tourdirector=20132-50393
Service Comment: Restaurant, bar, cruise director, housekeeping, crew… all superb
Product Comment:
The Highlights of Eastern Europe cruise was our 10th with  Uniworld.
The itinerary gave us excellent insight into the everyday lives of people trying to rebuild their lives after the challenges of communism 
Our cruise director, Mirela was superb.  The SS Beatrice is very comfortable and the staterooms are well appointed with excellent storage and large very comfortable beds.  We are used to the very small bathrooms, but it was lovely to enjoy the spacious showers in our Bucharest hotel!  The staff on board were all most pleasant, helpful and professional.  Our room was kept beautifully clean and tidy… the housekeeping team worked their magic whilst we were out on the trips. 
The itinerary offered was well balanced with something each day that was of interest to us.  We enjoy walking and keeping fit, but there were quite a  number of less mobile passengers on this cruise and some of the venues with rough ground, cobble stones staircases etc were a challenge for them, and we were surprised that they had chosen this particular cruise as  cruise literature does make it clear that  river cruises are not always suitable for everyone.
The after dinner entertainment was generally good… mostly down to Mirela’s engagement and vivacity.
All in all… an excellent cruise… Uniworld is going from strength to strength.
,

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Deborah Korge, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50085
Service Comment: Our cabin attendant Daniella was so courteous and efficient. No details was overlooked. In addition, we usually sat in Michael’s area in the dining room. By the end of the cruise, he knew what our preferences were and made recommendations based on them. One night we stopped to see “Saving Private Ryan” again in anticipation of our Normandy tour. Because we had an early start, we left early to go to bed. The staff made sure that we were not leaving because of anything they had done or failed to do to make sure we were comfortable and enjoying ourselves.
Product Comment: It was an amazing cruise with outstanding sites to visit, food, accommodations and staff. One issue we had was the Moulin Rouge tour. While the show was spectacular, if we had been advised in advance about the less than stellar food quality and how cramped the sitting area was going to be before we made the shipboard purchase, we would have done something else that evening. We were also told that we would see Paris sights illuminated on our ride back but that did not happen. The other challenge we had were that several guests were ill with at least coughs and colds without making any attempt to cover their mouths when they coughed and sneezed. One person slept the entire ride back from Normandy across two seats because he was so sick. As a result, by the end of the cruise several people in our group became ill which made the rest of our travels uncomfortable. We had a car service picking us up and not knowing what address to give until the day before we were getting off the ship was frustrating, both for us and the company we hired. When walking up the marble stairs in the central area, the first couple of times we almost tripped on the top one because it was proportionately smaller than the others. We had to constantly remind each other to be careful going up.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Daniel Perkowski, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Bon Voyage, tour=Brilliant Bordeaux (BOD-BOD) 25, tourdirector=20099-50033
Service Comment: see first comments
Product Comment: Great pre-trip to Biarritz and the 5-star Hotel du Palis, set high expectations.  Boarding the boat, less so ... confusion after initial desk sign-in/photo taking and sending us to lunch -- at lunch an announcement was made that rooms were ready. Rooms were unlocked, so we unpacked.  No one came by, so we went to reception to get key cards, only to be scolded (yes scolded) for not coming to desk from lunch for the key.  (No one at lunch said to go to the desk).  The boat is old -- most decks (inc lounge and restaurant floors) are old teak planks showing dirt/wear, the brass bar counter in the lounge was neither polished or clean, rugs in the state room showed wear and stains. Rooms incredibly small and difficult to navigate.  Food was inconsistent, with only a couple of entrees rating a "this was very good" and lunch buffets generally iffy.  Excursions were acceptable; venues visited very nice (cooking class was great but did not deliver promised receipes), and guides were very good.  The rivers are a constant mud brown (crew called it chocolate milk) from silt, not Uniworld's fault, but an unexpected feature which did not help overcome the other issues.  Transfer to Paris for the post-trip was flawless, and the Hotel du Louve is the quality expected of Uniworld, except that breakfast buffet was small for a Hyatt-affiliated property with consistent watery scrambled eggs.  We were not alone in overall disappointment -- past Uniworld guests with multiple cruises were disappointed and it was a frequent topic of discussion.

Product Comment: The cruise was incredible, Ruben and his team extraordinary (Maria Theresa). However the post cruise was a dissatisfying. Our tour guide in Athens (Jordana) was exceptional, our tour guide in Santorini was a no show. Maria from the “office” not a tour guide picked us up escorted us around the first day, check in on us day 2, we had a fill in guide “Vicki” for the village/ winery tour. Our promised guide   “Panos” showed up day 3 in the afternoon and told us he had a bigger group so decided to be with them instead of us. The post cruise trip cost more than the cruise and to be ignored and baled on extremely disturbing and unworthy of your company. Although the fill in “ tour” guides were conscientious they were not the promised person. This experience put a sour ending to an extremely enjoyable holiday.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Josey Wong, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (MXP-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20142-51605
Description: Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto
Service Comment:
VERY VERY disappointed. We've been to Uniworld for a few times and this was the worst dining experience. 
Shore Excursions and front desk services were excellent. 
But the food on board was so low quality which made us think should we come back again? And definitely has lost our confident to recommend Uniworld to our friends. We had the striploin steak on board which was so tough that after spending 15 minutes to fight with it and only 10% was eaten. And
the guest next to our table just only returned the steak and complained to the waiter it was too tough for him. The only good food we enjoyed most was the "Mushroom cappuccino" soup which was strongly agreed by the other guests. The choices on the menu were so bad that 3 dinner and 1 lunch were veal and was so over cooked. The fried shrimps we had for lunch, the batter was so hard that  scratched my mouth and we had to remove all batter to eat the shrimps.  
The room service attendant was not helpful while I have asked for help while I was holding two hot tea and had no hand to open the door, I asked the attendant twice to help to tap the room card for me, and she said she was really busy to clean the rooms. Which she has walked passed me and rejected me the 2nd time and she was only 4 steps away from me. Finally I was helped by the passed by guests. Which I felt very bad for our first day on board.
We used to love Uniworld ,  which we come back again and again. We enjoyed the food and the services,  but after this experience, we were hesitate to go back again and we had lost our confidence to recommend Uniworld to our friends anymore. 
I wish you could take this comments seriously which we really had very good times with Uniworld and don't want to take it away from our travel plan. 
Thank you.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=John Buttgereit, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Sao Gabriel, tour=Douro River Valley (OPO-OPO) 25, tourdirector=20113-50467
Service Comment: Butler Paolo -beyond excellent, Front desk -Sarah- got us a doctor and stayed with us while he treated us.- Paula - she made everything move perfectly..  Abdul -gave us several tv lessons
Product Comment: I would love to give the Cruise a 5 star but this has more to do with the excursions than anything on the Boat.  Everyone and I mean everyone was so very friendly, courteous and helpful.  Special shout outs to Paula the Ship manager, Sarah the purser, Abdul the front desk and Paolo our butler.  Paolo was kind, considerate, intuitive and all you could say about what he does is excellent.. He couldn’t be better. The man who organized the trips-Matthias was warm and friendly.  He has a big job to keep people entertained and moving.  That was the problem - it was the length of the bus rides to get to anything of interest.  They averaged 1 1/2 hours one way and left little time for free time.  I feel Uniworld may be trying to find something interesting in the vicinity of the Douro River and there is nothing close.  I am hoping that you think of something like the trips we took on Uniworld where we got off the boat and watched a cooking demonstration, or  some other activity that should be developed that does require such long bus rides. Coming back from Braga on a Friday afternoon was really too much and then we need to scurry for dinner..   Speaking of the food - it was again excellent in all ways …and the laundry service was wonderful.. So again this was our 4th Uniworld trip and being on the boat there was nothing but wonderful - it just a little disappointing in the excursion area- time to think out of the box on that.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Vanessa Harrison, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Uniworld Owned, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50529
Service Comment: The staff as always were amazing very attentive and personality is there which is always appreciated. Food was very good Gluten Free Pasta was ok but unfortunately we had been previously spoilt in Florence with the most amazing GF pasta so nothing was going to be quite that good but everything else was wonderful and the GF bread was on another level amazing
Product Comment:
The staff on board were wonderful and very attentive as always. Cruising the Venetian lagoon was absolutely wonderful and there were a number of days when this occurred so it was really lovely to experience this which really is why you take this cruise as it is an experience you don't have when staying on the island only.

The guides are very good and informative, walked slower than I would have liked but this was probably due to us not being very far from anywhere and wanting to provide as much information as they could. 

there is plenty of time to do your own thing as this is a less excursion intensive itinerary than some of the other itineraries so if you like doing your own thing or just want to take it easy after a busy holiday it is a great itinerary. the highlight other than cruising the lagoon was the ride to St Marks Basilica at night with the city light up. After experiencing the Vatican at peak time where you are herded through like cattle it is really wonderful to be on the exclusive tour at the Douges palace before it is open to the public. When doing major attractions in tourist places this is absolutely wonderful way to experience them.

The rooms are smaller than some of the other ships in the fleet. With this itinerary as you are the only ones docked you dont have to worry about walking over other ships which on the other rivers you do so you can just wonder on and off at leisure.

I would say all of the excursions were very enjoyable except the last part of the walk in Burano the lace demonstration, which was not a demonstration but rather a sales pitch, needs axing it is not a great experience as they show you no skills just a lady giving you a sales pitch around the shop.

Product Comment:
Always easy to cruise with Uniworld River Cruises.  Everything is included.  Always included is a tour every morning or afternoon.  Usually docks at small villages which are walkable if one chooses to walk around town. Itineraries vary. Travelers can always find something to meet their needs. Most tours also have a gentle pace walk with a guide.  If one has mobility issues this is a good tour to choose.
Buffets for breakfast and lunch.  A wide variety of food on the buffet.
Dinner is ordering off the menu. Very good food

Staff is friendly.  Most will know your name after a day or two.

Beautifully appointed rooms.  Standard rooms are small especially if you need to move beds apart.  Choose a suite if you like more room.
Some nights entertainment is brought on board for after dinner.
All drinks included all day.  Fun to come back to ship and enjoy a drink before lunch or dinner in the beautiful lounge.
At the end of the cruise, reception will book taxis or busses for groups going to hotels or the airport.

Description: Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto
Service Comment: Superb. Can’t think of anything that was nothing less than fabulous. The crew obviously enjoyed their jobs and it felt like visiting family (family that you just love!)
Product Comment:
Fabulous cruise trip with Uniworld.
We went in April 2025 and enjoyed it so much we booked again to go in September! It was even better than April as we were able to take things more leisurely. 
All of the crew are amazing - friendly, fun and professional service from everyone we encountered onboard and the guides for the trips we did were also excellent - very passionate and knowledgable about their subject areas.
Food and beverage also excellent. Staterooms are quite small but very luxurious and very well designed. We thought it was the best way to see Venice and considered it to be very good value for the price we paid as it included food, drinks and excursions as well as entertainment some evenings. If we could afford to go every year, we would as we fell in love with Venice and the surrounding islands. One of the best holidays we have ever had and we have travelled extensively.

Service Comment: Please see what I wrote previously, as I referred to everyone I could remember.
Product Comment:
These are the individuals I would like to thank especially:
Krista - in the restaurant - was always friendly & helpful; Dimitar - the wine person was funny and wonderful; Ilber - who worked in the restaurant, was another person I always liked to see as he was friendly & helpful; Arbi - also worked in the restaurant and was helpful; NIKOLINA - at the front desk - I capitalized her name because she is absolutely terrific - keep her at all costs as an employee; Ilker - who also worked in the restaurant was terrific, too; Nenad at the front desk was very helpful to me in selecting jewelry, printing recipes & in other ways; Christina, who cleaned our room, was always friendly.  Desilava - I enjoyed meeting was was very impressed as she speaks English, German & Bulgarian; Dusko, the chef, taught two of us how to make pear pastry! Milcho - in charge of tours who entertained us each evening as he told us about the tours the next day (& Lui Usane???- not sure about the name).  Milcho was very helpful in two particular ways - in Vienna, when the group leader left me at the coffee house, contacted one of the tour leaders who came back for me so that I could enjoy the rest of the Vienna tour.  Also, when the ship had to stay in Passau, Milcho arranged for my husband and me to get a ride to the Nuremberg airport.  There also was another person, Lui?  who helped with all of the tours - checking people as they left for tours and then checking us back in when we returned.  I don't remember his name.

I am interested in another cruise that goes further east toward the Black Sea

Product Comment:
The uniworld staff, as usual, are worth the trip!  They are caring, kind and impeccable, paying constant attention to  details. THEY make the trip worht going on.

Unfortunately  the air-conditioning began faltering one day and by the next it was not working.  I question the way this situation was handled [ie, basically ignored that it was happening]. 
 It would have made all the difference  in the world to have ongoing communication  about this. Many people slept with windows AND DOORS opened all nite not realizing that they could have taken advantage of the cool air at 11:00pm.  It would NOT have been difficult  to at lease slide  notes under the door to indicate it was working again.  

The lack of communication is why we no longer do cruises with larger ships as it is obvious they don't care about information.  Unfortunately this felt the same and put a negative mark on a rather amazing week. 

With all that said, I was so impressed at the way the staff powered through  the heat with their long sleeves, and apons or coats, with never a complaint.

Service Comment:
We were happy to have Marlo’s to help with everything.  We especially enjoyed eating dinner in the small dining room.  The food was delicious and just loved the variety served to us by the magnificent staff.  We enjoyed meeting the gracious crew and Marella is a gem.  
It was hard to get to the buses so early, but I understand  that it has to be.  We nearly skipped one morning, but were so happy we didn’t. We’re also sorry we didn’t stay over in Budapest or extend to Bucharest.  Met some lovely people as well.
Product Comment: Just fabulous accommodations, service and tour guides.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Ira Riklis, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50161
Service Comment: Really outstanding.
Product Comment: The cruise was wonderful until Uniworld put us in a taxi instead of the requested sedan to drive to Munich from Passau.  The taxi was not properly maintained and we were in an accident on the highway.  My wife suffered whiplash.  Thank God, we were not hit by another car as we careened across 2 lanes, hit the divider, then careened back across 3 lanes before coming to a stop.  If our taxi had created a multi-car accident, there might have been a more dire result than my wife's injury.  Uniworld needs to be more careful about the cars/taxis they order to take their passengers from the ship.  We relied upon Uniworld to put us into a safe transport, and that did not happen.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback which has been passed on for further review. We are happy to hear that you enjoyed your cruise. However, we are sorry to hear about the incident that occurred upon taking the taxi. If you would like to follow-up with our team regarding your experience, please email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Christine Haugh, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Client Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50161
Service Comment: We received excellent service from all departments throughout the boat!  Our daily housekeeping attendants and dining servers showed an amazing amount of energy, and with a SMILE, considering the long physically active hours they put in.  The Front Desk attendant was especially helpful with a small business matter we needed to attend to, and we were so grateful for her help.
Product Comment: We enjoyed our River Cruise very much and felt like we experienced a great value for the purchase.  The boat itself was very well appointed and the staff were always warm, welcoming and attentive.  We liked all of the included off-boat excursions that we chose and each tour guide was well educated in their topic.  The dining experience was wonderful with many options to choose each day, including the three extra options at dinner each night in case we did not like the chef’s recommendations.  I would happily recommend a Uniworld River Cruise to my family and friends.

Product Comment:
The staff is amazing (with the exception of Jaimie at front desk), the food incredible the location amazing, dinner wine selections & sommelier amazing! 
But the wifi was terrible. You could barely make a phone call on the boat or use a computer. I realize this is a vacation not some of us have to work a little to afford it. Also the every day wine selection (not at dinner time) was lsvking& the only Rosè wine they offered was from France but a US import we get at Costco. That felt so disappointing on a cruise in France. 

We used the Uniworld bikes extensively & a few need a tune up pretty bad. 

But 90% was perfect & we will be back!!!

Product Comment: I am a repeat uniworld traveller.  This Paris to Paris cruise did not disappoint.  This trip was a family reunion gathering.  As always, the food, service and accommodations were exceptional and above expectations.  Uniworld’s cruise staff went above and beyond to make our trip special.  Sopie our server remember all of our names after the first day and within a couple of days knew our regular beverages.  She always had a smile on her face.  Nicolya was also an exceptional server.  The food was unbelievable.  Tanya was our cruise manager and we also had her on our Portugal cruise in 2019.  She is the best, very organized and handles everything with a huge smile and positivity.  She is very approachable and appreciated feedback good and bad.  Thank you uniworld for another trip to remember!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Joan Kober, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50086
