Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jeanne Morris, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51861
Product Comment:
Desk staff was excellent , always willing to assist. Very polite , friendly and professional. Safety was a priority. They were very organized with everything  for a smooth trip!
Housekeeping was excellent. Room was cleaned while we were at breakfast and dinner, twice a day. Thorough , well maintained and professional. 
Food was delicious. Breakfast and lunch were buffet style. There were a plethora of choices. Dinner was a few menue choices , also with a couple of regular staples. Several evenings we first hand witnessed people who had been forgotten to have been served. As wait staff were clearing everyone else’s desserts , then realizing people had been forgotten so then immediately served them. However, these now very hungry people , had to rush to be able to clear out of the dining room by closing time. It was not us. Wait staff was friendly , attentive ( except the above mentioned) very professional. 
Bar staff. The male staff were knowledgeable, friendly, yet professional. The female waitresses were  highly unprofessional.  They continually winked at the men customers and flirt. Obviously they were trying to get some good tips. ( Even though those were inclusive). They know who is carrying the wallet. The blond waitress was beyond extremely flirting. She was touching the men’s arms,shoulders and rubbing her hand on their backs as well as the winking. She made comments about how pretty she she thought she was. Friendly does have a line. Couples going on these Cruises are out for a romantic adventure and this kind of behavior makes for a very uncomfortable situations for both the man and woman. This was a common complaint we heard from other couples. Extremely unprofessional
Excursions : The night time castle and the secret garden were awesome ! The villages were amazing. The tour guides were knowledgeable, friendly and professional. It would have been helpful if a town map was left with the nightly papers. After the guided tours, there was often free time , so finding our way around or back to the ship or boat would hbe been much easier. The last two towns were great but very rushed. There was barely time to «  get lunch on our own » much leess see anything else. I feel these town visits were average at best. There were many other cruise tours also crowding those same streets. Same places for all the cruises
An overall map of the voyage would have been a great way to track where we were. Let people know what country or village they were seeing. Not just a blank map with only the stops labeled. Entertainment was a nice variation. The 60s/70s band was spot on. A lot of people seemed to enjoy and dance. The one man guitarist again was spot on. Less dancing but people were singing along and having fun. The violin player was breathtaking ! A beautiful time. We chose to skip the silent dance. We chose to spend that evening on the top deck. A beautiful warm night, watching the eclipse of the moon as the ship gently moved on the river. 
I did use the pool most afternoons. I was lucky to have the pool solo! Loved it! 
Overall I’m recommending the uni world river cruises to my friends and family. It was a nice adventure ! We were pampered and enjoyed! 
Thank you.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jonathan Haber, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51893
Service Comment:
This was our first river cruise, and it provided a contrast to our prior cruising experiences, which include a Royal Caribbean voyage to the Caribbean (two decades ago with young children), National Geographic Lindblad expeditions to the Galapagos, and Atlas Ocean Voyages to the Arctic and Antarctica.

A notable aspect of our preferred travel experiences involves the educational enrichment provided by onboard subject matter experts. Regrettably, apart from a single afternoon's commentary on the castles along the Rhine, there was a noticeable absence of historical discourse or lectures concerning these significant landmarks, geography and political landscape.

The S.S. Antoinette exhibits signs of wear. Our stateroom (422) featured a noticeable stain on the fabric wall, approximately four feet from the base, and the bathroom emitted a persistent musty odor. The cross-hatched lattice work within the restaurant appeared aged and soiled. The ship's overall aesthetic, despite its homage to the Antoinette theme, conveyed a dated, somber, and uninspired impression. Several fellow passengers, drawing upon their prior river cruise experiences, remarked that the Antoinette's layout was the least optimal they had encountered.

The top deck is in need of refurbishment. Upon boarding, the brown carpet was saturated and remained damp for several days, despite minimal rainfall. Furthermore, the lounge chair covers were rarely fully removed, even on sunny days during the scenic journey past the castles. The cushions for the metal chairs were provided by the crew on only one or two occasions.

At times, the atmosphere suggested a focus on alcoholic beverages, with abundant wine service at dinner and the two other social areas frequently occupied by groups engaged in extensive drinking. This environment did not align with our expectations for this type of tour.

During a "black and white" themed dinner, the crew's disorganization and inattentiveness were such that a table-mate retrieved a bottle of wine from the buffet herself. A server, upon observing this, reprimanded the guest quite sternly, stating that their employment was jeopardized should any supervisor witness the action. This incident created a palpable sense of discomfort and intimidation among our group.

We also feel that the itinerary, while visiting several villages and cities along the Rhine, gives the impression that Basel, Switzerland and Amsterdam, The Netherlands was part of the tour. This wasn’t the case as there were no activities planned at either port, they were just ports of embarkation and disembarkation, nothing more.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Tracey Haber, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51893
Service Comment:
The Cruise Director was very good and hard working to ensure we had engaging excursions.  

The housekeeping and dining staff were average.  Lots of room for improvement.  We never met our cabin steward, where on other  lines they excorted us to our rooms, provided and overview and were frequently available to answer questions or take requests.  The dining staff was occasionally difficult to summon for tea or coffee after a meal.  My husband has a food allergy.  They eventually remembered and became proactive, but it took a couple of days.  On other cruise lines, the dining staff knew our names from the first day and were attentive to special requests and food allergies.  They approached my husband everyday at lunch to have him select his gluten free dinner and they had it ready when we walked into the dining room.  We did not receive the same service with Uniworld.
Product Comment:
The excursions and guides were very good.  I would have liked more time to explore each site.  The two best tours were the Secret Garden and the evening castle tour.  They felt unique and special.

The meals that were served were very good, however the staff seemed short handed.  Serving took a long time and getting attention for tea or coffee was sometimes very difficult.

The SS Antoinette is in desperate need of a top to bottom refresh.  My room smelled moldy and i complained about it several times.  The wall paper had large water stains on the wall across from the bed.  My room was 422.  Everything felt dusty, musty and in need of a deep clean or replacement.  The chairs on the top deck were covered.  Guest were expected to uncover the chairs themselves, while this should have been done by the staff.  The outdoor carpeting on the top deck held water.  The day we boarded the ship the carpeting was soaked.  I am not sure if  it ever dried as I stopped going up there.

Have you considered adding educational sessions about the history of the towns we visited or the local wildlife?  There were no educational sessions offerred other than the tours.  

We are experienced travelers and have been on several other cruises with  two other companies.  We experienced better service, clearer ships, educational sessions and an overall better experience on both of the other lines.  Not sure we would try Uniworld again.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=M Charlene Krummel, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube (BUD-NUE) 25, tourdirector=20111-50263
Service Comment: Very good.
Product Comment:
This was a quality cruise.  The only reason for not giving it 5 stars was totally out of your control: The water was too low to continue on from Passau to Nuremberg. 
   I will say that Milcho did a wonderful last minute job of rearranging things and finding substitute excursions for those of us not up for long bus rides.  The Alderman Brewery substitution was delightful.  I've never gone on a tasting before when the "sample" was a full 12-oz glass!
   The home visit in Passau with a local family (Heinrich and wife) was SO culturally enlightening .  Seeing the way locals live was way better than just looking at another castle.
   The waiters were friendly and not stingy with the all-you-can-drink policy. The people at the desk were extremely helpful.  Traveling through a lock was an education and rather awesome. 
   All in all the Delightful Danube was a delightful experience.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Robert Kemper, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (PAS-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20119-50177
Service Comment:
The room services provided to us was great.  We had butler and laundry services in our package.  
Dining room service was good., although I don't particularly care for buffet dining.
Product Comment:
This review must be taken in the context that although we are experienced ocean cruisers, this was our first river cruise experience. 
Our suite was great, especially the bathroom.  The TV technology was also obviously fairly new.  The problem was that we could not receive most of the channels most of the time.  
We had excellent butler and laundry service in our package.
As ocean cruisers, we prefer to order from the menu in the dining room rather than attend the buffet, where food is sitting out for a while under constant warming.  Unfortunately, the latter is how breakfast and lunch are served on the riverboat. Premade scrambled eggs and bacon are not the same as freshly prepared. (In fairness, there was an omlette station.) Breads and pastries were very good to excellent.  The salad bar was also very good, except for the very limited choice of dressings. We don't drink, but there was excellent bar service visible.
Except for a couple of special events, the included tours were average, that is no better than that of the tours of other cruise lines we encountered ashore, although the locations visited were great.  We learned that the tour guides contract to work all the various river boats, so there was nothing special about them.  They were fine. The classical music concerts were great.  
Getting off and on the boat was frequently a challenge for those with restricted mobility, especially dealing with steep gangways, and surprisingly there was no accommodation for that. Also, there was no onboard contingency for health emergencies.  Instead there is a complete reliance on onshore doctors for even simple issues.
Nevertheless, we had a good time and met some nice people within our demographic. 
Perhaps the comparison is unfair, especially based only on this single experience, but in general, for me, upscale ocean cruising provides a much better vacation value.

And kudos to the staff who arranged and oversaw the excursions -- all very informative, enlightening, and educational -- not to mention awe-inspiring in some cases! Even the weather cooperated -- will definitely be back for more in the future!

Service Comment: Generally great. We didn't have a dedicated room steward and mentioned a couple of things at reception which didn't seem to get passed on.
Product Comment:
We had a lovely week cruising on the Marie Antoinette and were very well looked after. Food was amazing and sleep quality great. All staff very friendly and helpful.  Other guests were great and we made lots of friends.

Only minor issues were more relating to the ship and its layout. No seperate quiet lounge, We were unable to access the pool via rear steps from the top deck due to staff/crew private area and no tea or coffee making facilities close to our cabin. (we did arrange delivery of early morning tea which arrived twice but then failed to arrive after that).

Over all we had a fabulous week, weather was better than expected and we were sad to leave the ship.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Eifion Williams, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=River Duchess, tour=European Jewels (AMS-BUD) 25, tourdirector=21041-50363

Product Comment:
The dining room staff on the first half of the cruise to Passau were excellent, nothing was too much trouble for them. They were charming & charismatic & remembered all the little details.

The staff on the 2nd leg (River Duchess) were not so efficient in their service or attentive. However, the big tall young barman on the River Duchess was superb, he cannot be faulted, nothing was too much trouble for him, had a great memory & always service with the smile.

I found the changeover of ships very strange... 
the onshore choice of cities were not great or memorable
onboard entertainment was awful
the one cultural experience in Vienna was with a good orchestra in a soulless modern setting that could have been in a cave
no cultural/historical talks (bar one)
the ship seemed to berth in the worst industrial sites I have ever seen on at least four occasions
There was little or  no communication about ship changeover, day trips which altered because of that and we were given food vouchers on two occasions like children at school - complete disgrace - what about organising a choice of decent restaurants???

the ships are tired and in need of a vast makeover.
Cleanliness in the dining area was a problem - I found a spoon I dropped on a dirty floor still there three days later.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Timothy Byrd, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (BUD-PRG) 25, tourdirector=20109-50192
Service Comment: The onboard staff were all wonderful and did their best to make our experience as perfect as possible. We very much appreciated our Wellness Director, as well as everybody else we encountered.
Product Comment: We had booked the cruise from Bucharest to Nuremburg/Prague, and this part of the cruise, from Budapest to Nuremburg/Prague felt more rushed than the Bucharest to Budapest part. It was still packed with great service, enjoyable excursions and onboard entertainment. We loved the private concert outside of Vienna, and the visit to Spitz (which was substituted for Krems) was made very memorable by our local guide who was, to us, the best of some very good local guides on the entire cruise itinerary. We would have liked more time in Vienna (to visit the Belvedere Museum briefly only to see Klimt's Kiss and not the other exhibits was disappointing), and to visit Nuremberg only for a walk in the evening was to miss out on much the city has to offer. Prague was fascinating (unlike on the cruise, only breakfast at the hotel was included). The local host in Prague was very good, too. We would have liked more advance warning about how many local guides there were to tip on each excursion, and that onboard food would be easier to identify for vegetarian/vegan/gluten-free than having to ask the very helpful restaurant staff each time. (That is mostly the reason for our rating.) We could have asked for specially prepared meals but did not want to do that. We did appreciate that the onboard meals tried to include some local specialties to add to the feeling of getting to know each country/region.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Timothy Byrd, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Highlights of Eastern Europe (BUH-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20133-50385
Service Comment: Onboard staff was amazing and made us feel very welcome. For a few days it was suspected that a passenger had Norovirus and the staff were excellent at disinfecting all surfaces and maintaining an outstanding level of service while observing all necessary precautions to prevent it from spreading. It turned out not to be Norovirus, but we would have been in good hands if it had been the case.
Product Comment: After initial grave concerns about the record low Danube water level this summer, our cruise itinerary was mostly the same as published. The weather on most days was hot and humid (July/August). Our onboard experience was mostly very good, with the Front Desk staff, the Housekeeping staff, the Restaurant and Bar staff, the Cruise Manager and the wonderful Wellbeing Manager all being outstanding in their welcoming, professional and thoroughly pleasant customer service, often anticipating our needs. Excursions were well put together, we learned a lot and saw many interesting sights. Local guides provided good details about the sights, and fascinating insights into what it is like to live in each of the countries we visited. Onboard entertainment showcased local talent and again gave good, engaging insights. Most everything is included, however we would highly recommend to pre-plan for local excursion guide tips which are not included. It turned out to be challenging to manage, both for having sufficient small bills (e.g. 5 Euro bills or the equivalent in the local currency) and the fact that sometimes there was more than one guide to tip per excursion. Not all of the countries visited are currently using the Euro as their official currency.  Also, for onboard dining, there is no indication if something is vegetarian or vegan or gluten-free, although Restaurant staff can answer that if asked.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Raymond Russo, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (PRG-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20110-50249

The hotel manager did give us a $150 credit on our extra expenses.
We were traveling with a small group , but made no mention of this, for fear of ruining their experience while we waiting to hear a fuller explanation. 
How can a top quality group accept this behavior ? Their system of controls is clearly deficient. 
It truly put a damper on the start of our experience. 
Gladly discuss further, my cell 908 313 7172.
Tell how to best report or describe this experience to others in Uniworld management.
We were hoping to hear from you , but alas there is silence.

Service Comment: The service on this River Cruise was beyond excellent.  I have only positive memories of this trip.  It would be hard for me to single out any particular person as every staff member onboard was smiling, prompt, helpful and always willing to accommodate the travellers.  Breakfast, lunch and dinner were always amazing, our room was always spotless, the dining room servers were always helpful and happy.  This was my first Uniworld trip, but won't be my last.
Product Comment: My first Uniworld tour and I loved it.  There was nothing wrong, inconvenient, uncomfortable about this trip.  Every crew member was very polite, hard working and willing to serve the customers.  The Bar / Lounge staff worked non stop, the Dining staff were always there to please.  We had Christophe as our server and he was always prompt, explained the dishes well and happy to be onboard.  The housekeeping lady always kept our room clean and tidy and the couple of times that we bumped into her she was always smiling and happy.  Rick was always on time for his informative daily updates, Eva was always available and smiling, Front desk staff were always on duty and ready to answer questions.  I would like to also comment on the hidden people down below who must be working non stop preparing all the delicious food that's prepared for us.  The kitchen staff are often forgotten which is a shame because the food on this trip was always perfect.  Well done Uniworld and I will look forward to my next adventure with you.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Carolyn Crisp, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50527
Service Comment: Impeccable! Hotel & room services were perfect. Meals, beverages, snacks were outstanding - no request too small.  Our favourite wait staff was Christophe and Bahjodiv (i know i got that wrong!... Lovely young man, 20's, from Bulgaria...)
Product Comment:
My second time doing this exact cruise.  It was just as amazing, 8 years later! The ship had been refurbished and it was a whole new look.  Everything is 5* perfect, when it comes to Uniworld!  No detail is overlooked.  The rooms, while small as they always are on a cruise, are perfectly decorated and the beds, linens, finishings are all top notch.  Meals are outstanding - every night we dined on local items, brought to the ship and the meals were exquisite, and paired with fabulous wines.  All inclusive beverages were top notch! Staff are outstanding - they are all helpful, friendly, personable and really make a difference! Most speak at least 3 languages! 
All of the excursions were amazing - some were off hours so our group could get private access   Can't beat that, in Europe during the summer!  
I love how Uniworld is "all inclusive" and they mean that - there are no hidden extras or fees.  My favourite company to travel with!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Donna Benaroya, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Burgundy & Provence (ARL-LYS) 25, tourdirector=20100-50127
Service Comment: Excellent in every way.
Product Comment:
The service on this Uniworld river cruise was exceptional.  The crew and staff were all wonderful and helpful.  I enjoyed the itinerary.  Things that made the trip extra special were the on-board workshops and programs.  They were so much fun.  Food and dining service were great. Having laundry facilities on board were a plus. 

I do wish there had been another time added to the bus schedule to take us from the boat into Lyon. 
Also, the pajama party (which I didn’t attend) did not appeal to me. It seems very unclean to me to wear my pajamas in a public space and afterwards get into bed. I would not wear my street clothes to bed. That’s just my opinion. Others may have enjoyed the party. 
Over all it was a wonderful trip and I’d definitely travel with Uniworld again.  Thank you!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jennifer Rader, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Burgundy & Provence (ARL-LYS) 25, tourdirector=20100-50127
Service Comment: The service was second to none. You have a superb team across the board!
Product Comment:
The staff was superb, very helpful, fun and professional. I felt the food was average, the soups left a lot to be desired, and would have loved to see some French food since that was the location we were traveling through. I had to leave the ship to get wonderful French food, which shouldn’t be the case. 
We didn’t get a ton of time in each location, and I felt Viviers could have been skipped to allow more time in other locations. The tours that we went on were good, the best by far was the lunch and wine tasting at the chateau in Burgundy. 
The pj party should absolutely be night one, it would allow everyone to get to know each other quickly! The flamingo dancing was long, and I felt it should have been interactive with the audience, I ended up leaving but heard they did finally bring people into the dancing, just a little too late. 
The accommodations were tidy and clean, the room was lovely and the ship was beautiful. What elevated my score from a 3 to a 4 was the staff.

Product Comment: Cruise was great, excellent food and friendly staff.  For the most part excursions were good.  The cruise itself was good.  Now the not so good!  The departure left a bad taste in my mouth.  We left the ship for what I was told would be a 1 hour and 30 minute ride to the Munich Airport.  It was over 2 hours!  We arrived at the airport with less than 2 hours to check in and get to our gate.  The lines were long and the service poor.  If it had not been for my wife in a wheel chair, which enabled us to go through the short disabled lines we would have missed our flight.  It was very stressful for all of us.  Not a good way to end our cruise!  Uniworld needs to follow the airport guidelines and get people to the airport at least 3 hours before departure.  The night before I asked for an earlier departure and they told me not to worry 2 hours is more than enough time, basically I felt like they told me just suck it up and go.  On the verge of being a deal breaker for future cruises on Uniworld.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Thomas McCaleb, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Rhine, Moselle & Belgium Grand Discovery (BRU-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21633-51805
Service Comment: Top-notch. The restaurant staff was especially helpful in accommodating my wife's low-sodium diet and advising her what was best to eat.
Product Comment: The cruise was somewhat disappointing. We had done a similar cruise with another company 11 years ago. Although the itineraries looked almost the same, the detailed itinerary on Uniworld was inferior. There was less time in ports and it was somewhat disjointed (two pieces to the Rhine Gorge sailing on two different days). On our earlier cruise, we often had two excursions in a day, morning and afternoon. Uniworld typically offered only one, or if there were two, one was extra cost. Many excursions included wine or beer tasting, which probably appeals to Uniworld's clientele attracted by the all-inclusive feature, but not to us. There was also a lock problem on the Mosel, which precluded sailing upriver, but that was not Uniworld's problem (although I did see a Viking ship beyond the damaged lock). On the positive side, the service from the staff was top-notch, the food was good, and this picky eater never had a problem finding something appealing. The cabin was also extra-large for a river ship, although much of the additional space was absorbed by the walk-in closet, the large bathroom (very nice with double sinks and more-than-adequate shower), and a king rather than a queen bed.

Service Comment: Service was outstanding.  There was nothing that this crew was unable to deal with.  The best!
Product Comment:
The ship was much larger than other Uniworld ships, which was very nice because that made our stateroom larger.  Our stateroom was very nicely appointed. I was very happy.
Breakfast and lunch were my favourite meals.  Dinner was always a challenge.  However, that is simply because I have very basic tastes.  Most passengers were quite pleased with the dinner options.  
I really enjoyed the travel itinerary, particularly Trier and Strasbourg.  Roermond was a complete waste of time.  Most people have been to outlet malls.  You don't need one on a river cruise.  Our visit to Düsseldorf began at 9:30 a.m. and we were continuously confronted with delivery trucks throughout the walking tour.  Perhaps a tour later in the day would make more sense.  I liked Düsseldorf very much, despite the trucks. 
The Uniworld staff are outstanding.  I offer a huge "congratulations" to those responsible for the training of these individuals. Perfection, and so much appreciated!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Michael Villaire, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51860

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Theresa Dallimore, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Grand France (CDG-ARL) 25, tourdirector=20131-50290
Service Comment: Every encounter  with every staff was professional, friendly and helpful!
Product Comment:
We did not start off well. Our flight was booked by uniworld with 4 family travelling to Paris. We arrived ti Terminal 3 to find no one to greet us. Checking our package info, it stated Terminal 2. Security staff recommended we would find uniworld at Terminal 2. Four seniors with varying disabilities  trudged thru Terminal 1 to Terminal 2 in blistering heat Aug 9 to find we needed to take a shuttle. We were unable to find anyone to give direction to find our agent or transportation. A very kind security guard, using his own phone,  was able to call the ship who sent an agent. He told us we had missed our transportation  and would have to pay our own way. We disagreed. Upon checking his documents, he advised that he did not have us on his list to transport.We had now been stressed for 2.5 hours !
He did arrange for a  van to transport us to Joie de Vive. 
Our week on Joie de vive was fantastic! Food, wine,service,  staff, excursions all amazing! We were thrilled to have Pierre escort us on the train to the next ship. We were quite nervous after our a
Terminal 3 experience.
Second week on SS Catherine was a different vibe, more people, less staff, lower energy. Food, wine, staff and excursion also amazing. 
The clientell, guests were less friendly, more entitled to preferences seating and we even had 2 rude experiences with 2 people who saved tables in the dining room stating they were "their " tables. I also had a encounter in the laundry room where someone had removed my clothing from the washer and placed on the counter. 
These experiences did not detract from the wonderful experience of France, it was a trip of a lifetime! Amazing service from staff George,  Eva, Violet, David, Pierre, Ivan, Dilyana, Laszlo, Bristol who made our trip amazing!
We would likely not travel Uniworld in the future only due to the transportation issue.

The food was delicious, tastefully served and plentiful.  Each excursion was well thought out and executed.  There is no aspect of our cruise that I would change!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Ronald Iverson, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Bon Voyage, tour=Wine Roads of France & Portugal (BOD-LIS) 25, tourdirector=21185-50939
Service Comment: I mentioned it in my previous comments.
Product Comment:
We took two river cruises. One in Bordeaux (Bon Voyage) and one in Porto (Sao Gabriel). The crew in on Bon Voyage was much better than the crew on Sao Gabriel. Much more polished, attentive, addressing needs, and paying better attention to the guests. Way better service! The trip in Bordeaux seemed like we went back and forth on the river many times. That was not fun. The Douro was way better. Uniworld could do better with plane transportation. They booked us from one cruise to the next traveling on Iberia Airlines. The incoming flight was late by 45+ minutes in Bordeaux (has a 33% on time record), making us late in Madrid and almost missing our connecting flight. The Iberia reps at the gate were very indifferent making us feel like they did us a favor allowing us to board...as the plane was loaded, but the gate door was still open. We made the flight, but our bags did not! Uniworld DID NOT take any special efforts to get us our bags! We have air tags in them and could see them move. We were docked and our bags were in the Porto Airport, and someone could have delivered them to the ship much earlier. It just felt like all involved had been there done this before and nobody demonstrated urgency or great efforts to deliver our bags, and they showed up at 7:15pm on the second day of the cruise. Maria, the Hotel Manager seemed like she could care less! Not impressed with her at all!
When we were in the lounge for cocktail hour many times, there were not enough wait staff attending to the guests and we went to order at the bar often. There should have been at least 2-3 more servers during this hour! This was on the Sao Gabriel. The staffing on Bon Voyage was much better, and I would say excellent! Jose was terrific! So was his crew! Estevez was spectacular too! This was what we came from after a week, and they were great. Once you get treated that way and then you go to the next ship, and the treatment is way different, it makes you take notice! Then we get on Sao Gabriel and what a difference! They could use some better training and take a page from the staff on Bon Voyage, as they were superb in comparison! I do want to point out that in dining the server Barbara from Brazil was "The Best" of all the staff during our meals on Sao Gabriel! My complaints are there was not enough effort/attention given to getting our bags to us (like you did not care), Iberian Airlines was not real friendly, we were told Uniworld person would be at the gate in Madrid to take us to the connecting flight...there was no representative to meet us, we had to figure it out ourselves, take a bus, take a tram, elevators, etc. , and the staff on Sao Gabriel needs better training. It was like all the ones who needed training were on this trip! I will also mention that many, many guests felt the same way we do, and they never experienced lost bags on top of everything else! We have done four (4) Uniworld trips! This last one was extremely disappointing!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Andres Costas-Centivany, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (BUD-PRG) 25, tourdirector=20109-50193
Service Comment: See above.
Product Comment: Beautiful overview of Central European cities on and near the Danube. The ship is very comfortable and well-appointed. The Staff on board were outstanding for their genuinely generous spirit and impeccable service. The Captain and sailing crew did a great job maneuvering the ship around unexpected changes in water level, even adjusting the sailing schedule. Sailing was smooth and safe throughout.  The dining room Manager diligently inquired about food allergies or sensitivities and always had alternative menu items at every meal.  The Sommelier gave comprehensive orientations to all the wines and was also a great comedian. She helped enliven the cruise and make it festive. The bar staff and dining room waiters learned our names and preferences by day 2. The food was plentiful, varied and healthy. I wished there had been more local flavor as it often tasted like we were back in the U.S. The offerings seem to be tailored for a mild flavor profile, nothing too unusual or assertive. Housekeeping was superb and always took care of  my cabin whenever I left it. I never had to ask for anything.  Our Cruise Director did a stellar job of managing all the groups, offering different tours, suggesting places to eat and things to do on our own, while adjusting for changes in the ship’s itinerary. Local guides varied but we were lucky to get some excellent ones, very knowledgeable and fluent in English. Excursions off the ship were interesting and varied, despite the need to travel significant distances by Motorcoach. Pre and post cruise extensions are highly advisable in order to fully experience the origin and end points of the cruise, Budapest and Prague. Otherwise there is not enough time in those two cities.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Timothy Finnigan, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (BUD-PRG) 25, tourdirector=20109-50193
Service Comment: Attentive as could be
Product Comment:
Accommodations in our suite w/sitting room, bdrm and bathroom were perfect. Great storage & closet area aboard the BEATRICE.
2 excellent butlers in Marko & Marko. Very good laundry service
Food, liquor offerings were very good. The am offering of bacon was BAD every day. The bacon was overcooked and when you went to pick up a piece it just crumbled—-never had one piece of bacon. Item would be great for sprinkling on a salad.
Table waiters were very exceptional. Going thru the locks while viewing from up top was spectacular—-maybe a guide explaining the whole process would be nice, not every time but occasionally 
Veronica our overall director was just so so. We felt like she talked down to us and way to much talking
The music and lectures on board before & after dinner were great. One of the presenters on Austria was very very critical of his subject matter—-a counter reply might have been warranted
I had a cut on my leg that required bandaging on 2 occasions and the front desk could not have been more caring.
Unfortunately we were disappointed the water conditions would not allow us to sail to Nuremberg.—-last stop was Regensburg. We throughly enjoyed the private performance of Mozart/Bethoven strings/piano/brass and vocal, a very lovely evening. The visit to a private residence in Passau ( I believe) was wonderful. We talked about everything in their homeland while they served beer/wine & pastries. 
The buses and their drivers were outstanding & most comfortable. 
The captain was rather inconspicuous but possibly that is his method of operation. This was our first cruise. I absolutely enjoyed sitting up top when we sailed. 
On tours thru the cities I received a head set to accommodate my hearing aids and it worked nicely. When we toured the Parliament  bldg we were not given headsets at all as other groups we encountered and as a result the soft spoken guide was quite insufficient for the tour. 
Pre & post hotel accommodations were very satisfying in  Budapest And Prague.
The entire staff aboard ship were very very accommodating and could not have been more friendly and nice.
Transportation to and from the airports was perfect——even the 3 am departure from Prague !!!!!
Overall hats off to UNI WORLD 
We shall highly recommend you

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Catherine Arapali, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (PAS-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20119-50176
Service Comment: As above
Product Comment:
The staff were all polite, courteous, engaging and friendly. Nothing was a problem. A special thanks to our butlers (Dragus and ??) and Phillipa who is an amazing woman with incredible skills as a masseuse. Although all the staff were good these 3 in particular stood out and as we are in the hospitality industry and used to 5 star service we want to mention them especially. 

In regard to the tours, although Ed did a great job in co-ordinating them, we felt the tour guides in general could have been better. They gave us al lot of information but not many great stories which colour a tour. Facts are easily forgotten but relatable stories about the tour are sometimes more entertaining and engage the participants more. Our tour guide in Budapest was very passionate but most of us couldn't understand her because she spoke so fast with a strong accent. Our Viennese tour guides were both good especially our guide on the culinary walking tour - although the cake at The Landmark was stale .... the other customers not on the tour seemed to have lovely food but it appears that the business has become complacent with having tours come and the 'never to return customers' did not get a great experience. 

The food each night was good and varied but exceptional on the last 3 nights. I had owned a restaurant (and my husband had owned a bakehouse and was an amazing pastry chef) many many years ago so we understand the logistics of serving all those guests in such a short period of time. It is difficult and requires great organisation. So well done to all the kitchen and wait staff. We appreciate that what you do is not effortless but was made to look effortless.

Kremena our sommelier, such a wonderful personality and so knowledgeable with her wines.  She gave us some tips and information that we will remember always.

I did not get the name of our house keeper for our stay, but he was excellent.  Cannot believe how clean and made up our room was every single day.  The service done by a house keeper was the best we have ever had(hotel or cruise).  
 They all valued our guest comments and needs throughout.  Bravo!!
Product Comment: This was our first River Cruise and just loved it!  From beginning to end, the treatment we received in all aspects was excellent.  We went with 3 other couples and we had a blast.  The tours were fantastic, Staff was excellent, food was great(wish they had an option of doing a la carte or buffet, as we prefer a la carte only), rooms were comfy. We have been on larger cruise ships, but this was perfect for us.  The worry going in is always hearing that a River Cruise is for the “older” crowd.  Our group had a few in their 50’s and 60’s and it definitely did not matter.  No need to worry! Talk about being organized..WOW!  Will definitely be doing another Uniworld Cruise again.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Shannon Topple, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50526
Product Comment:
Very nice cruise! I am 53 and was traveling solo, this was my first time cruising on a small river cruise as I usually go on Royal Caribbeans larger ships. Average age of guests was about 70 yrs old, with maybe 2 in their twenties. Not a boat for children really as no much to do for them. Loved my room (320) with large window and automatic shades which I could raise from bed and watch the early morning water traffic bringing supplies to Venice. TV had lots of movies in English and power outlets for US, British and European by the bed. 
Food was very nice and for those that enjoy alcohol, it’s all included. 
The private tour to St. Marks Basilica and Doge’s palace were great! To be honest though I felt like their excursions weren’t all great. Also, would be nice to have a later start for the excursions as some left at 8 & 8:30. I chose not to rush to eat breakfast and then get on an hour long bus (with no bathroom) when we went to Vincenza. The theatre was supposed to have an amazing light and sound show for us according to the description but when we got there and asked the guide said that hadn’t worked for 6 months to a year. 
 I heard the Row Venice excursion was good, I did what was supposed to be a gondola making tour which turned out to be an oar making place. Most tours are included. I signed up for the Jewish Ghetto tour which was extra and it didn’t go due to insufficient numbers. The local guides that took us on the tours gave good information and spoke English well enough. 
 They also offer cooking classes, wine tasting, and a few other things for extra- seemed expensive to me. 
A few nights they had entertainment on board, but nothing like RCCL of course. 
There’s a lot of walking on this cruise so be prepared. 
A sweet little cruise of Venice and surrounding area which I would definitely recommend but just wish the excursions weren’t a little better. I stayed a few days before the cruise in Venice and am glad I did.

We loved all the excursions.  They were outstanding.  The food was very good and the servers were outstanding!  
However, we were disappointed in the evening entertainment.  Three nights in a row that featured the very same music, with dancing as the focal point was too repetitive.  Our previous Uniworld cruise ( on the Danube) featured a variety of evening performances ranging from classical music, to Oompa Band, to dancing.  Those evenings were far more entertaining; giving us a flavor of the local culture.
Housekeeping note: although our bed was turned down each evening, and a chocolate treat left on our pillows, our toilet was never checked and cleaned during the week of our cruise.  
We love Uniworld Cruises and will likely select them again, but were a bit disappointed with the details listed above.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Kevin Thorpe, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Rhine, Moselle & Belgium Grand Discovery (BSL-BRU) 25, tourdirector=21641-51806
Service Comment: Consistently good and responsive.
Product Comment:
Great itinerary. We liked the small towns.
The staff was very good and responsive overall. Capt. Mihai was very visible and engaging – more so than most captains. Hotel mgr. Martin was always very pleasant and knew our names from the moment we met him. Cruise director Ramona was very approachable and personable – her signature talk (behind the iron curtain) was very compelling and a highlight of the cruise. Consistently good dining room service, most notably from Patricia, Sofia & Victoria. Violetta took good care of us in the bar and Christian always provided very cheerful and positive service. We enjoyed the wine pairing announcements each evening by sommelier Nedko.
Good shore excursions overall. Guide quality varied quite a bit from excellent to poor and sometimes even between buses on the same excursion. We enjoyed the biking options. Great on-deck commentary from Ramona during the most scenic portion of the Rhine with so many castles. Very good musical entertainment most evenings by some really talented local musicians. Providing a few lectures about local relevant topics would really enhance the educational aspect of the journey.
A map of the journey is desirable. Ramona did a great job customizing one for us.

Product Comment:
I loved the precruise trip in Switzerland and the post cruise trip in Athens. The cruise ship in Italy was definitely not up to UniWorld standards. I have taken about 15 UniWorld cruises and have always loved the spectacular ships. Unfortunately this ship was a great disappointment. The room was so small even though I selected the level I always use and the bathroom had no room at all to move around. The common areas like the dining room and bar were also much too small for the passengers. In the dining room both staff and guests could barely squeeze between the chairs. The biggest disappointment was the decor. Everything was a drab and unattractive color brown. 
On the positive side the food was excellent and the excursions were very good. The staff did an excellent job.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the ship could have been better. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the food, the excursions and the service of our staff onboard. If you would like to follow-up with our team regarding your experience, please email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Michael Gluhanich, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50525

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Bethany Neumann, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50525

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Trudy Radick, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Bon Voyage, tour=Brilliant Bordeaux (BOD-BOD) 25, tourdirector=20099-50028
Product Comment:
While the staff and food were amazing, the excursions and itinerary stays need to be improved upon! Your description of the first few nights does not state that you will dock overnight in Bordeaux so many nights! If I had known this, we would not have come in to Bordeaux a day ahead, which was added expense and was certainly not necessary!

Some of the best excursions were limited, so we missed out on them. 
So many any guests were very confused about having to book excursions all at once on the first night. Why can't they be chosen ahead of arrival or at the very least, have time to personally ask questions to clarify which excursion is best suited for them before losing out due to limited availability? 

The oyster tasting was a let down! Why wasn't there anyone to welcome the group, as well as talk about oyster farming!?! We were also peppered with dust as vehicles tearing down from an event drove up and down the road as we came and went. There should have been more oyster stations as people swarmed to get to the tables. And why wine in a paper cup?  This event could have been a much better experience with a little more attention to detail. 

The post Paris land package was VERY disappointing! We lost a portion of the first day while sitting on board until 11:30 for our transfer to the train and then did not arrive to Paris until after 4 pm, then had to go through the itinerary briefing! I had booked an outside tour and lost money because we didn't make it in time. Perhaps, you could let guests know this ahead of time so they can plan accordingly? Only when we arrived to our hotel on day 1, did we find out what Uniworld's tour included, as well as the time. We also had booked a full tour for day 2, not wanting to miss out on the main sites and therefore, did not attend Uniworld's tour. Why don't you have a tour of the main attractions??

I have traveled on Viking, Ama and Avalon and have never experienced such a lack of excursion information on any of those lines! And their excursions were top notch!

Some of the staff seemed unhappy to be there. The reception area was not very welcoming. A nice smile or quite simply, even an acknowledgement that one is passing through the area, would be a huge improvement. The bar attendant, Izabella, seemed to be annoyed when approached for a beverage. Jose and Venelin, however, were very attentive and always had a smile to serve along with our drinks!  Dining room staff, Luciano, Anna, and 
I brought along 5 other couples and we all were discouraged with the above mentioned that we experienced, especially after hearing so many great reviews about other Uniworld itineraries!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Janice Crawford, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Bon Voyage, tour=Brilliant Bordeaux (BOD-BOD) 25, tourdirector=20099-50028
Service Comment: The service of the onboard crew and staff was excellent.
Product Comment:
Overall I enjoyed much that this cruise experience had to offer.  However, had I realized when I booked this cruise how much time would be spent overnighting in Bordeaux itself I would not have arrived a week earlier to stay with friends who live in Bordeaux. I would have liked the ship to do more "cruising" on the rivers rather than staying/returning to dock in Bordeaux.

Most of my excursions were excellent (e.g., well managed, well-informed local guides, etc.) However, I signed up for the Golf Experience.  Although the first golf course was as advertised, the second course at/near St. Emilion was a complete disappointment as the course was in horrific condition.  The fairways were non-existent, just hard-packed, cracked dirt. Definitely not worth the price.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Joanna Gannon, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (PRG-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20110-50248

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Randall Donlen, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50159
Service Comment: Five stars for Viktor, Rado, housekeeping, and the fantastic bartenders.
Product Comment:
It was a nice river cruise, our first.  Not a fan of having other ships parked against us blocking our ability to open curtains and hanging out our room.  Defeats the purpose of upgrading the room if you can’t enjoy the open window and small sitting area. Noting this is a common thing for all River cruise ships.  Except for that we found service to be excellent and the tours  enjoyable. 

Special recognition for Rado and Viktor, the two gentlemen who were waiters, bartenders, etc. Throughout the cruise these two were fantastic!  They made my wife and I feel very welcomed and special. 

Aside from the ships all parking together, our biggest complaint was the short amount of time Uniworld planned for our bus to get to the Munich airport at end of cruise. Only guessing they scheduled the bus for later people departures and put us on it to avoid having to accommodate our flight time with an additional service. 

When we learned what time the bus was departing for the airport a couple of days prior to end of cruise and added in the check in time, clearing customs, security, I could see there was no way we would make the flight.  I’m a frequent global traveler with considerable experience in this. We called this out to the cruise director upon learning of the time.  I told him it is very questionable we would make our flight.  He pretty much dismissed us telling us we had plenty of time and that Munich airport processes people plenty fast enough for us to make our flight.  He claimed he knew Munich airport as well as anyone and we’d have plenty of time and just ignored us. 

That was a bunch of bs.  It was a nightmare in the airport and custom lines were long. Check in lines were long. We were traveling with elderly parents who couldn’t move well on top of it.  I’ll spare the unfortunate details and drama but we would have missed our flight had I not grabbed a wheelchair for my mother in law and have her sit as a handicap person so we could be allowed through the short handicap line in customs and again at  security.   Even with that we raced to the gate, no time for a bathroom stop, no time to grab water, and fortunately made it before they stopped boarding.  We would have missed the flight otherwise just as we told him. 

That was humiliating and the reason alone I will never do another Uniworld cruise.

The second issue we had.....it was suppose to be a wine tasting cruise and it truly was not.  We did have two wine excursions but I think they should have had more on the ship without an extra charge to participate.  BTW.......our first tasting off the ship, which was presented with oysters, was served in little "Dixie" paper cups.  Who wants to drink wine out of a paper cup?  The guide did not tell us anything about the oysters or the wine! The 3rd thing...........according to the "vino" app that takes a picture of the wine bottle label, the wine they served at dinner was under $8 a bottle.  When you pay this much and travel half way across the world to experience great French wines, it just wasn't there. The staff were great and the ship as well but it was not what we expected. Very disappointing!!!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Peter Ferrer, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50159
Product Comment:
Maria Theresa boat, food, Deluxe stateroom and staff were  all good. Regimented, repetitious (“this church is the biggest.”, “This church is the oldest” etc.) herd like mentality- one group of tourists having to wait or make way for another is not for us.
Great to only have to unpack and repack at beginning and end of cruise but just spending a few hours or at best a day and a half at a port left little time to fully appreciate each location. At the end of the trip it just blurred into confusion.
Some may like the 5 towns in 7 days schedule but it didn’t appeal to us.
Finally the transportation arrangements are sub par. At the start we were staying at the same Kempinski hotel as those taking the pre-cruise extension but even though there were 4 available spaces on their bus they wouldn’t take us as we hadn’t paid for pre-cruise. And, to top it off when we asked the front desk to order us a taxi for 4 at the end of the trip to take us to a specific local hotel a small taxi with room for only 2 showed up and dropped us off at the Train Station rather than our hotel. No excuse for not giving clear directions to front desk as the second taxi ordered by our friends managed to go directly to the hotel. All in all hardly the first class experience we were expecting.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear about the issue with your taxi and that the ports of call could have been better. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the food, boat, stateroom accommodations and the service of our staff onboard. If you would like to provide us with any additional information about your experience, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Service Comment: It was excellent. Waiters were obviously very well trained. The food was delicious with plenty of choice. The talks every afternoon were very helpful, explaining the next days excursions. All the staff were very friendly and helpful. Can’t praise them enough.
Product Comment: On Our arrival at the ship we were greeted with staff to carry our luggage to our cabins. Booking in was easy and we were given lots of information and afternoon tea. The cabin was small but clean and comfortable.( it was the cheapest). The boat was lovely with the added bonus of a small pool which with high temperatures was very welcome. The staff were amazing, always ready to help. Waiters in particular gave impeccable service in the restaurant and bar. All the trips were planned to perfection and there were plenty of activities on the ship. In all it was a brilliant holiday. It would have been nice to have toilet facilities on the top deck but appreciate space is limited.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=John Butterfield, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Burgundy & Provence (ARL-LYS) 25, tourdirector=20100-50126
Service Comment: Simply Outstanding
Product Comment: The crew and staff on SS Catherine were simply fabulous. I could not fault the service we received from everyone. The food was excellent, and the included wines superb. SS Catherine was very comfortable, however the indoor pool is a waste of space. The gym was very small, but it was rarely used so it was fine for my daily 30 minute work out. The included excursions were great that we did and the use of the bicycles a great additional facility. We did 3 extra cost excursions. The premier wine tasting tours, of which there were two, were very overpriced for what they were and NOT value for money in any way. We would have been much better going to the onboard wine tasting event with our Sommelier, Lazzlo, who was absolutely brilliant. Our trip to Chateau de Rully with wine tasting and lunch was superb. Will we river cruise again? Absolutely. Will we sail with Uniworld again? Most likely. Will I pay for wine tasting trips ashore? Most definitely not.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Lynette Chudleigh, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50082

Product Comment: From the room service people, to the kitchen staff, waiters, cleaners, tour manager and Captain, the whole tour was excellent. The food at every meal was delicious with the 6-7 course Captains Farewell dinner being a highlight. The day excursions and night entertainment were well organised and thoughtfully prepared. An extra excursion to Amiens and Villener-Bretteneux could be considered instead of visiting the Normandy beaches and memorials.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Yijin Wu, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50157
Service Comment: already answered above
Product Comment: When I called and inquired as to the difference between the French Balcony and the Deluxe balcony the customer service rep said that the Deluxe balcony was able to be separated into another room, which I understood to be a walk-out balcony.  She didn't say the the two rooms were exactly the same size and that the window only lowered down halfway.  Unless you want the top deck, don't pay more for the deluxe balcony!  We had excellent experiences with the following restaurant and bar staff: Cristina, Tamara, Vanessa, Norbert, Milan and Rado.  Cristina and Tamara in particular were very attentive and courteous and we always looked forward to seeing them each day.  All the staff were very friendly and helpful   Our room housekeeping (Sylviana) was excellent and our room was always clean and  fresh.  The wines were excellent and we enjoyed the daily pairings.  The food quality was good but the shrimp at times was overcooked, and there was a lack of premium seafood (lobster, crab) which was missed, as well as premium meats (filet mignon, etc) which we have had on other luxury cruise lines.  We really appreciated the use of the bikes when on shore excursions, and liked the choice of shore  excursions (active vs. less active).  Will definitely cruise Uniworld over Viking again, but hope that the food options improve.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Werner Balsterholt, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Rhine, Moselle & Belgium Grand Discovery (BSL-BRU) 25, tourdirector=21641-51807

The trip down the Mosel was breathtaking, and the American Cemetery in Luxembourg was inspiring.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Michael Sawyer, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Rhine, Moselle & Belgium Grand Discovery (BSL-BRU) 25, tourdirector=21641-51807

When several of us caught a virus during the cruise staff went out of their way to deliver food and fluids and ask after us.  Meals in dining room were never rushed, attention to detail and beautifully plated with so many options.
The cleaning staff insured our rooms were always ready for us after our tours with fresh water and linens.  Cristian and Alexandro were excellent wait staff and bartenders, on the spot.
Product Comment:
As this was our 7Th Uniworld cruise we knew what to expect.  Six years ago we had done the same one-our favorite-but now it included more days and countries.
SS Victoria is a beautiful ship with an outstanding crew happily serving our every need.  We invited along friends to experience the Uniworld river cruise, their first.

As it was summer there were crowds, we prefer fall but this cruise ended in late August.  The Rhine and Moselle offer much.  The main downside was lack of visiting speakers for enrichment on board and not enough variety in daily tour options.  Some of the local guides were not up to Uniworld standards we felt.  We really enjoyed the Secret Garden and private museum in Speyer and wine tasting in the "caves".  The vinegar tasting and cooking class was appreciated as well.  As we age we cannot do the Go Active tours we previously did and even took breaks from the walking tour to enjoy the scenery and people watch.
The Chocolate Nation tour was fun, tastings were so good!

Our travel advisor recommended we stay on in Brussels to have a private van tour of Medieval Bruges and Ghent.  We are so glad we did, that was a highlight.  Perhaps Uniworld will add that in the future. 
This was a wonderful trip with 4 dear friends and new ones we met on board.
Again the crew was outstanding, Sabon, Cristian, Alexandro, Hanna, Keristin to name a few.  Their attention to detail was so appreciated.  We used the Vintage Room for a private dinner party to celebrate our friend's 80th birthday which was perfect.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Arnold Sheldon II, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Rhine, Moselle & Belgium Grand Discovery (BSL-BRU) 25, tourdirector=21641-51807
Service Comment: Most of the service was excellent. Dining service had some issues as described in my overall review.
Product Comment:
Sirs:
We have taken 5 riverboat cruses on Uniworld and this is the first one that has been a disappointment. My disappointment is for several reasons.
1.The boat is too small. We had a “deluxe balcony” room, but it was smaller than standard rooms we have had on other ships. For example, you could not close the bathroom door unless you squeezed behind the toilet. The bathroom also needs a nightlight. Old folks frequently have to get up during the night to use the bathroom and not having any light in the room is dangerous. 
2.The pool is a total waste. Our room (#330) was alongside the pool, and I didn’t see a single person use it during the entire trip. 
3.The dinning service had several issues. Breakfast and lunch buffet were good, but limited, because of the limited space available for them. On 3 different occasions, our dinner orders were mixed up and ether had to be sorted out at the table or sent back and replaced. On 2 nights we waited 30 minutes or longer after finishing salads before the main course was served. This is not the meal service we experienced on previous Uniworld cruses. 
4.The excursions weren't up to Uniworld standards. We were in one of the world’s outstanding wine regions, but there was no vineyard/winery tour with included tasting. A “fun” winery tour with dinner like was done on the Portugal cruse would have been nice instead of “here are some tokens to use in the wine dispensing machines”.  The beer tastings had a similar issue. They were a single glass in a town restaurant, rather than a beerhall or brewery with an outside beer garden, of which there must have been plenty around this area, and some would have included a fun dinner with singing et. After 3 or 4 city tours with “half-timbered houses and 15-16th century churches”, another one with the same thing wasn’t very interesting.
5.On the nights when there wasn’t outside entertainment in the lounge, someone playing the piano for dancing would have nice as was standard on our other Uniworld curses.
6. The instructions for operating the safe are on it's door, but old folks can't kneel down that far to read them. Printing the operating instructions on a laminated sheet and leaving it inside the safe where it can be removed and read would save having to call someone from the staff to show how to operate the safe.

On the positive side;
1. the “gentle walking” group for the tours was very much appreciated. 
2. The staff were very friendly and helpful. 
3. The food and wine quality was very good
4. The outside entertainment was very good
My overall general impression is that the current Uniworld approach is to manage down to a lower cost, rather than up to the best cruse available.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that your recent trip did not meet the expectations set by your past Uniworld cruises. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the food, onboard entertainment, walking tours and service of our staff onboard. If you would like to provide us with any additional information about your experience, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=James Deakin, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Rhine, Moselle & Belgium Grand Discovery (BSL-BRU) 25, tourdirector=21641-51807
Product Comment:
The staff was excellent. The food was average or slightly better. Some of the day excursions were a waste of time. Roermond, N.L. in particular.
If I could design my perfect itinerary, I would prefer two nights in each port, allowing for more time to explore the city in depth. Additionally, I would prefer that breakfast hours be from 7:30 to 10:00 am. Then have a six-hour excursion without being rushed back to the ship simply to have lunch on board. I can buy my own sandwich! Cocktails from 4:00-6:00 with hors d'oeuvres every day (not only for "special occasions"), I thought every day on Uniworld was a "special occasion", and then dinner at 6:00 pm. For many guests, if we had more time for a large, relaxing breakfast, we would not be hungry for lunch on board. Have the entertainment begin at 8:00 pm. If you want more guests to attend nights like the "Black & White" party, plan the next morning as a "sleep-in". If this type of itinerary were offered, I would book one or two Uniworld cruises every year. If not, I'll have to shop around!

Product Comment:
Alas, this Uniworld experience did not live up to our previous cruise with Uniworld, which was outstanding.  

Our cabin bathroom had a very unpleasant odor beginning the evening of our first day on board.  We informed the front desk the following morning, and they promptly treated it.  By evening, the odor was back.  Next day, reported the issue, treated it, fine for a few hours, then the odor came back.  We requested to be moved to another cabin--no luck there.  We insisted that they treat the problem at least twice a day, which they did, and it made the situation tolerable.  One day, we returned from an excursion only to find a pile of soiled towels on the floor of our bathroom.  It seemed obvious that someone had attempted to get to the root of the problem, but why would they leave that evidence on the floor!?  Someone quickly came and cleaned it up, but that should never have happened.

Breakfast and lunch buffets were very good, but dinners were only OK.  Not as much selection, and presentation was not exceptional.

Personnel and service were top notch, though, so maybe we just picked the wrong date to travel.  I still think Uniworld is a great cruise line, just not this particular cruise for us.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Peggy Pinkerton, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50080
Service Comment:
Our butler, Sandor, was one of the highlights of our trip!  Very attentive and such a pleasure to have has our butler.  We had massages by Gorman and he was also top notch!  Eva, Yasmin, David, Tania and Paulo were also so impressive on this trip.
We did question the Hotel Manager's role (Miguel), as we found him at the front of the tours with a guest on several occasions and also reserved tables in the dining area, which we assumed were for paying customers!
The evening entertainment was really not as good as past cruises we have been on ... are expenses being cut?  We also noticed that the frequent cruise gifts were repeats (luggage bands) and far less quality (blue cloth bag) compared to previous visits.
We appreciated the acknowledgement of our 25th Wedding anniversary!
Product Comment:
We love Uniworld; however, we were a bit disappointed in many of the excursions.  They were too many people in the groups and it was difficult to hear if the guides went out of range.  Normandy was impressive (and the main reason we booked this trip); however, the time allowed for this excursion was so limited - we felt like we missed out on a lot and really need to look into another trip via another company to get more holistic overview.  I would like to compliment Uniworld on having the bugler and white roses for a moment of reverence - it was perfect.
The Claude Monet gardens were beautiful - but, way too crowded and difficult to keep our group together and be able to hear the guide.
The Musee d'Orsay tour was definitely not worth the extra expense.  I found the guide to be dismissive to questions and felt extremely rushed - he confirmed that we only saw about 10% of the museum.  The description of this outing was quite mis-leading and we honestly should have booked our own tour and had more time and paid less money!
We also did the extension and were surprised that there were only 6 attendees in Athens and just 2 in Santorini!  Our guide in Athens (Molina) was superb!  She was extremely detail oriented and very considerate to ensure our visit was inclusive and enjoyable.  She definitely went above and beyond! The local guide for the Athens City tour was also very good!
Our male guide who met us at the Santorini airport was quite disappointing compared to Molina - he really just got us to our hotel and left! Our second "expert" guide on Day 1 was to provide a tour.  It was so apparent that he really could care less about this job and literally dropped us off at the restaurant with no explanation of what the dinner included.  Our printed itinerary said that transfer was included back to our hotel; however, this didn't happen - in fact, it wasn't even talked about!  Refund? The catamaran cruise was simply fantastic - a highlight of Santorini, especially after such a disappointment on Day 1.  Day 4, which included the Santorini Villages and wines was a great way to end the trip - special thanks to our driver and the guide - they both were extremely respectful and responsive!

I got sick during the cruise due to the mislabeling of food in my suite.  I am lactose intolerant and cookies that were provided in our suite were labeled as lactose free, although we found out later that one jar was not lactose free.  Due to this, I got sick and missed an excursion.  We did love our suite!  It was beautiful and very comfortable.  The excursions were wonderful, although demanding in all of the heat.  We were invited to a River Heritage Club Party on August 4 at 6:30 in the Vintage Bar.  We went and nothing happened.  There was no party.  It was the usual people there having a drink before dinner.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback which has been passed on for further review. We are very sorry to hear about the issue with your dietary restrictions; however we are happy to hear that you enjoyed your Suite. If you would like to follow-up with our team further regarding your experience, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Janet Olner, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. Elisabeth, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51876
Service Comment: The bar staff and the dining room staff were all attentive, friendly and responsive.  They quickly learned your likes and dislikes.  Although we rarely saw our room attendant, she did a wonderful job of keeping our room clean and tidy.  The bus drivers were amazing!  They followed us from port-to-port, so we had the same drivers throughout the trip.  And our butlers (they came with the Suite upgrade) were wonderful.  To a person, the crew and staff of the Elisabeth were outstanding!
Product Comment:
Cruise Director, Maria, was outstanding!  The service onboard was top-notch.  All meals were excellent.  I particularly enjoyed how the buffet was set up on the Elisabeth.  It was separate from the dining room, so it was less congested than other configurations.  We were in a Signature French Balcony (and received a complimentary upgrade to Suite class!).  The room was very comfortable with a walk-in closet, which was fabulous.
The itinerary was very good and the excursions were very well managed.  The local guides were all knowledgeable and spoke excellent English.
One area that could use some improvement is the sun deck.  There should be more sun shade.  There were two canopies, but there really should be three.  We spent several hours crusing the Rhine, enjoying the scenery and commentary about all of the castles and villages.  However, it was a very sunny and warm day.  Several people had to resort to hand-held umbrellas to manage the sun.
The airport transfers were wonderful.  We were greeted on arrival and were able to leave almost immediately for the hotel (we did the two-day Lucerne pre-cruise extension...highly recommended!).  On departure, we were just the two of us in the car, and the driver escorted us into the terminal and turned us over to a Uniworld host, who directed us to our gate.
Overall, I couldn't be happier with the service provided by Uniworld.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Gregory Nuckols, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Elisabeth, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51842
Service Comment: The quality and professionalism of service onboard was far beyond what I could have imagined. Everyone, in all staff positions, was unfailingly helpful and contributed to our having the best possible experience throughout the cruise.
Product Comment: Our recent experience on the Castles Along the Rhine cruise was wonderful and exceeded our expectations. We started in the Netherlands, and the local host for our pre-cruise stay in Amsterdam was tremendously helpful and really made our stay there special. On board the ship, we were highly impressed with the skill and professionalism of everyone involved. All the staff devoted so much care and attention to anything we needed during the cruise. The bar/lounge always had a fun and festive atmosphere. All food and drink provided on the ship were of the highest possible quality--truly, I don't think I've experienced better meals at any restaurants or resorts at any price point. I also appreciated the informative wine pairings from sommelier accompanying the cruise. The dining room staff made the already spectacular meals an even more enjoyable experience and a true highlight of the cruise as a whole. Their detailed knowledge of each menu, the care taken to answer questions and ensure each member of our party made their best selections, and the enthusiasm and positive energy they brought really made a difference. Finally, we greatly appreciated the Tour Manager's intimate knowledge of all the sites and excursion locations and the great care he took to ensure that everyone could enjoy the activities and all the amazing places we visited to the fullest. This was my first-ever cruise experience of any kind, and I was deeply impressed in all respects. We would love to go on another Uniworld cruise soon!
