clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Thomas Broe, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (PAS-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20119-50175
Service Comment: Again I emphasize the crew was outstanding.   Heidicha is so fun and entertaining.  From the boat crew, the wait staff in both the bars and restaurants and the cleaning staff everyone was wonderful.  The food and drinks were also excellent.
Product Comment:
Once we got to the ship everything was very nice.  However the transfer from the Munich airport to Passau was a mess and a bad way to start the cruise.  That I still rated it 5 stars is a testament to how good the staff, the itinerary and the excursions were!  
Details on the arrival issue:  We walked past the person that was to meet us as we went to the assigned meeting place from our ticket.  After nobody showed up we called the help line and it was a while before we were met.  Then once we were all together (after an very long plane ride) we had to wait for additional people.  Then the latest arrivals had issues with their luggage.  Instead of sending the luggage later we all had to wait another hour.  All this time there was very little communication.  Like how long we would need to wait.  (Never got an answer - it was much longer than the up to 2 hours advertised.). Also there was no toilet on the bus (which Uniworld rep in airport didnt' know) and so at the last minute people had to run to the restroom before a two hour bus ride.  Also it would be a very nice thing to do to have water on the bus.  Remember folks have been on an airplane for many hours and this is their first impression of Uniworld.
Again I emphasize the crew was outstanding.   Heidicha is so fun and entertaining.  From the boat crew, the wait staff in both the bars and restaurants and the cleaning staff everyone was wonderful.

Product Comment:
UniWorld Rhône River Cruise Review: Arles to Lyon
Overview of My Experience
I am pleased to share my thoughts on my recent Rhône River Cruise with UniWorld, traveling from Arles to Lyon, France. For the most part, this cruise was a positive experience and, as my second voyage with UniWorld—my first being several years ago along Portugal's Douro River—I was able to compare them meaningfully. The ship itself was beautifully appointed and offered a comfortable journey.
Areas for Improvement
However, there were a few issues that impacted my overall satisfaction. Most noticeably, the absence of a pool on the upper deck diminished the appeal of spending time outdoors. The only available pool was indoors, but it was very small, lacked heat, and was generally unappealing.
Flight Assistance Incident
A more significant issue arose related to support with our Lufthansa flight from Lyon to Munich. When a complex issue with our flight booking required attention, I requested help from the UniWorld front desk. The staff member connected me to what they believed was the correct Lufthansa department. Unfortunately, the number dialed led to a scam operation rather than an official representative.
During a nearly two-hour call at the front desk, I was told by the caller—posing as a Lufthansa agent—that our flights were cancelled and would need to be rebooked, requiring payment of $2,990 per person with the promise of a refund to follow. The conversation was distressing, as I was misled into believing urgent action was required.
Aftermath and Response
It was only upon returning home that I realized I had been the victim of a scam. I promptly reported the incident to UniWorld, providing the date, time, and description of the staff member involved. The company stated they would investigate and contact the ship. However, it took over two weeks for UniWorld to respond, ultimately informing me they could not confirm the occurrence of the incident with anyone on board.
Given the commotion at the front desk, I found it difficult to believe the event was unremembered by staff. UniWorld offered a $500 credit for a future cruise as compensation. Thankfully, American Express thoroughly investigated and reimbursed us for the loss.
Final Thoughts
While the intentions of the UniWorld representative were good, the mistake led to significant stress and many hours of follow-up after the cruise. This unfortunate incident marred what was otherwise a wonderful travel experience.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear about the issue with your departure flight; however, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the rest of your trip with Uniworld. If you would like to follow up with us further, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Product Comment:
Uniworld River Cruises Review: A Disappointing Experience on the Rhine
I had high hopes for my Uniworld river cruise along the Rhine, expecting a luxurious and relaxing journey. Unfortunately, the experience fell far short of expectations, leading me to leave after just two days and book a flight home rather than endure another five days on board.
The ship itself was visibly old and showed its age in all the wrong ways. The decor, which seemed to aim for a grand Venetian villa aesthetic, came across as chintzy and dated, missing the mark on elegance. It felt like a poor imitation of luxury rather than the real thing.
One of the most frustrating aspects was the coffee machine, which was plagued with issues. It constantly displayed error messages, making it nearly impossible to get a decent cup of coffee without a hassle. For a cruise marketed as upscale, this was a surprising letdown.
The dining experience was equally disappointing. Dinner service dragged on for an excruciating two hours, with slow pacing that made meals feel like a chore. The food was well presented and tasty but quality varied from good to very poor. To make matters worse, no food was available outside of designated meal times. For a cruise that’s supposed to cater to comfort and convenience, this felt unnecessarily restrictive.
The entertainment felt more like an effort to keep people away from the lounge rather than attract them in. For an all inclusive cruise the amount of up selling was significant.
Overall, the combination of an aging ship, lackluster decor, malfunctioning amenities, and rigid dining policies made the experience far from enjoyable. I couldn’t justify staying for the full duration and opted to cut my trip short. I would not recommend Uniworld based on this experience, and future travelers might want to consider other cruise lines for a more polished and satisfying river cruise.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Robert Steinfeld, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Highlights of Eastern Europe (BUD-BUH) 25, tourdirector=20132-50392
Service Comment: Very mixed...For the Most part it was very good, but they were slow addressing the issues.  Dining room service also very slow
Product Comment:
I am on the Beatrice now and unfortunately there have been several issues. I have reported them to the onboard leadership but they don’t seem to be upfront and honest
This week has been unseasonably hot and more than likely the ship is not built for this
The a/c on the ship is not working correctly in several areas including the bar, the restaurant and my cabin
I have been extremely hot and uncomfortable for three or four of the nights and instead of the onboard team just saying there is an issue they continue to make excuses 
Last night I could not sleep at all and at 2am went to the front desk reception area and the guy came to my cabin to make sure the curtains were closed and the French door was locked which it was
He put his hand over the cooler and said that he can tell the room was cooling off. Well yes if you sit in the chair right in front of the cooler then it’s fine but you can’t sleep there
He told me to sleep with my cabin door open (imagine that) and he would notify engineering
I waited 30 mins and engineering never showed up 
I eventually shut the door as I didn’t feel secure sleeping with my cabin door open
Also at about 1230am we were startled to hear a full blown fireworks show taking place across the river in Romania. I brought this up and was acknowledged but nothing could be done
I even took my pillow on Sun deck thinking I would sleep up there but the music from this club in Romania was so loud that it shook the boat


When we came back from our tour from Ruse in Bulgaria on 7/26 and walked all the way down to the ship, we were stopped and told that we had to wait because the river boat in front of us had to leave at that point and they would not let anyone on.  They told us it would take about 15 minutes for that boat to move and for our boat to reposition.  It wound up taking over 45 minutes while we stood there in over 100 degree weather.  They knew we were all coming back from this tour (3 busses) and it seemed like they could have prevented that.  They didn't even offer water to anyone waiting during that time.  As a matter of fact about 10 minutes prior to actually getting on, the cruise director Ingrid came off the ship and apologized to everyone for the wait.  That was the first communication that we got after 30 minutes of waiting.  And of course dinner was at 6:30pm so not much time for anyone to get changed for dinner.  At that point, I said to my travel mates, lets just eat at the alternative dining venue that night because we were all exhausted and didn't want to get changed.  When we went there at 6:30pm, we were told that it was completely full that night with reservations and they could not accommodate anyone else.  I said to the server that one of the reasons we wanted to eat there was because the dining room was so hot.  He said to me that was the reason that people had been eating at the alternative dining room all week because they said it was too hot to eat in the main dining room.  So we all wound up in our shorts going to the main dining room.  They did move us from our regular table to a table on the other side of the restaurant which they said would be cooler, but it really wasn't.  Also that night when we went to the bar, it was completely empty (it had been pretty busy on previous nights).  When I asked the server why it was so empty, he said because people were just too hot being in there.  I did feel for the staff as they had to work in the heat as well.

Also, we had to dock short of our last port  in Giurgiu because of the water levels being low which was understandable.  It did add 30 minutes to our bus ride into Bucharest.  We were told that our luggage would be in our rooms at the hotel in Bucharest.  We wound up getting to the hotel around 1:00pm and we could not check in until 3 but the concierge desk set up for us told us to go to lunch and anytime after 3 we could check in and our luggage would be there.  So we went out for lunch and walked around and did not get back until 4:00pm.  At that point we were easily able to check in.  When we got to the room, our luggage was not there.  We went to the front desk and were told that the bus taking our luggage broke down and the luggage would be there by 5:00pm (it showed up around 5:15pm)

I did talk to the cruise director twice and the second time it was the cruise director and the Hotel Manager. They were open to the issues and apologized and said they would address the way the communication was handled with the front reception area.  The hotel manager said there were two commercial fans on board and he would have one placed in our cabin that night which he did and it was so much more comfortable.  He also said the reason the boat was so hot was because the weather was so hot and the river was much warmer than normal which caused the boat to be hot especially on the side of the boat that we were on.

Service overall was outstanding and tours tremendous but clearly not pleased with how they handled the above issues.  Overall I was quite shocked that such an upscale brand had so many issues and the lack of transparency was quite surprising.  As you know, I traveled with two other couples so felt like I had to constantly make excuses as I was the one that recommended the trip to them.  Both of the other couples were on the other side of the ship and never felt their cabins were hot.  They only felt the heat in the dining room and in the lounge area and overall, I would say that they had a great trip.


Thanks
Rob

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Elizabeth Craft, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50080
Product Comment:
Loved the trip. Excellent service, accommodations, food, excursions. 

One note: My co-worker and friend, fellow travel agent Kerry K., became ill on the trip and was diagnosed with Salmonella when she returned to US. I only tell you this in case you happen to have multiple cases. She obviously could have gotten it anywhere, but wanted to alert you anyway.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Daniel Paulos, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50080
Service Comment: As usual, the service was excellent, which made one breakfast experience stand out in a funny way. By the second or third day of these cruises, the waitstaff usually remember exactly what I drink and have it on the table before I even sit down. But one morning, that did not happen. I had to flag down a waitress and request an espresso, which arrived after my meal and had to be chugged like a college student’s last coffee before an exam as I rushed off to an excursion. I mention this only because it shows how consistently attentive and detail oriented the staff are, and the one miss was so unusual it became memorable.
Product Comment: This was our third Uniworld river cruise and once again we were very pleased. Each ship and river is different, and while all have been excellent, this was the nicest ship yet. The decors on all the ships have been unique and not what I would choose at home, but they work beautifully onboard. The crew was professional, attentive, and genuinely friendly. The only disappointment was the excursion to Normandy and the World War II sites. Unless you are a serious WWII enthusiast, it is a long 13-hour day focused heavily on bunkers, tanks, and military history. The American cemetery was moving, but the crowds and the long drive each way took away from the experience. Many visitors might think they will enjoy these sites, but they may not. We didn’t.

Service Comment: Easily edged out Viking...
Product Comment:
The only real problem with cruising is that I don't get to spend much time in any one place.  I like to go deep into local history, and there just isn't that much time when the boat leaves at 7pm.   If I were doing my own itinerary, I would allocate a bare minimum of a few days in any location, and that's just not possible on a cruise, but that's me.  For the time spent on board, it was all "first class".

Without exception, the people we met were all we would be pleased to have as friends, and that includes the staff.

Included a few pictures, but they don’t do the justice as everything was so beautiful.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Kenneth Nuger, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Rhine, Moselle & Belgium Grand Discovery (BRU-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21633-51804
Service Comment: The staff were all exceptional.  They all deserve big raises.
Product Comment: The ship has its problems.  The air conditionng in the lounge was broken on our first day.  We had a false alarm awaken the ship a bit after 5 a.m. one morning.  We had to change rooms since the first one we had had some strange knocking sound keeping us up all night long.  The water heater broke so we had no hot water for one full day.  It's lucky that the ship was able to replace the boiler where we were that day.  The food was good but seemed not much more than cafeteria food, with the exception of lunch roasted meats, which were excellent. The duck confit was the absolute worst I've ever tasted and it's one of my all time favorite meals. The thermostats in the state rooms are very difficult to set and keep accurate.  But now for the good news.  The whole staff was outstanding.  Special kudos to Antony, Martin as the managers of the ship.  The wait staff were all outstanding.  Special shout out to Maya and Victoria, Nuno, and Max.  Our housekeepers were very kind and efficient.  The excursions were generally fun.  This was our second Uniworld cruise, the first being in the spring 2024 from Budapest to Bucharest, which overall was a better cruise, mainly due to the ship being in much better condition.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Susan Wake, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Rhine, Moselle & Belgium Grand Discovery (BRU-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21633-51804

Product Comment: The route was interesting and daily tours well led. Excellent accomodations and service. Delicious food and endless beverages. All tips included so no surprises at end of trip. Uniworld Is the best.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Oguz Yildirim, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Rhine, Moselle & Belgium Grand Discovery (BRU-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21633-51804

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Andrea Roitman, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Rhine, Moselle & Belgium Grand Discovery (BRU-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21633-51804
Service Comment: They were wonderful and did everything to please you.
Product Comment: The crew made this vacation so wonderful. We especially liked the special treatment by Sorin in the dining room, getting us fresh juice and cinnamon rolls every morning - loved the Belgium waffles. Places we went to were all great. Did not like we had to make choices when both towns were great. Should have found a way to let us go to both. We could have seen Lucerne and Basel on one day since shops were closed in Lucerne due to a holiday. The cruise in Lucerne was a waste of time. Same issue the day we went to the villeroy and boch factory. We should have gone to Luxembourg instead. That visit was terrible. There should not have been entertainment the last night of the cruise since we were all packing; the  we night before would have been better. Games on board would have been nice and easy to orchestrate. Slow walkers missed out. Maybe they could have left a half hour earlier. Free laundry and quick turnaround was incredible. Lounge on the 3rd floor was freezing. They never got the temperature right in there despite many people complaining. Tables for 6 in the dining room would have been nice. Free transfers from the ship would have been a nice touch.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Maureen Frazier, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=Highlights of Eastern Europe (BUH-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20133-50384
Service Comment: Please see my last entry.  The Best Trip of My life.
Product Comment: Our 2 hr bus ride to the boat turned into a 3 hour ordeal as the bus broke down.  the minute I arrived on the boat I was met with Nikolai, tall elegant, a quintessential gentleman, exquisitely dressed.  He took my hand and said, "Madam, let us help you.  We are so glad to welcome to the Duchess". I knew I was safe and going to have a fabulous time.  And I did.  Nickolai, always greeted me on returning from the day's visit, inquiring as to my day, my thoughts, did I like it, etc, a dear friend; Ivan from housekeeping, always there to help with the smallest request; Benny from the restaurant, tall smiling, talking about his Grandma, so much kindness and love; Tiago from the whole ship, always moving with the speed of light, great big grin, teaching me simple Portuguese words; Miguel, from the Restaurant, charming, old world courtesy, smiles, also teaching me simple Portuguese, Fernay, the chef, cooking my omelet to perfection every morning, never got it wrong, teaching me how to say Thank you in Turkish, laughing at how I got it so wrong; Celine and (I believe) Suzette (If I got that wrong please forgive me), reception, again no request was too small to grant, no query to silly to answer; Szilard in the lounge, always knew what I liked to drink, always courteous and happy; Peter, always where you least expected him but most needed him, bounding up the gangplank to where I and another guest were struggling to get to the boat, a smile, a wink, an old European flourish and a simple "Please let me assist you Madam", Peter, my hero, patiently teaching me to say Bladugaria (sp?), such a dear friend; at the end were some new faces, Victoria, Ilyan, and Florin, exceptional as all the others in their job; lastly were 2 lovely people I must mention. Vladimir, head of the restaurant, making sure every meal was 5 Star (I would give 10 stars), arranging for a champagne celebration for one of our fellow guests, valiantly trying to recover from a serious illness who had successfully completed a walking tour one day; that was so special, and so much more than expected. And Of course, Milcho, patient, smiling, kind, totally professional, and completely friendly, a walking wikipedia, arranging the absolute best days, day after day after day.  These were not the only people I met on the ship.  If I have forgotten you, please forgive me, and know that your every kindness made this (my 4th trip with Uniworld), one of the best times of my life.  Thank you from the bottom of my heart, Maureen Frazier

Service Comment:
I have been on over thirty ocean cruises.  This was my first river cruise.  
Cabin steward was fantastic!
Most of the dining room wait staff was a disappointment.  There were only three dining staff that impressed me.  Once we finished dinner, dining staff did not offer more wine.

Product Comment:
I have been on over thirty ocean cruises.  This was my first river cruise.  The ports along the Rhine River were nice.  The food was wonderful!  Our cabin steward was fantastic!
Bike rides were great along with the tour guides!  Fantastic was to see the countryside.  Amsterdam canal cruise was a fantastic way to see the beautiful city.

Most of the dining room wait staff was a disappointment.  There were only three dining staff that impressed me.  Once we finished dinner, dining staff did not offer more wine.
The cruise director seemed stressed and hurried the entire time. 
We expected cruise talks along with regional music and dance from people who live in the area.
Tour guide in Speyer made us leave after a short time rather than on time  because he had a tour going to Heidelberg later that day.  
Horrible to go to a graffitti museum rather than go to one of the famous museums in Amsterdam. 
Tour guide in Amsterdam was more interested in getting his tips after the tour was over than answering questions we had.
While at port there were many occasions that we looked at a wall or another passengers cabin window from a different ship!  Creepy!

There was beautiful scenery along the Rhine River...castles and vineyards.  I wish we could have stopped along some of the smaller cities.  They looked so warm and inviting.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback We are sorry to hear that some aspects of your trip could have been improved, but we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the ports, food and bike/canal tours. Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Richard Wolf, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21658-51816
Service Comment: Some waiters were excellent but more than one seemed more focused on clearing the table rather than providing more of the included wine.  The Sun Deck often lacked any service where many passengers spent their late afternoon relaxing.  Yet, rarely did a server appear who could bring drinks to that deck.  The cabin stewards were excellent and reception desk very helpful.
Product Comment:
While the Rhine River is a great destination, the cruise itself left things to be desired.  Food and wine choices were very nice but dining service was spotty.  Some waiters were excellent but more than one seemed more focused on clearing the table rather than providing more of the included wine.  

The cabin was nice -- second deck with "French balcony" and well appointed. But on several occasions, the ship docked next to a wall or next to another cruise ship which meant our large window was useless and the extra charge a waste of money.  The cabin stewards were excellent and reception desk very helpful.  

Included excursions were a mixed bag.  Breisach and Nijmegen bicycle tours were great but Speyer excursion was very short and rushed as the leader had to get back to the ship for the Heidelberg excursion.  In Amsterdam, the canal cruise tour was combined with a tour of the Stadt Museum -- "street art" aka grafitti!  The canal tour leader was good but at the end was so focused on being sure every passenger gave him a tip that he completely ignored us when we tried to ask some questions.  

It was a cruise of missed opportunities by Uniworld.  There were no performances on the ship by a musician or dancers that reflected the culture of the area we were visiting, a seemingly obvious omission.  The performances on the ship were a singer singing 80s hits, a 3 piece band playing similar music and a disco headphones dance. 

I would heartily recommend a Rhine river cruise -- just not with Uniworld.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback which has been passed on for review. We are sorry to hear that your trip could have been improved, but we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the food and wine. If you would like to provide us with any additional feedback, please feel to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Julie Clark, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50521
Service Comment: The service was excellent.
Product Comment:
We have recently returned from our fifth Uniworld cruise and this, without a doubt, was even better than the previous trips (we thought that the previous time as well!). Mark in their reservations team took care of our arrangements again and he’s so helpful, nothing is too much trouble. 

The on board experience was, yet again, superb from start to finish. A lovely welcome from Giongyi, who was fantastic, friendly, professional and efficient throughout the whole cruise. She oversaw and planned some amazing excursions. 

We always really enjoy great food with Uniworld, although this time the quality and standard of meals was even more superb. The best. A really talented team in the SS Venezia kitchen, and a lovely friendly and helpful and talented Dutch Chef, Robbert. 

We were delighted that see a few of the crew from previous trips which was lovely. The lovely Karolina, who is the hotel manager and looks after everyone and everything with calm and friendly efficiency. Also wonderful to see Andre, who looked after us so well on a previous cruise and now this one. As well as Yordan who managed the excellent restaurant and beverage services on board. 

Thanks must also go to Anca, Ana reception, Purser Marta, Sophia, 
Petra, Kaloyan, Dalyon, Svetlana, Delyan, Paul and Simon. 

The sailing along the lagoon was incredible and topped off by great excursions. Managing to visit the main highlights of Venice and the islands without the crowds made such a difference. A highlight was also the Po Delta trip with knowledgeable guide, Jodi, and the bragazzo boat ride and ca’ zen trip with another excellent guide, Sabrina. 

The ship itself is stunning, absolutely beautiful and immaculate, with the best views. 

We have already rebooked onboard for our next trip, the Danube, and are counting the weeks away. 

We couldn’t recommend the Uniworld Venice sailing enough and will do it a second time in the future as it was so good it has to be done again!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Vanessa Garner, loyalty=NO, package=South America, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=Aria Amazon, tour=Peruvian Rivers & Rainforest Discovery (LIM-LIM) 25, tourdirector=20145-50632
Service Comment: World class service with attention to every detail and even ones you wouldn’t have thought of.
Product Comment: Best trip ever! This was our first experience with Uniworld and with river cruising and it will be a tough trip to top.  I am not sure what to rave about first. The itinerary was a once in a lifetime experience with surprises planned by the crew at every turn.  The size of the boat may have appeared small on paper but the rooms and ammenities were perfect for this trip.  The food was the best we have eaten on a trip and the attention to detail by the crew and naturalists was next level. That is just the ship and the people. The landscape and animals and birds we encountered were amazing. The naturalists and the skiff drivers were unbelievable at finding the Amazon’s inhabitants. We went in July which is the start of the dry season and it was a perfect time in my opinion. This is a trip for people who love nature, animals and a unique experience. If you are looking for nightlife and a “traditional cruise” experience this NOT for you.

Product Comment:
We recently enjoyed a cruise on the lower Danube with Uniworld.  We were attracted to it because it took us to countries that we hoped were less traveled — Beginning in Hungary and sailing south through Croatia, Serbia, Bulgaria and Romania.  The itinerary was amazing and we also cruised through the Iron Gates, a very picturesque area on the Danube followed by two locks.    
The crew were all lovely and supportive.  The food was enjoyable but what we hadn’t planned on was the heat.  A couple of days over 100 Farenheit left us staying on the ship rather than enjoying tours.  The ship’s air con also had difficulty handling this level of heat, so although it wasn’t intolerable, dinners left us feeling quite warm!  A barge stranded in the middle of the river due to low water levels changed our itinerary.  Through it all, our cruise director managed to provide for a lovely experience.  Ingrid was amazing.  Great itinerary, wonderful staff and passengers!

Service Comment:
Outstanding!!!

I provided much detail already. See earlier answer-
Overall, I did not have one question unanswered in a thorough and friendly manner, several times an employee went above and beyond. For example, Mina at guest services spent a lot of time helping me with details, curiosity questions, and helpful tidbits to improve our day touring with my mobility challenged husband and also with my Kermit photo project.

Teodora was outstanding and professional- I had 2 fabulous sessions and my very nervous father-in-law had his first ever massage with her. He was nervous and came out singing her praises! 

The dining room servers, lounge servers and bartenders knew our preferences within 2 days and greeted each of us with a smile and asked if we’d like ….. whatever our usual was: a beer, an Aperol Spritz, a specific wine, a seltzer with a lime…. They knew!

Tommy did a great job in our room and answering g questions… dry cleaning, luggage in hall, etc 

Everyone on this ship was professional, friendly, patiently guiding and answering questions, seeing something and helping without being asked. Always with smiles!!!

Thank you for the service, the quality of our experience and the wonderful memories & friendships, created on this journey.

The transfer to land and airports was also very smooth and clearly communicated. Thank you.
Product Comment:
Every staff member was friendly, helpful, respectful, professional and went above and beyond with a smile. I had delightful interactions with so many staff who were willing and helpful in helping me with a project I was doing- pictures of Kermit engaged in various activities that I sent to my grandchildren daily. From Mina at the front desk to Teodora in the spa, to Chef in the kitchen, to Tommy our cabin valet, to bartenders, waiters and cleaning staff to the Captain- everyone took a few moments  to engage in this project!


The ship was well maintained, clean, comfortable and attractive.

The tours were varied and well run. Only suggestion is to let passengers know the dress expectation for the synagogue. I would have easily worn a top with short sleeves had I known.

Food was delicious. Dining area lovely. Staff friendly and very attentive. 
Entertainment was fun and lounge enjoyed.

The tours with two different paces were greatly appreciated as my husband’s mobility requires a later or medical walking sticks.

The size of the ship was perfect. 
Compared to Viking it lent itself to crossing paths with most guests repeatedly, creating relationships and interesting conversations. 

The regular room could use and additional foot between end of bed and dresser/ desk area. It was difficult for us to move around with the furniture. We stuck the desk stool and the glass small table under the desk alcove and still had a hard time, given the rolator ( that folds up)

Overall- we would take another Uniworld cruise and would recommend to others.

The quality of the whole service has remained at an extremely high standard, from the initial booking with the lovely team in Guernsey to the final transfer to  Marselles Airport. Food choice and quality was first class. Great to see evening entertainment firmly back on the agenda. We were disappointed that there wasn't an option for the fine dining evening. The staff were exceptional, especially Tania and Ionut. The food was also delicious, with excellent menu selections. We found the excursions both interesting and varied. Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Judith Nolta, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50079
Service Comment: Covered in overall review.
Product Comment:
The ship itself is very nice.   Cabin is spacious for a cruise ship with plenty of storage and well maintained by the crew.  Food was very good along with the staff.  Special recognition for Tania the cruise manager and Andrea in food service.   Both are outstanding.   We had limited use of the top deck due to closures for low bridges.   When used, the chairs were not very comfortable.   Noticed other cruise lines have more amenities on their top deck.    

As far as the excursions, too many locations require bus time.   The selling point of river cruise is more time at sights but an hour or more on a bus is not acceptable.   Normandy was very well done and the highlight of the trip but probably would not have done this cruise to see it knowing over 5 hours on the bus.   Versailles was the biggest disappointment with no time for gardens and no ticket provided for the one hour free time.   Given the cost of the cruise that seemed petty.  We were in port two hours before the bus departed.   Have an earlier departure with lunch on our own to allow time for gardens and palace.   Potentially two bus time options similar to Honfleur for those that want less time at Versailles.   Rouen was a nice town and how any port should be - no bus needed to visit the sights (or less than half hour) and there all day.  The tour guide for Normandy and Honfleur (Camille) was outstanding.   La Roche Guyon from an historical perspective probably makes sense to be included but overall not something I would tell people to visit on their own.   Two stops in one day is a bit much.   The latin quarter tour in Paris was very good.  We enjoyed using the bikes in the afternoon to bike the Seine.   Crew provided them in a timely basis.   The only optional excursion we took was the St Wandrille Abbey and felt it was not worth the visit or cost.

Overall, my impression is Uniworld does their best to provide a cruise that hits top sights outside of Paris (Giverny, Versailles and Normandy) but a Uniworld river cruise may not be the best way to see them given the limitations of how far the ship can travel on the Seine and nearest port to sight.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Eugene Micco, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Rhine, Moselle & Belgium Grand Discovery (BSL-BRU) 25, tourdirector=21641-51806
Service Comment:
During our cruise, we had the pleasure of being served by a number of outstanding crew members who made a lasting impression. Our butler Alex was excellent, as were Christen, Alexsadria, Dada at the front desk, Ivana, and Jay—who is clearly a consummate trainer. We also appreciated the service from Martin, Violeta, and Micro at the bar, Levente, Shira (our tour guide), Remus in both the bar and restaurant, Gabriel (our waiter), and Nedko, who guided us through our wine selections. Martin, Maja, and Wikoria—the twins who greeted us with a smile each day—were a particular highlight. I'm sure I may have missed others, as the entire staff was consistently friendly, professional, and attentive.

That said, we were disappointed with the physical ship itself. It did not meet the expectations we’ve come to associate with Uniworld—particularly the lack of additional seating areas and the absence of the signature rear bar. As such, we do not plan to cruise again on this or other “Crystal” vessels.

We also noted some inconsistencies in the dining experience. Breakfast service was occasionally slow, and eggs and toast were often undercooked. Lunchtime offerings could use improvement, especially as some meats served were quite tough. However, dinners were consistently very good and much appreciated.

Unfortunately, our disembarkation experience was poor. Our bus ride to the Brussels airport was a logistical failure. Shortly after leaving the ship, it became clear we were headed in the wrong direction. While we initially assumed the driver was taking an alternate route to the highway, we quickly realized he was unfamiliar with the area. He repeatedly attempted to access a road that was closed to buses and trucks on weekends. After going in circles, we ultimately had to call the cruise director and arrange for taxis to get us to the airport. This last-minute scramble was avoidable and stressful—and it cost us 40 euros out-of-pocket for the cab ride. I trust that Uniworld will reimburse this amount.

Despite the shortcomings, the staff made the cruise memorable, and we are grateful for their efforts.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Michael Nolta, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50079
Service Comment: Please see prior comments about restaurant server Andrea and cruise director Tanya - outstanding!  The rest of the crew was mostly very good.  Fitness instructor Goran was a little rough around the edges, however - a little too physical and directive during yoga class.
Product Comment:
Restaurant server Andrea was outstanding - she really made my wife's birthday special!! - someone who was born to be in hospitality!!  Cruise director Tanya was also born to be in hospitality!  Tour guide Camille (Honfleur and Normandy) was great.  Tour guide Olesia (Versailles and Latin Quarter/Paris) was very good.  So was the food and cabin, and most of the staff.  The free bikes were appreciated in Paris.
Suggestions:
1) Uniworld needs to be more transparent with the details of the cruise prior to booking.  For example, we should have known that Versailles was the palace or the gardens, but not both.
2) Speaking of Versailles, suggest starting an hour or two earlier and including both the palace and gardens - leaving people on their own for lunch (similar to Normandy) - instead of starting the tour after an early lunch on the ship.  Realize Versailles is after a long day in Normandy, but perhaps one of the buses can bring people back earlier who don't want to do both (similar to Honfleur).  As it is now, Tanya should have handed out tickets to the gardens to anyone who wanted them so we didn't have to wait in the queue and purchase our own.

Uniworld claims all gratuities are included. That is false. I had to pay the local driver and local guide EVERY day. On other cruises we had a guide from the ship which I enjoyed more because we got to know them better. 

The shrimp was horrendous!  It was very mushy and couldn’t be consumed. This happened twice. I order the seafood risotto and the surf and turf and both entrees had mushy shrimp. I expected more from Uniworld which I thought was an upscale line. 

The cocktails were included which was a bonus, but my daily tour tipping was irritating.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Shirley Laudari, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (MXP-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20142-51590
Description: Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto
Product Comment:
Overall experience: Unsatisfactory

Locally hosted pre-cruise excursions:
•Upon arrival in Milan on 5/30 the Lake Como excursion was cancelled.  During the welcome and briefing by the local hosts it was not announced that it had been cancelled; I only knew after I asked about the excursion.  I was only told that it was “cancelled” with no other explanation…..options were not provided on whether a tour could be booked through another vendor nor was there a replacement excursion 
•The lunch & wine pairings at the Serego Alighieri wine estate was informative and excellent. 

Cruise/ship experience: 
•Front desk: Julian, Marco, Eve (sp?) were very helpful and personable.
•Cruise Director, Ricardo always seemed annoyed or exasperated whenever I asked a question or if the answer to a question was already provided in materials that were given.  Rather than showing patience his response was “it’s in the documents that I already gave to you”.
•Cabin housekeeping was inconsistent; the toilet wasn’t always cleaned. An extra toilet roll wasn’t always provided. Also, the bathroom always had a faint “sewage” smell.  We would leave the b/r door open when we left the room and kept it ajar when in the room.
•Rialto restaurant: Very cramped due, in part, to the large dining chairs. Also felt very rushed by the waitstaff at the end of the dining timeframe. Would have also preferred a standard start time for breakfast. Overall the meals and selections offered were very good. 
Cruise excursions: 
•The Doge’s palace and after hours visit to St Mark’s Basilica on 6/2 and Torcello island on 6/4 both guided by Susan Steer was outstanding; Susan is an outstanding guide; while being informative she was also personable with a great sense of humor as she provided us with the history & stories of the places we visited.
•The Bragozzo & CaZen estate on 6/5 was very disappointing. First, the boat didn’t look as sleek as it appeared in the video during the presentation but looked very worn. The estate visit started off being informative but then went “down-hill” from there…as we were being escorted from the church where the briefing and history of the estate was presented, the estate guide, tour guide and wine maker were talking and laughing among themselves in italian however, she didn’t turn off her mic. After the short walk and we arrived at the estate, the guide then provided history on the cobble stones in front and then walked us into the estate but with no introduction or instructions. Tables were set up along with appetizers buffet style and another table with the wines. When I asked if we were supposed to just help ourselves she said yes so I asked her if she was going to inform everyone else, she said yes, but didn’t. At the table where the winemaker stood along with the wine, no information was given about the wine but only poured a tasting either red or white.  I asked the winemaker if he was going to provide an overview of the wines to the group, he said yes but didn’t. 

Also, the estate matriarch was wheeled in front of the doorway to the room that we were in and it appeared that they were going to introduce her but they didn’t.  It seemed very awkward to me that when she was wheeled in front of the doorway, her caregiver just stood there with her and the estate guide didn’t acknowledge nor introduce her. Later, a few of us thanked the matriarch for allowing us into her home.

Post cruise excursions
•The excursions in Athens were great and the local host, Marilena was excellent. 

In conclusion, since our experience with UniWorld was unsatisfactory compared to our experience with other cruise lines, we do not plan on using UniWorld again.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback which has been passed on for review. We are happy to hear that you enjoyed the Athens extension but are sorry to hear other aspects of your itinerary could have been improved. If you follow-up with us further regarding your experience, please feel free to contact us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=James Champion, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Client Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (MXP-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20142-51597
Description: Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto
Service Comment: Staff very professional and accommodating. Attentive to our various needs. I give them extra credit due to limited space throughout the ship. Cruise manager very involved on and off the ship.  We were a mix of US, British, Australian and South African and the staff handled that well. Having the Panini Bar is a very nice touch. There were only 98 of us and I wonder how staff adapts to full load of 130.
Product Comment: Uniworld for the most part understands their audience. I would say that more than half of us were seniors in the sense of limited physical abilities. So we are not up to prolonged walks/hikes. Guides onboard and onshore were excellent. Tipping not clear enough. Food and service excellent. Head chef participated in serving which was a nice touch. Getting on and off the ship was smooth for excursions as well as embarkation/disembarkation. Most entertainment was good especially opera and disco nights. Cabins too small. Need at least another 50 square feet minimum for two people . Think Seabourn and Silversea and adapt. Introducing the entire staff at the end was nice. Excursions for the most part were well chosen and thought out. The age range was 40s to 80s and they did a good job with that. For many of us constant go go is not desirable on vacation and they seem to get that. It was hot and high tourist season and I get that. Humid as well.

- Make the last day feel as special as the first day.  The vibe we get on the last day is more like "let's get things cleaned-up, wrapped-up, and be ready and anticipating the next group."

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jennifer Riemer, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50522
Product Comment:
We love Uniworld. Venice and the Jewels of Veneto was our fifth cruise with them and we plan to do more. The staff was as always fantastic. For the Venice lagoon cruise I recommend a stateroom on the even-numbered side; when docked in Venice (4 nights) the odd-numbered side faces a cement dock and chain link fence whereas the other side views the lagoon. The disadvantage of the even side is that it does get hot in the warmer months so recommend keeping the blinds closed during the day when out and about. 

They talk about having a pizza oven/café on the top deck but it might as well not exist. We were looking forward to it but apparently it is only open after 10pm; with the average age of the passengers I suspect no one was even awake to ask for any.

The great: the view from the sun deck is fantastic. It was so relaxing to sit up top with a peach bellini or cappuccino and just take in the view of the sun shining off the water, the water taxis and other boats going by, hearing the church bells ringing. 

After-hours tours of Doge’s Palace and St Mark’s Basilica were amazing! It was quiet and we got to see so much and take great photos without being crowded. Getting there by water shuttle instead of bus was the icing on the cake. 

The chef and waitstaff are very attentive. My husband has significant weird food allergies and they were very careful to make sure he wasn’t offered anything that would be a problem, and even custom made things for him such as a soup without potato base (yes, he’s allergic to potatoes). 

In short, we absolutely love Uniworld and highly recommend them. Hoping to do 2 cruises next year.

Service Comment:
With Roberto managing the ship you can tell the staff like him and they gave you the same service that he would and in each department.  

From Ana, to Josa, to Isabella, everyone made you feel so welcome.  My husband was particularly impressed with he told him one time in the bar he preferred his tanqueray and tonic in a tall glass and they always had that waiting for him.
Product Comment:
Our third experience with you in the world was just as great as the first one. Getting on board and seeing Roberto was very exciting. He was on another one of our cruises and just makes the whole cruise incredible. 

A room was very nice and had a extremely lot of good storage. The bathroom and shower were good size as well. Afraid I did not get our housekeeper's name but it was a young man who did a very good job and was very accommodating if I had to run back to my room and he happened to be there.. 


We enjoyed the food tremendously. Especially being celiac I was taking such great care of. The restaurant manager would come out every meal and say" I got you",  this is what we gave for you. And it was many many choices.  Ana, would watch each table server to make sure they new my dietary needs.  Hosa was so genuine and always so friendly and who knew was such a good singer. Isabella took such care of us. She is absolutely delightful....

Your evening entertainment was very enjoyable well planned. Hats off to the entertainment director. 

Love that the ship had a pool.  And a wonderful service for drinks and cocktails and even just putting out lemonade and water was really a nice touch. 

The Bar in the lounge was just top notch. Service was always incredible.

We found our captain very friendly. I wanted to take a picture of him navigating the ship and he gave me a wave to come on in that was just a really big treat.  

We are planning for our fourth in a world Cruise and looking very much forward to it.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Edward Fatzer III, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50079

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Kay Eccleston, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Bon Voyage, tour=Ultimate France (BOD-ARL) 25, tourdirector=20173-50349
Service Comment: The service was excellent, from the Laundry to the dining room.  Everyone was most helpful.  The front desk on all 3 boats were most helpful as were all 3 butlers, cleaning staff, and laundry.  The waiters were very professional and helpful at meals.  All were well groomed and informative about any dish that you asked about.  The 3 tour directors were also most helpful and enjoyable, Laura, Emmanuelle, and Sabrina.  They did a great job “herding cats” as old people often get confused or don’t know where they are going.  We enjoyed the Progressive Dinner on the BV, seeing below decks and how the crew operated.  The laundry must be the hardest job on the boat but our laundry came back beautiful every time.  We travel light so had to do laundry about 3 times each cruise.  Having free laundry is a really extra perk for passengers and hope the accountants don’t take that away.  I used the massage people on each boat and they were all excellent.  Listened to my needs and gave a really good massage.  An enjoyable perk to have spa available and ok with having to pay for massage, as that is true on all cruises we have been on, Oceania and Ponant.  All in all, absolutely no complaints about the service.  It was excellent on all 3 boats.
Product Comment:
We were on 3 different boats.  I received only one feedback email which I thought was just for the Catherine.  Your review system is confusing.  So I did not review the Bon Voyage or the Joie de Vivre.
On the Bon Voyage, the cooking class was a lot of fun and we really enjoyed it.  The chef gave us a website with the recipes but it is impossible to get into so we have not been able to access them.   The cooking class on the Catherine, not so much, in fact, not at all.  The chef would not share recipes and all we did was chop vegetables and the food was not that good, especially for the price.  Also, on the BV, the excursions were really good, the staff, excellent.

Then on to the JDV:  that was the least enjoyable cruise.  Everything got messed up because of Bastille Day, which happens every year at the same day so why was it such a surprise!  Martin and his wife, Stephanie, were the guides and we avoided them after one experience but they were with us for 3 days.  They stood around a lot and talked about things that were not of interest.  Martin, especially, would get off topic frequently and go down rabbit holes.  We were given 15 minutes to go take pictures outside of Norte Dame.  It was a 7 minute walk there.  This was inexcusable.  There was only an 8 minute wait to get in but of course we had not time since Martin had us standing for a very long time just watching the flyover, which was very good.  We had been on this cruise in 2017 and went to Giverny early in the morning and there were no crowds and it was lovely.  This time it was after 1:00 and hot and crowded.  That excursion should absolutely be done early!  The wait to get into Monet’s house was 45 minutes so we skipped it, having done it before.  This was not a pleasant experience.  
On to the Catherine.  This boat was lovely and Ivelin our butler, was a joy.  We really liked the little table and 2 chairs that were in the suite, and we only had dock views one day so the room was very enjoyable.  The cooking class and the kayak tour overlapped so you could not do both.
Cooking class was a bust as described above.
One reason we have gone on only Uniworld river cruses is because, in the past, everything was included.  We were willing to pay more for the cruise so we didn’t have to be nickeled and dimed during the cruise.  Now that Uniworld has been sold to a corporation it seems the accountants have taken over, as often happens.  Where did those Masterpiece excursions come from??  They used to be included.  I know, because we have done this exact cruise in 2017.  It was wonderful and this one was totally different and not so good.  People, don’t let the accountants ruin Uniworld!  Bea would be very unhappy if she knew this was happening.  I really doubt we will cruise with Uniworld again.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Nancy Raddatz, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Bon Voyage, tour=Ultimate France (BOD-ARL) 25, tourdirector=20173-50345
Product Comment: We’ve been on three Uniworld cruises previously. I would say the French cruises were not as satisfying as the Danube, Rhine, or Mekong cruises. The crew on the Bordeaux cruise was amazing and totally lived up to our expectations, however the crew on the Paris cruise as a whole felt less than expected. We were told by the Bordeaux crew how great the Paris crew would be but were less than impressed, especially after experiencing the Bordeaux crew. Honestly we thought they were detached at best. The Provence crew was pleasant but we felt like the cruise director continually deceived us with her “estimates” of the travel times to each of the planned stops because we lost an anchor and were stranded in the middle of nowhere. If she had been honest with the travel times we would have dealt with the issues. But the continual fluffing up the real story was infuriating. As now 6 time Uniworld cruisers we are very disappointed.  We have encouraged friends to join us on these trips and may not do so again.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Claudette Saba, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Uniworld Owned, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50518
Product Comment: Our family, with two teenage boys, loved this cruise. It was a nice way to experience Venice and many of its districts, and some of the surrounding islands. The staff aboard the S.S. La Venezia was warm and friendly. Our glasses were always full. And the cuisine was first-class and varied. The tours were great, and we made so many friends. The early access to Doge's Palace before the crowds, and the private visit after hours visit to St. Mark's Basilica was everything. I could see why people love doing this trip again and again.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Kevin Kittinger, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51890
Service Comment: The crew went above the expected in providing a remember river cruise.
Product Comment:
We had a wonderful Rhine River Cruise.  The ship was in excellent condition, the crew provided outstanding service, food and drinks were great and plentiful.  We enjoyed the shore excursions and found the guides knowledgeable and provided interesting content.  

An example of the crew providing above the expected service, was when one of your engineers, Lucian, repaired my shoe during the cruise.  My hiking shoe's sole began separating from the rest of the shoe a few days into the cruise.  With a few upcoming challenging hikes planed, I was unable to buy new shoes with the limited time in the towns we were visiting.  Your team on the boat assured me that their engineer would be able to repair/glue the shoe back together.  Upon receiving the repaired shoe, I found your engineer did and excellent job and the shoe held together for the remainder to of the cruise. This added level of service really speaks volumes about the crews effort to ensure everyone is able to fully enjoy the crew - Thank You Lucian!

We had two disappointments with the cruise that I feel the cruise could improve upon.  1) It would be nice if the cruise would provide an anticipated schedule (including duration of docking) before the start of the trip.  This would provide us help with planning/arranging our shore time in advance of the cruise. While your excursion planners do an excellent job arranging tours that fill our time, there are times when we would liked to have spent more time focused on a private outing. 2) Shore excursions descriptions should note if it will not be available on certain dates should the boat dock on those days.  Specifically I got my hopes up on the vinegar tasting tour but learned on the cruise that it wasn't offered on Sunday (date of our docking in that town).  I chose to not sign up for the Romantic Heidelberg tour before the start of the cruise because I wanted to attend the vinegar tastings.  As a result I was disappointed and had to pay a higher fee for the Romantic Heidelberg tour which I would have booked before the start of the trip had I known that some excursions were not available on Sundays.  

Overall I was pleased with the trip and plan to take other trips on Uniworld.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Terence Hattam, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=River Duchess, tour=Ultimate European Journey (AMS-BUH) 25, tourdirector=21057-50480

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Arlene Yargeau, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50517
Service Comment: As mentioned in previous comment, simply outstanding service!
Product Comment: The staff on this cruise were all wonderful; friendly and went out of their way to take care of all our needs. Maria made the best drinks! She was able to create and taylor all our beverages to exactly what we asked for. All the servers were wonderful, but Ion went above and beyond to make sure we were well looked after. Loved his sense of humor and appreciated his excellent work ethic and customer attention. Louisa was a fabulous cruise director. Her daily talks were very well done and informative. She always steered us in the right direction. Overall, we loved the ship decor and our room was always clean and well attended to. The bed was comfortable and our room was elegantly decorated in Venetian style which I loved. We did not hear “our neighbours” at all. The fact that we could do our own laundry on the ship was appreciated as we only travel with carry-on luggage. Also loved the fact that drinks, excursions and gratuities were all included. We enjoyed all our ports of call and being able to visit The Doge Palace and Basilica after hours was incredible. Enjoyed the other excursions as well, but was disappointed by how expensive some of the other add on activities were (For example the progressive dinner, cooking lesson, visit to Jewish ghetto). Would have been nice to be able to sit by a pool on deck to cool off. Even a shallow tanning pool would have enhanced our experience sitting on the top deck. The meals on the ship were excellent. The breakfast and lunch buffets offered a variety of options. We never went hungry. We look forward to going back on an Uniworld cruise!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Martin Mayerson, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50156
Service Comment: Service was superior….very attentive! This was true in the suite, restaurant and spa.
Product Comment: Hi, We had quite a delightful trip….excellent service and food, good excursions and guides. The beginning of the trip was unpleasant as we couldn’t embark as planned. We had a 4 hour drive in an uncomfortable vs. with a non English speaking driver , no water or bathroom stop, and no rep from Uniworld. Then the next day we had to drive 2.5/3 hours back to see Budapest. The low level of the Danube obviously is not your fault. The handling of the matter, one we heard happens often, was not Uniworld level. We should have been put up in a hotel for one night in Budapest instead of after 9 hours of air travel we have a 4 hour ride, in uncomfortable conditions when we are exhausted. Then a long ride back and forth the next day. Also a tour in Budapest had to be cancelled. It was a charged for tour which they put through immediately upon booking. It’s 5 weeks later and still no refund…unconscionable. So much of it was lovely, but the initial treatment awful and the cancelled trip booking refund should not be taking this long. Btw, had they put us up in a hotel that night, we would not have had to cancel a tour which was important to us. We had a suite ad we were with a grandchild. This was very expensive. The treatment upon landing in Budapest was very, very unsatisfactory…we also had way too long to carry our luggage. Trips of this caliber should have better amenities in regard to older prople and bag carrying. These would be simple things to correct. For a luxury tour, highly recommended by our travel agent, above Viking, we were rather disappointed.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are very sorry that your cruise experience was impacted by the water levels. The refund for your cancelled excursion has now been processed back to your card on file. We apologize for any delay but are happy to hear that you enjoyed the service, food and excursions on your trip. If you would like to provide us with any additional feedback, please reach out to us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Kenneth Segars, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Sao Gabriel, tour=Douro River Valley (OPO-OPO) 25, tourdirector=20113-50452
Service Comment: Great service by staff!
Product Comment: The cruise was beautiful but basically down and back. For people who want to chill and less interested in inserting themselves into the culture it’s great. The wine tours were ALL Port wine . Most people left their tastings in their glasses. With all the great wines in Portugal, major disappointment. The wines on the boat were good. The food on the boat was repetitive and uninspired. Our previous cruise had phenomenal food. Not being able to insert into the culture was frustrating.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are very sorry that you would have enjoyed less port tastings and that the food could have been better. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the service, and the wines offered onboard. If you would like to provide us with any additional feedback, please reach out to us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jeffrey Eastman, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube (NUE-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20112-50272
Service Comment: Already mentioned this in other section.
Product Comment:
1. The ship and crew were all wonderful, especially Tomas the cruise director.  He was always available to answer questions or provide information.  
2. Was disappointed we did not transit the canal from Nürnberg, but it was nice of the captain to explain the reason why.  However, the bus ride from Nürnberg to where we met the boat was through some very beautiful countryside.
3. The extra Salzburg excursion I paid for was excellent due in large part to the singing talents of our guide, a former opera singer.  Every "Sound of Music" film location was accompanied by her rendition of the song that was sung at that spot.  Had time to visit the castle in my free time.
4. Included excursions in Vienna and Budapest were far too short and very superficial.  Would have been nice if more time and detail had been provided as well as a map of the cities.  Was glad I had been in both places on my own previously.
5. The use of Uniworld-provided metal water bottles in lieu of disposable plastic bottles was a nice ecological feature.  However, every time I turned around it seemed my towels were being exchanged for new ones - not ecologically sound.  We can use the same towel for two days or perhaps even three.  Was nice they asked if we wanted our sheets changed.
6. This was my first cruise on a river or on the ocean.  Personally, I prefer to explore more on my own and book local tours in new locations.  Also, several bus tours I have been on gave me time in the morning and evening to do more exploring in lieu of just spending more time on a ship.
7.  My mother loved cruises since they were a real vacation for her.  From this, my first cruise, I can see why she loved them so much.

Perhaps less time in Germany, which was also rich in culture, could have been shortened to give us more time on the end to stay in Budapest one extra day, leaving our time away the same.  Thank you for all you have done, UniWorld.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Cecilia Hendry, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51890
Service Comment:
Overall the standard of service was very high. The staff take a pride in doing their job well and always with a smile !
We were impressed by the number of staff who have had a long career with Uniworld.
Product Comment:
This was our 5th cruise with Uniworld and we intend to do several more. The standard of service by everyone is exceptional and it is difficult to single out individual staff but Silvia and Lena in the bar were terrific, thank you Lena for the number of times you went up and down the stairs bringing our drinks to the sun deck! All the staff in the restaurant were very attentive. Standard of cleanliness can’t be faulted, well done housekeeping! 
We were very touched to receive flowers, champagne and a card congratulating us on our 56th wedding anniversary which we were due to celebrate a few days after the end of the cruise. A thoughtful gesture we will always remember.
One negative point, on arrival at Basel airport, despite our flight being the last one that the rep was meeting we had to wait for almost an hour before the coach arrived to take us to the ship. I appreciate that there can be in avoidable traffic delays, we were given no reason for the delay.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Nolene Harris, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50075
Service Comment:
Excellent from all staff 
very kind, helpful, friendly & polite 
obviously all very well trained
Product Comment:
Arranged through ROL which I feel issues were as follows: 
Bad timing for flights - why book us on 5.30pm flight when we have to be out the room 8am. Other flights available 12 noon and 2pm. Just sitting around waiting all day.
Taxi Driver arranged by the ship dropped us at the WRONG terminal. NOT a pleasant experience with all our luggage (and husband nearly 83 years old!)
Flight going out to Paris was delayed 3.5 hours. Upon arrival at Paris CDG no one to meet us (surely they check time of arrival??). Big arguments! Not pleasant. Very complicated for taxi to find the correct ship and obvious difficulty with lack of language knowledge (french V english).
We filled out forms on EasyJet to apply for compensation for 3.5 hour delay but required "booking reference" which was not available (all references we had were not acceptable - possibly due to ROL booking flights).

Cruise was excellent, well planned, excellent service.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Robyn Duthie, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (NZ), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (PRG-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20110-50246
Service Comment: All the crew were excellent. Tomas worked very hard as they all did. The lady on the front desk (Nicolina?) went out of her way to organise a group transfer to the airport at the end of the cruise because I had missed the cut off date to book one. I really appreciated her help.
Product Comment: We thoroughly enjoyed every aspect of our trip. The staff were excellent, plenty of food and drinks. It was nice that it was a good mix of nationalities of both crew and passengers. I also liked that there was no blaring music in public areas, just very quiet classical if anything. The entertainment was also great, a good variety. The only small grizzle was the tiny shower, everyone found the showers too small but that is something they can't change now I guess.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Katarina Keller, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (NZ), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Rhine, Moselle & Belgium Grand Discovery (BSL-BRU) 25, tourdirector=21641-51806
Service Comment: The service was excellent because everyone aboard the ship was keen to make us feel appreciated and valued. The whole crew was friendly, helpful and cheerful. They even remembered everyone's name. The whole experience aboard the ship was very pleasant.
Product Comment: The 14 day itinerary was very good and that was a major reason for us to select this cruise. The longer cruises are more attractive, because 7 days seems rather short. We also liked the warm welcome and overall friendliness of the whole Uniworld crew towards all the guests. The  food was excellent and the excursions were interesting. The option of visiting Luxembourg or Trier was  not so good, though, because we would  have liked to visit both destinations. Instead, we were able to select just one, which meant that we missed out on the other. It would be better to offer both and not only one or the other. The Uniworld should really consider that when there are 2 equally attractive destinations,  then they should be both available. This particular offer of either Trier or Luxembourg was a big disappointment. Uniworld should find a way to give us both excursions since most people would really like to visit both trier and Luxembourg.

Service Comment: Probably overall best of 3 cruises w UniWorld
Product Comment:
Third UniWorld cruise.  First (Rhône) was overall favorite, this second (influence of boat design, itinerary).  Good service.  One soft day on the Rhein, need to move ship and less interesting cities around.  Not enough time in Koln.
Second sail (Danube) we hit flooding and river shut down; that may have been best if we could have done itinerary.
We are fans of the ornate ships rather than modern designs (which also have smaller French Balcony rooms).

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Carol Paulsen, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Duchess, tour=Highlights of Eastern Europe (BUH-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20133-50381
Service Comment: Our Butler, Uros, and the house keeping staff were excellent.
Product Comment: We enjoyed our suite.  Our butler, Uros, was a highlight of the trip.  He was super helpful.  We could have used some money exchange on-board so we could tip the tour guides and drivers. The brewery in Bulgaria was fun. We wanted more time in Budapest which was the last leg of our trip.  The tour guides and entertainment coordinator were great.  They were very informative.  Loved the local dancers and music. The trip from Bucharest to Budapest didn't feature regional food other than some fish.  Our comparison is the food served between Vienna and Cologne where regional food was highlighted, but that did go through Bavaria.  Overall, our trip was very interesting and Uniworld did a great job with details, again/still.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Paul Zupon, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Burgundy & Provence (ARL-LYS) 25, tourdirector=20100-50124

Dining with Ivalyo was attentive and so friendly.  Oh course we can't forget the amazing Sabrina:  Always there and so helpful and pleasant.  Great care from the cabin crew.  Room always clean and refreshed.  Many thanks for a great week on the Rhone.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=John Harris, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Duchess, tour=Highlights of Eastern Europe (BUH-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20133-50383
Product Comment: we were very pleasantly surprised by how well the trip was managed, how lovely the crew was, and the quality of the lectures and excursions.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jojilyn Siegel, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51872
Product Comment: This was our first river cruise with any cruise line. We booked with Uniworld through Costco Travel. Everything about our cruise experience was well above our expectations. The crew and staff were all friendly, accommodating, attentive and helpful. The ship was very nice and our stateroom was comfortable with an amazing wall-sized, drop-down window (French balcony suite). The food was delicious and plentiful. The variety of included (no extra cost) excursions were wonderful with knowledgeable, English-speaking guides. We were very happy that this all-inclusive cruise meant our tips/gratuities were included. We would definitely book with Uniworld again. Actually, it may be the only river cruise line we ever book with in the future.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Catherine Lockley, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=River Princess, tour=Highlights of Eastern Europe (BUD-BUH) 25, tourdirector=20132-50389
Service Comment: Wonderful service.  Everybody was very helpful and polite.
Product Comment:
Our cruise director was a contract employee that was only there for the week, someone else came and replaced her the following week.  The itineraries were regularly incorrect and chaotic.  The cruise director reminded us several times that someone else had planned it and took no responsibility. 

Nobody appeared to be ensuring that excursions were suitable for guests abilities.  Several guests who had very obvious mobility issues were allowed to participate in excursions which required physical fitness.  It wasn't fair to anyone on the excursions, uniworld didn't send anyone to help the less mobile people, leaving it to negatively impact both them and the others on the trip.

There was no entertainment.  Except for one evening when a band came aboard.  One night a man came with a laptop and played music.  Another night the cruise director played music from her laptop.  One would expect that if there is a piano bar that someone would play the piano from time to time.  The only person who played the piano was another guest.

There was a wonderful steak for the final dinner, it would have been nice to have been given the same choice throughout the week. 

The room was always neat and tidy.  I was happy with the cleanliness and the friendliness of the crew.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. For guests with mobility concerns, we ask that they speak with the Cruise Manager prior to excursions to determine which options are best suited for them. We are sorry to hear that the entertainment could have been better. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the service of our housekeeping staff and the steak which is offered as a daily option for dinner. If you would like to provide us with any additional feedback, please reach out to us at customerservice@uniworld.com.
