Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=James Syverud, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51838
Product Comment: The boat, crew, meals, drinks, our suite, service, and amenities in general were excellent.  However, the shore excursions were uneven and some of the guides were disappointing and / or difficult to understand.  This was true in particular for Cologne.  Our guide provided very little history, the cathedral was extremely grimy, and the city was very short on both beauty and charm.  But from that point on, the cruise became progressively more interesting, beautiful, and charming. We made some good “cruise friends” — always adding to our enjoyment — and would definitely recommend UniWorld.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Nancy Patterson, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50156
Service Comment: Everyone was so great-professional and REALLY cared about the experience they were providing. Engaging and enthusiastic crew who CARED about the job they were doing,
Product Comment:
We thought the boat was really nice and our cabin was SO comfortable, We both slept so well! The housekeeping staff that took care of us was amazing and everything was SO clean on the entire boat. Very nice! The air conditioning in our room was incredibly effective and our weather was SO hot so it really helped with comfort level.  The crew in general was just AMAZING. Our waiter each night at dinner was terrific. He was personable and so helpful every meal. Overall,  we enjoyed the meals at night very much,. We also enjoyed the breakfast buffet in the morning,. Other amenities we appreciated were the laundry service and cool towels when we came back on the boat from excursions! The excursions were so interesting and we enjoyed the variation of choices. Our guides in Vienna at Schonbrunn and walking guide in Bratislava were especially passionate and outstanding,. 
Only two suggestions. One is to include more activities/food/drink on the top deck of the ship. It was so much fun to be able to sit up there and we took our lunch up a few times. How about  a signature cocktail served up there or maybe a nightcap when you are sailing. Also the last night meal was kind of a huge disappointment, We were moving ports in Passau. Understand this is just a have to as far as convenience (and the disembarkation process the next day was incredibly organized) However, we sailed to a concrete dock and blocked our dinner view. Then another ship pulled up beside us,. It was the last night on the ship and we had no view and it as dark and depressing in there. Why not do a little dinner cruise down the river a bit and then come back the other direction and back into port? Would've really made the last night a lot more special. That is a very small suggestion but it was kind of a downer way to go out. 
Overall a fantastic trip. We are only 57 but heard this river cruise was great. It was fine but we will not take another cruise for about 15 years because we are just not old enough yet but when we do we will come back to Uniworld and suggest for other older people. thanks

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Julie Regan, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=River Duchess, tour=European Jewels (BUD-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21049-50371
Service Comment: The service we recieved was second to none all the staff on the ship were very friendly and very attentive from the housekeeper to the waiting staff to the chefs and of course the staff that ran the ship it’s self. We went through 55 locks all up and we watched as we sailed in and out of these locks with such precision thanks to the captain and his staff, they take their job very seriously it was amazing to watch them. Ewa had a bright smile to greet us in the morning and when we came back after our days of exploring what ever town we were in. The staff on the reception desk were always happy to help in anyway.
Product Comment: I absolutely loved this cruise, because of the low water in the river our cruise actually started on a bus trip from Budapest to Vienna which was ok just a little disappointing. Once we were on the ship we were looked after by the staff so well. The housekeeper we had was Yanna she could not do enough for us, the room was so beautifully clean everyday and nothing was too much trouble. The food was absolutely amazing everyday something new every meal the kitchen staff were so good and the waiting staff were very attentive and very friendly, Fernando, Nikolai, Desislav, and Toby were stand outs. also Lucen overseeing everything was amazing. The fact that there was a laundry that passengers could use on the ship was a big plus’s. The other thing I did enjoy was the entertainment on the ship thanks to Ed. I would recommend Uniworld and this trip to anyone, in fact I am already planning my next river cruise.  I have met and made friends with so many of the other passengers and I’m sure we will stay in touch, this was also a highlight. You will definitely see me on another Uniworld ship in the future.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Carolyn Bayless, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Sao Gabriel, tour=Portugal, Spain & the Douro River Valley (OPO-LIS) 25, tourdirector=20153-50423
Description: Portugal, Spain & the Douro River Valley
Service Comment: The best, as noted. I would not think of traveling with any other company.
Product Comment: This was my 11th Uniworld trip and one of my favorites! Everything was perfect on the ship, starting with Tomas and Paula at the front desk. They were so friendly, quickly knew my name and room number, and were always helpful. The food was excellent, served by my favorite, Ana. She knew what we liked immediately and took the best care of us!! Sylvia was outstanding, keeping our glasses full of delicious wine. We went to one of her classes and will forever remember how she introduced us to Port Wine. The pastry chef, Lianna, made the best desserts we have ever had on a ship. We went to her food class and brought the recipe home to attempt ourselves. Mohammed kept our room organized and clean, even when we we a bit messy. Matt was an excellent cruise director, thoroughly explaining every excursion, and always available to answer even the most simple questions. Our bus driver, Victor, was amazing driving the narrow, winding roads, and I never was worried about our safety. We really enjoyed Ricardo, one of our daily guides. He was very knowledgeable about the history and details of the places we visited. As you can tell from my writing, we will fondly remember all our wonderful people and places for years to come!

Service Comment:
Exceptional.  All the staff were unfailingly polite, helpful and quickly learned, if not our names, then our preferences - be it for 4 cups of coffee with breakfast, to a particular way of serving G & T before supper!
Rob, our Cruise Manager, deserves a special mention for his unflappability even in the face of unexpected and radical last minute changes (e.g. finding cold water for a coachload of tired travellers when the ship had not turned up on time one day when the temperature was 39 degrees; transfer from ship to coach to get us to Vienna and Budapest due to lack of water in the river).
Product Comment:
Best holiday for many years!
It was so good to unpack once, and let the scenery come to us.  
The staff were outstanding - nothing seemed to be too much trouble for them.  They all went out of their way to ensure we had a marvellous time.  The food was exceptional: so much variety, so many healthy options and some wonderful seasonal and regional specialities.
The excursions and evening entertainments were all carefully thought out and designed to appeal to as wide a cross-section of passengers as possible.
And the icing on the cake was that, with the exception of a few euros to tip excursion hosts, there was nothing extra to pay - a particularly nice touch, we felt.

Product Comment:
Uniworld excels with service, food, and excursions. It’s the only cruise line we will use. 

But the ship, River Queen, was below par and showing its age - torn and stained curtains, creaky worn or broken furniture, chipped glassware, malfunctioning fitness equipment.
Also this was our third Uniworld cruise and we were disappointed with cost cutting that others may not have noticed, like not getting pre-trip luggage tags or not getting the packet of recipes on departure. Also hear that free transfers will not be offered next year unless purchasing their more expensive and less flexible flights. This is disappointing for a luxury cruise company.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Margaret Weaver, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Grande Italia (MXP-FCO) 25, tourdirector=21272-51533
Service Comment: Brilliant, every member of staff on board and on the tours made our experience special.
Product Comment:
This holiday was the best experience on every possible criteria. As a solo traveller, the fare was expensive but also great value for money when you consider what was included and the high quality throughout. I was looked after every step of the way, from the airport to the Milan stay, the ship SS Venezia, and onward to Rome, then home. The standard of hotels for the pre cruise and post cruise add-ons were world class. The Uniworld hosts were attentive and approachable. Nothing was too much trouble. I felt really valued. Venice and the itinerary was exceptional, it was on my bucket list and I am so pleased I chose Uniworld to show me the city, sights and beautiful countryside of Italia!
The ship was so comfortable and clean, I was very pleased with my cabin, and the small ship ambience was so relaxing. The included tours were great, all the key sites were covered, well planned, on time and no queuing so the stunning monuments could be fully enjoyed without getting too hot and exhausted, despite the high temperatures. Water taxis just appeared when needed! I particularly enjoyed the historical guide Susan who knew everything and could put it across in an entertaining and informative way. Early and late tours were arranged, hats off the the tour manager!
The food was six star, every meal was a culinary delight with paired wines and unlike some cruises, there was never a problem being served. The bar personnel knew by magic what you would need. All fresh and locally sourced.
The staff were amazing, going the extra mile; consistently, with ease, forethought and willingly. I was very impressed with the positive culture throughout the holiday.  The itinerary  for the whole holiday was spot on; it exceeded my expectations. Absolutely loved visiting  Vicenza and the Teatre Olimpica, the Last Supper in Milan and of course all of Venice!
Some of the additional tours that you could pay for as an extra however I thought were over priced, and with hindsight you could have booked these yourself for half the price. An example of this is the La Scala Theatre trip which cost £100 and the Colosseum trip which was £150. That said the Lake Como trip at £200 was worth every penny. A gondola ride would have been an nice inclusion (though I did this myself, with help from the cruise manager). 
There was plenty of time for shopping at Choggia market and at other stops. 
I made some fab new friends and the high standard of this holiday and the wonderful experience makes me want to always cruise with Uniworld!
Thank you for a superb holiday with wonderful memories made.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Kenneth Buck, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Elisabeth, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51839
Service Comment: Superb!
Product Comment: Uniworld did a fantastic job and the cruise and service was exceptional with one exception.  That being that they do not have an after hours customer service line.  We found out on Saturday afternoon that we did not have transport set up from the hotel in Lucerne to Zurich for our return flight early Tuesday morning because our travel agent had not provided the flight information to them.  We as well as our travel agent tried to reach them in order to provide the flight information and to make sure the transportation was arranged, only to find out that their offices would be closed until Monday at 9:00 AM pacific time (eight hours behind the time zone we were in).  At 9:00 PM Monday evening (when we were going to bed in order get up at 5:00 AM to make our flight) our travel agent asked us to set up transportation through the hotel to the airport, which we did.  This stress at the end of what was a fantastic experience could have been avoided.  We did make our flight and Uniworld did reimburse us for the cost of the transportation to the airport, but we found it incomprehensible that a company with clients in different time zones all over the world did not have an after hours customer service line, and that while booking the trip with our travel agent they did not request our return flight information.

Product Comment:

Every request or need was accommodated with graciousness and smiles! This was our third cruise with Uniworld. We have another one booked in March to Vietnam and Cambodia. Looking forward to it!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Mark Hunsel, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (BUD-PRG) 25, tourdirector=20109-50190
Service Comment: Most of the staff was excellent. There were a few that appeared disinterested in serving you and would look past you and keep walking, Instead of Stopping and checking to see if you needed anything.
Product Comment:
I expected more for the price. We went with Uniworld because we thought it would be a step up, but I’m not sure it was. It was very warm while we were there and the cabin was not all that cool. Maybe it was just the limitation of the air conditioner, but still it was very warm. Three days of our cruise had to be stopped because of the low water and I know that’s not the fault of uniworld but still, we were bussed to our Excursions and it seemed like we spent more time on the bus than we did at our excursion. In Prague we took the foodie tour and it truely was the worst tour I ever took. The guide was very monotone and she was not invested in the tour at all. When we went to the sandwich shop, it was so small that we had to separate. Some ate in the shop and some ate outside at a picnic table, which was very warm and separated our group. Then we Went to a wine tasting store and it was so small. We had to drink our wine out in the hallway. Our restaurant was supposed to have air conditioning, but it was so hot. Everyone on our tour was fanning themselves at the table and complaining. there was no way to enjoy our food because we were so warm. 
The best stop and excursions was in Budapest. The guide was excellent he was so knowledgeable and shared many personal insights. The Rock Hospital was a very good tour.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the Foodie Tour in Prague could have been better, but we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the Rock Hospital tour in Budapest. If you would like to provide us with any additional information about your experience, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Product Comment: I loved the ship! This was our fourth river cruise with 3 cruise lines, and this ship was outstanding in its decor. Food was comparable to others, which is to say very good, and the service was outstanding from all the staff. My only two complaints were that daily local guides (and sometimes drivers) tipping should be included. On the Danube we needed 3 currencies! And this was the only cash we needed, as everything else was either paid for in advance or bought by credit card. So it was extremely inconvenient to need small bills every single day. My second complaint was that the excursions were very ordinary and not in line with the luxurious ship experience. The groups were large and the offerings were things we could have arranged on our own. We would have enjoyed more unique or exclusive experiences on land.

Product Comment:
We enjoyed the cruise very much. The staff were top notch and the cuisine was very good (especially the soups).
Unfortunately we did not expect to be docked in Bordeaux 5 out of 7 nights. We expected to be docked in different locations - ie Sauterne area, Medoc area, Blaye but they ended up only being day excursions.
We were there during a heat wave and the ship's air conditioning was not up to the challenge in the common areas. The room was small but it had ample storage. Our cabin had a French balcony, however it faced the wharf masonry in Bordeaux most of the time. 
Most of the excursions were good but a few of the guides could have provided more information. 
We got a great deal on this trip and it was worth it but it would not have been at full price. The all inclusive aspect part of the cruise is great but the optional suppers and excursions are quite expensive.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Julie Bellitt, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (PRG-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20110-50245
Service Comment: Dining room service was great. Our room was keep very nice with clean, fresh towels. The staff at the front desk were so friendly and very nice about taking a photo of us each night as we headed to the dining room. We really appreciated that.
Product Comment: Our cruise on the Danube was wonderful! Staff on the SS Beatrice were excellent and very friendly and helpful! The dining experience was good, however, we had had better food and food presentation on a previous Uniworld Cruise 8 years ago, so we were a bit disappointed. If Uniworld is going to have a "dress code", (which I highly think they should !, people should dress up for cocktails and dinner) the "dress code" should be enforced. One night my husband and I were dressed very nice for the dining room and we happened to sit next to an older man traveling with his grandson. Both were wearing shorts and the grandson had on a graphic t-shirt. Hardly dining room attire! Overall we saw some poor dress in the dining room at dinner time. Please have "dress codes" and please enforce "dress codes"! The dining staff always looked nice and professional the guests should look nice as well. We feel going on a European cruise should be a fancy experience and folks should dress the part. The excursions were wonderful and we were keep very busy, almost too busy. It would have been nice to have a bit more cruising time on the River Boat relaxing. Just a bit. We felt somewhat hurried in the evenings. We did choose the excursions because we wanted to see as much as we could, but sometimes a bit more relaxation would have been nice. Things seemed a bit rushed. Cha Cha, our Cruise Director ( I think that is her title) was most helpful and just a wonderful person! We felt the music in the lounge at night was a bit too hip, maybe a bit more danceable would have been nice. The music seemed to be a bit geared towards a younger crowd when the customers were a bit older group. The River Boat was beautiful! The dining room was very nice and our room was very comfortable and was keep so tidy. That was treat for me, as a housewife, it was a little break for me. We look forward to our next cruise on Uniworld and hope that it will be soon!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Nancy Nowakowski, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Bon Voyage, tour=Ultimate France (BOD-ARL) 25, tourdirector=20173-50345
Service Comment:
The dining & lounge service was the best!
Our butler, Dino. On the Catherine was a dream come true.  I cannot imagine that he could have done a better job.  The butlers on th other two ships were good, but Dino was top notch.
Product Comment:
The food was very good…..the service in the dining room & lounge was excellent.
We had wait staff who remembered our names & remembered what we lied to drink or how we liked our coffee or tea.
On several occasions, when we had transfers to another ship, or at the airport, we had to drag luggage with us.  There was no one to help.  On one occasion, we had to sit on a bus for an hour to wait for additional at a hotel in Paris  A second transport would have been nice.
We were left off the list for the shore excursion to the Queens Hamlet.  The tour manager, Pierre, blamed it on the reception staff & initially said that he was sorry, but we could not go.  After some discussion, he agreed to get us there by taxi, & went with us ( a very expensive taxi ride paid for by Uniworld). On arrival we were to meet up with the Uniworld group, but we were not allowed to use our headsets because the venue employees would know that the group had exceeded the limit for group size.  This would endanger the license of the local guide.
A day later we received a bottle of sparkling wine along with a note of apology.  This was a bit redundant since, in a suite, we could have ordered this bottle of wine any day of the cruise.  Perhaps a more appropriate gesture would have been a credit for whatever Masterpiece excursions were available.
One of the high points of this cruise was our butler, Dino.  He was there whenever we needed anything, although, it was unusual that we needed, or wanted anything, because he anticipated our wants & needs before we did.  He always had a smile on his face, & really seemed to enjoy his work.  Uniworld is fortunate to have him as an employee!
A few days before our cruise ended, our ship lost its anchor.  This was an inconvenience because we had longer bus rides for the excursions, & to the airport.  We were never told why this anchor was lost………was it incompetence, poor maintenance, or just a freak accident.

This was our first Uniworld trip, & likely will be our last.  We just did not feel that the excursions & local guides were as good as they could have been.  There was not enough attention paid to detail on occasion.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear about the issue with your excursion choice which has been passed on for further review. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the service of our staff onboard, particularly your butler. If you would like to provide us with any additional information about your experience, please feel free to contact us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Product Comment:
Our Unicode Castles along the Rhineland was fantastic. The staff on the riverboat are all excellent and take care to learn your name, preferences, anticipating your needs and helpful and friendly. The staff find out it was our 20th anniversary and decorated our bed with hearts and chocolates our side table had a bottle of champagne and chocolate. Covered strawberries. Lovely.  

The sights on this cruise are breathtaking and excursions well organized. 

Besides the castles, one of my favourite sights was seeing storks in their nests high on top of buildings in a medieval village in the Alsace region of France. Brought back from near extinction because of conservation efforts to build platforms for the nests. 

My only mild criticism is the limited number of excursions available on this cruise versus Paris to Normandy trip we took last year. 2 per day to choose from vs 3-4 last years Uniglobe adventure.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Elaine Mair, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (BUD-PRG) 25, tourdirector=20109-50190
Service Comment: Everyone was very attentive, especially Istvan and his wife Reena, dining room servers, and Kalo, drinks’ waiter. Nothing was too much trouble for them. Luggage was efficiently handled, delivered speedily to our quarters, shuttles turned up on time, and dining room etc hours were closely followed.
Product Comment: Was very pleased with the cruise overall. Service was amazing, everyone was so attentive, food presentation was awesome though I did find the food rather bland. Only problem I had was when we had to remain docked in Passau because of the low level of the water. Not the company’s fault but our cruise director decided the alternative was a ten-hour day visit to Regensburg, from 8am to 6pm, culminating in a performance by a local boys’ choir. I thought this was rather extreme, given the average age of the passengers. Another woman, I think her name was Becka or Becky, was traveling with her 90-year old father and 88-year-old mother. She too thought it was too much. We approached Tomas to see if he would consider asking the passengers if there was any interest in a shuttle returning earlier. He refused to ask for a show of hands, instead deciding that anyone interested should go talk to him, but of course he didn’t mention that to the other passengers! On the bus trip to Regensburg, he announced there would be a shuttle returning at 3pm for anyone interested. 34 passengers chose to take the earlier shuttle! Had he announced that earlier, perhaps those who chose not to go, myself included, would have gone on the excursion! But, because of his stubbornness and inflexibility, we missed out on seeing Regensburg!

Product Comment:
Had another wonderful experience with Uniworld!  Almost the entire staff were very friendly, tour guides each day were fantastic, and the overall experience was well worth the money and effort.  We had a large, multi-generational group and lots of opinions every day on what to do.  

Although the tours were great overall, they were often quite far from the ship so required bus tours.  No one likes bus rides when we could be exploring and even less exciting than sitting in weekday traffic.

The food was always served with smiles and decent selection.  The options cater to the older crowd but our teen was happy to fillup on whatever was being placed before him.

If you're going as a couple, enjoy every moment and dont forget to sleep in once or twice.  If you're going with a group, you might want to look at the Rhine River trips.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=David Levine, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21658-51817
Service Comment: With the exception of the dining room which was understaffed, service was excellent
Product Comment:
First, our butlers Yuri & Renata were fantastic as was the bar service. 
Laundry service was first rate. 
We found UW's communication lacking - We should have been notified immediately once UW changed the start of the cruise from Basel to Vogelgrun.  
We should have been advised that the prepaid vinegar tasting (6/19/25) was cancelled.  Additionally, a similar culinary excursion should have been arranged in its place.   We are still awaiting a refund from UW.
The food was very bland, and the breakfasts were repetitive.   There were almost no dishes featuring local produce such as white asparagus and strawberries which were both in season.
The kitchen was clearly short-staffed.
The buses should have WIFI like premium ocean cruise companies.
Smoking should not be permitted anywhere on the ship, or the smoking areas should be enclosed.
We stayed in a suite that desperately needs updating - The electrical outlets could not "grip" the European plugs.  The USB outlets are out of date.  For some reason the electrical switches were toggles.
The position of the toilet in our suite was odd - way too close to the wall.
There is nowhere to store luggage - suitcases don't fit under the bed.
2 days in Nijmegen was too many.  Another day in Amsterdam would have been more rewarding.
There was a dining area on the top level that should have been used but wasn't.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Marie Giroux, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50515

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Juliana Sterenberg, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Uniworld Owned, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Bon Voyage, tour=Brilliant Bordeaux (BOD-BOD) 25, tourdirector=20099-50020
Service Comment: extremely friendly and high-quality on-board service
Product Comment:
Some journeys stay with you like a fine wine — rich, layered, unforgettable. My first river cruise through France aboard Uniworld’s S.S. Bon Voyage was exactly that.

Sailing from Bordeaux across the Garonne, Dordogne and Gironde rivers, I was immersed in elegance: all-inclusive service, gourmet dining with wines curated by sommelier José Luke, and impeccable hospitality (many crew members even speak Portuguese). The décor is refined, the atmosphere warm, and the views ever-changing — from my cozy cabin to the breezy top deck.

Daily excursions revealed fairytale towns like Saint-Émilion and Blaye, local wineries, bike rides, historic castles, and even sweet moments like tasting canelés fresh from the oven. Uniworld’s expert guides brought each stop to life with charm and insight.

More than just luxury, Uniworld embraces sustainability: no single-use plastics, food waste reduction systems, plant-based menus, and genuine support for local communities. It's what they call Tiny Noticeable Touches™ — and they truly elevate the experience.

From the first glass of Champagne to the final farewell, this was travel with soul. If you're dreaming of a river cruise that’s poetic, pampering and purposeful, look no further. Uniworld is where luxury flows — just like the rivers of France.
Ju Nakad @natripdaju

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Paula Rice, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Bon Voyage, tour=Brilliant Bordeaux (BOD-BOD) 25, tourdirector=20099-50016
Service Comment:
Could not fault the service. The staff at every level were excellent. The rooms were impeccably serviced every day, the restaurant & bar staff were attentive as indeed were the reception staff 
I particularly enjoyed the dancing evening & guest singers. It was obvious that great care & attention was given whilst on board.
Product Comment:
The ship is beautiful, the staff are welcoming and the food is fabulous but the rivers Gironde & Garrone not so much, which maybe the reason when most of the ships movement was done before dawn. There is not a lot to see on those rivers. As a floating hotel it was lovely but I would hardly call it a cruise, and this was my very first experience.
I found the tours rather dull, I think more free time to do whatever one wants would be better 
As a first timer, I found the schedule a bit overwhelming and the chat/ information gatherings. The best outings were the market and the Toulouse Lautrec outing. 
I know Bordeaux city but my accompanying friend did not. It's a shame since we were docked in Bordeaux a good deal, but failed to explore it as individuals.
I suggest a visit to the Marche du Cappucines on the Tuesday in Bordeaux, far more interesting & engaging, great food and real ambience.
You didn't call it Brilliant Bordeaux after all

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=James Gary, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51856
Service Comment: Service was good. As with any ship, you had servers that were definitely better than others.
Product Comment: The ship was going on 15 years of age and lacked a lot of technology updates you would expect - such as usb & power outlets. Wi-Fi connectivity was quite bad as well. The suite wall coverings and curtains were quite dated so much so that the curtains no longer would cover the windows fully letting in more light than you should expect. We had unseasonably hot weather during most of the cruise which made the ship quite hot and the air conditioning was not able to maintain anything close to cool in rooms, dining or gathering areas. When we mentioned that our suite was hot, we were told that engineering would check it out. Got frustrated with receiving that response numerous times when we asked about the heat in the areas. Uniworld would have served themselves much better by telling everyone that the air conditioning on the ship was going to have difficulty keeping up given the extreme heat. We would have appreciated an honest answer of the issue versus being told we would have engineering look at it when, in fact, they knew what the issue was. The food was fine and service was very good. I definitely would not do another Uniworld cruise on this ship (S S Antoinette) if it was the one offered, so it would have to be on one of their newer ships before I would sail them again.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the ship and air conditioning could have been better. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the service of our staff onboard. If you would like to provide us with any additional details about your experience, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

The food was consistently good with something for everyone. I especially liked having access to espresso drinks and cookies 24/7. Another great feature is the complementary laundry room. I would probably opt out of the tour portion and use the time to explore independently. Would recommend this cruise to anyone, even those who aren't "cruise people."

Product Comment:
We have never used Uniworld before so we were very excited especially as we were doing the Bordeaux wine tasting River cruise.
When we arrived at  Bordeaux airport we were shown to our coach for the transfer to the boat, we were informed when we booked up that the time from airport too the boat would be 30-40 minutes. Unfortunately we were left in a cold (air con to low) coach for 1 half hours with no communication whatsoever so ever! So you can imagine this wasn’t a very good start!!!
When we arrived to our boat we were greeted by friendly staff & offered lunch because our rooms were not ready but that wasn’t a problem.
The service we had was excellent from the waiters, bar staff, housekeeping etc. the food was first class and plenty of it. We were also on an all inclusive package which included everything which I would highly recommended.
The rooms were smaller then some other boats we have been on and they had to much furniture in them, I would also have like coffee making facilities and a mini fridge.
The boat had a pool which was very nice especially as a lot of the European boats don’t.
All the Excursions were all very well organised and very interesting, so I would definitely recommend to anyone, especially the sidecar ride around Bordeaux.
I

Service Comment: First class service in all areas - including housekeeping, waitstaff and bartenders.  I had a massage and facial treatment which were excellent.
Product Comment: I did the Castles on the Rhine Cruise with my mom (who was turning 80) and my aunt.  We had an amazing time.  The ship, SS Victoria, was fantastic - so well equipped, stylish decor and great attention to detail.  The cabins were beautifully decorated, very modern and luxurious, with space for everything.  So many thoughtful details like underbid storage, floor to ceiling windows that you could open, a desk/vanity, loads of outlets for US and European plugs, comfortable bed, easy to use shower and electronic Do Not Disturb and Housekeeping display.  We really enjoyed the food - breakfast and lunch were buffet and dinner was served at the table.  Lots of food including healthy options as well.  There was a great selection of tours and a few which were an additional cost.  We did one of these to Heidelburg and did not regret it!  The best part was the staff though.  So kind, enthusiastic and professional.  They went above and beyond to make this the special trip that I had hoped for.  Thank you Uniworld!

Product Comment: My wife and I were on the Castles Along the Rhine cruise with Uniworld River Cruises in late May 2025 traveling from Basel, Switzerland to Amsterdam. This was our first ever river cruise and it was absolutely incredible! The boat was elegant and the cabins well-appointed and much larger than we expected. The staff were welcoming and always attentive to our needs. Kudos to the cruise manager (Anthony) and hotel manager (Romica). The dining room service was superb! Our thanks to the restaurant manager (Vasil) and the executive chef (Tomas). Also, our compliments to the dining room and housekeeping personnel, Service was exceptional! The food, as well as the selection, were excellent and each night the dinner wines were chosen by the on-board sommelier to reflect the wine producing region through which we were passing. Service Comment: We enjoyed the tall food server from Bulgaria(?) in the dining room, Petru, our bar attendant was a lot of fun.  Sabrina was the best cruise director we have experienced on Uniworld.  The room was always very clean and fresh.  The dining room was very loud though and crowded.  Bartenders were all very skilled and friendly.
Product Comment: We took the Burgundy and Provence cruise starting in Arles July 13- Lyon July 20.  The Uniworld SS Catherine was a beautiful ship.  Our Cruise director Sabrina was awesome.  She was a lot of fun yet very professional and business like when needed.  We enjoyed the Black and White party, Chris the Crooner and the silent disco.  The singer in Lyon was good but we would have liked more songs we could dance to.  This was our 4th Uniworld Cruise and I must say that previous cruises had higher level included excursions.  When we did the duoro river, our favorite excursion was to Pacheca's Quinta where we enjoyed a private tour and delicious dinner.  In our Enchanting Danube trip, we were able to enter museums and enjoyed after hours concerts in which only our ship was  present.  Also our included cider tasting excursion at Linz and the winery at wacchau valley were exquisite and very memorable. These type of included excursions made us feel that Uniworld was of higher caliber than other river cruise companies.  On this cruise, we were among the Viking crowd in the included excursions, and we could have done the same trip with Viking and save $2000 per person.  I am hoping that this is not a sign of how all Uniworld cruises will be with the change of ownership.  I am hoping that the difference in the included excursions were just because of our cruise destination.  The masterpiece excursions were too expensive since we bought a lot of products at each location (olive oil and truffle farm).  Because of the reduced quality of included excursions, we will explore other cruise company options that offer an elevated experience.  We still will consider Uniworld but will be more selective and careful in exploring the types of included excursions.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Anthony Crook, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50075
Service Comment: The service from all staff in all departments was great.  They went out of their way to try and help or accommodate your requests.  The room was always spotless.  The dining areas were always clean and the food beautifully presented.  The Reception staff were helpful too.
Product Comment: We loved our Paris & Normandy cruise.  The pace was just right.  We saw beautiful Chateauxs, Monet's garden and Versailles Palace which were highlights.  It was our first time with Uniworld river cruises, but we have done several other river cruise holidays.  The food was great, especially the a la carte service in the evenings.  The staff were very polite and courteous, and always on hand to refill our glasses or offer drinks.  We had a lower level cabin with water level fixed windows, the room was spacious, and the bed and linen of a high quality.  I would highly recommend this cruise.  For the first time on our cruise we had a very mixed age range, from 12 to 85, usually the demographic was older.  But the youngsters seemed to enjoy themselves as much as the older folk.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Irwin Hoover, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Grande Italia (MXP-FCO) 25, tourdirector=21272-51526
Service Comment:
How does it get any better?  An individual is assigned a very few cabins and or guests to be responsible for so you almost feel like you are their sole responsibility. AND, they are great!  The meals are fantastic, with food
and drink delicious and service excellent.  Entertainment is very nice as well.  The toughest part about UNIWORLD is the end of the voyage, you have a hard time trying to figure out where your next trip should take you.
It is a little easier for my sister and I as we have traveled very little out side the U.S.
So as you can see. UNIWORLD offers so many ways to see the world and a superior way at that, decisions of where and when is the real decision.
Bon Voyage!
Product Comment:
This was our 2nd cruise.  And again, it did not disappoint. All the bases were covered as outlined in all of the brochures.  The cruise in itself while onboard is extremely first class and the crew makes everyone feel special. Your suite onboard is extremely nice and can be arranged to fit your needs, ie: split bed to two singles.
Daily attractions off ship meets most every ones criteria, lots of sight seeing and history of areas visited. Do wear good walking shoes as you are visiting by ship and ground transportation, and there is lots to see.
Bon Voyage!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Kevin Carr, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51868

Product Comment: As always with Uniworld, the whole crew goes well beyond the norm.  Onboarding was a piece of cake and effortless, as well as disembarkation.  90% of the crew knew our names by site the first morning - and not because we stood out on the night before.  Our room was perfectly kept the whole time.  The food was exceptional each day and was paired to the region as well as the wine.  The bar was well stocked and the bartenders had excellent suggestions for something new to try based on our standard beverages.  The Rhine has some very beautiful areas and some that are a little more industrial, but Uniworld did a great job to give us the best advice on what is just "city" and what is more interesting.  They also provided very delightful and unique for purchase tour options which we did three of them.  We extended our trip before and after on our own, so we can't speak to the extentions that Uniworld provide.  We had a wonderful time and it's amazing how quickly the time can pass.  Until we cruise again "Bon voyage".

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Marcia Kobialka, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Burgundy & Provence (LYS-ARL) 25, tourdirector=20101-50139
Product Comment:
Took the same trip in 2013.  Dismayed to discover many of the excursions we enjoyed were no longer included in the trip except with additional fees.
It was very disappointing that the sundeck had only limited availability while the ship was in motion and during that availability, the canopies would not be in place, so there was no chance to be out of direct sunlight. Food and service were as wonderful as ever.  We would call out the Sommelier and Restaurant Manager for excellent service.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Claire Wilson, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=European Jewels (AMS-BUD) 25, tourdirector=21041-50358
Service Comment: See previous comments
Product Comment:
This was our 2nd Uniworld river cruise and we absolutely had a fantastic trip.  We have travelled heavily for over 50 years, on every kind of ship/boat imaginable.  Although I'd like to try another company's river cruise out of curiosity, i can't imagine choosing another company because we have been so happy with our 2 Uniworld vacations.

The staff/crew on the ship were top-notch.  No matter what you needed, the service was perfect.  We had favorite servers in the dining room and they always took such good care of all our food preferences and allergies.  

The Cruise Director was great. She handled all of her varied tasks with professionalism and good cheer.  She also helped us individually with special requests.  We signed up for 2 future Uniworld cruises through her, and she made that process super-easy.

Lastly, just a shout-out on the food quality.  I can't imagine any food being better.  We had many 5-star dinners, in our opinion.  The number of offered dishes for the breakfast and lunch buffets was amazing, considering it is a small ship.  We never lacked variety and great food choices.

We like the format of these cruises so much.  The organized tour guides were generally excellent, with the exception of one that was too pedantic and focused on excess historical details.  We like that we have the option to wander and explore on our own--more so than other tours.  

I think river cruises are excellent for older adults--some of whom may have disabilities or slower pacing needs.  There was almost always a "slow walker" group, which we were happy to join and met our needs.

Thank you, Uniworld for a great vacation!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Barry Adams, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21658-51816

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Michael Boldt, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=European Jewels (BUD-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21049-50370
Service Comment: Excellent! The staff and crew were all friendly and very helpful.
Product Comment:
We loved our cruise from Budapest to Amsterdam. All of the staff and crew were great. We especially enjoyed the restaurant and bar staff. Cruise manager Ed and his “apprentice “ Robert were also exceptional. 
The pace was slower than our usual vacations but that was fine; we had a nice relaxing time. There was enough variety at each stop to make us excited for each day’s activities. Perhaps a few more activities geared toward the more fit and adventurous guests would have been nice. 
The ship itself was clean but several of our fellow passengers mentioned small but annoying maintenance issues in their rooms. Mostly cosmetic things (ripped curtains, dents and dings, etc). My suggestion to Uniworld is the give the River Princess a thorough makeover. 
In summary, we will definitely take another river cruise with Uniworld!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Donald Koch, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51838
Service Comment: The two butlers were EXCELLENT as was the head of the dinning room service....he saved us a table for all nine of us....the room was always well manicured and attended too....
Product Comment: The cruise on the Victoria was less than optimal. The two bulters were great. Outstanding. But clearly it was visible that the Victoria was and old rented ship that needed attention. The air condition did NOT work as you came into the entry of the ship. DID NOT WORK adequately. The former ship had gone bankrupt, Chrystal and there were still signature items with the Chrystal name on the ship. The entertainment was less than high schoolish....and the day trips were not very interesting. Heildleburg was excellent, but we were on the bus for FOUR hours without water or being told we needed to take water....we had NINE people and paid a premium price but were told we had to get ourselves to the train station despite the brochure indicating transportation to and from the ship on day of arrival and day of departure.....The Uniworld seems like a struggling company that needs new leadership. We have been on nearly a dozen Tauck tours with NINE people.....Uniworld seems to get their act together and learn how to operate smoothly a vacation cruise...a lot of very nice staff, but not a lot of thinking through detail that is a sign that management really is not paying attention. Why have a ship where the air conditioning does not work in the summer as you board the ship? PLEASE...simple things....
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that air conditioning could have been better and apologize that you were not advised we are only able to provide airport transfers, not train station transfers. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the service of our butlers onboard. If you would like to follow up with us regarding your trip, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Bernard Colasuonno, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (BUD-PRG) 25, tourdirector=20109-50189
Service Comment: Service was good.
Product Comment:
Overall, the cruise was very good.  Food, service, guides, sights, all good.  We are disappointed that after a month Uniworld has not responded to our travel agent regarding  an issue we documented.
Uniworld falsely claimed in their website description that guests can come out on deck on the day we were on the Danube/Main canal.  This was not possible as they closed the deck the last day while cruising from Ragensburg to Nuremburg. That day, a Uniworld lecturer came on board to give a 1 hour talk on the Danube/Main canal.  I asked and he said the vehicle overpasses were built in 1995, before there was river boat traffic.  Riverboats started in 2005.  That is why the deck must be closed.  So, this is not a “high water” event, nor was it a change due to circumstances.  This was always the requirement, just not described accurately on the website – before the cruise.  It ahs since been changed.
 Uniworld description mis represented the last day of the cruise.  All deck rails must be folded down, deck chairs removed and the deck is closed to guests to navigate the canal, every time, regardless of the water level.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are happy to hear that you enjoyed the food, service, guides and sightseeing. A member of our team has reached out to you regarding your feedback. If you would like to follow up with us regarding your trip, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Timothy Seider, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Bon Voyage, tour=Brilliant Bordeaux (BOD-BOD) 25, tourdirector=20099-50017
Service Comment:
Lack of beverage service at cocktail hour and on the top deck. 

Food service was lacking at times and not attentive.  Ship seemed that it was short staffed and not up to Uniworld standards.
Product Comment:
When we arrived to the ship on May 25th, 2025, we were provided with a letter from Ellen Bettridge.  The letter stated “ Our onboard crew is ready to provide you with outstanding service, delicious cuisine, memorable experiences onboard and ashore…”.  Unfortunately, the cruise did fall short in a number of areas.

First, let us acknowledge the staff that we felt did an outstanding job - Esteves our Sommelier, Marta our Purser, and Zonka our Cabin Steward.  

We were also very impressed by the excursion guides, particularly Alexandria and Diana.  Their knowledge and local history was outstanding.  

Our special dinner in the Cave du Vine was outstanding and the service provided by Esteves and other staff members was special.

Our overall rating of our cruise is 4 out of 5.

We do feel that Uniworld fell short with food and beverage service, one vineyard/wine tasting excursion, and overall directions from our Cruise Director. Our comments in various areas are below:

Breakfast

The breakfast was basically the same selection every day with the exception of pancakes and waffles rotated every other day, and different sausage on a daily basis.  A variety of food selections on a few days would have enhanced our breakfast experience.

Lunch

With the exception of the two days we had beef sliders and ham & cheese croissants, the lunch selections were not very appealing, and we had to settle for salads on these days.

Dinner

The dinner selections were generally good, however, the last evening dinners were extremely disappointing.  We had ordered the tenderloin entrée cooked to medium well for my wife and medium for me.  Both tenderloins were only 1/2 inch thick where others at our table had beautiful thick cut tenrdloin slices.  Both of our tenderloins had obvious grill marks and were very overcooked.  The asparagus, carrots, and potato sides were cold.  We were not able to bring the issue to our server since he never returned to check on the meals.

Food Service

Our food service was generally poor.  On Tuesday of our cruise, our entrees took an exceptionally long time to be brought to our table.  On Thursday of the cruise, service again was very slow, which we brought to the attention of Roberto our hotel manager and Marta our purser.  On Friday we again experienced a substantial delay in the delivery of our entrees.  Our waiter came and apologized for the delay and stated he had to serve the guests in the Cave du Vine dinner.  On Saturday evening, our server seemed to disappear, never checking on any issues regarding the food, and forgetting what each one of us at our table had ordered for dessert.  We also experienced delays throughout the week in refilling our wine selections and numerous times had to request refills.  On Saturday evening, we were lucky to flag down our Sommelier Esteves, and he responded quickly to refill our glasses.  

Bar Service

The service in the lounge, particularly during cocktail hour was nonexistent or very slow.  It was allmost impossible to get a second drink!  Many times throughout the week we had to go to the bar and order our drinks.  

The bar/drink service on the top deck was also very slow and nonexistent.  We had to go down to the lounge to get a drink.  

Excursions

While we loved the majority of the excursions and our guides, two particular excursions were disappointing.  The Medoc oyster tasting served wine in paper cups (really?) and the vineyard tour/tasting at Chateau Balestard La Toppelle where the vineyard tour guide served the Chateau’s Grand Cru Classe in the same glass as what the vineyard tour guide called the first wine as a “good pizza wine”.  Not very professional for the number two vineyard in St. Emilion!

Over the years, our experience with the Uniworld excursions on the Rhein River, Danube River, and the Milan/Venice Cruise included vineyard tours/tastings that were outstanding and professionally curated by the vineyard owner or a qualified sommelier. Many of the vineyard tours included chese/bread offerings and some even included lunches. These two excursions fell woefully short in comparison to what we have become accustomed to with Uniworld. 

Cruise Director

Our Cruise Director could have been much more engaging and offer more descriptive of the excursions and Masterpiece offerings.

When we approached her regarding our disappointment with the vineyard tour/tasting at Chateau Balestard La Toppelle, she stated that Uniworld does not choose the winery,

Final Comments

On our final day of the cruise, our Hotel Director informed us that on the next cruise there would be only 66 people.  We also learned that a number of cruise staff, including waiters had left the ship on Thursday for vacation.  This only leads us to believe that the reduction of ship staff, for whatever reason, had a direct impact on the service levels and our negative experiences.  Or, is the new ownership having a direct impact on our negative experiences?

Over the years, we have been faithful Uniworld cruisers, this being our fifth river cruise.  We have introduced others to Uniworld.  Our daughter and son-in-law recently completed their fourth river cruise with Uniworld.

In Ellen Bettridge's thank you letter provided in our cabin on our last evening of the cruise, she stated that she hopes the staff has met or exceeded our service expectations.  She also stated “At Uniworld, we pride ourselves on our service. Our goal is to offer our guests the absolute best river cruise experience."

While we have had many wonderful experiences with Uniworld over the years, our recent cruise with Uniworld has fallen short and clearly has not been "the absolute best river cruise experience”.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback which has been passed on for review. We are sorry to hear that your trip did not meet your expectations set by your past Uniworld cruises. However, we are happy to hear that the staff and the special dining experience was a highlight of your trip. If you would like to follow-up with our team, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=John Scott, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21658-51816

Product Comment:
Let me start out by saying that this was our third Uniworld River Cruise. As like the previous two, the ship's Crew and Staff were excellent, all friendly and helpful.
The ship itself was beautiful and accommodations were very nice.
 Loved doing the biking excursions.

Now for the review:
Unlike the previous two, I personally found the food selection and servings were not quite up to the standards that I expected.
The itinerary and shore excursion were adequate.

Now for the unpleasantries:
This Cruise was more of a Land tour by bus than an adventure on water.
Most docking locations were away from city centres and in undesireable locations. Did not dock at gangways but alongside walls. Security was lacking. Anyone could have hopped on boat unnoticed.

Most times, buses were needed to take you to town centres.
 Infact, most people had to be bused from(our initial port) Basil, to some 30 minutes away in Germany. When I embarked the ship, it had moved 1hr away in France, as our flight into Basil was delayed. Next day everyone had to be bused back to Basil.
Seems Uniworld could not get proper docking facilities throughout this cruise.

Another dislike, anyone who had rooms on the Starboard side had no views while in port. I got to use balcony once while on this cruise.

Uniworld was sold late last year and perhaps are in the initial stages of cost cutting.

It will be hard for me to continue to promote Uniworld as the Premier RiverCruise line, it use to be

Service Comment:
Excellent,  very friendly especially if in a large group, except for the long wait at lunch and dinner for a mineral, sometimes having to ask three times.
Everyone always said hello or have a nice day, our room was looked after very well, towels replaced without question, I had medicine that had to be kept in a fridge and that was not a problem for the lovely lady on reception, my favourite waiter was a tall dark man Vad??? His name was so long I could never remember the spelling, he was so polite and always seemed to be working everywhere on the ship.
Product Comment:
We enjoyed our Cruise, the ship was beautiful as was our French balcony room a little small ,the day trips were well organised, and varied, our tour director was efficient, the staff were very friendly and efficient, I don’t drink alcohol and on many occasions at lunch or dinner had to wait a long time for a mineral to be got from the bar, had to ask more than once for my mineral, perhaps keeping a few bottles of mineral in the dining room would be more efficient,I am a plain eater , and don’t eat fish, so my choice for food was limited to Omelette and salad for breakfast, salad and fruit for lunch, and I had steak every night, one night I had a very grizzly steak and when the waiter was collecting my plate he asked me if I enjoyed my steak as half of it was on my plate, I said it was very grizzly and he replied yes that happens sometimes, on my last night I got another tough steak and asked my waiter to return it to the kitchen and to replace it , he told me they were all the same as it had been cut fresh by the chef , I insisted on getting another one as I was really hungry as I did not eat any of the starter or soup, he was really hesitant but he did and came back with  much better steak if significantly smaller, it was the only cruise I have taken that I lost weight on , the couple next to us one night at dinner the Husband ordered chicken and part of it was uncooked, he was really upset , the ships manager came and apologised, 
I feel I did not get value for the cost of the trip.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Lyvia Kalisky, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50517
Service Comment: The cabin cleaning staff left the room very clean and orderly.  After a few days we ran out of facial tissue in the bathroom dispenser, but we never asked it to be refilled, as it wasn't important.  The soaps and lotion were excellent, and the treats were nice.  The staff in the lounge were great and the restaurant staff were excellent.  After a couple of days they knew what we liked to drink and always gave attentive service with a smile.  Particularly kind and friendly were Ana of guest services and Luciano who gave extra special service willingly and with a very positive and helpful attitude.  All the excursion guides were outstanding -- their clear explanations and attention to detail were great.  Fiorella gave us a wonderful demonstration of mask-making which I especially enjoyed because I am fluent in Italian.  Unfortunately, the woman who accompanied us was not always able to translate well for the guests who needed an English translation.  The glass blowing demonstration and showrooms were a little overwhelming.  But the personality of Signor Lazzerini was charismatic.
Product Comment: The accommodations were comfortable, the air conditioner and thermostat worked well, we enjoyed the thermal water bottles and the treats.  The service in every part of the cruise was excellent, staff were efficient, friendly and helpful.  I especially appreciated the self service guest laundry and coffee station.  It would be helpful to have ice buckets in the cabins.  The excursions were uniformly excellent--the guides were knowledgeable and spoke clearly in excellent English.  The vox box worked very well.  I would have liked more detailed preparatory lectures about the sites visited.  Also, the ship had several jigsaw puzzles, but there was no table that seemed suitable to put them together!  The chef and his staff were outstanding -- the food was by far the best I have had at sea or on land.  The ingredients used were all fresh and of best quality and the sauces were delectable.  The wait staff went above and beyond to fulfill every guest request.  The cabin and all the public spaces were always very clean and orderly.

Service Comment: already stated
Product Comment: Having the very best experience a few years back on Uniworld on the Grand River cruise (Istanbul to Amsterdam) we had always told our friends how great it was and we really looked forward to the trip on the Rhone, it was extremely disappointing. The dining room staff and the house keeping crew were  very good, but the rest rather poor. Reception medioca and the shore excursion the same except for the local guide who took us up through a village to an old convent. Very poor group management. Most of our fellow passengers were great, but one group of Americans travelling together spoilt it for all. There is little to see along this section of the Rhone. I would not recommend this trip to anyone and we would strongly consider our next river cruise to be Viking. We have an ocean cruise this December / January in Norway and up to the Arctic , with a Japan - South Korea cruise in March / April. We did the Mekong early 2024 with APT and they left Uniworld (this trip) in their wake. Very disappointing given what a fantastic experience we had before; definitely off our travel list.

We are happy to hear that you enjoyed the service of our housekeeping and dining staff. If you would like to follow-up with us further regarding your experience, please feel free to contact us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Brenda Willson, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50517
Service Comment: Okay -nothing to comment on really.
Product Comment: The ss Venezia was tired looking, huge bunches of plastic flowers in the bar, worn fabric on couches, springs very worn on settees in bar. No comparison with last years beautiful S.S. Sao Gabriel which set the bar for me with Uniworld The PO Delta sailing excursion towards the end of the cruise was ridiculous. We were driven (50 mins) to get onto a small boat to be taken to nothing - the tea & biscuits were not offered by the cruise director - and only the more ‘forward’ passengers helped them selves to a drink and:or biscuit. Very very unprofessional & a fill in excursion. We would have preferred another cruise up & down the Grand canal again - would have been nicer & hopefully tea & bikkies served properly. The boat captain & his son were delightful.  In returning from excursion there were white bread finger  sandwiches - white bread / goodness me very low budget The guest entertainers eg: Maria - consisted of Maria arriving, setting up & playing - no introduction from cruise Director and she just up & left - again no farewell, very poor behaviour. Our previous cruise introduced the artists to our clapping & excitement- as did the farewell. The same happened with the Opera singers another evening. The sommelier was fabulous & we really enjoyed her knowledge & enthusiasm every evening. The private trips to The Basillica and Doges Palace were excellent & our historian full of knowledge.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the onboard entertainment and decor could have been improved. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the sommelier and private excursion options. If you would like to provide us with any additional information about your experience, please feel free to contact us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Rosemary Peart, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=European Jewels (AMS-BUD) 25, tourdirector=21041-50360
Product Comment:
Our small group were extremely impressed with everything about the River Princess cruise we went on in June 2025. All staff on board were extremely obliging and efficient. The food was absolutely delicious and the presentation was just delightful. The bar staff were extremely helpful in finding a drink to suite everyone's taste and it was wonderful that the drinks were included in the overall cost of the cruise as you did not have to worry about paying each time you had a drink.
The tours were very well planned and informative. It was very helpful to have a choice between a hard or easier walking tour although a certain amount of fitness was necessary for the easy tours. The use of earphones to hear the guide's descriptions and history of the surrounding areas and buildings was extremely appreciated as no information was missed.
The cabins and entire River Princess's decor was extremely exciting, inviting, pleasant and comfortable.
We all had an extremely happy adventure on the River Princess, which we will remember with very happy memories and smiles.
Thank you to all involved.

This cruise was wonderful. From the two nights pre cruise in Budapest in a good quality hotel and the walking tour which we both enjoyed with a characterful tour guide.
The transfer to the ship was seamless and our cabin did not disappoint (401). The ship based staff were, without exception, excellent. Friendly, willing to help, and really wanted to make our holiday the best possible. 
Our cabin was lovely, so much room! Ample hanging and drawer space. Easy access to plugs for charging devices. Fantastic bathroom. The bed was so comfortable and the pillow choices were great. 
The free laundry service was made full use of and meant we could return home with clean clothes which was fab!
The cities and towns we stopped at, the choice of tours, the special events in Vienna - we really felt special. We very much enjoyed the cycle ride! We did not go on every tour, we prefer to explore on our own and our one “negative” was the amount of time taken every evening going through the next day’s itinerary on offer - all of which we could read about anyway and had heard about at the briefing at the start of the week. 
The food was exemplary and the service in the dining room was fantastic. 
We enjoyed this cruise so much more than we thought we would and that was down to the amazing crew on board, and the lovely fellow passengers.

Service Comment: Great - friendly and efficient.
Product Comment:
The boat was only about 75% full - if it had been at capacity the dining area especially for buffet meals would have been to small as the space was quite narrow
We were expecting the Murano glass demonstration to be a cruise highlight BUT  it was exceedingly disappointing  - not an art glass studio just a very commercial outfit . The other disappointing trip was Burano -the lace centre -all shops sold vast quantities of machine made lace
Apart from the above the rest of the trip was excellent especially the after hours trips. The guides were excellent , except for the Burano guide, seemed genuinely interested in us enjoying the visit
The ships crew were friendly and very efficient. All trips usually started early so ensuring that they were done and dusted before the tourist coaches arrived. The trips to the market was very interesting and always were in small enough groups to enable one to ask the guide  questions. 
My wife and I had visited Venice three times before but thoroughly enjoyed this trip as it gave us a more thorough guide to Venice

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Christine Holt, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (MXP-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20142-51588
Description: Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto
Service Comment: Very good
Product Comment:
The ship, the staff & food were all of a very high standard & ensured a very positive time.

Our only criticism is the fact that while berthed in Venice, which was four out of seven days our room was dockside & we were looking at a brick wall. Given that we had two nights in Venice before leaving for Burano & Chioggia for three nights we presumed the ship would dock in reverse for the last two nights in Venice. This was not the case. We again stared at a brick wall while the other side enjoyed a beautiful view of the lagoon. Given the fact that the cost was the same it was extremely disappointing.
I would not recommend this trip unless your travel agent is able to secure a cabin facing the Lagoon. Or a reduced fare.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Prudence Abeln, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50157
Service Comment:

Product Comment:
We had a wonderful experience.  Housekeeper was friendly and did a great job.  We liked several of the waitstaff.  I did observe a newer waitress being trained, and I thought her supervisor was a little rough with her, especially in front of the guests.
We enjoyed all of our tour guides.  I do think you need to come up with some level 1 adventures since several of the guests had some trouble walking.  I was good for a 1/2 day but often wasn't up to a second walking tour in the afternoon.
Enjoyed Budapest and bus trip to Salsburg.  Also, would have liked a list of recommended shops in each town where we could find regional gift items, not souvenirs.

Thanks for organizing a great Danube experience.

Service Comment:
Paul, our butler was fantastic.  

Andre, drink server, was the BEST.  

Cruise director was a people pleaser…. But not all info was accurate.
Product Comment:
It was a good cruise.  Some of the info on tours was not accurate and a bit disappointing. 

The food did not live up to the hype.  Not one single meal was outstanding including the special private dining with the chef.  It was good but not fabulous and frankly the ‘premium’ wines were not premium.  

The staff was great, particularly our butler, Paul and the drink staff, particularly Andre.  Our cleaning staff neglected to introduce herself until the day before departure and we felt like it was fishing for an extra tip.  

While we enjoyed Uniworld, I don’t think it was the premium line we had expected and found it to be no different from our experience on Viking.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Thomas Surowiec, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Uniworld Owned, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50157
Service Comment: All staff and service was very attentive and very good.
Product Comment:
The Danube is stunning. The ship and staff were excellent. 
The food however was not very good. Three of us had the Duck and all sent it back. 
The steaks were frozen and dry.  Breakfast was edible IF you got to the buffet early. 
There was a lack of fresh fruit. 
The wines chosen were very good. 
The entertainment was similar to that found in a nursing home, with the energy being shut down at 10:00 pm and virtually “lights out” by 11:00 pm. 
The sun deck was virtually unused and was often “uninviting” with all furniture put away.   
You can do a lot better.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that your dining experience and the onboard entertainment could have been improved. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the Danube River, the SS Maria Theresa and our onboard staff. If you would like to provide us with any addition information about your experience, please feel free to contact us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jeanette Young, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50157
Product Comment:
Notwithstanding the average age of the cruise passengers, Uniworld offered only slight accommodation for those who moved more slowly, and/or were challenged with stairs, cobblestone or brick streets & walkways.  A solution that other cruise companies utilize is to divide tour groups in half, with slower movers in a separate group.  Even the layout of the dining room tables & chairs made it difficult for some people to maneuver.
In dining, the expected etiquette is that meals are served from the left, and plates are removed from the right, and women’s orders and food delivery occur prior to the men.  None of this was demonstrated in the dining room.  Despite requests to 3 different servers, no coffee was served for at least 3 meals.
Generally the staff was pleasant and helpful, but when problems arose they were defensive and not apologetic or accountable.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that your dining experience could have been improved. Regarding mobility, we ask that all guests with mobility concerns contact us in advance, so accommodation can be arranged when possible. We apologize if accommodations were not made in advance for you. If you would like to follow-up with us further regarding your experience, please feel free to contact us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Joan Gabin, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50078
Service Comment: Dining and housekeeping were excellent.
Product Comment:
The ship, the food and the itinerary were excellent.
The cruise director, Emmanuelle was outstanding, accessible, knowledgeable, funny and helpful.
However, the reception desk personnel were pleasant, but clueless.
Every question was answered with, I will have to get back to you, I need to ask a "higher up" (my words)
Uniworld changed the itinerary because of Bastille Day. News flash, this holiday was not just added to the calendar!  It caused inconvenience and expense to people who had expected to be in Paris on another day as per the printed itinerary.  Having cruised twice on Uniworld, and on many Ocean cruises, Seabourn, Regent, Oceania etc. I am aware that the cruise line can change ports and itineraries, as needed, based on weather, docking conditions, etc.  The rearranging of the Paris schedule was not based on those conditions.
We booked a "french balcony" cabin, as stated on the website.
NO french balcony. Even though, the" Balcony " is not a true balcony, there are sliding doors, and it gives a more expansive feeling to the room.
This should be noted that there are windows and no balcony on your website.
Two major misrepresentations, in your online marketing.
I hope these observations are noted and addressed,
