Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Carol Urben, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50078
Service Comment:
Terrific
See comment above
Product Comment:
The cruise was great mostly because Emmanuelle the cruise director was terrific.. Her enthusiasm and attention to detail are unmatched..Due to what I would call stupidity on the part of whoever designed the schedule and didn’t realize one of the days was July 14 and all the locks would be unmanned and it caused a shifting of which days we would be in Paris. Uncalled for error by corporate. Emmanuelle did a great job of calming the guests that were not happy with the change.  The day at Normandy was terrific and the ceremony that we enjoyed was a lasting memory. The cabin was fine, housekeeping great. The entire staff was terrific. The food was only ok. I have had much better on river boat cruises. The menu choices were ok but the preparation was lacking. Ordered steak, the special first night, and it tasted like it has been previously prepared and reheated, and was tough. Duck breast good but half of it tough and not edible. Transfers to and from Airport were great. 
People turned out after dinner for nights that there was entertainment but not on other nights. How about Trivia nights. That always gets people engaged especially if you form teams with people you don’t know. Also a fun activity that gets people to mingle is to have a table w a jigsaw puzzle on it and people will stop by and add a piece or two and eventually it gets done. 
This survey should have been sent with Uniworld as the sender. I had no idea who Feefo was and almost deleted it based on not knowing who sender was.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Lai Kwan Chan, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21650-51809
Service Comment: Please refer to my earlier trip review.
Product Comment:
S.S. ANTOINETTE (Stateroom 304)
11 Days Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland 
Amsterdam > Basel
27 Apr 2025 ~ 7 May 2025 

This is our first river cruise and what an amazing and unforgettable experience brought upon us by Uniworld.

From the day of embarkation until the end of cruise, every single crew member we met greeted us with a smile on their face, this exudes joyous and cheerful vibe throughout the journey.

The journey was filled with surprises, enjoyment, good food & wine, touching moments and valuable knowledge shared by local guides on our excursions.

One incident that touched our heart deeply and left indelible impression took place on the evening of 3 May 2025. We had just finished our excursion at Idstein and are travelling on the coach going back to the ship. Shortly before arriving at our destination, it suddenly start to pour and we are stranded on the coach waiting for the rain to subdue (there is a linear park that separate the ship & where the coach parked). A short while later, the rain became smaller and those of us with umbrella start to get off the bus to walk back to the ship. As we are walking back, we saw 4~5 nautical crews braving the rain to send umbrella to the coach for those who needed. This simple and thoughtful action by the crew definitely worth tons of gold!

Another incident that left an indelible impression happened on the first day of excursion. We met with some hiccups and missed the canal cruise. Cruise Manager — Daniele did such a wonderful job in the service recovery by making arrangement to give us a night canal cruise! My, oh my, the lightings along the canal was such an amazing & lovely sight that all the inconvenience in the morning were long forgotten.

The food onboard SS Antoinette prepared by Chef Mario and his culinary team is simply delicious and tasty. Some dishes, e.g. Lamb Chops, Duck Confit, Tuna Crudo, Opera Cake, Creme Brulee, are even beyond remarkable and are capable of rivaling those put together by fine dining restaurant!

Salon du Grand Trianon and Leopard Bar are well managed by three gorgeous and capable ladies - Bartender Silviya and wait staff Lina & Maria, they are ever so welcoming and ready to serve, attentive but not intrusive. Among them, Silviya has such a present, wonderful, extraordinary & helpful character. On top of that, Silviya possess such marvellous mixology skill that no request is too difficult for her. No alcohol, not too sweet, No Problem! Something to refresh the mind after a tiring excursion, Easy! Any beverage you like at any time of the day, she will come back with some nice surprises. And because of her, Salon du Grand Trianon is a venue onboard SS Antoinette we always hang out, to chill, to relax and to rejuvenate!

Last but not least, Mihai of Restaurant de Versailles is a jovial, observant and efficient wait staff yet maintain a high level of professionalism. We prefer hot water than coffee/tea to go with our meal. After a couple of seating, Mihai will automatically prepare a pot of hot water on the table the moment we sat down. It is such meticulous understanding of your customer needs that build a lasting impression. On a seperate dinner occasion, we have difficulties in selecting our dessert, Mihai aptly describe the desserts on the menu, allowing us to make an informed choice. The dessert turn out to be very good.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Evelyn Mintz, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Sao Gabriel, tour=Portugal, Spain & the Douro River Valley (OPO-LIS) 25, tourdirector=20153-50422
Description: Portugal, Spain & the Douro River Valley
Service Comment: Everyone was very helpful and excellent at their jobs.  I had a marvelous time.
Product Comment: The only thing less than wonderful was on the Lisbon extension.  Both within Lisbon and at the airport, the guides were not receptive to who they were leading.  There were a number of stragglers in Lisbon and I requested a slight slow down.  He said he's sorry and slowed down for 5 minutes at most.  Then he would start talking before the stragglers caught up.  Nice enough guy, and knowledgeable, just not sensitive to the poor walkers.  Same thing at the airport.  She met us and ran way ahead, not paying any attention to the few people trying to follow.  Then when we caught up, she asked if we were okay with escalators - one person had two very large suitcases, a backpack and a carry on (helping me).  Are you serious?

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Lisa Soderlund, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Burgundy & Provence (LYS-ARL) 25, tourdirector=20101-50140
Service Comment: See my previous paragraph. Excellent service that goes above and beyond expectations!
Product Comment:
We loved the excellent service provided by the staff. The food was excellent, and the quality of the cleaning was stellar - both were as good as the food or cleaning we got in a 5-star hotel after our cruise. Any time we sat in the lounge together, we were greeted with prompt service and smiling welcomes. The names of some of our favorite cruise personnel (apologies if I spell them wrong):
Boyko, Petru, Ivelin (butler), Laszlo (sommelier), Sabrina (cruise director).
Some of our party on the first dinner meal felt the water tasted strange like filters that hadn't been changed, so for the remainder of our trip, we were given Gerolsteiner mineral water which was something they didn't have to do for us but did. It was greatly appreciated and went above and beyond all expectations in my opinion. Amazing service and kindness to our needs.
We loved the French lounge singer as well as the Spanish family of musicians/dancers. Excellent on-board entertainment this trip. The black & white party was fun and had good music too.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Elizabeth Weisenberg, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (BUD-PRG) 25, tourdirector=20109-50191
Service Comment: Wonderful.  The servers at dinner were there to please.
Product Comment:
We decided on the Uniworld cruise specifically because it offered the Jewish Heritage tours. However we were extremely disappointed in the Vienna tour.  We were rushed through the tour and were not allowed time to go into any of the buildings.  The only thriving synagogue in Vienna has a 10 am tour open to the public but this was not offered to us on our guided tour.   We had to make our way back after the Uniworld tour to go into that building.   What a complete waste of time and effort.   Poor planning on Uniworld's part.   The other tours in Budapest, Reagansburg and Prague were very worthwhile and well planned.

I will say that the cruise staff were great.  

The ship is in need of a little update.
The stateroom we had on highest floor was very small.  In order to open the 3 dresser drawers you had to sit on the bed and be careful not to bang your knees.    The balcony was also a disappointment because there was no real balcony.   It was an operable sliding door with a guardrail.  Is this called a French balcony???

The fellow travelers we met on the cruise were wonderful and made up for anything lacking by Uniworld.  They made our experience very pleasant and memorable.
Thus I rate my overall cruise experience as AVERAGE.

I believe since Venture Capitalists acquired Uniworld the cruise line quality has declined.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Dale Vizcarra, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Grand France (ARL-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20130-50303
Service Comment: Excellent- prompt, courteous and friendly.
Product Comment:
Absolutely fantastic experience from start to finish!  
Purchased via Costco travel.  
Suggestions would be as follows:
More transparency regarding excursion opportunities- the fact that most masterpiece experiences could be combined with regular excursions would be extremely helpful to know.

Assistance with transfer to river boat would be helpful- providing better pricing on pre tour hotels- block rates are provided on other lines that are lower than what you could otherwise get ( opposite is true with uniworld)

Small change tipping of guides and drivers- inconvenient to the traveler.  Would prefer tipping of guides and bus drivers should be all inclusive to cruise price- just as it is for cruise line staff.

Dining staff was excellent in every way- as was food.  On the Paris/normandy cruise the dining chairs, although beautiful, are difficult to get in and out of-heavy and awkward.

A mention to cruisers regarding festivals/ public holidays/ special events occurring at the time of cruising through areas would be helpful also.

Absolutely did not appreciate ( may consider another Cruiseline because of this) the announcement of the Louvre cancellation due to no availability of tickets at the last second!  One would think a Cruiseline of this caliber and reputation would ensure this experience for its clients.  Extremely disappointing to many guests and ironically- we were able to secure an excellent guide on our own( who obtained tickets for us and at a lesser price)

On the final night, all staff members were introduced and celebrated. As the passengers applauded the crew, we could hear the crew members clapping for one another—a beautiful moment that reflected the team’s strong bond and mutual respect.

Despite the initial disappointment with the ship being older and some dinner meals were not quite up to par, the warmth, dedication, and teamwork of the staff turned our experience into something truly special. Their spirit made the journey one to remember.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Christopher Beatty, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Elisabeth, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51874
Product Comment:
I don’t think I can say enough good things about this cruise.  
Room: French balcony. Nice size and plenty of storage.  The bathroom was larger than Andean cruise bathroom and had nice features and included soaps,body wash, and shampoos. The closet had a safe and probably a dozen hangers. 
The ship: smaller than the ocean cruise ships by far. A top deck with lots of seating and a bar, a 3rd floor bistro/ lounge with drink service until midnight. A coffee machine and water taps for sparkling and still water. Snacks come out a few times per day and there are always cookies available, a second floor reception area and dining room, and the first floor laundry (free), gym and spa room.  
The crew: This is the jewel of the cruise. The cruise manager Daniella is amazing! He made the cruise. He knows about all the stops, the ships, the food and the entertainment .  He is so much fun. Our waiter Adrian (Rocky), kept us well fed and entertained. He made sure our every need was met from the moment we entered the dining area. The sommelier Mila educated us about wine and made us laugh at every meeting.  Irene our cabin steward made our room sparkle multiple times a day.  The bursers, Gonzalo and Daniel, were so attentive and ready to answer questions and make sure every need was met.  
The food: Amazing at every meal.  Breakfast and lunch are a small buffet with some tasty choices. Dinner was a gourmet plated dinner with wine pairings. Some incredible food was prepared by the Chef and their staff. 
The day trips or excursions were fun and informative. The guides were all local to the area and they knew history and little gems of information about the areas we visited. You get free time every day to roam the area or go back to the shop and enjoy the amenities. 
The coach drivers and the coaches: the bus/ coaches were high end and clean. We traveled in comfort when we had to use the coach.  The drivers were world class pilots of these giant vehicles and it’s a treat to watch them navigate the tiny streets. 
The bar tender: Marionella and her staff made drinks nd delivered them to you all day long. I dont think these people sleep!  I tried to order from the bar and I believe they were offended that I did not them them deliver the drink to my table!
Overall I can’t say enough good things about the staff or the trip. I’m sure I missed a few people that made our trip special.
We wanted for nothing and were kept fed and well hydrated the entire trip.  We always felt safe and secure as well as welcome.  
The staff knew our name or cabin number by the end of day 2 and they already knew our drink orders or fav foods.  They do a great job of adding little special touches to everything that happens. 
We will be back and next time we will bring more people with us!  
Thank you add Elizabeth crew!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Mark Shwartz Mekler, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Burgundy & Provence (LYS-ARL) 25, tourdirector=20101-50140

We didnt get to see the complete cooking of the meal, and felt it was rushed. We didn’t get the recipe either. Our memories are filled with soft sheets and comfy beds, perfect cup of coffee and fresh baked bread, sauces created by chef on dishes that we will remember always, sitting in the lounge watching the world slip by, when out of nowhere the perfect cocktail appears, perfectly made, just how I like it. Always smiles, always going to extremes to make your stay perfect, and it was!
Product Comment:
Everything, everything was perfect. The entire crew was above and beyond any reasonable expectation, the service, beyond par. Better yet the understanding by the crew to recognize our needs, how we like things was so superlative that we were quickly spoiled. 
This was our second cruise with Uniworld and will be back again. I wish I could remember all the names of crew members that made my stay so special but from Chef on down I give a very warm Thank you for your selfless devotion to excellence and professionalism. 
With all this, having to end our boat stay in Passau due to low water in the river, was painless. We very much enjoyed an extra day to see the old town and feel that Uniworld handled the situation so well that it was seamless. 
Thank you all so very much, we will see you soon,
 Brian Reagan and Monica Yost.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Derrek Enderley, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Burgundy & Provence (LYS-ARL) 25, tourdirector=20101-50140
Service Comment: Sabrina the cruise director was very good at her job and seems like she really cares.  Laslo the Sommelier is amazing and can honestly do whatever he wants in the wine world.  He put on a tasting in the dining room one afternoon that blew away the two tastings we went ashore for, he also jumped on a bike one day in Arles to source wine because his delivery didn’t show up which most people could be like well I’ll just service them the same stuff again, but not him that’s above and beyond.  Bartenders Dilyanna and Augustin were awesome and made our evenings special, they both really cared about the craft and made sure you were always taken care of.  The front desk staff were great and you had to interact with them more than expected to ensure you were signed up for shore excursions, and they were on the ball, each night making sure you had reminders in the room of what the next days itinerary was tailored to your excursion.  We had a major hiccup in the dining room after a few days, you generally have the same server and he sort of went Jekyll and Hyde.  I won’t name his name unless Uniworld reaches out, but he had a major attitude problem and then never apologized but instead tried to explain his reasoning again, we never provoked this attitude but I also removed myself from the situation to not escalate it.  Robert the service captain in the dining room handled our table from then on and he was a professional.  Service is not an issue on this boat.
Product Comment: I will start off with the Locations we visited were great and the majority of the Staff we encountered regularly were amazing, Laslo the Sommelier was the highlight, Dilyanna the evening bartender in the Leopard Lounge awesome, Augustin the bartender in the Van Gogh Lounge was awesome, Robert the service Captain in the restaurant was amazing, Sabrina our cruise director was awesome and very accommodating but some things were out of her hands.  Now for the not so good, the ship was just ok compared to other ships we saw, and as previous people have stated with river cruising you can’t really use your balcony in port because of other vessels being tied up next to you, but that’s not correctable, it just is what it is.  There was also no top deck outside bar or lounge which every other boat we saw had, and we were envious of those other boats passengers when we saw them out there in the evenings.  Now for the things that can be corrected, when you call Uniworld to speak to an agent about the cruise they state you can get whatever you want food wise 24/7, that is not accurate at all.  Bars close at 11pm and nothing is available after that time unless you want a sad sandwich that was made hours ago, same with between 1pm and 7pm, and if yo get to the dining room for breakfast or lunch a half hour before the end of service they start cleaning up which was disappointing.  Also a major concern/waste of money were the Masterpiece Collection Shore Excursions, I’ll explain.  We did in Mâcon a Land Rover Wine Tasting for $170 a person, because Mâcon is tiny and theirs honestly nothing else to do.  You get taken via vintage Land Rover to multiple Vineyards, tasting 10 wines and then brought back to the boat after a lunch.  Seems like a great way to spend an afternoon in Burgundy wine country right?  It would have been nice if they stated ahead of time it was all white wine which no one drinks from Burgundy and certainly was not worth $170 a person.  We went to one vineyard and one in town co-op which is just a wine store, then to a cute B&B with a great garden for cheese boards not lunch, and that’s where we finally got a small tasting of our first and only red wine.  This seemed more like using us to try and promote white wine in the region which we should not have to pay for, very disappointing and yet it happened again at another stop which I’ll get to now.  Our next masterpiece was in Tain-L’Hermitage, now we certainly could not get screwed in the heart of Syrah country, and certainly not for a tasting that was marketed as a high end tasting with 4 wines, at $270 a person, no certainly we wouldn’t get screwed.  Well, we were wrong again, they used it as an opportunity to try and push white wines from the Syrah Region on us which is just 10% of the grape they grow there, we were pissed and asked the gentlemen running the tour to please try and modify the tasting and he said that wasn’t in his contract with Uniworld.  So we got a tiny tasting of a $19 white, $29 white, $49 Syrah and a $110 Syrah.  Now correct me if I’m wrong but that’s not a high end tasting and certainly again not worth $270 a person, after that we spoke to our cruise director Sabrina who was amazing and she ran it up the ladder on our behalf but nothing was done.  We suggested that they should honestly go on these tours themselves or send a representative along so they can see what actually goes on because this was a money grab.  We also suggested they should consult the vessels Sommelier, Laslo, who was also amazing to see if the value is honestly there before passengers get screwed.  Laslo the Sommelier put on a tasting for $50 in the dining room a few days later that blew both tastings out of the water, and we are seasoned wine people, one person in the group of 4 is in the industry and all of us regularly go to Napa, Italy, Spain, France for wine trips, we just never had been to Burgundy and Syrah country.  This was one of the main reasons for us choosing this vessel because it was on this route.  I have heard nothing from Uniworld since getting off in regards to these issues, have filled out every survey they have given me and am very disappointed and would recommend friends and family look at one of the many other river cruise companies out there.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback which has been forwarded to management for further review. We are sorry to hear that additional Masterpiece excursions could have been improved, but are happy to hear that you enjoyed the locations and the staff. If you would like to follow-up with us directly, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Service Comment: Every crew member was fantastic. The organisation was excellent. Service with a smile and friendly, warm and welcoming.
Product Comment:
This was our fourth cruise with Uniworld and quite possibly the best so far. All the crew were outstanding. Service throughout was excellent, warm welcoming and friendly. The cruise manager Anthony was superb. Despite the challenges of the river levels he managed to secure an excellent alternative excursion and handled the challenging situation of onward journeys with calm efficiency and humour. Adriana from the front desk did an excellent job with all the onward transfers for us. It is testament to Uniworld how things are handled should they not go to plan and we have to say the customer service here was exemplary. 
The food was superb, the restaurant team attentive and friendly, thanks must go to Christina the restaurant manager, the wonderful chef, and of course the stars who looked after us, Kristyan, Andre, Maria, Mike, Renata, Lina, and our cabin was kept impeccable by Piri. The staff are unique and this is one of the main reasons we choose Uniworld. 
The highlight was the Rhine gorge sail on the top deck with commentary by Anthony and also all the places we visited along the way. 
We loved it so much we already booked the next one on board where Anthony helped us with a great offer. We travelled on the first cruise of the season at the end of March. 
Mark at the Uniworld reservations team is always so helpful at helping us with the booking and the trains so thanks must go to him too!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Lorraine O Grady, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51866
Service Comment: Very good.  Our housekeeper, Dory, was very helpful and friendly.  Nuno in the dining room was also very good - Friendly and professional.  There did appear to be quite a lot of new staff being trained.
Product Comment:
We took this cruise in early May 2025.  Overall, the cruise was very good.  The ship was of a very high standard - our state room (French balcony) was well-designed and very comfortable; the food was excellent quality (only comment is we felt it should have reflected more the cuisine of the regions we were sailing through).  We also felt the entertainment, while good, could have better reflected the regions we were in.

The excursions were very good - knowledgeable local guides & some interesting and unusual choices.  We especially liked the vinegar tasting at Doktorenhof, the visit to Marksburg Castle and the trip to Heidelberg.

We felt that we didn't have enough free time in some locations (Koblenz, Cologne, Speyer).  Although there were guided tours in some of those locations we think the itinerary should allow for more time for self-exploring them.  This wasn't helped by the port locations being quite remote in most stops, which didn't allow for going into town in the evenings.

We were also disappointed to miss out on the Alsatian villages (Eguishem & Kaysenberg) due to delays at locks along the river.  We got the sense that some of the rushed nature of the itineraries might have been due to river traffic.

Overall, an enjoyable cruise.

Cabin space: the smallest cabin space. Cannot even fit my two Rimowa suit cases under the bed.  NO refrigerator in the room! NO electric kettle even upon request! The candy jar were not filled to the brim daily/being stingy? 
Dining room: could not save seating for us/party of 8, but saw there were reserve seating for a party of 10?!  Food: breakfast was always the same. Had to wait more than 45 minutes for our entre for dinner often. It was a small ship, so doesnt understand the delay. 

My god mom booked the biggest room on the ship. Room 400? Her coffee pods were fully filled on the daily base. Were rationed to 3 pods/day! Seriously?! Butler service was practically none existence.

ONE good thing on this cruise:
Daniel, the cruise excursion director, was the BEST! He was most helpful in helping calling for a taxi to rush my god sister back to the ship when she was suffering from her gull bladder pain.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Paul Marotta, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (PAS-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20119-50169
Service Comment: Overall, service was great except for the incident described above.
Product Comment: This was our first river cruise.  Uniworld is on top of it's game.  The food, excursions, and service were remarkable for the most part.  The attention to detail was impressive.  What a wonderful way to see the cities and towns of Europe.  Uniworld has the formula down as far as to how to treat a customer etc.  The only reason i didn't give a 5 star rating is because there were a couple of incidents that happened that were out of character for Uniworld.  We signed up for the Progressive Dinner.  We loved the tour of the boat, seeing the kitchen, laundry area, captain's perch, etc.  The dinner after the tour was not great.  The steak that was served was very tough and tasteless.  I am sure it was noticed by the staff that several of us only ate a few bites.  The other incident happened on Thursday, May 15th at dinner.  There were 6 of us and I think 3 of us ordered the Perch dinner.  After almost 90 minutes we had not received our dinner.  For some reason, the other tables around us all received their dinner.  I told one of the wait staff about the lengthy wait for our food, and he said it would be coming out shortly.  A little later out waitress said our meal would be out in about 5 minutes.  Long story short, the food never arrived.  We are now approaching the 90 minute mark.  At this point, our waitress returned and said there was a problem with the Perch dinners that were ordered and we would need to order something else off of the menu.  We didn't understand why we weren't notified earlier of this problem?  Why did they say our food would be out in 5 minutes?  The frustrating part was that we weren't given an explanation.  We assume the kitchen ran out of Perch, but we were never told that.  We all have run businesses before and we understand incidents like this can happen.  It's just that the communication from Uniworld  that night was not great.  Other that these two unfortunate happenings, the Uniworld experience was wonderful.  We will use Uniworld again and we will refer Uniworld to friends and family.  Thank you.

Product Comment:
The land tours were very well executed and informative. In the past few years I’ve cruised with OAT, Linblad and Tauck. These were the best tours I’ve had. Particularly the Last Supper and the gondola rowing class.
The staff on the boat were all wonderful. Kind, helpful, patient. To be commended! 
The food was good to ok.  Coffee was a problem. Coffee served early morning in the lounge was not usually hot. Why can’t there be a large coffee urn set up each morning like there is in most hotels. Many people prefer to have their morning coffee in their rooms before coming to breakfast and such a set up would allow that. Machine made powdered coffee is a very poor substitute. 
The boat was nice but the dining room very crowded. We loved the upper deck.
One problem : there were no comfortable places to sit, neither in the lounge or on the upper deck.  The seating looks nice but is not at all comfortable. You would do well to replace all the banquets and chairs! 
The room was way too small tho we appreciated that the window could be opened.

We repeated the cruise to join friends and weren't disappointed. The stops were similar but his time we tried to different activities - biked along the Rhine and hiked through a vineyard in Rudesheim. I thought dinner was excellent. I didn't really eat breakfast (coffee and croissant) and just had a light lunch. I really thought the staff on this ship were top notch and enthusiastic, perhaps because we cruised earlier in the season and everyone was still fresh. We really enjoyed Ramona, the Cruise Director,, and one of the servers/bartenders, Matthew, from Slovakia, who always had a smile on his face. At first, I was disappointed with the actual ship, since I'm used to the more ornate Uniworld ships. The entry where we checked in was underwhelming. No Murano crystal chandelier? However, overall, the rooms were larger on this ship and the beds faced the windows, which we enjoyed. It was a former Crystal ship and it's nice that Uniworld has options regarding style.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Edwin Kramer Jr, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (VCE-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20175-50512
Description: Cruise & Rail: Grand Alpine & the Jewels of Veneto
Product Comment:
The title of this review says it all as after over one year, Uniworld still did not communicate the correct itinerary to me via their portal. We completed the Venice Lagoon cruise with an extension to Athens and Santorini and no rail was involved. After a year of letting them know of the issue, they still haven't fixed it. Cruise portion of our trip was fabulous and our tour manager Louisa was a true professional with plenty of wit and humor. Excursions were well planned and led by excellent local guides. Our private after hour visit to St. Mark's in Venice was a highlight for me and a moving experience. Being able to walk the streets of Burano at night was also a unique experience and allowed you to see the island at sleep.

Our extension on the other hand was well below expectations. Poor communications both prior to the trip and during the extension. Tour manager was ill prepared, provided little insight in Athens and managed to lose a traveler although not her fault but we wasted time trying to locate he which cut down on visit to the Acropolis museum which is stunning. Hotel in Athens is not near anything of interest and is worn down and in need of an update. Had little chance to enjoy breakfast in Athens as we needed to be out the door at 7:30 and breakfast started at 7am. City tour involves driving around congested streets and being told your passing the palace or tomb of the unknown soldier. Stop at ancient Olympic Stadium never happened as they didn't realize there is no place to drop us off. How does the driver and tour manager not know this fact. First night meet and greet was also a fiasco with tour manager apparently reading our itinerary for the first time with us. 

Santorini  tour manager was better and caldera cruise and swimming/snorkeling was a highlight of this stay. Nice, clean hotel but rooms faced the highway and sleep can be interrupted by noust atv's driving by all night. Promised transportation to city center was never provided so a 30 minute walk was required both ways when we did the tour. Ok if you are able to do it but a struggle if you use a cane.

I would recommend the cruise portion of this trip. Ship and crew were outstanding (bartender had moods but ok, he was overwhelmed at times). Decor matches the Venetian golden years so it respects the Era so don't expect glass and chrome. Cruising around Venice and the lagoon offers extraordinary sights. Entertainment was fun and varied but I have to admit, after a day of excursions, trying to participate after 9 pm was a struggle with early breakfast and off the ship for excursions.

I won't be booking extensions with Uniworld in the future. They contract them out to I believe Trafalgar and this one did not meet mine and Uniworlds standards.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We apologize for the inconvenience regarding your portal issue and are sorry to hear that the extension for your trip could have been better. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the cruise portion of your itinerary as well as our staff onboard. If you would like to follow-up with us further regarding your experience, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jane Hardy-Atkins, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=S.S. La Venezia, tour=Milan, Venice & the Jewels of Veneto (MXP-VCE) 25, tourdirector=20142-51587

Food fantastic! Although for me, dinner was a bit late.
What I didn't like was the lack of information and the drip feeding of important details. I did not know what time the cruise actually finished until I got on board, making it difficult to organise transfers. Your uniworld agent in Australia told me the final port was across the road from the train station so I could walk. In fact it was €80 trip. I was unaware I could add a transfer to Rome, and I would have done this (as a solo traveller with lots of luggage).
Many of the tours were too early and, as these details were unknown until the night before, it was hard to plan the best way to enjoy the holiday. I would prefer full details asap, even at the time of booking would be best.
The ghetto tour and the gondola ride were each €130 and neither offered value for money. Having a 40 min walk to and from the gondola ride caused much distress and frustration for some of the travellers, when we could have got it cheaper right at the boat. Again, with accurate and timely info we could have made informed choices.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Sherry Schneidewind, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (ANR-AMS) 25, tourdirector=20168-49963
Service Comment: Already answered this.
Product Comment:
Thomas (excursions) was fabulous.  Most of the waiters were exceptional.
The Captain was extremely nice.
 Our only complaint was that we came back from a morning excursion on about the 2nd or 3rd day and were told we could not go to our room for a while, they were cleaning the carpet.  Well, cleaning a carpet involves chemicals so we asked to sleep somewhere else that night.  The front desk was not helpful.  "We will look into it" in what I felt was a haughty tone. Much later she said there was a room near us and we could move there, but it backed up to the coffee machine so the closet shelves were itsy bitsy.  We told her we just wanted to just sleep there for the night and got the same "we will look into it".  They left our room unlocked all day to try to air it out.  All our stuff was on display all day while we went for the afternoon excursion..  Finally by the end of the day, the head of housekeeping came to tell us that they would move us into a different room that was empty.  She told us that she couldn't upgrade us for "no reason" but she would make an exception. Seriously? I think they handled it badly.  They never told us ahead of time that our room would be out of commission, they left our door open all day with our things inside and they made us feel like we were being unreasonable.  Who wants to live in a very small room with the door closed and a wet, chemical carpet?  The next day they apologized and that was nice.
   The only staff that rubbed me the wrong way the entire trip was the woman at the front desk.  She never had a smile or a pleasant word.
    We would definitely travel on a Uniworld river cruise again.  Every other staff member was wonderful and the excursions were really a lot of fun.

Service Comment: The ship is lovely but older, and it shows that wear. Especially in the hallways and dining room. The ports of calls excursions felt rushed. And the villages visited were obscure. The coaches used to commune the passengers were first rate. Comfortable and well appointed. The French balcony room was small but excellent. Our room attendant was Aleksandra. She performed her duties exceptionally. The dining service was good and so was the food. Silvia, the bartender was absolutely phenomenal. Her drink concoctions were very much appreciated. By the end of the cruise she knew just what to make me. My husband and I were left behind because the guide gave us the wrong time to return to the meeting point. Daniel, the cruise director was very helpful in navigating how to fix this problem. Uniworld apologized for this and paid for our taxi back to the next port city. They also paid for our lunch and provided a small credit to our cruise account for the inconvenience. All in all, a rating of 7 out of 10.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Michael Miller, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Client Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Sao Gabriel, tour=Portugal, Spain & the Douro River Valley (OPO-LIS) 25, tourdirector=20153-50413
Description: Portugal, Spain & the Douro River Valley
Service Comment:
Excellent.
If the butler leaves a soap gift, why it is from the butler on the card instead of Uniworld? Are we looking for a tip?
Product Comment:
We were very pleased with our uniworld experience in Portugal! The staff on the Douro was enthusiastic and helpful. We really enjoyed the dinner at the Quinta in the wine cellar too. 
After we discovered that our TV only showed the bow view and movies we reported it to the person in charge and after repeated visits by other crew members, it never worked. That was a disappointment because we wanted to get updates and plan changes instead we requested and received a printed copy of the next days plans. However, when a new pope was named we could not watch it in our cabin and had to go to the lounge where music and people were in another mode.
The chef did a great job and the pastry chef entertained us with a cooking demo which she put her heart into telling us all her tips along the way. Her enthusiasm was contagious!
If privacy prevents you from giving us a list of passengers and their hometowns, it is harder for us to recall the the many new names as we all socialize.
The ship’s gift shop was disappointing. The selection was narrow and uniformly upscale. Other riverboats have more local colorful products on board.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Robert Farnsworth, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51843
Service Comment: Excellent  crew throughout our stay. First rate!
Product Comment:
We arrived early, luggage was collected, and we were ushered onto the beautiful ship to enjoy lunch and beverages. Very nice ship (SS Victoria), great crew, and very roomy due to keeping guest count lower than others (like Viking).

We were told the room was ready, it was, very nice Signature French balcony. Spacious, comfortable bed, roomy closet and great bathroom. However, our luggage was supposed to be delivered to our room. We then learned it was misplaced. Not good. Eventually it was found (delivered to the wrong room). Whew. 

Food was excellent throughout our stay. Really top notch with variety and quality. Excellent wines and cocktails all included. Appreciated breakfast and lunch being extensive buffets as we wanted to eat and run not linger. Dinner was a very nice sit down.

Multiple included excursions each day as well as a few special extra cost add ons. We loved three additions - Romantic Heidelberg , Wine Tasting with the Sommelier, and Chef's Table. If you are comfortable self-navigating towns we suggest this in places like Cologne and other small towns. We tired of well done but endless walking tours. We like to wander. But we are very comfortable self-navigating and scouting out what we want to do.

Entertainment on board is minimal. Sad to see a lovely Steinway Grand unplayed during our stay except by me. Thankfully I'm very good. But I would suggest a free crew cabin to a musician for these trips.

Beds were amazing, shower pressure amazingly good, and all the staff were really phenomenal. Strongly approve.

From the warm greetings each morning to the thoughtful touches in the cabin and the personalized attention during meals, it was clear that guest experience is a top priority. The team was not only professional but also genuinely kind and accommodating, creating a welcoming and enjoyable atmosphere every step of the way. It truly felt like being looked after, making the entire journey feel personal.
Product Comment: From start to finish, the cruise was an absolute delight. The entire crew was not only professional but also incredibly warm, fun, and genuinely accommodating—always going out of their way to ensure every guest felt comfortable and well taken care of. Their attention to detail, friendly attitude, and ability to make everyone feel special truly enhanced the overall experience. Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Edward Hryn, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (ANR-AMS) 25, tourdirector=20168-49963
Service Comment: Excellent service from the entire crew.
Product Comment: This was our third river cruise with Uniworld. As with the first two everything about the boat, crew, excursions and food met or exceeded our expectations.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Ellen Seeherman, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (ANR-AMS) 25, tourdirector=20168-49963
Service Comment: All staff, from the room steward to the servers in the lounge and the restaurant, were all lovely and helpful.
Product Comment:
This was my fifth Uniworld cruise. As with them all, the service was outstanding, every staff person went out of their way to be helpful and pleasant. The excursions were all excellent; ironically there were so many, and so good, that for me there wasn't enough down time to relax! 

But there was a serious shortfall that would make me hesitate to take another Uniworld cruise. I am a vegetarian, something that I stated when I booked, and confirmed was in my record before the cruise began. But I found that the vegetarian choices were totally inadequate. Most of the lunch buffets had no hot vegetarian items containing any protein. Not even pizza or grilled cheese sandwiches. The mac and cheese served one day was the best thing they had in all 9 lunches. And while almost all the dinner courses included a vegetarian option, many were not very good. The best was veggie meatballs, they were so good (and other choices so inadequate), that I requested they serve them again to me. 

I also would not consider another cruise on the River Princess. Except for the suites, the cabins, even those with French balconies, were just 151 square feet, and this is frankly too small to be comfortable. The ship does not have a pool, and while for this sailing it would have been too cold to use a pool on the sun deck, it made me realize that in the future, I would only choose a ship with an indoor pool.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Deirdre Senkewicz, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50068
Service Comment:
Exceptional! Everyone was kind, efficient and knowledgeable about the events and offerings! Never was there a moment when we felt unsure about something.
Our needs were anticipated and met at all times. A wonderful experience.
Product Comment:
Such a wonderful excursion on the Seine River from Paris to Normandy and back! From the moment we arrived on the ship the wonder began. Everyone was friendly, helpful and accommodating to us, even though we arrived a bit earlier than the posted embarking time!
The Joie de Vivre is a beautiful ship! We were thrilled with our cabin and with the beauty of the layout and design of all of the public areas of the ship, including the lounge and dining room. 
Pierre was well informed about our trip excursions and so helpful in facilitating the trips and answering any questions or concerns that we might have!
Our dining room assistants were exceptional as well! I have some food allergies and Mariela, who attended to our table, was right on it, and made sure that any of my dining requests were “safe.” She even brought me an alternate dessert when she realized that the offered dessert contained nuts!
In the lounge the attention to detail was just as wonderful. Pablo (Pablito) and Eva were extremely attentive to us, and knew when the group of us arrived that our friend would ask for some “nibbles.” It was always ready for our arrival!
Although I have mentioned these few helpful attendants, it is by no means suggesting that the others on the ship were less so. Quite the contrary. We always felt as if no request was too small or a bother! Bravo to all on the beautiful Journey de Vivre ship!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Pierre Boivin, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=River Tosca, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the River Tosca (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=20162-51300

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Janet Olner, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Client Direct, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (ANR-AMS) 25, tourdirector=20168-49963
Service Comment:
The staff were all friendly and personable, which is important to me.  They weren't just 'doing their jobs', although they did their jobs very well!
The dinner service was quite slow on a few occasions, but that's not really surprising when 100 guests all arrive at the dining room at the same time.
Product Comment:
The itinerary was very good.  We were on the last sailing of the season, so we were prepared for not seeing tulips in all their glory...and we were right.  We visited the tulip bulb farm and Keukenhof Gardens on the very last day of availability.  The flowers were all being cut and removed the very next day.  The flowers were not all in the greatest condition, but it was still wonderful to see.  If this is important to you, I suggest that you make sure you book an earlier date.  The cruise itinerary was actually changed so that we arrived in Haarlem earlier than originally scheduled; if it hadn't been changed, we would have missed it all.
The staff onboard were all marvelous.  The local guides were mostly very good.  The guide we had in Nijmegen could have been better; we felt some of his comments were somewhat inappropriate.  I don't remember his name, but he had a student with him, who he mostly dismissed throughout the entire tour.  
I did not enjoy the Fabrique des Lumiere experience.  It dragged on too long for me, and I do experience motion sickness when trying to watch objects flitting around on screens or walls.  I would have preferred a shorter presentation of the various artworks that didn't add animation to them.  It made them appear cartoonish. 
We were in a deluxe stateroom (River Princess), which is the same size as the classic and french balcony rooms.  I expected it to be small, but I did not expect that it would not be possible to physically separate the twin beds.  They were made up as individual beds, but due to the furniture choices they could not be moved apart.  Narrower (and more practical) side tables would have allowed space between the beds.  Also, the bathroom was surprisingly cramped.  I travelled with a friend, and we managed.  But if my husband (6' 2", 260 pounds) had been with me, he would have had a very challenging time showering.  And he definitely would not have been able to dry off in the bathroom.  Our room was on Main deck, pretty much right at the water level.  Many times throughout the cruise our window was blocked by the pier. 
I was again surprised at how many people with mobility challenges were on the ship.  And then complained about the 'speed' of the walking tours.  Europe is an old country, and was not built to North America's accessibility standards.  None of the walking tours that we took could be considered fast-paced; we actually had trouble staying at the slow pace at which the guides were walking.  I would stronger suggest that you consider abilities before committing to any river cruise.  They just aren't designed for people who need walkers or wheelchairs.
And on that note, The Netherlands is a bike-friendly country (as advertised).  So much so, that tourists are at risk of being injured if they linger too long in a bike lane.  And we (North Americans) are not used to dedicated bike lanes being where we are used to there being sidewalks.  We were warned continuously throughout the cruise about the bicycle mentality of Amsterdam, and they did not exaggerate.  So be prepared and aware.  
Overall, I very much enjoyed the cruise (the food, the staff, the entertainment, the excursions).

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Joseph Kavalsky, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (ANR-AMS) 25, tourdirector=20168-49963
Product Comment: The Cruise was very nice. Tours were done well and the tour guides for the most part were knowledgeable. Cabins were kept clean. Staff was friendly. Bar personnel were truly responsive as well as the waiters & waitresses. Food choices were fine and appetizing. I would have liked to see more activities on board during the day especially if you didn't go on a local tour. Sitting & relaxing until the next meal was OK for a while. But some other routines such as exercising topside would have been greatly appreciated.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Marlene Groves Fleming, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (ANR-AMS) 25, tourdirector=20168-49963

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Ian McNeill, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Sao Gabriel, tour=Portugal, Spain & the Douro River Valley (OPO-LIS) 25, tourdirector=20153-50413
Description: Portugal, Spain & the Douro River Valley
Service Comment:
All of the staff were excellent. We enjoyed the evening talks by the cruise director, which were very informative. As a local to Porto, she was very well informed.

Bar and restaurant service was quick and efficient. The evening entertainment was mixed, but we particularly enjoyed the black and white night where the DJ judged the mood just right.

Our suite was always immaculately cleaned
Product Comment:
Overall, we enjoyed the cruise, but felt that the ship could do with some improvement. The decor in the dining room and bar, which featured leatherette covered couches, did not feel as luxurious as advertised. The space available around the buffet area at breakfast and lunch was very tight, although we appreciate that the ship size is dictated by the geography and the locks on the river. Our suite was nicely furnished with a very nice bathroom. 

The space at the front of the boat described as the bistro was not very attractive, and there was no space available for outside dining.

The river is absolutely beautiful, and the trips off the ship were all good. The trip to Salamanca was particularly good.

We did the after cruise extension to Lisbon and were a bit disappointed with the foodie walk. The guide was excellent, but the establishments we visited were average at best.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Lynne Billing, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (ANR-AMS) 25, tourdirector=20168-49963
Service Comment:
The ding and bar staff provided exceptional service.
The lobby staff needed to smile more and exude a bit more friendliness,
although their supervisor was a great role model for helpfulness and warmth.
Product Comment:
We had a wonderful experience with Uniworld again on the River Princess for Tulips and Windmills. The tulips at Keukenhof Gardens were beautiful beyond description and our excursion to Kinderdijk to ride bikes through the famous windmills was a highlight. All the local guides and city walking tours provided were excellent. The ship was lovely, if a bit retro. The cabins were smaller than on the SS Catherine but very functional with adequate storage. The bathroom was the only real disappointment- really small but did have decent storage and a very good shower head. Most of the staff, especially in the Lounge and Dining room provided excellent, warm service. The food and cocktails were really good. We particularly enjoyed the expansive lunch buffet.
We really enjoyed the river cruising experience after years of  upscale ocean cruising. We will definitely cruise with Uniworld again. They made the trip seamless and provided great attention to detail.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Gregory Kizeik, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Sao Gabriel, tour=Portugal, Spain & the Douro River Valley (OPO-LIS) 25, tourdirector=20153-50413

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Virginia Mcclure, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50068
Service Comment: The service on the Joie de Vivre was exemplary. From the beautiful and comfortable staterooms, to the magnificent restaurant, to the tour guides and cruise director, all staff made our cruise user-friendly and stress-free. Again, personal kudos to David, Pedrito, Andreia,    Valya, and Benoit. Kudos as well to those behind the scenes making our food and excursions so spectacular.
Product Comment: Our group of four ladies in our 70´s had a spectacular time on our Paris to Normandy cruise. None of us had been on a river cruise before, and we were struck by how relaxing and comfortable the experience was.  This was in large part due to the staff's genuineness as they connected with us on a personal level and made us feel at home.  Of particular note were our compassionate stateroom attendant Valya, our superb wait staff, including David, Pedrito. and Adriea, and our engineer Benoit, who patiently reset our safe multiple times.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jeffrey Schmoll, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (ANR-AMS) 25, tourdirector=20168-49963
Product Comment: Uniworld are outstanding. This was our fourth with them. The food, guides, luxury, ease, itinerary, tour manager, all were absolutely top notch. They have these river cruises down to a science. Highly recommended. My one critique would be that the clients tend to skew older and the pace on the walking tours can be slow despite them trying to segregate "gentle walkers".

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Brian Rowe, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50068
Product Comment:
We are familiar with Uniworld’s river cruises and were not disappointed with this one. Outstanding!! My wife is coeliac and, as had occurred previously, we were delighted with how the staff cater for this. Their care and attention takes a huge worry load off my wife.
All in all we can’t fault it. The onshore and on board experiences were enjoyable. The guides used by Uniworld were first class and very informative and knowledgeable. Our next river cruises will once more be with Uniworld. High quality does have a name - Uniworld!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Geri Alt, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (ANR-AMS) 25, tourdirector=20168-49963
Service Comment: Everyone was very attentive, but it was definitely understaffed.  When we were not at Vivienne’s table, service was very slow to top off our water, etc.   There weren’t enough bartenders to cover the bar and to be on deck - but they definitely did their absolute best.  Housekeeping was very accommodating.
Product Comment: The reason this review is not a 5 is because of the ship.  While the decor was lovely, it was old and the bathrooms were far from luxurious as in the other boats.  The food wasn’t as good as the other cruises, the steak which was ordered was grizzlely and the buffets were just adequate.   Even though I believe the ship was understaffed, the staff was wonderful.  Our server, Vivienne was fabulous and our bartenders Paolo and his team were equally great.  We wish there was more staff to cover service on the deck, though.  Our housekeeper was so sweet and efficient.  Our cruise director, Tomas, was enthusiastic and on top of things.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Deborah Forsyth Petrov, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (ANR-AMS) 25, tourdirector=20168-49963
Service Comment: Please read prior box for comments on service. Everything is in first box.
Product Comment:
Cruise good and most excursions good. Visit to tulip farm was a disappointment as too late in season and most tulips dead.; this was mostly a shopping trip for gardeners. Tomaz helped me find grave of a family relative in Canada War Memorial Cemetery at Groesbeek.; I appreciated that.  Scheduling Rijksmuseum on last afternoon before flights home following day was poor planning, especially for seniors who need a more relaxing day before a big flight day home to North America - museum should have been in AM. We arranged our own tour of Rijksmuseum in Amsterdam in morning, even though it cost us more to do because of this timing.  Food and wine on boat were good and some of staff were great - Tomaz (cruise Mgr), Maria (housekeeper) and Desislav (waiter in dining room)

Shopping Boutique on ship was open only at weird times - you mostly needed to sacrifice your cocktail hour to shop.  Inconvenient timing if you really want people to shop.  Lovely merchandise but most way too ‘high end costly’ to appeal to most passengers - perhaps that’s reason for shopping hours - not so much shopping will occur anyways because of merchandise on offer. We purchased two pairs of sunglasses (Rayban and Polo)  that we liked.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=David Brown, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Duchess, tour=European Jewels (AMS-BUD) 25, tourdirector=21041-50357
Service Comment: The service from the staff was excellent! Most staff remembered our names and preferences within a day or two. Any questions or needs were responded to promptly.
Product Comment:
First the positive. The staff on this cruise were excellent. Very responsive to needs and questions. The beds were the most comfortable I have ever slept in. On shore excursions we were divided into small groups of approximately 20 people per local guide and all the guides used whisper devices so everyone could hear. The cruise director was always out and about touching base with people throughout the excursions. We had an evening out at the Strauss Experience in Vienna and it was lovely and included a 40 minute live concert!
Now the less then expected. On a luxury cruise I did not expect to be having buffet meals at every breakfast and lunch. The food was good but not excellent. 
The evening entertainment was also lacking. On several nights we had prerecorded music, on those nights most people just went to bed early. One night we docked next to another ship and through the window we could see they were having a lovely folk dance performance. This kind of local entertainment was what had been advertised for this cruise and wasn’t delivered on.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the entertainment and buffet meals could have been better. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed our staff, excursions and beds for your stateroom. If you would like to share any additional feedback, please email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Service Comment:

Product Comment:

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Roger Landrein, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (AMS-ANR) 25, tourdirector=20167-49958
Service Comment: Service & staff could not have been more excellent. Of particular note were the tour guide (Tomas), head of hospitality (Jana) & bartender (Paulo). Well done, everyone.
Product Comment: This was our first sailing with UniWorld after having sailed with Avalon just last year. Tour Guide (Tomas), excursions, staff & service, food quality were all excellent. The age of the Royal Princess, dated decor, musty smell in our cabin room prevented a grade of excellent. We booked our second Uniworld cruise for August but am concerned that the Royal Duchess is as old of a ship as the Royal Princess. Given the higher end price of UniWorld, ships should be newer with more state of the art amenities.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Nancy Dailide, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (PRG-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20110-50242
Product Comment:
The first 2-3 days on the ship our cabin had a terrible musty odor. After much trying to figure it out it was determined to be caused by the heat/cooling unit. They replaced items in it and eventually the smell dissipated. Unfortunately, I am allergic to molds and get migraines, so this was quite uncomfortable for me. Our cabin steward, Vladimir was wonderful. He tried everything in his means to rid the smell. Daniel ( Housekeeping) and Alexander were also very helpful and concerned.
We had wonderful staff in the lounge as well as the dining room. We so enjoyed being served by Istvan and Reena. They took care of our every want!
The only other problem we had was all of the common areas were cold. While the lounges looked inviting, they weren’t as we were always cold and had to wear extra clothing.
In Prague and Regensburg we had tour guides that paid no attention to how many of us were with them. Never counted and apparently didn’t even know or care that we turned back because of how fast they were walking on the wet cobblestones. In Prague  our friends used the restrooms with the tickets she gave them, came out and she was gone. They couldn’t even hear her on the audio box!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=George Bartlett, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50152
Service Comment: Excellent. The staff are exceptional. Nothing is too much bother for any of them. From the captain down. They are always cheerful and happy.
Product Comment: My wife and I  enjoyed the whole cruise experience. Every aspect of it. We especially like the fact that the price we pay is all inclusive. We don’t get landed with another bill at the end of the cruise.. all in all good value for money.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jennifer Parker, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube (NUE-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20112-50268

Product Comment: I loved everything about this cruise.  This was my first river cruise, but I have been on many ocean cruises.  The crew, food, entertainment, the ship, and excursions were wonderful.  I will highly recommend Uniworld to my friends.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Natalia Arellano, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (PRG-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20110-50242
Product Comment:
The vessel and crew were amazing, the food, amenities and overall service are exceptional.
We travel with friends to celebrate both couples 15th wedding anniversary. 
we have a few suggestions, while we understand the cruises are heavily oriented to older people we would have appreciated having a more flexible schedule allowing for our own plans and not necessarily joining one of the cruise excursions. Also the docking /sailing schedule is not clearly communicated before hand. We really didn't see many of the cities listed in the cruise schedule and we had very limited time to explore the cities that we did stop on. 
Overall good experience but would do more research for our next trip and might not necessarily  chose Uniworld again.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Patricia Malec, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Client Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Burgundy & Provence (ARL-LYS) 25, tourdirector=20100-50119

The shore excursions were very well done, and the local guides were very knowledgeable and likeable, adding to the charm of their city. 
My only negative was too much talk on wine, as a non drinker, it felt overdone.
Having traveled on other cruise lines, I found Uniworld, over all, the most enjoyable, anxious to please, very organized and I would look forward to vacationing with them again.
As a vegetarian, there was always a wide variety of choices at every meal, So kudos to the kitchen.. I take home wonderful memories… and a few extra pounds as well.
Marilyn Marwill

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Shulamit Magnus, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50152
Service Comment: Please see my previous comment.
Product Comment: I had never been on such a cruise before and had no idea the level of service that would be provided. It was stellar, exceptional, extraordinary. I have a long and complicated series of dietary needs and was sure they could not be met; the maitre d', Millen, knew the basics of my needs and met me as soon as we boarded, asking all further details so that he could work with the chef and the kitchen. He got my whole list-- and for the entire trip, saw to every meal so that I had what I could eat and enjoy. Every other staff member was similarly eager to seek out needs-- not even waiting to be asked-- and serve them, with friendliness. It was an astoundingly wonderful experience because of the staff. They are obviously highly trained but clearly also recruited because they truly like people and making the experience of guests wonderful. The pool (on board SS Maria Theresa), was a wonderful treat, oasis of calm and beauty, and, unlike some other ships, was indoors so could actually be used (the weather was often cold and rainy). The 24/7 tea, coffee, and hot chocolate was wonderful! It all was. You have my highest rating and recommendation.

Product Comment:
This was our first river cruise, having only previously been on a short ocean cruise and we definitely enjoyed the river cruise a lot more. The ever changing scenery, smaller numbers of passengers, the on board service and shore excursions gave us a better experience than on a large ship. We particularly enjoyed meeting new people, the dining experiences and the excursions on offer. We weren't pressured to do anything and took the opportunity to chill out in our cabin or elsewhere when we felt like it.

The staff were terrific from the cruise director down, always friendly and attentive without being over bearing. I don't want to single out any particular crew member as there were many that were behind the scenes keeping that ship running that we didn't see.

I was very impressed that our group of 4 were met at the taxi and our luggage was whisked away before our very eyes soon to appear in our cabin.
Product Comment: With the exception of a missed stop on Day 2 (Breisach, GE) due to unusual and unexpected lock congestion, this was an excellent trip. Our waiter, Alfredo and our beverage hostess., Adriana, were very friendly, efficient and actually got to know us in the few days together. We were addressed BY NAME by many members of the crew and that was a nice touch. Food was excellent throughout the cruise. My wife especially liked the the Escargot; an unexpected treat for her. I liked all the fish I had - prepared to perfection. We are thinking about our next Uniworld cruise now.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Diane Cassella, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Duchess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (AMS-ANR) 25, tourdirector=20167-49957
Service Comment: The service was amazing from everyone from housekeeping to tour Director. The dining room rooms were very busy, but the weight staff were able to recall many requests such as iced tea with no sugar and Pepsi instead of wine. One waiter, who was not even assigned to our eating section new our names on the second day.
Product Comment:
The River Duchess is an elegant beautifully decorated boat. The sailing was flawless and the care taken with all aspects of the trip was spot on. Most surprising were the size of the Closets which  easily accommodated two full suitcases. The food was elegant and delicious. The tours offered were comprehensive and wonderful, and even a gentle walking group was added for those of us who were in need of a slower pace.
I did think that the level of entertainment was subpar. I had hoped for a bit more local flavor. I also was surprised at the lack of modern plugs as each plug in the rooms required an American adapter. A USB port in  several areas of the room would be helpful.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Claudia Saldarriaga, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51866
