Product Comment: The staff were wonderful and accommodating.  The rooms and common areas were beautiful also.  Food was FABULOUS. I would recommend this cruise to anyone looking for a fantastic, all inclusive river experience!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Demi Riggs, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51866
Service Comment: The onboard service provided was terrific. The wait staff were always nearby to refresh one's drink or get something else. The cleaning crew worked hard to refresh each room twice daily. Martin did a fantastic job running the ship as did the chef in preparing meals. Anthony kept us up to date each day and shared his wealth of information with us all.
Product Comment:
This cruise was just fabulous. The crew really made an effort to get to know everyone. From the twice daily room service to the chefs and servers, every effort was made to accommodate every person. Beverages were always on hand no matter where one was on the ship. The snacks before dinner at the daily briefing was a nice touch. The service exceeded my expectations at every point. Choices at breakfast, lunch and dinner were amazing . Nightly entertainment was a nice way to end each day. Most of the entertainment was enjoyed by the guests but the last night duo was a little lackluster. Every activity, excursion, etc. was done in a timely matter without a delay, extremely punctual! I enjoyed all the excursions, especially the Black Forest. Unfortunately, due to traffic on the river, we missed the first excursion to Breisach. I wish there was a video to show us what we missed. 
The secret Garden Tour was very pretty. However, the museum of African Art and Treasures was a little too specific for me. I can't say enough about the local guides who were all knowledgeable and very informative. During the post-extension Rita te Boekhorst did an excellent job and always had a smile on her face! Thank you Uniworld for a wonderful vacation.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Cynthia Grieve, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51866

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Robert Wiedenmann, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51866
Service Comment: Everything was first class and all staff members were professional in all aspects.  Room attendants ensured everything was spotless and well managed.  The wait staff members were efficient, well-trained and even recalled drink preferences.  We felt very well cared for, even pampered.
Product Comment: The river cruise with Uniworld was incredible.  Everything was first-rate and well considered.  They were so good that the ONLY thing I found to offer as a criticism was that the carpet in the dining room was so plush that it made scooting my chair away from the table difficult.  The ship and its facilities were excellent and the staff were the best we have ever encountered—not just attentive, which we would expect.  All the wait and other staff were professional and genuinely seemed to enjoy their jobs and being attentive.  The hotel manager and dining manager knew and called us by our names and knew our room number, beginning on day 2—how do they do that for 100 guests?  The food was excellent in preparation and presentation—some of the best meals I can remember.  And all of the excursions were very good, well thought out and with excellent local guides.  I recommend them without hesitation and we’ll definitely travel with them again.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Mary-Ann MacDonald, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21650-51809
Service Comment:
Everyone was fantastic from the housekeeping team to the kitchen/service teams and the Sailing team

Do ask for anything you need, a blanket instead of the duvet, different pillow, extra hot water for the coffee (too strong for us) etc.  They are only too happy to oblige with your requests.
The onboard self serve guest laundry was also good.  If you need it try to get there early as later there was a queue.
Product Comment:
Overall it was fantastic.  The Ship and Crew were amazing, a really fabulous onboard experience.
There were some issues with some of the tours, I think it may be a new itinerary with some teething issues.  The first day in Amsterdam the canal boats would or could not get to the pick up point.  The was missed and a night time canal cruise replaced it.  The night time cruise was amazing.  It would be a great idea to offer it on the arrival night as an optional extra.  I'm sure the day canal boat issue will be sorted before the next tour.
A good tour option from Amsterdam is also to Volendam (look it up), and other than the red light experience there was no "walking tour" of Amsterdam
After this we sailed to Harlingen.  Which was about 14 hours up the Ijselmeer and through the Afsluitdijk and lock (which was amazing to see the lock and road transitions), however the next night we sailed the same back to Amsterdam to pick up the Amsterdam/Rhine canal.  I don't think the time spent sailing for the one day at the destination was really good value (destination comments below).  Perhaps you could check out the option of sailing to Lemmer, also in the Ijselmeer but not to the Waden sea (half the sailing time).  Here is the unesco world heritage site of the Wouda steam pumping station, also close enough for a tour to the Planetarium at Freneker and other local attractions like Giethoorn.  Being only half the distance as Harlingen, we would then be able to arrive in Nijmegen some hours earlier.  On our cruise we arrived in Nijmegen at 12 noon and with a tour bus leaving at 1:30 we only had 1 hour to do a self tour of the city, when at least 2 would be required to see the main oldest things.
Getting back to Harlingen.  The tour of the Planetarium was amazing, however we only had 40 minutes for this when 1.5 hours would be the minimum to look at everything properly.  Our guide gave a detailed walk around Freneker (we were the second bus) and we were then late to the museum.  Later the trip to the windmill, the first bus was still there so we had to drive around another 20 minutes as there was not room for two buses.  If this is the situation in the future (2 buses), then the second bus should go to the windmill first, and the two buses swap over, so no need to wait or waste time.

The tour guide in Cologne spent too much time talking about minor local details (and waiting 10 or 15 minutes for the clock to strike the hour), the time would be better spent with a tour in the Cathedral, or just more free time.  The following day the local tour guide walked us around in circles at the start and waiting for many to use the facilities (Really!!  All adults here)
After these first couple of days there are no further recommendations.  The rest of the tours and guides were fantastic.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Janet Niles, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21650-51809

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Joan Moyer, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21650-51809
Service Comment: Already provided
Product Comment:
Evening dinner experience leaves a lot to be desired. Food so do.
Not sure what it should take an hour and a half to receive main course. If you were seated in the rear  of the dining room that’s what happened. 
The head bar tender was wonderful. Remembered my drink preference and presented it without my even having to ask. 
Room attendants did a great job as did the servers in the restaurant. 
Uniworld no longer provides the little treats on the bed in the evening. In the past received a fan, a  small pouch, toiletries, Hermes, and once a little beaded bracelet. Don’t think they are that costly but go a long way to feeling special.
Public restroom stalls on upper floor way too small. Nothing to hold on to. 
Lunches were much better than evening meal. Buffet style more effective and efficient.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=David Kim, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Burgundy & Provence (ARL-LYS) 25, tourdirector=20100-50118
Product Comment: Overall it was a great cruise. Especially our waiter Petru who took care of us very well.  The only negative was the disembarking at Lyon. Finally the cruise director had to run and grab a random taxi from the street.  We’re glad she did but the experience was unpleasant as we were late for the train and the driver tried to rip us off. We felt pampered from the moment we arrived at the port, with the staff taking charge of our luggage and greeting us as we stepped onboard.  Our rooms were spacious by cruise standards, and were well thought out and felt luxurious.  The staff on board, from the ship’s manager, to the wait staff, restaurant manager, sommelier, activities director, bar staff and housekeeping were all outstanding at their jobs. Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=John Harris, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Client Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Remarkable Rhine & Historic Holland (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21650-51809

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Michael Debolt, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Portraits of Eastern Europe (BUH-PRG) 25, tourdirector=20147-50496
Product Comment:
We had a sewer smell in our stateroom room the first six days of our cruise. I complained to the Purser, Nenad Petrikonja, six different days, and on the 6th day he told me I've complained enough and there was nothing they could do about it.

The first few days that I complained, the housekeeping staff would pour bottles of something down the toilet, but it did not get rid of the sewer smell. After complaining 4 or 5 times, they sent a engineer to our room to fix it. He did something to the toilet, but again, the sewer smell remained.

In addition to my 6 complaints, I included an explanation of the situation on a survey that was left in our room sometime between Bucharest and Budapest. The survey request was signed by Hotel Manager, Alexandru Marinescu, so I thought he would be reading my comments. I turned the survey in to the Reception Desk and never heard back.

The sixth time I complained to the Purser, I felt he disrespected me by his comment that I had complained enough and they could do nothing else about the smell. At that point, I argued that we had paid a lot of money for the cruise and should not have to put up with a sewer smell for 6 days. 

At this time, we were in Budapest and some of the crew were changed out. We had a new Hotel Manager. His name was Slaven Teofilovic, and he overheard my conversion with Purser Nenad Petrikonja. Mr. Teofilovic asked if he could help with something. I explained the situation to him and he apologized and said he would get it resolved immediately.

My husband and I went back to our stateroom and shortly thereafter there was a knock on our door. It was a maintenance man and as soon as he entered our room, he knew right away it was the shower causing the sewer smell. He lifted up the the shower drain and there all kinds of black gunk there. We left the room and when we returned after dinner, we immediately noticed the sewer smell was gone. 

My question is why did they treat the toilet for six days when it was the shower drain all along? Why did I have to report the problem to the Purser for 6 days? Why didn't the daily housekeeping staff report the problem before we boarded the ship as the sewer smell was so strong?

After the sewer smell was fixed, our air conditioning stopped working. We were told it was because the French balcony door was ajar. We made sure the door was tightly shut and locked, but it constantly unlocked by itself. Hotel Manager, Mr. Teofilovic, sent maintenance crew to our stateroom. Come to find out, the door was not the problem, it was actually the air conditioner. We had to leave the room for several hours while it was being fixed by two crew members. I don't know what took so long. They also left a lot of dust in the room as they had to take the air conditioning panel off on the counter where we had several items stored. There was even dust balls on the chair we used. They could have done a better job with the clean-up.

I know this is a lot of information, but before I sign off, I want to compliment Hotel Manager, Slaven Teofilovic. He did an excellent job in resolving our issues expediantly once he boarded the ship in Budapest. Too bad he wasn't there the first 6 days of our cruise.

I would hope that Uniworld compensates us in some way for all the problems we endured. This was our first Uniworld cruise and hopefully not our last.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear about the issues with your shower and air conditioner in your room, but we are happy to hear that our Hotel Manager, Slaven, was able to assist you. If you would like to provide us with any further details about your trip, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Kenneth Anderson, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (BUD-PRG) 25, tourdirector=20109-50186

This trip seemed like we got the B team, boat was clean, the crew did a fantastic job, house keeping perfect. Cruise director couldn’t get the times right, on the daily schedule. We asked her about booking another trip and her response was go to the website she couldn’t be bothered. The kicker for me was when we got to Nuremberg we were rushed through dinner, jumped on the bus and 30 minutes later got caught in pouring down rain. The only person that had an umbrella was the cruise director, she was the last person on the bus and never said a word about grabbing an umbrella to the 100 people on the outing. When we got back to the ship no one was waiting with towels or anything to dry us off with, 100 soaking wet people. That was a complete disappointment. Our fair well dinner was nothing more than hamburgers. We had already booked a Christmas holiday cruise so that will set the stage if we use them again or look at other options. The staff on the boat were great, the cruise director leadership was a disappointment.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Sindee Sommer, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (AMS-ANR) 25, tourdirector=20167-49958
Service Comment: Service was exceptional. Our butler "Z" was wonderful and anticipated our every need. Dining room and Piano Bar staff were also tops. Cabin steward for our suite was very prompt and helpful too.
Product Comment:
Enjoyable cruise. Excellent service, excellent food.

However, the itinerary should be amended so that one does not have to choose between Rotterdam and Delft. These are both important cities to visit.  Either there should be an extra day docked in Rotterdam to afford time to see both cities, or at least, the ship should offer a day tour that combines both Rotterdam and Delft.

The major shortcoming of the cruise was the lack of evening entertainment except for the 9-10 PM "show". Although the quality of the 9-10PM entertainment was very good, after 10 PM there was nothing to do except for "elevator style" piped in music in the Piano Bar.  The Piano Bar was the main entertainment room and had a piano in it, but there was no piano player on any evening of the entire cruise! Not everyone wants to go to sleep at 10 PM and the cruise should offer live music for dancing, singing around the piano bar, and just enjoying the late evening. Games or other such activities could also be organized in the evening for additional variety. The nighttime experience on the ship was sorely lacking.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are happy to hear that you enjoyed the overall cruise and food onboard. We are sorry to hear that the visit to Rotterdam/Delft and the late-night entertainment could have been improved. If you would like to provide our team with any additional information about your experience, please feel free to contact us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Product Comment:
This was our fourth river cruise with Uniworld.

Everything was as expected from a Uniworld cruise. My experience, however, was soured towards the end when the bill/invoice was sent to my husband instead of me. The reception at the ship explained that the main office sends the invoice to the main contact on record. I explained that would be me. I always mark myself as the main contact when planning and booking all of our trips, I receive the travel documents and pay with my credit card, yet Uniworld insists on sending the invoice to my husband. As a woman in 2025, I find this almost offensive.

Please consider reviewing gender and Diversity Equity and Inclusion policy and training.  Thanks.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Shokrina Beering, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51855
Service Comment: Dining staff was very good, although one waiter spilled juice on me my first breakfast and the laundry charge ended up on my bill despite the waiter and front desk saying they would take care of it.  Daniel, Andre, were two of the great waiters.  Maria was a bartender who provided excellent service.  The housekeeping staff was unobstrusive.  Rooms were cleaned while we were at breakfast.
Product Comment:
Overall very good.  Wine was better than competitor's. Food was very good.  I would have appreciated more snack options - pretzels, chips, nuts should always be served with alcohol.  Decor in staterooms was very nice and loved the pull out drawer in the bathroom.  We missed an excursion due to low water for one stop and a second excursion because of an accident on the river which delayed our travels.  Something else should have been offered as two is too many.  One was a wine tasting at a castle so we were extremely disappointed.  A wine tasting on board could have been organized.  Running movies in the theater would have been a no cost option for the cruiseline.  
My mattress was lumpy, which was disappointing after all the hype about the special mattresses.
Dining staff was very good.
I had a ship board credit some of which I couldn't use because the inventory in the gift shop was so low.  I did use part of it for a spa treatment, which was lovely.
I was extremely disappointed that no precruise option was available.  We signed up for the cruise almost a year in advance planning on doing both a precruise and post cruise extensions.  There was no reason for not giving the option other than high number of tourists.  That shouldn't be my problem; you're in the business of bringing tourists to cities.  The website continued to state there was a precruise option, so false advertising.  You should have offered transportation to the ship for us.  It was the least you could do after causing us to make our own pre cruise days in Amsterdam.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Laurel Brunell, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Burgundy & Provence (ARL-LYS) 25, tourdirector=20100-50118
Product Comment:
Our desire to go on a Uniworld cruise began many years ago as we walked through your opulent foyers from our ships on other cruise lines to reach “ground level.”  We heard that the entire experience easily matched that grandeur. When planning this trip, we were thrilled to see that the timing of your cruise fit perfectly with our schedule. When we learned that bar service was offered throughout the day and that tipping was included, our travel budget was even more grateful.

As we boarded the S.S. Catherine that Sunday afternoon, we thought for sure that all our expectations would be met, if not surpassed. Indeed, the foyer was splendid. The front desk check in was welcoming and efficient. But sadly, the Uniworld standards, in our opinion, were not met as the ship sailed down the Rhone.

1. As there were only 3 nights of entertainment out of 7 evenings, we were disappointed in the quality of what was offered. The bagpipe and cello concert after a festive welcome reception was clearly anticlimactic. Many of us stayed longer than we wanted only to be considerate of the performers. The next evening, the acclaimed Black and White Party took place and we entered the lounge expecting a more uplifting live performance only to find we’d be dancing to someone’s playlist from a crew member’s phone or iPad. Now granted, we did enjoy ourselves, but being touted as a boutique cruise line, this was not what we expected. Thank goodness, the final performer, Marion Borius, was thoroughly enjoyable.

2.  As to the culinary experience and the restaurant service, several meals were quite good. Still, 3 of ours did not meet our standards of quality for a cruise ship. Namely, one of the fish entrees was overcooked and hence no flavor and was similar to eating cardboard. Another night when the Tagliatelle and shrimp was delivered to us, the guests who ordered it at our table all laughed. 2 shrimp in a bowl of noodles was comical…only it was our dinner. Finally by the time my husband removed the fat from his steak one evening, he was left with a few forkfuls of meat that were edible.  

Two other issues: the staff was very efficient at removing plates during breakfast and lunch but often removed the cutlery as well. We often found ourselves than returning to the buffet to find the cutlery we needed. Perhaps, they could wait until the guest was present to see if they wanted to keep it.

And finally, I am certainly aware that accidents can happen especially in a crowded area with people always moving about. Hence, I was not upset when sauce fell upon my dress from over my shoulder the evening of the Captain’s Farewell. Offers to help me clean it off were given as was a promise made to have it cleaned the next day. I hesitated to just put it in the laundry bag as they would not know the offer was made. I was hoping a notice of some sort would be given to me. It was not. In fact it was never mentioned or monitored the next day. Luckily I was able to clean the soiled area myself. 

3. As I mentioned before, the Tipping Policy was considered by us to be a cost-saving measure that we certainly appreciated. However, since we saw that tips were being accepted throughout the cruise, we then felt it necessary to join in. Point being, it should be noted on your brochure that although a tipping envelope for each of the crew departments is not required, tips for personal service are not exempt.

4. Finally, as lovely as the front desk was the first day, that treatment was not consistent throughout the trip. We were admonished once for not checking in when we did, only to find out that the device was not functioning at the time. And again, when we didn’t check out although we pointed out that the self-checker was not on the counter. We were told it was. We certainly appreciate the system as we wouldn’t want to be left on shore, but we are adults and I would have hoped to be treated like such. The final straw occurred on the last evening when we were discussing leaving the ship the next day at our dinner table. Everyone was talking about the tags needed to identify the luggage. We knew nothing about tags as we didn’t receive them. So off to the front desk I go and when I reported the problem, I was told they were in the envelope with the transfer information. I responded that I knew that but they weren’t in my envelope. I was told again they were, but they would give me more. Not the concern or respect I expected.

In closing, I regret that this review is so negative as there were experiences and staff members that were very entertaining and enjoyable and staff members that were beyond helpful and courteous. But based on your desire to improve your products and services, I am hoping our comments will help you to return to the degree of excellence that we heard was your standard in prior years. We have traveled on AMA and Avalon previously and have been very satisfied. Perhaps our expectations were too high for Uniworld as we thought it would be above and beyond these 2. In retrospect, apart from the bar service, decor and tipping policy, they were every bit as good if not better in some respects. 

I sent this same review to customer service. As I was telling our friends about it last night, I remembered one other minor detail that was lacking and surprised me. No shower cap. Luckily I brought two from the Hilton Garden Inn in Marseilles. However, I left one out on the drying rack the first day and it was thrown away by the cleaning service. From then on, I put the other one in a drawer to keep it safe.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Marguerite Stella, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Bon Voyage, tour=Wine Roads of France & Portugal w/out LIS (BOD-OPO) 25, tourdirector=21249-51322
Description: Wine Roads of France & Portugal w/out LIS (BOD-OPO) 25
Service Comment: Staff is always attentive.
Product Comment:
Trip was mostly wonderful.  Glad to see some nighttime entertainment again.   Bring back the 70s night, always fun.  Did enjoy black n white night. 
All the staff was great, esp Roberto, Anna and Patrick on Sao Gabriel.  
Transfer to airport from Bordeaux to Porto wasn't great once we got to the airport. Uniworld employee should have come onto the bus {as it was pouring rain} to tell us where to go. Instead we all had to get off the bus, She had an umbrella, we didn't.    Also, our boarding passes were printed out in French.  Luckily we used the google translate app.  We told Laura, cruise director,  she didn't even know or attempt to fix it. 
On Bon Voyage, the front section of the boat was constantly being used by a convention of people, so most of us could hardly use it.  Get rid of those throw rugs in the lounge, more people, including myself, almost fell as they got bunched up.   The DJ was great but the music was way too loud, numerous people asked to have it turned down but it never was.    Not fun having to yell to talk with the person right beside you.   And more than one person didn't like the vinyl? chairs and couches.   Too hot.  
Tour guides on both boats were great.  
We had gotten used to, on past cruises, getting a little nighttime gift, special candy, fan, Hermes lotion, bookmark, etc.   One night we got ONE cookie in a bag, another night ONE small box w 2 little gummies in it. The candy jar was hardly ever filled and it was only 1 type of chocolate.  
On Sao Gabriel, there were various chocolates and always refilled.   It's the little things!  
But, all in all, we always enjoy our cruises.   These were numbers 11 and 12 we think.     So we know what we've gotten in the past.  And what we expect in the future.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Katherine Kerns, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (PRG-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20110-50241
Service Comment: Excellent service and professionalism by all.
Product Comment:
This was our first river cruise and we found it to be a very relaxing.  We really enjoyed the pace of the boat, the beautiful scenery, great food, service and very interesting crowd of people.  

The excursions were good overall but would love another option offered that is not necessarily a Masterpiece one.  Of course, some tour guides/experiences were better than others.  One was very disappointing where we were to see a boys choir in a cathedral but when we arrived, it was a mens choir in the university chapel.  We understood that it was a school break and the boys were gone but should have been told ahead of time of the changes and we may have made a different choice.  The night cruise of Budapest was completely awesome!

We were very fortunate to sign up for the Celestial Dinner that was outstanding and worth the upgraded cost.   It was unclear except to previous cruisers that you can sign up ahead of time for things like the progressive dinner.  We would have enjoyed that but it was full when we were told of it and could not get off the waitlist.  We enjoyed the option of dining in Schuberts but if it was explained to us we would have done it more often.  We knew you could eat there but didn’t realize until later in the week that you needed a reservation, that they had a special menu AND you can order off the regular menu.  We enjoyed our dinners there very much.  The breakfast in Schuberts was very poor.  I tried several times to get coffee and it was always out and the selection of pastries sub par.  The food served  in general was great and we appreciated the good selection and the honoring of the local cuisine.  The wine sommelier was excellent.  The bartender was excellent as well.  

The ship is lovely and the room well appointed.  We did notice in passing some of the other river boats that they had some table and outside seating on the dining level.  We think that is a nice option. We also feel that the top deck should be better appointed and a real feature.  It would be nice to have beverage service up there.  There was a bartender who circulated but maybe more bartenders or a bar set up there.  

We loved our trip and will be returning to Uniworld in the future for another river cruise.  Overall rating is excellent!

Service Comment:
Service was consistently great.  Our waiters at dinner were attentive and personally friendly.  We did not have much interaction with our steward; our room always was well kept.  The staff in Prague was great, too.

The local guides consistently were informative and helpful.  The guides on the bike ride made it a fun morning.

Product Comment:
Everyone on the staff was friendly and did their best to ensure each passenger had a great time.  Food was delicious.  We especially enjoyed the cruise director narrating the scenery as the boat sailed through the UNESCO valley of vineyards  and apricot orchards and into Budapest.  The lights of Budapest at night were spectacular.  The Black and White party was a lot of fun.

One drawback was the crowds at the major sites we visited in all three cities.  This was the end of April; what are the crowds in the summer?  I now have a greater appreciation of our special tours of Doge Palace and St. Mark’s Basilica during our Uniworld Venice cruise.

Product Comment:

We wasted over an hour trying to find out where to go and what to do and ended up paying for a taxi. We were reimbursed for the cab fare but it still wasn’t a great way to start our cruise. 
The staff and crew couldn’t have been more helpful and accommodating and the food and accommodations were great. We were quite surprised though at being charged for an excursion into Heidelberg.

We took advantage of the laundry service and I am quite particular about our laundry and was pleased that it was handled beautifully. Overall  great experience other than the initial glitch.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Kimberley Robinson, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=S.S. Sao Gabriel, tour=Douro River Valley (OPO-OPO) 25, tourdirector=20113-50446
Service Comment: All in the detail previously7
Product Comment:
I have to say my husband and I were a little disappointed overall with our cruise. Whilst the cruise itself was beautiful, the standard of Uniworld was quite different to what we experienced  with our Uniworld cruise on the Danube a few years ago. To illustrate, our cabin and standard of butler service was nowhere near what we previously experienced. (eg our previous butler found out i liked peonies, berries and macaroons so every few days we would come back to our room with a small thoughtful gesture of this nature). Perhaps this is cost cutting.....
Another factor for us was the poor choice of included wines. Not only was the quality poor which reflects poorly on Douro wines overall which had not been our thoughts previously. Also, champagne was not included in the included wines (Danube cruise included champagne and we had a welcome bottle in our cabin upon our arrival). My husband and I, therefore decided to purchase champagne (which was not cheap) and the boat "ran out" less than 48 hours into the cruise (much to our surprise and all of our other friends on the cruise). This is clearly due to poor planning and management. Another illustration of management and communication issues was the lack of consistency with the ability to order food on the top deck. One minute you could, 5 minutes later you couldnt until we asked for some clarification from Matias who communicated with the chef and subsequently the staff. This was all totally unnecessary and added angst to our experience.
I would like to add that our housekeeping staff were fabulous. Also, a big shout out to bar and restaurant staff (particularly Joal) who went above and beyond to provide genuine service with a smile.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Amy Arons, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50066
Product Comment:
The hospitality crew was not very enthusiastic, warm, welcoming or helpful.  The cruise director, Judith, seemed inexperienced and was difficult to understand. She was not gregarious or perky. Georges, the dining director had a chaotic dining room --short staffed-not trained to clear plates quickly.  I had to bus my own lunch dishes at one point.  Coffee was not always hot.  I had to request a "very hot" pot of coffee each morning.  Cups were only filled halfway unless a request was made to fill a full cup.   Nicolai and Ivan and Michal in the dining room were excellent.  Ralitza had to be coached to smile...seems very unhappy.
Food was well prepared and tasty.  Fruit offerings were meager.  Tiny bowls were displayed with small amounts of berries-often slow to refill. Housekeeping was wonderful--did a fantastic job.
This was not my first river cruise--in fact it was my fourth.  I expected a first class experience and Uniworld's Joie de Vivre did not deliver.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are happy to hear that you enjoyed the food and housekeeping staff. We are sorry to hear that the dining and service could have been improved. If you would like to provide us with any additional feedback, please feel free to contact us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Christopher Sutton, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Client Direct, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Bon Voyage, tour=Brilliant Bordeaux (BOD-BOD) 25, tourdirector=20099-50012

The entire ship’s crew were exemplary: cheerful, courteous, knowledgeable and passionate about providing platinum quality service, day or night. It would be disingenuous to single out any particular individual for praise: they were all outstanding. The Cruise Director made sure everything ran like clockwork both on board and ashore, even in the face of some unseasonably dire weather for the first half of the cruise.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Shui Kum Wong, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (BUD-PAS) 25, tourdirector=20118-50151
Product Comment:
Pros
1. Small ship, trust she should be able to continue sailing along the river even if water level goes high, in most cases.
2. Sufficient storage space eventhough the room was small.
3. Decor not over stylish.
4. The couple performing ballet waltz was good.
Cons

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Hwee Bin Koh, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Delightful Danube & Prague (PRG-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20110-50241
Service Comment: Overall, it was very good.
Product Comment:
Overall, we have enjoyed the cruise experience with UniWorld. However, we would like to leave a few comments below for your attention:
1. Food - the variety and quality of the food on board was very good. However, I would like to suggest that whenever if you have Asians onboard, it would be good to have some Asian foods like Chinese or Thai on your menu. Also, for a number of occasions the plates are not thoroughly cleaned and there were still some bits of residue food remained on it. Please pay attention to it.

2. The cruise itinerary stated that we will visit 5 Unesco heritage sites. However, until today we still unsure which are these sites we have visited. It would be good that the Cruise Manager can point it out in the daily briefing or the tour guide can introduce it on the individual tour.

3. We noted and were not surprised that majority on the cruise were seniors, some with age over 80 years old.  Some of the guides ( like the one in Salsburg) was going on her own pace without giving due consideration to the age profile of her group.

I hope you will make improvement on your services and I hope to feel it when I am on your cruise next time.

Warmest regards
JT Foo and Koh Hwee Bin (cabin 431)

Product Comment: Overall excellent experience. Food was terrific! Service was excellent, especially Peter in dining room and Vivian and Paulo in lounge. Entire staff was welcoming and very attentive to any needs. Tomas did a great job explaining what to expect on tours and activities. Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Timothy Moore, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (ANR-AMS) 25, tourdirector=20168-49961
Service Comment: Excellent, at times getting change for toilets, tipping of guides and drives was a bit difficult.Great Trip
Product Comment: We thoroughly enjoyed our initial cruise on Uniworld.  There were many things to love about it, food, WIFI, service, sights, tour guides, etc.  But there are a few minor touches that could be improved.  The store hours and selection were little to non existent.  Might be better off using those funds else where.  The television channels were mostly in German, Dutch or other languages that were not English and this was advertised as an English speaking tour.  I've traveled on other lines that were able to have stations all relevant to the passenger make up..  Based on other passengers comments we felt the organization, directions and control for the excursions left room for improvement.  Some groups went the wrong direction when unloading or loading and no one was there to guide them.  Several times we were unsure which bus to get on for our chosen excursion.  Last is just a personal issue.  There were a couple of groups of passengers who were playing cards in the lounge and when the evening entertainment or preview of the next days activities began they just got louder, talking and laughing.  I'm not opposed to having fun but one evening we were forced to move just to hear the person brought on board to educate us on the beer's of Belgium.  Other travelers mentioned this also.  My concern was that no one from the staff seemed to recognize this or ask them to reduce the volume a bit.  Again this may just be my desire to show consideration for others or being too polite but it was irritating during those few situations.  We hope to be able to join you again.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Patricia Overall, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (ANR-AMS) 25, tourdirector=20168-49961

Service Comment: The service was outstanding and quite professional. The waitstaff quickly called us by our names, making us feel as if they knew us and cared. Product Comment: Our Uniworld Cruise “Delightful Danube and Prague” was a truly wonderful experience. The ship was superb with every luxury we could want. We had a beautiful cabin. Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Autumn Wade, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Portraits of Eastern Europe (BUH-PRG) 25, tourdirector=20147-50496
Product Comment:
We had high expectations for our Uniworld River Cruise, but unfortunately, the experience fell well below what I was promised. The food onboard was consistently disappointing—bland, poorly presented, and lacking variety. For a cruise that markets itself as a luxury experience, the quality of the meals was simply unacceptable.

The stateroom was another letdown. It was uncomfortably hot throughout the trip, and despite multiple complaints, the temperature issues were never resolved. This made it very difficult to relax or get a good night’s sleep.

Even the common areas were not up to standard. The lounge chairs were hard, outdated, and uncomfortable—definitely not suited for long periods of sitting and relaxing, which is a big part of what you’d expect to do on a river cruise.

Overall, this was not the premium experience I was expecting. Uniworld really needs to address these issues if they want to maintain their reputation as a luxury cruise provider. We will never recommend Uniworld nor ever be a return customer!
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback which has been passed on to management for review. We are sorry to hear that your trip did not meet your expectations. If you would like to provide any additional feedback about your trip, please email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Product Comment:
The hospitality was great n the itinerary well curated covering the quaint destinations though, though not big on some of the masterpiece collection:-
1. olive mill visit was a bit underwhelming it should just be a regular itinerary not something we should pay for extra;
 2. the chef during the la Mirande cooking class was disorganised and it was hard to follow as there was no structure to his cooking ie what he is doing etc if it’s for appetiser, mains, dessert etc and no recipe too. we missed certain steps as he cooked it himself. I had to take notes, arranged them myself and shared it with the rest as nobody had any clue. Still, I loved the experience of cooking in the 19th century kitchen in the iconic hotel.
3. loved meeting the truffle hunting labs but  them just dig in the little lawn outside their home underwhelms (was expecting a bit of a hunt in forest) though the truffle oil and produce were wonderful and the lovely labs.
Separate note, one of The waiter accidentally poured champagne on my velvet coat (his foot tripped on the chair) and I was appreciative to the laundry madam who cleaned my coat and attire so quickly and efficiently and on the house.

The events manager debriefing daily sounds rather matronly she can tone down a notch :). 
The daily process of group pairing for coach logistics can be more organised as we often don’t know which group or number to tune to on the ‘gizmo’. 

All in all, it was lovely and perhaps will try other ships next.

Product Comment:

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Marcia Yampolsky, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Charter, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. Sao Gabriel, tour=Douro River Valley (OPO-OPO) 25, tourdirector=20113-50443
Service Comment: Fantastic crew!
Product Comment:
The ship staff were all excellent. I didn’t enjoy a lot of the tours. I understand the weather changed some plans, but some of the tours were not good. We had a guide that loved embellishing every fact with blood and gore, beheadings, skinning alive on and on. In the bus he started talking about his childhood, at length, no one cared. The worst was when he talked about lambs brains being a delicacy. Cramming all the brains into a glass tank at the butcher’s and the brains getting stuck on the glass. That was when I was compelled to yell out for him to stop!  I have never had to behave that way, but it was so gross and graphic. On the same tour, we were given 45 minutes to shop. Shop at the same stores that have the same merchandise at every single store. When we walked around the corner to the bus, there was a beautiful big church and beautiful gardens. I asked him why he didn’t tell us about this area, some of us didn’t have any interest in shopping and I was shocked to see this beautiful attraction that we could have enjoyed. His answer was, he wanted to shock us! He was our guide twice and he really soured our experience. 
I want to reiterate that the staff on the ship were really fantastic! The ship was very, very nice. We really enjoyed the entertainment, especially the local group of singers, many of whom were family members. I hadn’t laughed that much in a long time. The pastry chef prepared a beautiful and delicious Challah bread for us so the Jewish passengers could celebrate our Sabbath. The staff was very nice to do that!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Andrew Zima, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (PAS-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20119-50166

Product Comment:
This was my wife and I 6th cruise with Uniworld, so we obviously enjoy Uniworld River Cruises.
On this last cruise, at 82 I was the oldest on the cruise. You do provide a good balance of active and less active tours. For me, I could have used more down (rest) time. Maybe start the cruise with a bit less active things; let us get rested from out flights.
As always, the food and drink were great, staff even more so.
We met some really nice people and even contacted each other after the cruise. Thank you Andrew Zima

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Itta Winter, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Charter, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. Sao Gabriel, tour=Douro River Valley (OPO-OPO) 25, tourdirector=20113-50443

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Ma Josefina Jalandoni, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Elisabeth, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (BSL-AMS) 25, tourdirector=21690-51863
Service Comment:
The ship had everything we needed and was so well appointed. The rooms though small (expected) had ingenious use of space and there was a place for everything! The food was very good and I loved the inclusion of local fare at some stops. The staff were always courteous, friendly and knowledgeable. 

The croissants, danish, coffee, tea and hot chocolate were delicious! The Asprey toiletries smelled great and were good at cleansing. Very efficient check-in and disembarkation process, made it so easy! The wines onboard had a good variety and I enjoyed the daily wine talks of Mila.

Areas to consider further improvements:
1 Options for pillows (ours were too soft)
2 Option for smoothies in the afternoons not just afternoon tea
3 Consider serving matcha lattes for a healthier addition to teas and option to coffee
4 More brown bread options - rye, pumpernickel, multi grain not just white bread with afternoon tea sanwiches
5 Have a menu for the drink list (like when we go to a restaurant) as there is so much choice but its nice to know what's available vs anything you want
6 More local fare options with meals as I really enjoyed the sausages, fish n chips, pork knuckles and chocolate fondue. Consider serving raclette or cheese fondue, weiner schitzel also
Product Comment: It was our first river cruise and the experience with Uniworld was top notch! The service, knowledge, efficiency of the crew from the cruise manager (Martin), hotel manager (Fernando) and our captain who sailed us thru most calmly! Will only choose Uniworld for any future river cruises.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Robert Cotter, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Charter, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. Sao Gabriel, tour=Douro River Valley (OPO-OPO) 25, tourdirector=20113-50443

Another concern specifically for her was the size and layout of the room as well as the fact that there was no chair in the room. Because it was a charter trip the lounge area was used many hours for presentations to the doctors aboard so there was really no area to sit aside from on the bed and that was very uncomfortable. I think I will have a difficult time convincing her to do another river cruise.
On a positive note, the food was fabulous and the staff so kind and caring. Because of the smaller size of passengers it was easy to meet and enjoy chatting with many of the cruise staff. Shout outs to the Hotel Manager, the Sommelier, serving staff, bartenders etc.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Cynthia Perry, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Bon Voyage, tour=Brilliant Bordeaux (BOD-BOD) 25, tourdirector=20099-50009

Our chosen excursions and tours were very good and informative, I especially enjoyed the Bordeaux walking tour and bike riding. Thankfully the weather was fairly kind which didn’t spoil our holiday. Product Comment: Our Bordeaux cruise was a wonderful experience in every way. The excursions, entertainment, food and staff were outstanding. Attention to detail was evident in every encounter.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Nancy Behme, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Charter, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=S.S. Sao Gabriel, tour=Douro River Valley (OPO-OPO) 25, tourdirector=20113-50443
Service Comment: All staff were friendly and responded to requests promptly.  If they couldn't solve the problem right away they created a timeline to let me know when I could expect a solution.
Product Comment:
The S. S. Sao Gabriel is a beautiful ship with great facilities and very freindly crew.  I really enjoyed the one week cruise.   
I recognize that this was the first cruise of the seaon and all the staff were working out some of the things that can only be done once you have guests on board.  They were apologetic for any errors and acted upon requests and recommendations promptly!
The most significant criticism I have was with the menu.  The food was almost always too salty. In fact there were several selections that I could not finish due to the salt.  I recognize that certain cultural dishes would have been created with saltfish, but the mushroom soup should have been edible.   
The pastry chef, Lilliana was a delight.  I loved her live cooking demonstration!   I also loved the delicious foods she made every day.
I appreciated the numbering system for identifcation of foods with potential allergens.  I did have to ask about several items on the buffet tables though as not everything had a label.  I do not have the risk of anaphylaxis, but for those who are vulnerable it would be essential. 
On another cruiseline the passengers with food allergies have a private meeting with the chef and Maitre D to confirm the foods to avoid.  Each waiter is aware of the guests in question and are very careful to check with the chef when there is any question about an item on the menu.

Overall these were minor issues and I am happy to recommend Uniworld River Cruises to anyone!

Also the cruise was designed perfectly as far as stops and sightseeing trips were concerned!  Special mention to Martin the cruise manager, who was very personable and I believe got to know all the passengers, Fernando the hotel manager, Katarina our server who got to know our likes and dislikes at the beginning of the cruise and the very knowledgeable sommelier whose name I forgot, but who was knowledgeable and friendly!
A special mention also to all the tour guides in the various towns who were very knowledgeable about the places where we stopped.
Altogether a wonderful trip which I would recommend to everyone!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Amy Menard, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Danube (PAS-BUD) 25, tourdirector=20119-50166
Service Comment: All of the staff were friendly and helpful. Aleks, the room steward, did an excellent job of cleaning my stateroom.
Product Comment:
Overall, I rate the experience as excellent. There were just 2 problems that arose. One was with the menu in the dining room; it labelled both French onion soup and Caesar salad as vegetarian which they absolutely are NOT. This is distressing for plant based eaters, as we then begin to question what other mistakes are there on the menu, and will we be unknowingly choosing foods that have meat in them or are made with meat broths.
The other issue was that our 1 hour guided tour of Bratislava was only 30 minutes long because the Quietvox device the guide had wouldn't work, so none of us could hear her. They tried to relace it, but the same results occurred. We stood on the dock for 30 minutes trying to get the issue fixed, which cut our tour down to just 30 minutes. No compensation was offered for this.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Peter Longo, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Holland & Belgium at Tulip Time (AMS-BRU) 25, tourdirector=21584-51792
Service Comment: Tremendous service
Product Comment:
Typical super high-quality service and experience we've come to expect on Uniworld (this was our 5th river cruise with Uniworld). No complaints at all.  

One suggestion would be to develop a mobile app that passengers could use as a tool to manage the experience before, during and after the cruise.  The app could include daily itineraries, weather, airport transfers, etc.  The hardcopy itineraries that our left in the stateroom should continue because they usually include a map of the upcoming village/city on the reverse side.  They are also a handy reference to keep on your person.

The app should also include your full account information with past trips, etc.

Also, and unfortunately, this was the first cruise of the season, and the tulips really were not in full bloom, but I guess that's out of your control.

However, all in all, we had a great experience, and we hope that the high standards of Uniworld will not change with the acquisition of TTC by Apollo Funds.  I am very familiar with the typical strategy of PE firms, and I can tell you that many passengers on the ship (of which there were mostly returning Uniworld customers) expressed worry about the future quality standards without the Tollman fingerprint!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Carin Smith, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (SA), ship=River Duchess, tour=Tulips & Windmills (AMS-ANR) 25, tourdirector=20167-49955
Service Comment:
1. Fantastic service by the amazing staff. First I want to say that ALL the staff members on board were great - from the Captain who made me feel safe, right down to whoever is lowest in the order of seniority.

2. Thank you to Coline with her super smile and helpfulness and small chats to me in French - Merci Coline! I noticed how you were always around to check that all went smoothly.

3. A big thank you to Astrid who went the extra mile to arrange with the driver Miguel for me to board a bus early one morning so that I could start an important work meeting online and not have to disrupt it half way through to board - all worked out 100 percent!

4. Of course Rik is one of the best tour managers I have ever had. He makes one feel welcome immediately and well taken care of (and Rik gets my award for best dressed!)

5. Marina and Szimonetta at reception: Thank you for always being so friendly and helpful and for patiently answering my questions no matter what I asked.

6. Consuela - thank you so much for making my room so clean and fresh every day! And for being such a warm and friendly person! You are a real asset to Uniworld!

7. Emil - thank you for creating some interesting mocktails for me! And for your funny wine jokes during the evening briefing.

8. To the chefs - sorry I did not get your names - thank you very much for ensuring that there was something vegetarian for me every day! And all delicious!

9. Norbert - you are another person I would say is a great asset to Uniworld. I wish you a great career ahead. You are very attentive (could basically read my mind as to which menu options I would choose and whether I was going to have the Coke Zero or ginger beer. And he is so strong that he can clear a table of 8 people in one go! I saw it!

10. Bhagoj (sorry I am not sure of the correct spelling) - thanks for your great service too and for always being friendly and helpful (and my travel companions and I agreed that you look like James Bond in your white jacket!) Thank you for never hesitating to run down to the bar area from the dining room for more ginger beer or Bailey's!

11. The two Vladimirs - thank you very much for your friendly service too. Much appreciated. My glass was never empty when you were around!

12. Petar - thanks for being such a great person - and for serving me endless glasses of Coke Zero with a great smile. You have just the right personality to work with people. Best wishes.

13. Krisztian - you seem more quiet than some of your more extroverted colleagues but I noticed how very hard working you are, especially at the bar, and you always ensured that everyone was helped and quickly too.

14. Costin - thank you for arranging a baked potato for me at short notice! Great service!
Product Comment: This was my second Uniworld cruise and it was once again a top notch experience thanks to a well-curated programme of excursions (including options and additional options to choose from). The amazing staff form the backbone of the quality offering and it is convenient to have on-board drinks and the tip already included in the price. Apparently there is a lady who has done about 50 Uniworld cruises already - and I can understand why!

Service Comment: ABSOLUTELY AMAZING!!! I shared my review regarding the outstanding service in my previous feedback notes. Our amazing cruise experience were due to their service!  PLEASE share it with them so that they may be recognized and receive our sincerest thanks and how much we appreciated all of their fantastic service.  Our Cruise Manager, Tania (amazing), Goran (he was one of the best people we met in all of France!), our Sommelier (expert!), Violeta, Georgi, Our entire waitstaff (Nicolai, Ivan, Andrea Maniela) Josip , Milen, Maria, and the entire front desk crew!
Product Comment: My husband and I had an amazing time and the majority of our experience can be credited to the crew! Every single crew member we encountered went out of their way to ensure we had everything we needed and that we enjoyed every moment.  Our cruise manager, Tania is an amazing woman and is OUTSTANDING at her job! She is organized, knowledgeable, her recommendations and guidance is much appreciated and her humor makes the cruise such a fantastic experience.  She is a huge contributor to why we can trust UniWorld to provide us with another wonderful vacation.  Thank you Tania!  Our waitstaff (Georgi, Nicolai, Ivan, Andrea and Mariela) were so kind and we looked forward to seeing them each night!  They were so helpful and responsive to every request, by the end of the cruise we were looking for ways to help them!  Please share our sincere thanks.  Our Sommelier - ROCKED! His charm (dinner will begin in 1 minute and 17 seconds!) was so enjoyable and the wine selections each night were absolutely divine.  We came home and had a glass of wine from our shelf and almost spit it out!  Exposure to great wine and his expert choice selections were more than we can describe! We enjoyed every glass.  Please, if you would be so kind, send us the names of the wines we enjoyed.  Our entire housekeeping staff (Milen, Maria), we are so grateful for their amazing help.  Others we want to recognize and say thanks to, Josip Topic and Goaran Dordic, Violeta Sarbu and the entire front desk staff were all so kind, helpful and knowledgeable. We will be back for another UniWorld experience.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=David Evans, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Elisabeth, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51830
Product Comment: Fabulous trip, our 4th on Uniworld.  The maiden voyage on the S.S. Elizabeth went without a hitch.  The food with Chef Thomas was superb.  Our cruise manager Martin was informative, helpful and light-hearted.  We had a new waiter , Daniel, who did a great job with a large group of 6 people throughout the cruise.  Our cabin attendant,  Nickolay cared for our every need.  Another waiter we always looked for was Mauriz who was very witty and always pleasant to see.  We loved our pleasant, energetic and informative Sommelier , Mila, we hope she will visit the Sonoma and Napa valley wine regions.  Ours night time visit to Rheinstein castle was magical. We loved the Vinegar Estate tasting it was so eye opening.  The Black Forrest tour was inspiring.  A future suggestion would be to spend more time in Strasbourg where Louis gave us a great tour, and a little less time in the Alsatian villages.  Our most favorite part of the trip was the post cruise tour in Lake Lucerne- such a gorgeous , quaint region made special by the incomparable guide Maria.  She was gracious, funny, informative with a fine eye for detail.  Uniworld will need to snap Maria up for a cruise manager before someone else does.  All in all a great cruise.  Danke Schon.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Mary Stadick, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50062
Service Comment:
See comments on first page re: timing of requested service. 
All the service people were excellent.  In the dining room we had exceptional service from Alexie in the dining room and Sophia in the lounge.  I hope I got their names right.  The nametags look cool, but they could be a bit bigger and/or have more contrast for those of us who have aging eyes.
Product Comment:
Overall service and amenities were very good.  I gave 4 instead of 5 stars due to the following:
1. rooms have everything except a clock! So many excursions are scheduled each day that it would be good to have a clock somewhere in the room that gives the local time so that it is not necessary to constantly be checking our phones. 
2. The food served is always top-notch, and the service is exceptional in both the dining room and lounge. An option for a smaller dessert at dinner would be appreciated.  For example, I had a delightful lemon tart one day at lunch. That same size dessert would be ideal for dinner. Even the option of a cookie would be appreciated. Not all of us want a full dessert in the evening, but a little "taste" would be nice.  Have you considered the option of cafe gourmand for dessert, especially on French cruises? 
3. The gathering place and time for some of the excursions felt a bit chaotic.  For example, the level on which we were meeting wasn't always clear and access to the buses was sometimes unclear as well. 
4. This last one is hard to explain. Service is sometimes too fast.  For example, I had a large suitcase which needed to be stored somewhere other than my room.  I asked the front desk to move it, but not until later in the day.  Instead, it was moved almost immediately.  I still had one or two things in the suitcase I needed.  It's ok to wait on service if the passenger indicates they would like it done later rather than sooner.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Dianne Bell, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Elisabeth, tour=Castles Along the Rhine (AMS-BSL) 25, tourdirector=21682-51830
Service Comment: Everything was simply exquisite.
Product Comment:
10 years ago, my husband and I cruised the Rhine River with Uniworld to celebrate our 30th wedding anniversary. To celebrate our 40th anniversary, we decided to repeat this adventure, including both the extensions in Amsterdam and Lucerne.

In summary, this cruise went above and beyond in delivering exceptional service. Every detail was considered, and every crew member seemed committed to making our journey as seamless and enjoyable as possible. A special thanks goes out to:

Martin Jancovic, Cruise Manager, 
Valenta, Butler, and 
Matus, Server.

It was clear that their dedication to providing world-class service wasn’t just part of their job—it was a passion. This cruise was more than just a vacation; it was an unforgettable experience, and we can’t wait to book our next trip. Highly recommended for anyone seeking a truly luxurious and attentive cruise experience.

Many thanks for a memorable journey.
Dave and Dianne

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Ronald Puszynski, loyalty=NO, package=Vietnam/Cambodia, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=Mekong Jewel, tour=Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong (HAN-SGN) 25, tourdirector=20165-50585
Description: Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong
Product Comment:
Unexpected surprises.
My wife and I have been on a few river cruises (Germany, France), land/bus tours (Italy) and custom designed private adventures (Poland, UK, France, Sicily).  Of those experiences, the Uniworld Vietnam & Cambodia along the Mekong trip most closely resembled the latter.  Food was exceptional, so much so that my wife is now looking for Pho in the Chicago area.  Service was attentive, rooms and public areas clean, including my refreshing experience at the on board spa.  Our guide, Quong, had most every answer, including a last-minute-arranged round of golf at Phokeethra in Siem Reap, Cambodia.  And the land excursions lead us to places we would not have known existed to experience a wide variety of local crafts and life along the river.  A recommended adventure.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Michael Hartnell, loyalty=NO, package=Vietnam/Cambodia, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (AUS), ship=Mekong Jewel, tour=Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong (HAN-SGN) 25, tourdirector=20165-50585
Description: Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong
Product Comment: Great tour. Exceptionally well organised by Quang. Had terrific tour guides in Chaay in Cambodia and Tri in North Vietnam, who were always willing to answer questions and go that extra mile for us with courtesy. Thoroughly enjoyed the trip.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Susanne Carney, loyalty=YES, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Joie de Vivre, tour=Paris & Normandy (CDG-CDG) 25, tourdirector=20143-50062
Service Comment: Very good as mentioned in my overall review.  The service is consistently good, from department leaders to housekeeping staff.  Everyone makes you feel special.  Love it!
Product Comment:
I’ve actually done this cruise before, this time I accompanied my sisters and a friend.  It was as good as I remember.  The food was better than ever, which is saying something!  Tanya is an outstanding cruise director (I’ve been with her twice before).  I really liked Camille, one of the guides to Normandy and camembert farm.  Staff overall was fun as usual.  Several of the wait staff were great, Nikolai, Pedrito and Ivan.  The bartender, Violetta, was good too.  She remembered what I liked and always asked if I wanted that.
 I would like to see more broth based soups as opposed to creamy soups some days.  Also the comforters on the beds are way too much and I end up getting too hot every night.  Some options would be good.
  I love the feeling of being totally cared for, and that is certainly what Uniworld delivers.  Thanks for a great time.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Sharon Cohen, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Catherine, tour=Burgundy & Provence (ARL-LYS) 25, tourdirector=20100-50116
Service Comment: Excellent service, the waitstaff were fantastic and attentive and the housekeeping service was above and beyond (minus the beds issue).
Product Comment:
This was overall a good experience. The best parts of the cruise were the deliciously prepared meals (soups and desserts were a highlight, some lunch buffets missed the mark for me) and the exceptionally attentive service. 

There were a few areas that could have been improved for me. First, I didn’t find the beds that comfortable, and we had an issue where our beds - separated as we were father end daughter traveling together - leaned inwardly because the floor was not straight. The housekeeping staff made an effort to fix the more egregious bed, but it was still not straight and we dropped it as we felt we were putting them out. 

Secondly, I was surprised that many of the excursions required an extra fee as I thought the trip was truly all-inclusive. This was a disappointment. 

I probably wouldn’t do a river cruise again as it wasn’t good value for me but it was a fine trip and I have nice memories from the travel with my dad.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Trevor Darrington, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=River Tosca, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the River Tosca (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=20162-51292
Service Comment: Service was excellent throughout. All the staff and guides were great and so enthusiastic about Egypt's history.
Product Comment:
Excellent. Top class hotels and accommodation on board. The food was great and plentiful. The staff were attentive at all times and Wa el the guide was brilliant. The sites were great and a bucket list trip was made very memorable by the Uniworld experience. 
We felt very safe in Egypt and felt reassured by the presence of security personnel, including one on our coach for excursions. 
The only criticism was there were some optional excursions that should really have been included within the price of the trip. One, to the Grand Egyptian Museum, should definitely as there was nothing else to do on the return flight from Luxor that day until the hotel rooms were ready a few hours later.
We took the five day pre cruise excursion trip to Jordan. What a great decision that was. A wonderful country "with noisy neighbours", but again we felt very safe in everything we did. Highly recommended.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=George Griffiths, loyalty=NO, package=Vietnam/Cambodia, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=Mekong Jewel, tour=Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong (SGN-HAN) 25, tourdirector=20166-50596
Description: Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong
Service Comment: It was exemplary - possibly down to the nature of the Cambodian crew being so proud of their country and keen to show it in a good light. European river boats tend to have mixed nationalities of crew on board making it a little more diffictult to work as a team. I'd say that the Mekong Jewel crew were more like a family.
Product Comment:
This was the second time in 12 months that I did this cruise. I loved it so much I booked it again - and have also booked it once more for 2026!
The 3 hotels chosen by Uniworld were superb and the vessel was up to and beyond my expectations. Despite the fact that the vessel was owned by Lotus Cruises it was Uniworld standard and service all the way. The restaurants we were taken to exceeded my expectations. Shore excursions and the local guides were outstanding in every way. To summarise - these were the best "value for money" holidays I've ever been on in my extensive travelling life. The vessel (Mekog Jewel) is of a size and shape of a Mississippi Paddle steamer - making it much bigger then European river boats. Despite this it takes only a maximum of 68 guests. On my most recent cruise it had only 59 guests on board - so more then enough free space and room to sit and move around.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Steven Morgan, loyalty=NO, package=Vietnam/Cambodia, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=Mekong Jewel, tour=Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong (SGN-HAN) 25, tourdirector=20166-50596
Description: Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong
Service Comment: The crew was attentive and polite at all times.
Product Comment: Overall, it was a fantastic trip. Mr. Lee, Uniworld’s lead, was outstanding, as were several of the guides. One guide, Hong, was difficult to understand. We did have one very unsatisfactory experience that Uniworld has not addressed. After paying a significant amount for an upgraded cabin, our cabin was infested with bugs for several days. At least one crew member told us they were harmful and were in several rooms. The crew sprayed toxins in our cabin which required that we vacate for some time. I would have expected that Uniworld would have compensated us.

To whom it may concern:
We took this cruise with Uniworld because of the excellent experience we had with their Egypt cruise along the Nile.  The accommodation on the Vietnam/Cambodia cruise was great, and the staff was excellent.
The food however was not great on the river barge.  My wife and I do not eat red meat that often and prefer fish.  I order fish for dinner almost every night and there were two problems: 1. the fish was invariably overcooked (fish should be served moist not dry and crusty) and 2. the fish was overly salted and too much pepper.   The first night, on board, I ordered the salmon dish which arrived overcooked and over seasoned and cold!!

Product Comment: Uniworld brochure says the best all inclusive journey you can get including all tips. This is inaccurate. The first thing you see at reception is a gratuities box . We were made to feel awkward if we didn’t leave additional tips. Jules Verne all inclusive includes ALL drinks. Uni world charges for drinks outside dinner and happy hour. The gem excursion was 110 dollars yet asked the ticket price in the museum( which was open as opposed to exclusive for us) and the price was 20 dollars. So 90 dollars for the bus transfer which we were already on. Blatant profiteering!!! The bird watching cruise was a joke.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Emma Wassel, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=ASTA River Cruise Expo (VIE-VIE) 25, tourdirector=23934-55260
Service Comment: Please see other comments where I addressed this.
Product Comment:
I was a bit disappointed with my Uniworld experience, and I am not sure if it is representative of the line.

Pros:    Staff was pleasant in dining room.  Housekeeping was proficient.  Loved the Leopard Bar.   (Pool was cold.  Not sure I understand that...  But, it was lovely).  Self-serve laundry was wonderful (however, no detergent available  - even for purchase.)  Staff on arrival were helpful.

Cons:  However, there was a sewage smell on the ship.  Even though I was in a deluxe, the room was VERY tight. Hard for 2 people to move around, so I can't imagine a lower category.    When there were private functions, and I wanted something from the bar, I was told I couldn't go in.    Eventually, I was told I could order via room service.  It was a bit awkward. Breakfast ended at 8:30 AM every morning with no other options.   This is a bit early for folks on vacation.  Even to get a small yogurt was an issue.   No snacks available between meals - except pastries.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that the stateroom accommodations and dining could have been better. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed you the staff, the Leopard Bar, and the self-service laundry. If you would like to provide us with any additional feedback, please email our team at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Deborah Kuznitz, loyalty=NO, package=India, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=Ganges Voyager II, tour=India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges (DEL-CCU) 25, tourdirector=20138-50661

Service Comment: Excellent service.   The room stewards and other staff did a wonderful job
Product Comment:
Enjoyed the itinerary very much.  The Oberoi Hotels on the land part of the trip were beautiful and deserve their high ratings.  The one in Jaipur was exceptional.  Beautiful grounds and the room was outstanding.  They did a lovely welcome dinner where they wrapped the men’s heads in turbans.
The ship was very nice and the crew was great.  We had some problems getting our electronics to charge even tho we brought pretty much every plug configuration and an adapter.  The ship provided two usb chargers which worked, however there really should be usb plugs in the room.
The food on the ship was very bland for us and not very interesting, I assume to cater to American tastes. We spoke to our tour director and the chef modified some of the dishes for us which helped.
The tour guides were very good and knowledgeable.
My only criticism would be that there were no opportunities to shop for trinkets to bring home.  In Jaipur We were taken to see Jewelry, expensive inlaid marble items and $4000 rugs.   I really wanted to shop for a Saree and some embroidered little items for the grandchildren.   I was not the only one unhappy with this.
