Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Elizabeth Fischer, loyalty=NO, package=India, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=Ganges Voyager II, tour=India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges (DEL-CCU) 25, tourdirector=20138-50661
Description: India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges
Service Comment: The staff was amazing! Every minute on board was perfect!
Product Comment:
This was an incredible experience! We learned so much about India. We saw the cities and the country, while far from seeing all of India we feel we got a good feel for Indias culture, history, people and beliefs. 

Our guides were first rate, I don’t know if we have ever had any so well versed in all aspects of their country as ours were. Every time we turned around was a “wow” moment and our guides were there to tell us what we were seeing, the significance and answer all our questions. 

The hotels were incredible, so comfortable and relaxing after the chaotic cities, each was a peaceful oasis within the bustling cities. The staff in each hotel was very welcoming and gracious and food was delicious and plentiful. There is nothing like having a view of the Taj Mahal from your hotel room. 

The ship was fantastic. The beds were so comfortable! The food was so delicious, the chef was right there to serve us and answer questions about Indian food. The staff on the ship was so friendly and went above and beyond to ensure every need was met. 

Activities on the ship were very fun and added to the whole cultural experience. The yoga classes were wonderful and introduced me to a yoga I enjoyed and from which I benefited rather than the pain I have experienced in the past. 

The excursions off the ship were very interesting and the perfect length. It was nice to see the countryside after visiting the cities. Again, we learned so much about life in the villages that we feel we got a good overview of India. 

I can’t recommend this trip enough. Definitely a five star experience

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Bernadine Cruz, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Sphinx, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the S.S. Sphinx (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=23862-55055

Product Comment:
I have been on several other river cruises and tours.  I have never been treated to such impeccable service from the moment I was met at the airport to the my final farewell.  
The pace was was brisk but not overwhelming.  The choice of venues we visited was well thought out and captured the essence of the Egyptian experience.  The cruise ship was wonderfully appointed with staff that made you feel like a cherished guest.  
I am a foodie and the cuisine did not disappoint.  We were offered a wonderful array of ethnic food and Westernized options.   
Our Egyptologist was outstanding.  I left with a much better understanding of this spectacular ancient society and its impact on mankind.  He had a marvelous sense of humor and his patience when having the 'herd cats' (keep our tour group together) was noteworthy.  
It was the little extras like having the wait staff anticipating my morning coffee, to our guides negotiating with the locals to help us get the best deal for purchases that were unexpected and endearing.  
I would highly recommend Uniworld to all my friends and a cruise on the Nile is a bucket list must do!

Product Comment: In general the cruise was good. However 1-2 guides were not easy to understand in English and gave too many details and went on tangents. . The cruise staff and dining staff are the best. One complaint was that we were charged $140 penalty upon arrival as our Vietnam to Cambodia transfer  ticket name didn’t identically match the one on the passport.  We were told this on day 1 of the cruise manager briefing. He also said that each trip this is an issue with travellers and that this airline is very picky with names. We feel Uniworld should have warned us of this fact happening with Air Cambodia so we could have corrected it before arriving. Subsequently We contacted Uniworld 2 weeks ago while on the cruise for a refund but they have not replied yet. It’s not the amount of money, it’s the principle of it. The cruise manager told us that now they plan on changing the flight to a different company due to this recurring issue with air Cambodia Meanwhile handling of this issue has been poor which puts a damper on our overall experience.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Adriana Hajas, loyalty=NO, package=India, pbbbooking=RA - Charter, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=Ganges Voyager II, tour=India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges (DEL-CCU) 25, tourdirector=20138-50660
Description: India's Golden Triangle & the Sacred Ganges (DEL-CCU) 25
Service Comment:
Crew has to be commended for excellent work. Their dedication was above and beyond. They were helpful, knowledgeable, funny, very artistically inclined.

The chef Mayuk and his crew, had made our experience a delight, understanding Indian cosine is a challenge, that the chef made it look easy, and delicious.

All in all compliments to the Capitan.
Product Comment:
Overall the trip (land and river) was ok, but.... there are some down points to it:

1. The brochure that was presented to us (at the time of booking) was... well,... 5 years old and not up to date. We did not get to see some major objectives as we hoped to (and paid for) i. e. The Red fort in Jaipur, advertised but not seen!

2. because of the schedule issues, we got to see some 2nd hand objectives, like marble factory, some unsignificant small villages or rice fields, and a lot of road side garbage deposits, that were just fill-up.  

3. several issues were encountered when on our "Free-time" we would struggle to obtain restaurant reservations @ locations, India is not a place were a tourist will venture out to eat street food or some sort of local restaurant. 

4. SAFETY, this one was a big issue, while on the trip, there where an instance where we had to cross on foot, a railway crossing WITH the BARIERR  down, but our local guide decided to cross it anyway despite our concerns, in the group there were elder persons (majority of them) and that presented a REAL safety concerns, making us refuse to attend the next objective. The local guide was oblivion to our expressed concerns.  We understood that India is what it is, but... we are  not from there, we are coming from countries where safety is a practice not an adventure.

5. Commuting, well,... we spent sometimes more on the road than in the objective (5-6 hours driving for about 160Km /trip...?). I strongly believe that this is an organizational failure and it could be mitigated differently, logistically a nightmare, India is not a country to drive through it, isn't it? The same applies to the transfer to and from the ship, the "jetty" transfer was logistically a disaster, we spent hours for the bus to turn "right" or "left", once we had actually walked to the boat not wanted to spend time for the transfer.

The GOD things: 
local guides were knowledgeable, venues and hotels were excellent, the cruise ship was old but well maintained and the crew EXCELENT, funny and most of all very helpful, concerned for our security, congratulations to the Capitan. 
Food was good, but on the boat the Chef Mayuk and his crew was above and beyond to accommodate us.

All in all a decent trip, not as high on standards as the one on the Nile (EGYPT), but decent. 

The concerns that we had will probably make us to move for  a while to other similar CME conferences/trips. Sorry!
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are very sorry to hear that you received out of date itinerary information from your travel agent. We would recommend checking our website in the future for the most current and up to date information. We are happy to hear that you enjoyed the food and crew onboard. We have shared your feedback with management for review regarding safety but encourage you to reach out to us at customerservice@uniworld.com if you have any additional information for our team.

The food was excellent and the chef took note of food that we liked and often would bring items not on the menu because he knew that we liked them.  The cruise on the Ganges was a little too long, as there is not much to see that is different and excursions were generally quite short.  We enjoyed the visit to the rescue school in Kolcata where they are doing excellent work.  Personally speaking, I would not visit the headquarters of Hare Krishna as I remember too well that as a cult they did a lot of harm in Canada.  The Uniworld trip was a very interesting and cultural experience that was of excellent quality and we are happy that we did it.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Elizabeth Darling, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Sphinx, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the S.S. Sphinx (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=23862-55054
Service Comment: Everyone was great, and anxious to do a good job/
Product Comment:
We had an amazing time.  Our entire tour was well managed, we were well informed, excellent service and planning.  Probably best of all was our Egyptologist Essam.  No only did he inform and educate us about Egypt, it's history and culture, but he tirelessly led us every step of the way with humor, grace and endless patience.  We had a wide array of ages and abilities and personalities, which he managed and put up with in a kind and professional manner.  The trip would not have been the same without him.  His partner was Ramadan, who also did an excellent job, we just did see him as much as he was assigned to another bus.  
The boat was lovely.  Small, but efficient and comfortable.  The food was great, especially considering you are going down the Nile!  We had beautiful fresh produce everyday, and fun new Egyptian food everyday.  The service was excellent, every steward and waiter seemed happy to help, and quick to solve any issue or need you might have.
The 3 small things that I think would improve the trip were: replace the furniture in the Lounge for more comfortable seating.  The chairs/love seats while visually nice, were not good for curling up with a book, or even sitting for a chat and drink.  Number 2 recommendation is buy a new massage table for the "spa" room.  The table was truly uncomfortable. And lastly (and this might not be possible) is there anyway to heat the pool topside?  The whole top deck was a lovely place to watch the countryside, and see the amazing sites...the icing on the cake would have been to enjoy it in the pool.
Overall it was the trip of a lifetime.  We felt safe, well cared for, educated and in awe of the fabulous sights and experiences.  I highly recommend this trip to everyone!!

Service Comment: Excellent overall, but our cabin attendant did not understand enough English to know how to read the notes we left, and I had to talk to his supervisor. The service by the dining room staff, the people who made things to order like omelettes, and Reception staff were good. The bartender on the top deck was wonderful. Our bus driver and guide Mohamed provided exceptional service. The guide told us what to expect along the way, and handled my carryon bag on internal flights because I could not lift it up the stairs of the airplanes we took on those internal flights. The most difficulty I had was with your headquarters. I and my TA could talk to them one day and get a completely different answer the next. My TA was told someone would meet me at the Cairo airport, but the next day I called, and they said I had no transfer and it was too late to buy one. Since my flight arrived at 11:10 PM and I was by myself, I arranged Welcome Pickups for transportation to the hotel. But then a Uniworld rep greeted me at baggage claim and told me I DID have a transfer! I wish I had kept Welcome Pickupz, though, because it took an hour to drive to the Four Seasons, get the other people checked in, and then drive me to the Ritz-Carlton. That was not explained to me before I got in the van. I had a massage on board, and it was wonderful. Ship crew helped us on and off the ship and directed us to the buses.
Product Comment: The ship was beautiful, with spacious cabins, good food, a good crew and the best Egyptologist ever, Mohamed! I loved going to Abel Simbel, the MEMC, the pyramids of Giza, and and the sunset yea at the Cataract hotel. The hot air balloon ride, the local entertainment that came on board, and the barbecue on the top deck. I toured the bridge, didn’t have time for the galley tour, thought the buses were clean, modern and comfortable, and appreciated the bathroom breaks and guidance at the airports for the internal flights they provided us. We always had bottled water on board the ship and the buses. I thought the evening tour was a waste of money and dangerous, because the local guide led us over uneven terrain in the dark. The boat we took to the island stalled out several times, and I wondered if it were safe.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Kathryn Mullen, loyalty=NO, package=Vietnam/Cambodia, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (CAN), ship=Mekong Jewel, tour=Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong (SGN-HAN) 25, tourdirector=20166-50593
Description: Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong
Service Comment: Great service. No complaints! Any requests we made were quickly met.
Product Comment:
We had a wonderful trip with Uniworld on the Mekong River and then onto Siem Reap and Hanoi. The ship was wonderful, spacious rooms and everything was close together. The staff were so friendly and they amazed us by learning all of our names very quickly! We loved the food… usually eating lunch in the upstairs dining simply because we love the soups and sandwiches while watching the river go by.
It was fun eating with others and getting to know each other and their world wide adventures.
The excursions were informative and well paced. While on the river in Vietnam, using the sampans to head off to an excursion made it feel like a real adventure. We also enjoyed the tuktuk tours of the different cities. Another highlight was visiting the school Uniworld sponsors in a village for the purposes of teaching children English.
On the itinerary it felt a bit daunting to see the number of meals we were on our own in Cambodia, but by then we had more of a handle on how to get around and navigate menus so it became fun.
All of our guides and the tour director, made us feel safe and they taught us so much about the culture and history of the countries.
We booked another Uniworld trip while on board. Looking forward to more adventures.

Service Comment: While on the trip, Spring Tours did provide information and guidance on all the excursions and activities.  The guides were excellent,  the staff on the ship helpful and accommodating. The  ship was great and well maintained,  the staff available and ready to help at all times.
Product Comment: •Egypt 9-14-24 Booking #3664890.  Uniworld does state that the tour is operated by others, which is Spring Tours. We called Uniworld to get more specific details on the tour. Information was scarce and contradictory. Example: Masterpiece Collection excursion Dune Surfing is listed as EASY.  We asked how do we get to the dunes to surf.  We got an answer of I am not sure and another that you get a dune buggy up the dune.  We did not do that excursion when we found that the information provided was not accurate.  Those that went on that excursion said they did have to climb the dune to surf down.  It was not easy.  We heard that an additional excursion for a balloon ride was available by someone who  had recently been on the Egypt cruise.  Uniworld representative we called said there is no balloon ride.  On the cruise, the balloon ride was offered and we signed up for that while on the ship with the balloon representative.  Uniworld should be more transparent on this.  A revised itinerary was presented to us after we arrived which was not necessarily a surprise, we were notified by Uniworld that this could happen,  but Uniworld should know the itinerary and advise us before the trip began.  Uniworld should have better contact with Spring Tours so as to give accurate information about the tour.  Our feeling is the Uniworld is not well enough informed by Spring Tours on the details of the excursions.  While on the trip, Spring Tours did provide information and guidance on all the excursions and activities.  The guides were excellent,  the staff on the ship helpful and accommodating. The  ship was great and well maintained,  the staff available and ready to help at all times.  We expected the farewell dinner to be more typical of Uniworld where all the guests of the trip are together to share the experience and a representative to give departing comments. This was nothing more than a dinner in the hotel restaurant with other hotel guests scattered about the restaurant.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Lawrence Jones, loyalty=NO, package=Vietnam/Cambodia, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=Mekong Jewel, tour=Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong (SGN-HAN) 25, tourdirector=20166-50593
Description: Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong
Service Comment: All staff are incredible.  They clearly are trained to memorize names in short order.  They are personable and friendly constantly
Product Comment:
This was a trip of a lifetime.  One of my favorite vacations.  I was surprised and very pleased by the excursions to see life along the Mekong in various villages.  So much more intimate and realistic than I expected.  Not touristy at all.  I really loved the small group approach of dividing the 60 guests into three groups of 20 which made the tours much more personal.  Lee Trien our director was meticulous in his organization of the tour and very attentive to problems or concerns.  I have to compliment our Cambodian excursion guide, Rous Ry, as one the best part of the whole trip.  His knowledge and personal approach was incredible.  Meals on the Mekong Jewel were excellent especially for a small ship like that.  Breakfast and lunch were better than dinner.  The service and all the crew were incredible.  In fact I don't want to single out anyone.  
   I would highly recommend this trip to anyone with an adventurous spirit and decent mobility.  I think the mobility thing should be emphasized though.
    That being said there are only a few comments for improvement or revision if possible I might gently suggest.  The group lunches when we were in cities were average quality.  This has been true on other cruises with other companies so it may be the best that can be done.  Loved all the hotels, 5 stars.  However the rooms at the Metropole were smaller than average.  Breakfast buffets at all hotels were incredible.
   Comment on gratuities:  it says in all materials that all gratuities are included but then it was frequently mentioned that you could add gratuities to anyone you wanted.  This is uncomfortable.  I don't think you should be allowed to tip individual crew members on ship.  All were great and very hard to prioritize favorites.  Not sure how much to tip tour guides and trip director because I don't know how much of a gratuity they already get.  Also think that Americans are used to tipping more than Europeans making this unbalanced again.  In general I would prefer that company pay everyone enough that tips are not allowed.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Jeffrey Kelley, loyalty=NO, package=Vietnam/Cambodia, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=Mekong Jewel, tour=Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong (SGN-HAN) 25, tourdirector=20166-50593

However, the food was mediocre at best and the restaurants were so loud (especially during dinner) that it was virtually impossible to carry on a conversation without shouting. For this reason alone I doubt we would consider another trip with Uniworld since there are so many other options available. Sad, because we really had high expectations for this trip.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are very happy to hear that you enjoyed our crew, the cabin and the ship. However, we are sorry to hear that you found the dining room to be loud and the food could have been better. If you would like to provide our team with more information about your experience, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Christopher Handley, loyalty=NO, package=Vietnam/Cambodia, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=Mekong Jewel, tour=Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong (SGN-HAN) 25, tourdirector=20166-50593
Description: Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong
Service Comment:
All staff on the Mekong Jewel were superb.  Our butler Thy Thy was exceptional.  There was much getting on and off the boat, often in difficult conditions and the crew were very helpful and patient, especially with the less agile.
However, you should provide tables for two in the dining room, as some couples prefer to eat alone, rather than be surrounded by noisy, hyperactive Americans.  (Our request for such a table was quickly met.)
Quality of food was mostly very good, especially if you kept to the local cuisine. Our cabin suite was luxurious and spacious. Excursions were well chosen and organised.
Product Comment:
This was a logistically complex tour, incorporating a seven-night cruise, when many things could have gone wrong.  It is a tribute to Lee, our superb tour manager, that almost everything went perfectly to plan, and the few things that didn't were beyond his, or anyone else's, control.  It was a truly memorable experience.
The only criticism we have is of the appalling transfer service you offered on arrival at Ho Chi Minh airport.  We were first off the plane and had to wait for well over a hour while six others arrived to fill the minibus.  At these prices, you should have offered us a taxi.

Product Comment: Everything was so well organized. Sam the manager and his crew were excellent on the ship and could not do enough for us. Out stateroom and other facilities were great! The massage gentleman was wonderful.  If the ship was bigger I’d have like someone to do nails and facials.  Our guide/Egyptiologist, Ahmed Tamtawy, was the best in every way!   He keep us well informed to navigate every situation, made the Egypt history come alive, was entertaining, answered every question and gave us a love and respect for the Egyptian people!  The only negative we complained about was the noises at the Ritz which prevented us getting good night sleep on several nights and we came down with colds dur to lack of sleep.  When we arrived at the beginning of our trip, even though we stayed on a high floor,  the party boat on the Nile played music until 5 am.  Upon our return they Riz tried to make it up for us by giving us a suite on the other side of the building. They gave us a heater and then we had maintenance in to fix the freezing cold thermostat.  Unfortunately, they gave us a king bed suite, which we asked for two queen beds, so my husband slept on the couch. The two requests we made were disappointingly, not met - a quiet room and 2 queen beds. Other than that the food was good in every restroom at the Ritz.

Now, why a 4 instead of a 5.  The length ofime at the three Oberoi hotels was far too short. We hardly had time catch our breath and didn’t have any down time to explore the properties.  The tours ere great the sites incredible, but, the bus rides were so long that we were exhausted by the time we got to our rooms and then it was time to go again. 
Uniworld’s customers can well afford another 3 days. Each of the hotel stops should be increased by one day. 
Thanks for a rewarding but exhausting trip!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Isabel Dupre, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Tosca, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the River Tosca (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=20162-51285
Service Comment: The boat team was of a four stars hotel or a private crew. Always there for anything needed or ahead of it, extremely polite and always smiling, we couldn't have asked for better service.
Product Comment:
They promise you the trip of a life time and they do not disappoint.  Hardly back home and we want to go back. 
They have two boats, Tosca and the Sphinx. Both great boats, you can't go wrong with one or the other. We loved the Egyptian decor and felt like being in an Agatha Christie book.  Our egyptologist Sabaa was not only knowledgeable but easy to understand and pretty funny. We enjoyed discovering the history of ancient Egypt and the Egypt of today with him. We had a big comfortable bus where we could leave bags.  They organize lots of activities, we did them all from the balloons ride to the sailboat trip, loved it all. By the afternoon, we would usually sail down the Nile and that was just magic, from the view to the noises..  January weather was very pleasant, some of us used the pool. 
The staff couldn't have been more helpful from butler service to the dining room. They knew all of us by name and made sure we were taken care of. The food was unbelievable, we got recipes for our favorite dishes from the chef.  We stayed at the Ritz in Cairo which was great. We wished we had booked the after trip as we didn't want leave this beautiful country and our new friends.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Olga Hawwa, loyalty=YES, package=South America, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=Aria Amazon, tour=Peruvian Rivers & Rainforest Discovery (LIM-LIM) 25, tourdirector=20145-50626
Service Comment: Excellent! Accommodating to food restrictions
Product Comment:
Overall loved the cruise and experience.. wonderful and accommodating staff. I don’t give it 5 stars for the following reasons.
1- we did not visit Nauta as stated on itinerary and the omission was not addressed
2- tips are included in our cruise price.. it has become an annoying trend with uniworld for the cruise director to mention tipping extra if we want to. (This isn’t the first time we’ve encountered this) I would encourage you tell your directors to stop this practice as we don’t appreciate being guilted into tipping more when we have already paid it.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Mary Blackwell, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Tosca, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the River Tosca (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=20162-51285
Service Comment: Every staff member treated their job as if hospitality and attention to detail were an Olympic event. I have never experienced anything like it before. Abdo always greeted me by name when I entered the dining room and pulled my chair out for me. He also made sure my wine glass was full! The housekeeping staff combed beautiful designs into my blanket for me to discover when I returned to me room and were standing by to let me into my room when I turned the corner onto my hall, so I didn't have to rummage for my key. Noby, from Butler Services insisted on delivering a pot of coffee and some pastries to me at 4:00 am when I had an early morning meet time for an optional excursion, even thought I told him I would just go down and grab a cup of coffee from the reception 24 hour service. Ibrahim was always making sure things were running smoothly and on schedule and made us feel like welcome friends. Abdo in the spa gave me a wonderful massage. And Sabaa, our tour guide, knew his stuff inside and out and presented it to us in an informative and entertaining manner. And he became a dear friend to us all.
Product Comment:
The Uniworld Splendors of the Nile cruise exceeded my expectations in every way imaginable. The large, luxurious staterooms provided amazing views of the Nile passing by and an incredibly comfortable bed to fall into at night after a day of sightseeing and a delicious meal in the dining room.
The bathroom was huge with both a tub and a shower. 
The food on this cruise was absolutely amazing - delicious choices from local selections (my favorites) to Beef Wellington and a myriad of dessert choices as well. The service in the dining room was superb. We were greeted by name when we entered and Abdo would pull my chair out for me and make sure my wine glass was never empty. The Chef was present in the dining room for every meal and took great pleasure in presenting his delicious creations of the day. He also did a cooking demonstration one afternoon on the sundeck which was enjoyable! Also, if we liked anything he made in particular, we could ask for the recipe and he would hand us a print out next time we saw him!
About the sundeck - I think it might be what I enjoyed most. Lying in the sun, cool breeze blowing across my face and just watching life on the Nile go by. It was especially fun to see the children on shore chasing the boat and waving vigorously!
The service on this ship was unlike anything I have ever experienced. One morning, I had a 5:00 am meet time for an optional excursion. Noby, from butler services insisted on delivering a pot of coffee and some pastries to my stateroom at 4:30 am. Incredible. 
The manager, Ibrahim, greeted us all with a smile whenever we returned to the ship and made sure things were kept running smoothly and extended his hospitality to us, making us feel like treasured guests in his home.
Our tour guide, Sabaa was incredible. He has such a vast body of knowledge in his head and he conveyed it in an easy to understand manner, keeping it interesting but also entertaining! He was professional and friendly with a great sense of humor. By the end of the trip, we all felt we were leaving a dear friend. We learned so much about ancient and modern Egypt from Sabaa and I will forever be grateful we ended up with him as our guide.
I also did the Jordan pre-cruise extension and it was absolutely amazing.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Katherine Fitzpatrick, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Tosca, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the River Tosca (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=20162-51285
Service Comment: Every single service was top notch.  The housekeeping, the dining, the bartenders, the spa... everything was of the highest quality and care.
Product Comment: Best Vacation EVER!  The River Tosca and her crew provided the perfect hub from which to experience as much of Egypt as possible in 12 days.  Luxurious and yet personal - I would not change a thing.  Sabaa was an amazing guide to the history of Egypt.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Karen Vorhes, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Tosca, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the River Tosca (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=20162-51285
Service Comment: All of the employees on board the Tosca were friendly and professional.   Everyone we interacted with took pride in his position on the boat.  The employees often anticipated our needs and treated us like family.  Our Egyptologist was very knowledgeable and interesting.  He was also very patient in answering questions.
Product Comment: This was a bucket list trip and it did not disappoint.  Uniworld and Spring Tours put together a first rate itinerary.  Our boat, the Tosca, was beautiful and the staterooms were much larger than we've experienced on other boats.  The crew was attentive, friendly, and very professional.  The food was an amazing and included several Egyptian selections at each meal.  Our Egyptologist was very personable, patient, and most importantly, knowledgeable.  We couldn't ask for a better trip.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Tracy Lee, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Tosca, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the River Tosca (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=20162-51285
Service Comment: For the first part of the cruise, I knew the service was impeccable.  My husband started feeling poorly with a cold around mid week.  The staff made special drinks, soups and rice for him and delivered it to our room on the night he didn't go to dinner.  The following days, they brought him teas and/or soups to the table for each meal to ensure he was well taken care of.  They truly seemed to have our best interest at heart.
Product Comment: I never wanted to go on a cruise.  I saw pictures and heard people talking about their cruise experiences and I was not interested.  My husband researched and found this cruise.  He talked me into it saying it was "top notch".  So... I agreed to go.  This was nothing like I expected.  The River Tosca crew made my first cruise experience amazing.  I don't think this trip can be topped.  The food was definitely 5 star quality and the service was impeccable.  I was seriously spoiled.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Pilar Bescos, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Tosca, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the River Tosca (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=20162-51285
Service Comment: See review.
Product Comment: It has only been one week that we returned from a Uniworld Cruise Splendors of Egypt & the Nile.  It was truly an amazing adventure made perfect by Uniworld and the wonderful people that made this trip one that I will never forget!  We sailed on the Tosca which was a beautiful ship equipped with all the modern conveniences to enhance our experience.  Our room was impeccably tended to twice a day by Amr, Qurashy and Telb who ensured everything was in perfect order in an unintrusive manner.  Our butler, Noby, ensured that every detail pertaining to our room was to our liking--he even found me on the ship every day to ask me what time I would like my morning coffee delivered to my room prior to going to the dining room.  Adel, on the wait staff, always greeted us with a genuine smile and knew our names by day 1 and our daily drink orders for each meal during the rest of our trip.  Abdu, the chef, created the most delicious Egyptian meals that words can not describe--upon inquiring about specific dishes, he would immediately have the recipe printed and left in my room.  I look forward to recreating these.  I learned that Uniworld ships that sail the Nile (Tosca/Sphinx) have their own farm in close proximity where they obtain fresh organic produce to create these delectable dishes.  I must give credit to Ibrahim the cruise director who was personable with each and every passenger, always smiling and approachable and had a perfect command of his ship and staff.  Now on to the egyptologist, Sabaa--I applaud his passion and enthusiasm!  He truly made the mysterious ancient Egyptian culture come to life.  His explanations, knowledge and mastery was instrumental in making our experience enriching and certainly unforgettable.  The itinerary was jam packed but organized to perfection.  I highly recommend a visit to Egypt with Uniworld River Cruise.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=David Lee, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Tosca, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the River Tosca (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=20162-51285
Service Comment: Very personalized service. They will make you feel like the king and/or queen of the Nile.
Product Comment: Me and my wife had never been on a river cruise before . The standard that the River Tosca has set will be very difficult to top in the future. Outstanding service , outstanding food and excursions . If you want to see Egypt and the Nile river , this is the way to go !!!!!

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Andrew Smith, loyalty=NO, package=Vietnam/Cambodia, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=Mekong Jewel, tour=Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong (HAN-SGN) 25, tourdirector=20165-50580
Description: Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong
Service Comment: Staff were excellent. Quality of cuisine questionable (see earlier comments)
Product Comment:
Cabin excellent
Cabin servicing (Pisey) excellent
Bar service excellent.
Pool side service excellent.
Guides excellent
Restaurant staff excellent (friendly and helpful).
Restaurant food should have been excellent but was frequently nowhere near as hot as it should have been and often overcooked (prawns, chicken and pork in particular) resulting in the meat becoming hard and dry. Our alfresco evening and the final evening were good and food reasonably hot (which proves it can be done) but the other evenings and lunchtimes well!!!!
Sounding a gong to announce lunch or dinner are served made it feel a bit “holiday camp ish”. If you’re not quick you’ll miss it. Not the standard one would expect from this class of trip.
Wine (inclusive) choice reasonable.
No feed back from our mid voyage survey.
Entertainment reasonable.
Would I use Uniworld again. Well, I’d certainly consider it but might do rather more research and consult reviews before commitment. We organised our own independent add on Ha Long Bay cruise which was excellent, about half the Uniworld price and judging by talking with others (who had done Ha Long Bay cruise organised by Uniworld) significantly better quality.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=John Morrish, loyalty=NO, package=Vietnam/Cambodia, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=Mekong Jewel, tour=Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong (HAN-SGN) 25, tourdirector=20165-50580
Description: Timeless Wonders of Vietnam, Cambodia & the Mekong
Service Comment:
The good:
Ha Long Bay pre-cruise was excellent, including the Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi Hotel and cruising on the Paradise Grand. The tai chi, swiming and kayaking was very enjoyable.  Food and staff on the boat were very good.
Main river cruise - hotels were all very good for their position, cleanliness, service and food. The Mekong Jewel was excellent, including staff, food, comfort, cleanliness.
Most of the excursions were very good.  Organisation of the groups, transport and general information was very good.
The not so good:
Ha Long Bay - the excursion description to Viet Hai Village was incorrect.  We were lead to believe we could walk however when we arrived at the dock we were advised that walking was not really an option due to the distance and no paths.
Main river cruise - the groups were too big on certain excursions.  Where we were in a long line we could hear the tour guide but had no idea what he was looking at/referring to.  The tour guides did not allow enough time for personal exploration as too much time was spent explaning in great detail aspects of the site we were visiting, including reading the boards to us when they were in english.  The tour guides came across as teachers on a school trip with children, rather than treating us as responsible adults.  This detracted from our overall experience as walking round sites quietly with our own thoughts would have been preferable in some cases e.g. Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum, the group was too big and the museum too busy to stay together when viewing the rooms.  The Killing Fields needed more respect, tour guides from all tours (there were six when we visited) were audible at all times, making it difficult to simply think about what when on.  Especially as there were signs asking visitors to be silent as a mark of respect for the deceased.     
Uniworld - communication was not always good prior to the trip.  Emails were not always responded to, email services were blamed.  We repeatedly had to chase for some information.  Issues with the website e.g. trying to make payments and the tour extensions appearing very late.  We travelled at the same time as our friends but booked and paid  separately for the cruise.  At no time were we asked if we wished to be put into a group yet Uniworld sent our friends all the information and did not send us anything or advise us the information had gone to our friends.  Whilst this did not cause us any major issues, it proved confusing as we were lacking information about the cruise until we had spoken to our friends and they started forwarding all emails to us.  This also proved mildly problematical when checking in at the airport desks.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Steven Youngentob, loyalty=NO, package=Egypt, pbbbooking=RA - Group, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Sphinx, tour=Splendors of Egypt & the Nile on the S.S. Sphinx (CAI-CAI) 25, tourdirector=23862-55042
Service Comment: Great service - wonderfully attentive staff.
Product Comment: Overall, the cruise, itself, in terms of sites and cultural experiences was a 5-star rating, despite some negatives, noted below. First the positives: (1) Overall, the ship was beautiful and the staterooms were great. (2) The food and service was over the top terrific. We couldn't ask for a nicer, more accommodating staff. (3) The egyptologist that guided us on our excursions was wonderful. He was incredibly knowledgable and engaging.  Now the negatives: (1) I am personally concerned about the air handling quality on the indoor portions of the ship. I use a medical device for sleeping, and in all my years using this device, I have never seen a filter that was rendered jet black (and this occurred over the course of 7 days). (2) There were two different boat excursions (lasting about an hour each) when we were docked in Aswan. They were a complete waste of time, as they served mostly to offer the boat guides an opportunity to sell us their trinkets. If they continue to be offered I would strongly recommend that guests should skip these. (3) By all accounting, Uni-World does not have a clearly articulated set of procedures for handling sick individual such that other guests are protected. In brief, our bus group had roughly 23 plus people, and one or more of the guests were clearly sick from the first day. Note that, the group spends many hours traveling in the bus over the course of the cruise, By the end of the trip, 50% of the people on the bus were missing excursions and 8/10 of our own travel group became severely sick over the course of the cruise, missing several important sites. Uni-World needs to address this problem!!!

Product Comment:
Another great Uniworld experience. The tour starts with stays at Oberoi hotels in Delhi, Agra and Jaipur which are magical and a huge contrast to the pollution, smoke, poverty and rubbish covering the rest of the country. I found this confronting but despite the challenges the people are friendly. 

Special thanks to the bar and cigar room staff in the Delhi Oberoi. ( two separate areas in the bar thank goodness) 

The tour manager and guides were friendly, and approachable with excellent local knowledge and very helpful. 

The drivers are incredible. You probably need to experience Delhi etc traffic to understand what I mean. 

The staff and crew and the Ganges Explorer II itself were wonderful and very friendly. The entertainment was also very good, as was the food. My husband won’t normally eat Indian but loved the flavours as the heat level was adjusted to western pallets. I found it too much so, but there were plenty of choices.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Darrel Norris, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Victoria, tour=Belgian Holiday Markets on the S.S. Victoria (BRU-BRU) 24, tourdirector=21471-51693
Description: Belgian Holiday Markets on the S.S. Victoria (BRU-BRU) 24
Service Comment: We had a royal suite, and the service was very good. With my husband being sick and unable to make it to meals the butlers were excellent at making sure he had something to eat and plenty of water.
Product Comment:
It was a very enjoyable cruise, however compared to one of their competitors, there are a few things I would rate them very low on. 
1) Uniworld only accepts credit card payment. We do not carry a lot of credit cards and like to keep our limits very low, thus requiring the use of several credit cards. Their competitor allowed a direct payment from our bank account. This was much easier and simpler for us. I would prefer having the option as to whether or not I have to use my credit card.
2) We arrived on board the ship at 10:30 in the morning after flying all night but were not allowed into our room until 4 hrs. later. Consequently, my husband became over tired and was sick the entire cruise and was never able to participate in any of the shore excursions. I feel they should have advised us on making the cruise reservation the rooms would not be ready until 3 PM so we could have arranged our flight accordingly. Or, since they knew there were a few of us arriving earlier, have an area available for us to rest (actually lie down not sit in the lounge) or have our rooms ready earlier.
3) The drop off at the airport was very poor. Again, with the competitor, on the departure flight we were dropped at the departure at the front of the airport terminal and had someone there immediately to direct us to the correct ticket counter and make sure we got through security. Uniworld, the driver of the van merely pulled in dropped us off in a parking lot and unloaded our luggage. The driver did not even indicate where the door to the terminal was or what level we needed to go to. I had to ask where the door was and upon entering the terminal there was a very nice airline pilot who directed us to the proper level for check in.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Joseph Burgoyne, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Client Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Antoinette, tour=Rhine Holiday Markets (BSL-CGN) 24, tourdirector=18317-48071
Product Comment: 1st river cruise...staff onboard was exceptional, can't say enough about each of them. Food selection & quality was excellent. Very disappointed to have to take a bus to markets in all but one stop I believe and the Cologne stop, where we could walk into "town" was totally compromised because the boat had to "sail" on the river for about 4 hours to nowhere seemingly because there was another boat moving into our slip at the height of the day. The choice was walk into town for 2 hours or close to 6. Quite surprised to also have to walk through another ship to board ours on a few occasions including 1st board. Almost never opened the blinds in our room because we were either facing another room on a ship sitting next to us or docked up against a wall. This was quite unexpected. The ship also had one day where there were serious plumbing/pump issues...no toilets working for most of a day. We weren't there to watch TV but many on board had sound issues with the TV's including us. I wish I had better news.....
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We apologize for any plumbing issues onboard. We are also sorry to hear that the rafting of a boat next to ours was a surprise as this is a common experience with river cruising. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the food and staff onboard. If you would like to provide us with any additional details about your trip, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Catherine Burns, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Beatrice, tour=Grand Christmas & New Year's Cruise (NUE-BUD) 24, tourdirector=18286-47988
Service Comment: Most of the staff were polite and friendly,a few incidents of rudeness seemed to be a language issue
Product Comment:
All pre cruise correspondence was excellent and I received everything by post .Flights were well co ordinated for the transfer to the beautiful ship .SS Beatrice which was very luxurious,as there were less than 100 passengers to 68 crew service was very good.
Although the ship wasn’t full,no upgrade was given which was disappointing.Cabin was very clean and my cabin stewardess was excellent.The heating wasn’t working,the bathroom was very cold ,only a towel heater for warmth.I had to have heating on full,I asked for extra duvet but was given a blanket.
Food breakfast was extensive with a special dish each day,lunch was a buffet with local dishes on offer ,dinner very varied with steak,salmon also available.
Drinks,as a cider drinker I was disappointed that none was available.Each evening the wine sommelier gave a talk about the two wines which were especially chosen to match the meal. Also premium wine available to purchase.
Maybe baseball hats could be banned from the Dining Room 
The shore excursions were good but spoilt by certain individual always arriving late ,impacting on the whole coach .The excursion which was an extra was excellent New Year’s Day concert in Vienna.
Entertainment was on offer ,but sadly as some passengers continued to talk through it , spoilt it for others .As we suggested maybe passengers who don’t want to listen could be asked to sit somewhere else for 45 mins .On the last night ,the singer actually stopped and asked was there a party !!!
Health and safety,one passenger left her walker outside her suite in the corridor compromising staff and passengers safety when service trolley was in use .
Highly recommended

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Cynthia Violette, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Christmas & New Year's Cruise (PAS-BUD) 24, tourdirector=18269-47987
Product Comment:
Subject: Feedback on Danube Christmas Cruise
We enjoyed many aspects of our recent Danube Christmas Cruise, particularly the beautiful scenery and the charming towns we visited. We particularly enjoyed our accommodation and were consistently impressed by the service provided by the majority of the staff, including Bjorn, Seve, Catlin, and Isabella in the restaurant and bar, as well as Cici in our room. The itinerary itself was well-planned, and the local guides were informative.
However, we encountered several issues related to the performance of the Cruise Director, Martin.
•Mismatched Expectations: Upon boarding, Martin abruptly dismissed our anticipation of visiting Christmas markets, stating that this was a "festive Christmas and New Year's trip" rather than a "Christmas market trip." This initial interaction set a somewhat negative tone.
•Lack of Passenger Engagement: Throughout the cruise, Martin demonstrated a lack of engagement with passengers. He rarely interacted beyond scheduled announcements and seemed unaware of the presence of Heritage Club members. In addition, he did not introduce some of the local entertainment and was not present to thank them and escort them off the ship (they finished and looked confused about what to do next).
•Dismissive Attitude: On two entertainment occasions, we expressed concerns about the volume of the music. Instead of addressing our concerns, Martin told us to relocate to the back of the lounge. This dismissive response was disappointing.
•Inadequate Communication regarding Christmas Markets: Despite the initial statement, there were instances where opportunities to visit Christmas markets may have been available, but we were not informed.
•Poor Handling of New Year's Eve: The New Year's Eve dance floor was primarily occupied by the ship's Officers and their families, facilitated by Martin who did not address his customers at all. This significantly limited dancing space for passengers and detracted from the festive atmosphere we had anticipated.
We understand that unforeseen circumstances can arise, but we believe that a more proactive and engaging Cruise Director would have significantly enhanced the overall passenger experience.
Despite these concerns, we are looking forward to our upcoming Cruise & Rail: The Sacred Ganges & The Maharajas Express later and hope that Uniworld will consider these observations to improve the quality of service provided by their Cruise Directors in the future.
Product Vendor Reply: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that your experience with our Cruise Manager could have been better. However, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the itinerary and stops. We look forward to welcoming you onboard later this year. If you would like to provide us with any additional feedback, please feel free to email us at customerservice@uniworld.com.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Hamish Kean, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (UK), ship=S.S. Maria Theresa, tour=Enchanting Christmas & New Year's Cruise (PAS-BUD) 24, tourdirector=18269-47987
Service Comment: The staff were generally attentive and very courteous. My stateroom attendant (Sisi) and the restaurant and bar staff were excellent.
Product Comment: Overall, I was disappointed. The cruise did not come up to my expectations. My deluxe stateroom was small. There was nowhere to sit in the evening, apart from on the bed, once the curtains to the French balcony were pulled. I objected very strongly to the captain and officers and their families (including children) occupying deluxe staterooms from 28th December and using all the facilities of the ship used by paying passengers (including excursions). The excursions were of variable quality. Some were excellent. Others were good. Two were a complete waste of time and money. To disembark from the ship with our hand luggage on 2nd January we had to climb up to the upper deck and climb over another ship. We were told the previous evening that the ship was being moved to make disembarkation easier for us! The upper deck was icy and covered in frost and snow and dangerous to walk on, particularly with our bags or if you were unsteady on your feet or disabled. There were no crew members to direct us. The Uniworld representative at Budapest airport was young and inexperienced. We had to find our own way to the BA check-in desks. He did subsequently apologise. The meet and greet at Munich airport on 23rd December was good and efficient. The food onboard was generally good, but not exceptional. The staff were very attentive and helpful. I would not be minded to book another Uniworld cruise or recommend the company to friends.

Service Comment: Superb service!
Product Comment: Our knowledgeable guides, daily adventures, incredible flavors laid out at every meal, classy river cabin … the trip was splendid. Visions of the Nile riverbank floating past conjure up the most delightful memories. From the moment we landed in Jordan for our pre-trip, Ahmed our guide tended carefully to us, bringing us to excellent lunch spots, helping us know how to shop and I’ll never forget the huge piping hot flatbread he brought into the bus one day for us to all get a luscious taste of authentic fresh baked deliciousness. Then, in Egypt, our Egyptologist Mohamed Ragheb was an absolute gem. He was like an adventurous father teaching his habibis with so much passion and excitement. With our special dietary needs, we thought we might get skinny during the cruise, but restaurant manager Ahmed Helmey took excellent care of us at every meal. He went above and beyond to welcome us and present us with our vegetarian versions of the various dishes, and he walked us through the spread to show us our options. We looked forward to his precious greeting each day as he watched over his wonderful staff, including server Saleh who paid careful attention to graciously serving us. Ahmed and Ahmed were our friendly housekeepers who kept our room comfortable and impeccably neat and welcoming. My Egypt cruise is a treasure that will forever brighten my soul.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Anna Clayton, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=Classic Christmas Markets (NUE-FRA) 24, tourdirector=18247-48124
Product Comment: This was my first cruise, and it was fabulous! I feel like I've been spoiled for all other cruise lines because Uniworld is the best. Gourmet food, really attentive staff (1 staff person for every 2.4 passengers). The 4-course dinners ran a little late and it was hard to get enough sleep afterwards but it was totally worth it! I was traveling solo but there was an instant feeling of community and I made friends right away so I had people to sit with for most meals. It was fun to watch the camaraderie of the crew, and they sang us Christmas songs on Christmas and some of them danced along with us for the 70s dance party which was so much fun. The excursions were great, beautiful medieval towns. The Cruise Manager, Ed, was wonderful, he was open to feedback, and when I mentioned that I realized not every tour guide was mentioning Jewish history (different subgroups of the same excursion had somewhat different content) and I was disappointed, he went out of his way and found me a Jewish History museum that was an easy walk from the ship. When a new friend of mine caught a bad cold, she told me that he went out and bought medication for her and the staff brought her meals in bed. This trip changed my life, it made me remember my passion for travel and now I'm going to reorganize my life to make that more possible. It was a magical experience and I can't wait to do it again.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, displayname=Glenna McLane, loyalty=NO, package=Europe, pbbbooking=TW - Client Direct, region=Uniworld (USD), ship=River Princess, tour=European Holiday Markets (VIE-NUE) 24, tourdirector=18273-48070
Service Comment: Excellent!
Product Comment:
I understand the time constraints of being on a cruise and meeting schedules, but the first few Christmas Markets were a real rush job with little time to look. I think that it would be helpful to inform guests of this in advance and assure them that at some of the bigger markets they will have more time to look around so that the first markets don’t end up being a disappointment. Also, I’ve said before that allowing a 5-year-old on that cruise was a mistake. There is no way a young child could have the patience to walk on those town and city tours without becoming impatient and bored and then whining about it for all to hear. This boy was given a whistle/flute toy at the first market, so we got to hear him blow on it the rest of the tour and on the bus. For me, at least this was very annoying. Also, the accommodations on the ship are not geared for entertaining young children, so more opportunities for a child to be bored and everyone close at hand to hear about it. At one mealtime at least, the boy left his table and was lying on the floor in the dining room. 
Other than this, I thought the cruise and service were excellent, and I enjoyed the meals a great deal. I hope to use Uniworld again in the future.

Tags: clienttype=RetailClient, loyalty=NO, pbbbooking=RA - Agency, region=Uniworld (USD)
Service Comment:
Very good in regard to food allergy.
The front desk was very helpful when needed. 
Staff was friendly and performed thier roles well.

"I am loving this vacation" was one of my frequent thoughts during this cruise.  The temples were extrordinary, and so many to see! Try the Hibiscus cocktails if you imbibe.
Product Comment:
Our vacation was beautifully coordinated in all aspects. I greatly appreciated the care of our guides upon arrival and departure with navigation of the airports in addition to transportation to and from the hotels. All of the guides were excellent. They were informative, knowledgable and true professionals. Bottles of water were readily available on the tour bus. 
On the ship, the chef made it a point to go over the meals and to point out what I could and could not eat due to a wheat allergy. The food was  excellent. 
The sites that we saw were awe inspiring. My favorite was the dawn hot air balloon ride, it was spectacular to see from a different perspective as the sun rose. We took the long camel ride, it was and experience I will remember. However, when the camel gets up and kneels down you will be at a 45 degree angle getting up there! So hold on! It was a little scary and I'm not faint hearted. 
Yes, I recommend this trip. Yes, I would do it again. What would I do differently? Bring more cash (USD) in small increments and more ones. This would be for street vendor purchases, where I did not feel comfortable using a credit card.
